input_text:
Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: My friend and I came for happy hour and we loved it! We got three things on the happy hour menu: the chicken & waffle bites, the sausage & garlic pizza, and the buffalo cauliflower. We also each got a Raspberry Smash to drink & they were pretty strong!

I loved the buffalo cauliflower. I make a baked version of this at home, so I was surprised when it came out fried, but it was DELICIOUS. My friend liked the sausage & garlic pizza but I thought the garlic was a bit too raw. The chicken & waffle bites were okay; neither of us liked them too much. I wouldn't order it again.

But our server was a sweetheart, which is why I'm giving Finney's five stars. He kept cracking jokes when he came by to check in on us. Made our experience even better! Overall this is a solid happy hour spot in SB.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Cafes, Mediterranean, Middle Eastern | Review: Basic mediterranean nothing special here. Points for being after midnight go-to. Owner is a nice dude, Kilo or Phylo or something. Staff b.s. around with each other (talk, laugh) so orders take longer than they would if they were concentrating. Seems to attract a more ghetto late night crowd than the Blue Owl & I'm sure being right next to a smoke shop (hookah shop? Don't know I didn't go in) helps with that. Mad amounts of Taxis park in front so unless you like shady looking mexican dudes staring at you while you grub get it to go. Not much room to eat inside, never tried it.

Categories: Caterers, Event Planning & Services, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts, Food | Review: The best ice cream ever!  A bit pricey...
Don't miss the Passion Fruit Cream or the Cup of Joe flavors
I hope Rori's expands its locations to Orange County, CA 
Amazing gourmet ice cream
PS - I'm very picky and they did not ask me to write a review

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Ramen | Review: I was surprised to hear that a new ramen shop had opened up in Goleta and honestly did not have very high expectations at all. Goleta has a lack of good Asian food in general, so I was expecting some kind of pseudo-Japanese-y restaurant serving random noodles because there is no actual ramen in the area to compare it to. To my pleasant surprise, I was wrong and the ramen here is delicious!!!

Having lived in Tokyo for the past year, I have tried many, many ramen shops (including the famous one Michelin-starred Tsuta, which is actually cheaper than this place btw!!) and often find myself missing the convenience and comfort of a good bowl of ramen here in Goleta. This place fills the emptiness I feel sometimes and is comparable to ramen I've had in Japan!

I've been here 3 times now and have gotten the Kuro ramen twice and Aka ramen once. I definitely prefer the Kuro because I love garlic, but they share the same broth so they aren't too different. The tonkotsu broth is suupeeerrr thick and fatty just like it should be, but that also means it feels really heavy and makes you tired after. It's still definitely worth it though and I crave it all the time!! The only thing I didn't really like was the chicken karaage; it was wayy too expensive and really not special in anyway. All in all, I highly recommend this place!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: I got the curry noodle soup it was really good!!! The service could be better tho. I found a little fly in the sauce but it was overall a clean place.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: This is a must visit in Santa Barbara. The ambiance is great, the food is hands down amazing, and the drinks and service here is impeccable.

Came here for dinner on a nice Saturday night - my friend had made reservations for 9 pm (called earlier in the week and that was the only slot available). We arrived around 8 to see if we could get in, and they happened to have a free table for us.

We were seated outside near a small fire pit next to the bar so on a cool southern california night it was perfect. This restaurant is great for groups because the menu is designed for sharing and you can try all the different flavors the Lark has to offer. 

After ordering drinks (I got the Smoke 'n' Roses - that had a great smoky mescale), we were served some of their signature popcorn - AMAZING. We even had seconds! 

For our table of 4, we ordered the Crispy Brussel Sprouts, the Pear and Red Kale Salad, Crispy Smoked Pork Belly, Pappardelle and Lamb, and Roasted Arctic Char. 

The brussel sprouts are a MUST order - the most amazing I've ever had. The kale is nice and refreshing, and the Pork Belly is sweet and tender that it just melts in your mouth (a must order as well!) . The pappardelle is handmade and the lamb was tender and crispy. The Arctic Char had a great flavoring too. 

The entire staff there is accommodating and attentive and my whole party was very pleased. Our waiter even mentioned how all the chefs begin preparing in the morning at 7 am so that all the food served is the most fresh! 

I will definitely be coming back!!

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Ugh...really sad about this place. Maybe I got something super basic, which was the "Pretty Please Sundae." Or maybe it was because the guy that served me was just ok. (He was wearing a backwards hat and a north face jacket). Wasn't rude or anything, but didn't seem very welcoming as the other staff seemed to be. On top of that, it took forever for my order. He prepared the first part of my sundae, which was the ice cream, chocolate grenache and sprinkles. Then he left it out and went to the back to what I think was refilling the whipped cream canister thingy.  

I was waiting on the side and can see him standing there shaking the thing for a good 10 minutes. Naturally, when he finally finished, the ice cream came melted and watery.  I'm like, that's fine, hopefully it's bomb and it will make up for it. Nope.  I think the self serve ice cream that you make your self is about equivalent to what I had.

If I ever comeback, which is a maybe..I'll just go for another specialty flavor which they have so much of.

Categories: Chinese, Food, Dim Sum, Desserts, Coffee & Tea, Restaurants | Review: Great tasting Chinese food at China Pavilion. There were hits and there were misses but overall I would recommend.

The hits:
Silver Sprout Duck Soup - Wooow! Google won't help much with this dish as it is a Chinese Pavilion exclusive. But I took a chance on it and it was great. Broth is like hot and sour but the duck takes it much further. I was under the weather but this soup cradled me like a mother. I only wish the bowl was bigger! 

Spicy Basil Ginger Beef - The name says it all, and the dish is exactly what it promises to be. And it is so so good.

The miss:
Crispy Prawns with Garlic
Weird batter, overall just underwhelming dish. The batter is just weird, see the picture.

Interior and service = solid.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, American (Traditional), Venues & Event Spaces | Review: I found my meal ordinary and average, but quite expensive.   I expect and am willing to pay extra for gourmet food, or very healthy food, but my meal here was neither.   I didn't have a drink.   I gave it three stars, because it wasn't bad food, just average and over priced.  I won't return.

Categories: Breweries, Nightlife, Food, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Restaurants, Brewpubs, Gastropubs | Review: My friend was just walking by the Cruisery and a guy was outside blocking the sidewalk & they yelled at my friend to get out of there zone & he apologized & then the guy said to my friend your a real smart ass , my friend ignored him and went and  ordered food from " Hana Kitchen " ( they have great food ) while waiting for his order another guy came out and my friend said to the guy that other people might get confused also & he told my friend " your a breath of fresh air and you probably will die from cancer soon 

How is that for customer service I want everyone to know about what happened and to think twice about going there

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants | Review: Overpriced as are most restaurants on the warf. However, good place to take kids (they can be loud and not bother anyone) and has a pretty good clam chowder, decent food and drinks that pack quite a punch.
Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Carlito's has a nice outdoor area, but the hostess was snobby and the service was slow. More expensive that the $$ rating shown in the description IMO...

Categories: Food, Bubble Tea, Chinese, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Hot Pot | Review: There are so many different combinations of hot pot and every one I tried was delicious! My personal favorite would have to be the Korean Army Stew. It really hits the spot for Asian food! The service is also amazing and their COVID outdoor seating is nice and seems very sanitary as well. Definitely must try!!

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Best ice cream ever!!! Great service, let us sample as many flavors as we wanted and gave recommendations. I'll be back for some more tomorrow :)

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars | Review: Great location. Friendly Staff. Conveniently located inside Canary Hotel. I ordered the breakfast Slow Poached Eggs, Toasts, & Fresh Fruits. It was okay not amazing. I've had better. Eggs were over cooked for being slow poached. Overall experience was 4 stars!

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Herbs & Spices, Specialty Food, Health Markets | Review: Really nice owner and ingredients, however the store hours were fairly inconsistent in the past.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: The Lark is The bomb! Pricey but worth it! Every bite we took created an amazing happy sensation! 
A true party in your mouth!! 

Strongly recommend the crispy Brussels Sprouts and the Southern Pimento Cheese! 

The Goat cheese cheesecake Profiteroles -- amazing!!! Very flavorful! The Carrot and Creamsicle cake is great if you love Carrot cake. Hazelnut (alcoholic)  latte will warm up your body and soul!

Superb experience! Friendly staff! Decor is warm and classy! Delicious drinks too! They surprised me with two drinks based on some ingredients I like... Winner!!

Categories: Garage Door Services, Restaurants, Live/Raw Food, Food, Home Services, Mediterranean, Keys & Locksmiths, Burgers, Vegetarian, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Vegan, Salad | Review: so...it's about time I've written a thoughtful, well deserved review for mesa verde. Ever since I started coming here, maybe two years ago, it has been my favorite restaurant in Santa Barbara. This is my boy friend and I's favorite spot! The service is so friendly and accommodating and the two co-owners are great! Although I myself am vegan, my vegan AND non vegan friends have loved the food (especially the STREET TACOS)! the atmosphere is really beautiful, unique and surprisingly feels really intimate and quiet for its location. If I'm not mistaken I've seen some of the staff at the farmers market before, I assume getting the best quality produce for their restaurant, so that's an awesome sign. The desserts keep getting better and better I love the chocolate cake but the cheese cake is also incredible... and I thought I didn't like cheese cake! I literally have nothing bad to say about this place. Do yourself a favor and go here, seriously!

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: I ate here Sunday afternoon after driving to Norton's and discovering they are closed on Sunday! It's near my work so I figured what the heck!? I was starving and for some reason the fish and chips sounded like a good idea. Normally I only order fish at a fish restaurant and it has to be near the water; Sambo's only meets one of the aforementioned criteria. Fish on the menu claims to be battered but definitely came out of a frozen box, already breaded. Fish was chewey. The locals have described this place as the original "Denny's." Usually packed out front - ocean front views!! I will have to eat here for breakfast to see if they can win me over.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Latin American, Caterers, Food, Restaurants, Empanadas, Argentine | Review: Freshest Empanadas. Right out of the oven. Super yummy taste. Outdoor sitting. The only downside is that they are only offering limited options.

Categories: Bars, Nightlife, Pubs, Comedy Clubs | Review: One of the few places on State St. where you can get a unique drink. The bartender was really helpful in helping choose which drink to order. There are several booths (which you don't see at bars) and high tables.

I got the Washington Apple and it's definitely apple with a kick.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: My boyfriend and I LOVE this place!  We ate here about a year ago while staying in Carpinteria and decided to go back for Valentines Day this year. The chef, who is the owner's son, has greeted us every time we have been in and has served us our dinner previously.  The food is wonderful, plated well and the service prompt and caring.  We went for lunch after a hike on V-Day and then went back for dinner.  They had no problem getting us in on such a busy night even with no reservation.  The patio was pretty at night with the twinkling lights and the heaters were great for such a cool night.  One of these days we are going to have to try someplace else in Carp.  =)  P.S. The ravioli is amazing, bruchetta too...

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breweries, American (Traditional), Brewpubs | Review: Sub par beer and the chimmichurri tri tip sandwich was pretty bad, first of all there was no chimmichurri sauce in the sandwich. I don't think they know what chimmichurri is. There was salsa of some sort and sprigs of cilantro, the tri tip was dry and over done, the ciabatta bread was the best part of the sandwich. Duck fat fries, didn't taste the duck far at all.
Categories: Szechuan, Chinese, Nightlife, Karaoke, Asian Fusion, Restaurants | Review: Great meal delivered hot and on time. Loved the chicken wings and green beans. Nice and spicy.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Don't do it.  
No seasoning, no passion, no quality, no flavor. 
The beef salad was inedible--I think rancid rice was scorched for the rice powder. The dish tasted like kerosene. 
Pad Thai was devoid of flavor. 
Duck buns were gristly and gloppy.
Lettuce cups totally bland.  
Really, truly awful food.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, French, Coffee & Tea, Bakeries, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Amazing croissants and kouign-ammans (pronounced like "queen Amman"), just like in France! They make scrambled eggs like nowhere else - seriously you'll become addicted because they are so incredible! The owners are delightful francais too :)

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: Enterprise has been a must go to place whenever we're in town, but this last visit was questionable. 

We were seated in the happy hour section and waited there for 20 minutes.  I finally got my own happy hour menu, waved down a waitress to ask where our server was, and were offered drinks. The server was apologetic and said there was a misunderstanding on who was supposed to serve us. Okay, I just saw you taking the order for the table next to us that was seated just 5 minutes ago but okay. Food was good and prompt. We have oysters on the half shell, Hawaiian poke, and the lobster special. Plentiful for the two of us. 

The check came and the happy hour specials were not applied. We pointed it out and it was corrected promptly. 

That was just too much. We don't know if both were honest mistakes but it would've been nice to be given a free dessert or appetizer for all of this.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: It is such a cute place and they have friendly service. Still, the best I can say of the meal is that it was inoffensive.  The vegetables didn't taste like they had been integrated with the sauces.  It was like the green beans were tossed in the tempura or the curry and they just tasted like green beans. The yellow curry was on the sweat side.  The Pad Thai was good but again suffered because the flavors weren't melded.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: My husband and I opted for breakfast here on Sunday 4th of July weekend before heading back home. The wait was LONG however we didn't mind because from our experience it's usually a good sign. Hmmm... There was this guy walking around, but mostly behind the bar for the most part....his shirt was white and extremely DIRTY not food dirty but DIRTY DIRTY and I must say really grossed me out (he wasn't cooking or I would have walked out). Everyone was really nice but looked frantic/disorganized. They kept apologizing I think they knew it was an off day maybe they were extremely short staffed. A LOT of people were complaining... My husband & I sat at the bar so we noticed it all. My food was pretty good (not sure if it was worth the hour wait out side + plus hour wait inside) & my OJ was delicious. He LOVED his food so we were happy campers when we left. 


Will give it another try and see what happens.

Categories: Delis, Gluten-Free, Fast Food, Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: First time here and had an absolutely incredible experience! Not only was the food amazing (I got the crispy chicken west) BUT the ambiance and people were friendly and very eager to create a genuine experience! The manager, Owen, greeted me as soon as I walked in the door and gave me a complete run down of the menu, even recommending some things based on what wanted! He was helpful and knowledgeable about the menu, very easy to talk to and created an all around wonderful experience. I will definitely be back for great food and even better customer service.
Categories: Burgers, Cafes, Restaurants, Mexican, American (Traditional) | Review: Good food, they didn't rush us when we were eating last closing. The fish tacos were BOMB!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Honestly, this place is all hype. It's basic. Yea the tortillas are good because they're handmade, the guac is basic, the bistec is basic. The horchata was delicious though. I just wasn't impressed....chipotle is better.

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese, Vegetarian | Review: This was our first time ordering food from
Shang Hai and it's a great discovery!!! They have an extensive vegetarian menu and as a mainly plant-based family, it made us happy to have so many choices. We ordered the vegetable chowmein (soft noodles), vegetable tofu dish and veggie combo (veggie chicken and veggie beef) pan-fried noodles. Each entree was delicious and full of flavor. We were amazed at the deliciousness of the veggie chicken and veggie beef. As vegetarians, it's always a nice treat to find faux meats prepared so well! The only downside was that the pan fried noodles were a different noodle from what other places use and not crispy. It didn't have the pan-fried, crunchy texture that is typical of that dish. At this
time, the restaurant was take-out only. The main man who took our order was helpful and nice! We will certainly return the next time we visit Santa Barbara. It's made it on our list of favorite food places in town.
Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Nightlife | Review: Solid everything. Excellent beer list, and great food and pizza. More vegetarian options would be nice.

Categories: Mexican, Vegan, Restaurants, Vegetarian | Review: Really tasty.  The "meats" have a lot of flavor and you leave with a lot of food.  (I usually take it to go.) Only complaint is it's EXPENSIVE!  A burrito shouldn't be $14!  Otherwise delish, I wish there were more quick/easy vegan options like this in town.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: The food was a bit disappointing.There is no attempt at innovation, just blandness and too much salt. 50\50 quality :(

Categories: Food, Italian, Pizza, Fast Food, Gelato, Chicken Wings, Sandwiches, Salad, Restaurants | Review: Best pizza me and my husband have had. Very busy walk in restaurant that has their wood fired pizza ready within 10-15min. We live our wine and it was a nice surprise they had a good wine selection to pair with their amazing pizza. A must try for pizza lovers!

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Salad, Restaurants, Sandwiches | Review: Food and wine were great, service good but not amazing. Fish tank bar was really cool, but the best thing is the view of the pacific in the plentiful outdoor seating. If you're staying at the 4 seasons, def worth checking out.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Overall 3.5 stars is all Rose Cafe (2) gets?  
This is an amazing anytime place.  

This review is based on the ambiance and one glorious dish.  

Rose Cafe is relaxed, low lit, old red vinyl seating, and delicious.  I know everyone's got their SB hotspots and hangover cures, but I'll be damned.  The guadalajara chicken tacos are a great.  Loaded with guacamole, these lusciously stuffed shells of amazing, burst with flavor and scream satisfaction.  Rice and beans, loaded with lard and salt are a perfect side car.  And balanced with the light refreshing lettuce and and tomato, are super tasty.  

Please try them.

Categories: Bars, Burgers, Restaurants, Nightlife, Sports Bars, American (New) | Review: Best local spot in town. Amazing wait staff and the the manager Brandy will go above and beyond to make sure you have a amazing meal and experience! She adds a personal touch to every trip that makes you feel welcome. Best burgers, beer and Brandy in town!!!

Categories: Restaurants, Italian, Pizza | Review: OH MY GOODNESS! The pizza here is incredible! Very pricey but very good. I have never eaten in house because it's always packed so I always order for pick up. The salads are small but very good as well. I will have to call and make Reservations if I'd like to experience the sit down dining. Every time I go to pick up my order the staff is a little snooty and has a bit of an attitude like I'm inconveniencing them by my Togo order, but Iv lived here long enough to know that attitude comes with the territory....mooonteeecitooo!

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Coffee Roasteries | Review: Sadly, this isn't my favorite coffee in town. This location is ideal because its right off of State Street and can get a pick-me-up in a pinch. I have had the drip coffee and the cold brew and both seem watered down and no flavor. The staff is great and they are open later than other coffee shops but maybe their espresso drinks are a better option? 

They have a table with chairs outside and then more seating out in the street which is lovely, now that they closed off State Street to cars. I will have to just try again!

Categories: Food, American (New), Thai, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Desserts, Cafes, Sandwiches, Caterers, Event Planning & Services | Review: Quick, clean, scrumptious, with nice employees and interior design.

Categories: Restaurants, Pizza, Italian | Review: Pizza was probably the best I have ever had. You could taste every flavor on the pizza. In the bar ( cooking) area it was very hot (some what expected). I look foward to a dine in experience and some more great food!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Bakeries, Food, Australian | Review: Would give half a star if possible the WORST! I ordered the jalapeño biscuit with egg,cheese n bacon plus a avocado I received a hugh hard biscuit , a sliver of bacon no avocado and dried up hard cheese.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: This place is good, their views from the patio are gorgeous especially at sunset. The clam chowder and oysters were delicious. Service was great!

Categories: Delis, Restaurants | Review: So from all over the place I've been hearing about the late night menu which includes some supposed great mac n' cheese so I figure I'd give it a shot...and ofcourse get a Gyro to go with it...
1)I'm pretty sure this is the best Gyro in SB but I don't think it has any competition in that regard, please let me know if i'm wrong because Greek House is just a step above Daphne's (a southern cali...greek chain) I'd gladly try any other Gyro's in SB, but because i'm not aware of any others, I'll keep going to GH for it
2)The Mac n' Cheese is a joke..pretty sure its Velveeta crap and for a couple dollars extra some crappy chicken with tobasco sauce( 'Cajun Style') and fried onions...folks keep your money in your wallets, this stuff is garbage..

Categories: Gelato, Food, Restaurants, Italian, Vegetarian, Gluten-Free | Review: Soooo bummed. Grainy Alfredo as if from a packaged cream sauce. Not edible. Pasta with sundried tomato and shrimp was literally soupy. My kid that loves Italian went home hungry. Upside was the service. Quick to get drinks. That's all folks!

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: This place is epic! Caught ghastly here and was nothing but good times. The patio bartender was the most entertaining drink slinger ever. Unquanifiable amount of crass and class from this Asian individual. I highly suggest coming and tasting love and good times!

Categories: Nightlife, Dance Clubs | Review: Bouncers are professional. The Bartenders are electric. The drinks are tasty. The DJ spins the right record. The strobe lights are well done. I was grateful the ladies restroom line didn't take too long. Its a cute place and the energy of the crowd was great. I hope this continues...I like being a bit of a party goon.  There are shelf like areas on the dance floor to place your drink or phone.

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: My favorite restaurant in Santa Barbara! Great food and fun atmosphere. I have never had anything less than a stellar experience here.

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: Flavor of India on upper State has been the go-to for Indian lunch buffet for years now, but I'm making the switch to India Club in Goleta after just one visit here. 

The flavors are more homey and authentic, versus the generic quality offered by many other Indian lunch buffets. While not opulent in variety, the quality of the dishes is markedly superior to that of the competition in Santa Barbara.

While I love the spicy coconut chicken curry at Naan Stop in IV, the $11 India Club lunch, while not at all cheap, is a better deal if one is hungry enough to do a buffet justice.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: Beautiful setting and service decent but food just ok. My pasta was homemade but the bolognese tasted like - hate to say this - Chef Boyardee. The rest of my party agreed.

Categories: Ramen, Vegetarian, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Restaurants | Review: One of our favorite spots for take out! Friendly crew - great food - we've never gotten anything here we didn't love ( or crave in coming weeks ;) ) Highly recommend!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: i wasn't sure what a previous yelper meant by "vertical lines" and now i know. this place gets packed and there's a tiny little waiting area at front, made up mostly of the stairs leading up to the place. tsk tsk, this place is not wheelchair accessible!! is this legal in cali?!

anyway, it was great. skip the large sized menu and order from the small folded one. tried the shark sandwich. to my surprise, the shark meat was light, delicate and flaky. excellent but wished they had a more original bread other than white toast to pair with it. btw, hated the hard sourdough they serve at the table. god, i don't want to break a tooth, you know?

the fried scallops were surprisingly light and non-greasy. a perfect little bite to pop in your mouth. i would suggest getting this as an appetizer for the table. it's perfect to share. can get loud and rowdy inside. for interesting atmosphere and seafood at a great price, this is the spot!

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: This is the best deli in Santa Barbara. I have tried the marks panini, pesto panini, south coast turkey, tri tip sandwich, BBQ chicken Sammie, Mexican Caesar salad and I can say they're all worth 5 stars. This place is great, they have friendly staff and the online ordering is quick and easy!

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: Best sushi restaurant in town period. The fish is exceptional. The rolls are delicious, delicate, and sophisticated. The service is always great, the staff is so friendly and committed to insuring you have a great experience. As far as ambiance it's funky and cool without trying too hard, you can come dressed up or down. The outdoor patio is great, and the sushi bar is also fun. The chefs are wonderful, the food they prepare is masterful but they're also funny and engaging if you sit at the bar. No reservations, my advice is to come early between 530 to 615 to be seated without a wait, otherwise you will have to wait but it is so worth it. I can't say enough about how much I love this restaurant, you really must try it.

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Seafood, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Venues & Event Spaces | Review: The absolutely best fish and chips in town. Great friendly service with lots of smiling faces.  Sat outside and took in the nice breeze.  Highly recommend imho.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: Seriously one of the best meals we've ever had. Every dish was a discovery. Service was impeccable. Great cocktails too. 
Ambience is a bit loud but you can find your corner if you're looking for a romantic dinner.

Categories: Korean, Restaurants | Review: Update 3/5/13: We had lunch here a 2nd time recently, and they now have a non-AYCE lunch special for $11, yay!  They were very generous with meat portions, which we appreciated.  It seemed like there was only one waiter, so he was working hard trying to keep up with all the requests.

I think their banchan could use some improvement, more variety.  But overall, this is a good lunch spot if you're not in a rush.

***

We went here for lunch on a weekday.  There was one other table taken when we first got there, and later 6 (out of 10) were occupied.  The staff was nice, but they still need to get used to the menu.  We had to ask for items that came with what we ordered that they forgot to give us.  But when asked, they were quick to bring it.

We ordered the all-you-can-eat option.  They only had one choice of 3 meats for $15.95 even though their website lists a 2 meat option also.  My favorite was definitely the spicy chicken.  It was extremely tender and tasty.  I don't normally like spicy foods, but this had just enough flavor without being too spicy.  The pork was pretty tender, but the beef was tough.  The beef came sliced extremely thin, and there was no way to grill it without it becoming tough.  The rice wasn't bad, but it could be improved.  Korean rice can be really good (like the stone pot sticky rice that you get at korean tofu houses), but the rice here was a bit too mushy.

For me, $16 per person is too much to go on a regular basis, but once in a while is okay.  If they added a less expensive option to the menu, maybe one that wasn't all you can eat with a pre-set amount of meat, it would become a more feasible lunch option.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: A Santa Barbara classic. If you want to know what American/California cuisine is, all you need to do is dine at Opal. Its definitely upscale dining but you don't need a tie or dinner jacket here. OK shorts and a t shirt might be pushing the envelope but rest assured Opal is not a pretentious place. Located outside the standard tourist zone and up in the "theater district" you will find mostly locals on any given night and it's likely to be crowded, even on a Monday. The menu boasts steaks, chops, seafood and salads, all the things you'd expect but most are done with a twist such as the chili crusted filet mignon or the warm seafood salad, both excellent by the way. Full bar for cocktails but the wine list is fairly special. If you're not sure though just go for the house wine, the Opal Syrah produced specially for Opal by the best winery in town, Jaffurs. Order a bottle, you won't be disappointed.

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: Pretty good fish place.  Went there on Saturday afternoon with a larger group.  We had the calamari and the clam chowder in bread bowls.  Others had the Cioppino and clams.

All the food was very tasty and service was really good.  I would probably come back here again.  Eight people including some wine and beers was around $200.

Categories: Vietnamese, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, Wine Bars, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Noodles | Review: The food at this Vietnamese place is a little pricey compared to other restaurants. My boyfriend and I ordered the combination pho, egg rolls, and the combination rice plate. 
--The pho was in a pretty small bowl and I expected more meat in it for the price I paid (I believe it was around $11-$13 for the bowl). This restaurant gave me a very minimal amount of noodles and the broth was pretty tasteless & had no flavor. (I came here at lunch time and the pho was just NOT GOOD). 
--The egg rolls ARE OVER-PRICED! I believe I paid around $7-$8 for 4 TEENY TINY egg rolls that my boyfriend gobbled up in literally 5 seconds. The egg rolls were about 3 inches long, super duper tiny. I was very surprised because I thought the egg rolls would be regular sized since its so expensive here. 
--The combination rice plate included a minuscule amount of grilled beef, rice, a fried egg, and 3 tiny pieces of fish cakes. My goodness, I paid $13 for this plate and I honestly just want my money back. The amount of grilled beef they put on my plate was RIDICULOUS. I am a small person and I have small hands, but seriously if the amount of grilled beef on my plate could literally sit in the palm of my hand, YOU ARE DEFINITELY OVER CHARGING ME FOR THE AMOUNT OF FOOD ACTUALLY GIVEN. I did not place the beef in the palm of my hand of course, but geeezz I deserve more than 2 spoonfuls of meat for a $13 plate don't you think? 
--The Thai Iced Tea was the ONLY good thing about my lunch. 
Overall, I don't think I will be coming back to this restaurant because it is not worth it & I rather not have to give another disappointing review..

The last thing I wanted to address concerns the middle-aged Asian lady who constantly paces back and forth/waiting on tables in the front of the restaurant (she might be the owner). This lady has a huge attitude problem and is very rude to the other waiters/waitresses. The young waitress taking my order forgot about my thai iced tea so I politely reminded her about it about 10 minutes into my meal (she was a very sweet girl). However, when I reminded the young girl about the drink, the older Asian lady was standing behind her and immediately starting berating/yelling at her in Vietnamese all because she messed up on one thing. The older Asian lady continued yelling at the young girl as she proceeded to walk away and get my drink as well. I really don't think the young girl deserved to be yelled at like that and she seemed pretty sad afterwards (but I left her a good tip to thank her). The young girl is sweet and I hope she makes more tips at this restaurant.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Was visiting Santa Barbara and stumbled across this place. Place is literally against the sand with a gorgeous view. We could even see dolphins from our table. I ordered the strawberry pancakes 3-2-1 and it was delicious. Food was really fast too. If you want a quick bite with a view this is the place to go.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: The manager was great, we were served quickly even though the place was packed. The agaves burrito was probably one of the best seafood burritos I'v ever had in my life, the chipotle sauce was the perfect level of spice. The salad was also great, topped with some sort of sweet mango sauce

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: I love tyger tyger! Food is always fresh and delicious. Everyone that works there is friendly and on it. I work in the funk zone and they will deliver my order to me free of charge which is wonderful! Eating in is great too. Grab a coconut with your order or have a tasty glass of wine. I eat here weekly and I love it!
Categories: Restaurants, Thai, Food, Chicken Wings, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Nice enough Thai place, especially good for seafood. Is a bit expensive.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Another very expensive over rated ice cream place. I've been there twice, it about $7-8 for one person. I enjoy ice Cream regardless if it's rolled or smashed in a cup lol so for what I'm spending there I could buy a pint of ice cream some where else. So I guess it depends on how you look at it... also those loooong Lines are horrid. I ended up waiting 1 hour for my order! The set up seems odd the cash register is wayyy too far.. they may wanto consider moving the cash register to the front of the store...

Categories: Tapas Bars, Restaurants | Review: The service and drinks are great here! Our server was super friendly, especially for a busy Saturday eve. The tapas choices are really diverse so there were a lot of great options if you go in a big group. Also, a very good place to go if you have any allergies as the server was transparent about any items that can be dangerous. 
The drinks were good and strong! The bartender really puts their craft into the drinks! Everything we ordered was very unique and tasty! 

Definitely give them a try if you are in the area.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Took my boyfriend here for his 21st birthday and it was so wonderful. 
The restaurant's location is so sincere engulfed by huge coastal cliffs, vast water, and happy people with pets walking down it's shoreline.

Food was amazing, we got Salmon and Mahi Mahi. Yum.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: Located in Downtown SB my girlfriend and I came here for a quick snack/meal after visiting a couple breweries. We just parked on the street and walked 5 minutes to get to the restaurant. We decided to order the bruschetta and chef's selection meat and cheese plate. The bruschetta came with 3 slices and was great and the charcuterie board was huge! That included porchetta, prosciutto, and capicola. The capicola was probably the best I've had! The service was friendly and helpful and I would definitely recommend this place to anyone looking for somewhere to eat in DTSB!
Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Oyster shooters and drinks. Nuff said.

Categories: Steakhouses, Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: My bf and I came to Santa Barbara for the weekend to celebrate his bday. We walked around the strip looking for ideas to have for dinner. We arrived at Hibachi about 30 minutes before closing and they still welcomed us and seated us. Our servers name was Omar, His energy was great! Omar gave exceptional customer services, he kept it very professional all while making us feel like friends! His energy was bright & cheerful. Even recommended a couple of places to visit. The food was also very great. The restaurant resembles Benihanas but way more reasonable in pricing. Only payed 80 something bucks for all the food ordered. This place is a must! I definitely recommend it.
Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars | Review: 遅い朝食と言うかランチを食べました。バーガーは完璧なミディアムレア。卵をワインビネガー？で漬けた赤い茹で卵が美味しかった。明日は朝食を食べに来ようかな？

Categories: Restaurants, Bagels, Coffee & Tea, Food, Bubble Tea, Donuts | Review: Update:  I don't know why, but this location is better then the other locations that I've been to.  For some reason, the Downtown Spudnuts and the one in Oxnard just don't compare to this one.  I'm not sure why, but that's my impression.

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Breaks my heart but Delgado's Carp is not the same great restaurant under new management. Margaritas not as good, food consistently cold, service TERRIBLE.  We've been coming here for years but after the last 3 visits I doubt we will be back. Customers from surrounding tables discussing how bad it was. Mid week, not busy, just painful to see the servers absolutely lost. And then overcharged on the bill, insult to injury. Bye-bye, so sad.
Categories: Grocery, Food | Review: Everything is super expensive here...The deli counter is always slow... Its not that hard!! Come on pick up the pace..

Categories: Fast Food, Food, Acai Bowls, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants | Review: This place is delicious. The prices are good selection is impressive. We came here a couple weeks ago and ordered the Piña Colada and the Green Tea smoothies, both in the smaller size. The texture of their drinks is interesting, with little ice crystals in both and small shreds of coconut in the Piña Colada. Personally, we enjoyed this. Their service was very friendly, and the atmosphere of the smoothie place was happy. We didn't have to wait too long to receive our fabulous blended drinks.  It would be nice if they offered a medium size, as the small was a little too small and the regular was much too big, but I digress. We will likely visit if we are in the Downtown Santa Barbara area again.

Categories: Korean, Restaurants, American (New), Mexican, Tacos, Nightlife, Fast Food, Bars | Review: BEST FISH TACOS IN SB.
I always get the batter up, 2 crispy fish and 1 shrimp. FIREEE. The sauce on the side is spicy though but I LOVE IT!!!!!! I make everyone go here. Nothing but good vibes. A classic locals spot. Hit the batting cages right after. Cheers

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: If you want to actually pay for the crappy,low quality breakfast most hotels give you for free, then go ahead and have breakfast here. It was overcooked like yesterday and reheated in steam pans this morning. My husband's blueberry pancakes were made with dried blueberries that were like little rocks.  Sadly, our hotel owner sang the praises of of this place and we took his advice. We will not trust him on anything else. Go to Andersen's or Jeannine's if you want a decent breakfast. The beach side location doesn't even come close to making up for the awful food. The service was decent so I'm giving it 2 Stars.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, American (New), Wine Bars, Nightlife, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Event Planning & Services | Review: One of my favorite restaurants, which boasts really fresh, flavorful ingredients. Favorites include Mixed Seafood & Vegetable Stew, Fettuccine & Chicken (but I substitute shrimp instead of chicken), and also the Baked Eggplant from the vegetarian menu. Also, the "Pots" of Eggplant Pate & Olive Tapenade with grilled flatbread could be a meal in itself.

Reasonable prices. I need to try more things on the menu, but I keep ordering the same things I love!

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Seafood, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Venues & Event Spaces | Review: The atmosphere was great and the staff was super friendly and inviting. I had the chicken bah mi sandwich and it was delicious! The fries were a nice asset to it also. My sister had the fish & chips and said it was excellent and that the tartar sauce was unique. I will definitely be returning!

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches | Review: This place is " hit and miss" the first few times they were great. Lately the last 3 times not so much. One constant is the lack of enthusiasm demonstrated by the cashier. If she had any less energy she would just fade away. You can tell she really does not want to be there. I ask for something to put the pickles in and you would have thought I asked her to wash my car or something. 
Side note . The pickles were turning brown.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegan, Gluten-Free, Falafel, Mediterranean, Salad, Sandwiches | Review: During the COVID19 lockdowns, I have been in search for a few good take out spots to support some of our local faves. (Not sure if this is a chain or not- but it's local to me). Anyway, I was looking for something fresh for a lunch to take to the beach. I ordered the Falafel Plate.... and I was NOT disappointed. It was beyond my expectation for a good healthy take out meal. Everything was so delicious and fresh, and well prepared. I came back a second time the next week. As spots in the Public Market tend to be caught in a rotating door and don't stay long, I hope this spot stays. A great find for sure.

Categories: Steakhouses, Bars, Brewpubs, Breweries, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Sports Bars, Restaurants, Food, Burgers, Comfort Food, Seafood | Review: The Brewhouse is the go to bar and restaurant for locals and tourist alike. Great food strong drinks and the best handcrafted beers in Sant Barbara. The Jerk chicken is a must
is amazing! The outdoor patio is a plus. The staff is great! Especially the bartender! Great TVs for watching sports!

Categories: American (New), Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Love, love this restaurant! Our only night out for dinner is Saturday night and we always go to Senor Frogs in Carpinteria. Love the staff, love the chefs, love the atmosphere. It's our home away from home. You won't be disappointed!!

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Wow! We went here last Sunday on whim with hopes of not being caught in the Mother's Day mayhem. We achieved the first goal. Next goal was to fulfill my lust for interesting, uniquely delicious food, ! This place is amazing! I ordered the crepe which was beautifully plated but even more pleasing on my palate. Others in my party ordered the crispy rice salad, amazing and a special grits bowl, fabulous and shared an order of Brussels sprouts. Everything was unique and delicious! Only hitch was our food did not come out at the same time, but we still loved it.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: If you are looking for good value for your hard-earned money, The Lark is the best place to be in Santa Barbara. This town has a lot of restaurants that charge the SB zip-code premium (if you know what I mean). Not this place. It is not cheap but the food is fantastic (seasonal, tasty, fresh, well prepared), the service is great (friendly, helpful, knowledgeable) and they don't make you feel like you have to leave once you started the last quarter of your plate (I was at places where they literally asked us for desert with the check in their hands right after they served us the main course). Bottom line: There are just a few places in town that offer such good food/service for a reasonable price and The Lark is one of them.

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: My friend wanted Indian food. I love the flavor of Indian but not all the oil that many restaurants add to their dishes. I always feel kind of heavy (and thirsty) after eating Indian. I found this place on Yelp and I am SOOOO glad I did! The food is super clean, not oily at all, and very authentic! The owner was there and made our visit extremely enjoyable. You can tell he takes such pride in what he does. The place is clean, the chefs are Indian, the whole experience was authentic and super yummy! I think whatever you order will be great. We had a lamb dish, a cauliflower and potato dish and basmati rice. Sides we had mango chutney (super yumm!) and yogurt sauce. We also got the pickles which are an acquired taste which I haven't mastered yet, but again, hey are authentic! To end, the rice pudding wasn't heavy nor too sweet! Major thumbs up here!

Categories: Asian Fusion, Poke, Seafood, Food, Restaurants | Review: I'm from the Bay Area and there are many poke restaurants. This is one of the best poke places I've ever eaten from.  I especially liked the quinoa rice and salmon.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: At first we were a little thrown since the place is described as being in a motel, but it's actually kinda nice. We've been twice now. Had a coupon for $20 off a bill of $50 or more both times. At that price this place is spectacular. The chips and salsa are are better than average. The shrimp cocktail(light and fresh, more like a salad) and the artichoke are damn good and the guacamole is worth paying an appetizer price for. We make a point of asking the servers what is good everywhere we go and they haven't steered us wrong so far. The fajitas are "not very good" apparently. haha I've gotten the chile relleno both times. Both were good but the shrimp option is the standout. My gf got the fettuccine con cilantro which was surprisingly good considering the place is called "Las Aves". The strawberry cheesecake and the toasted apple, wow. My gf loved the cheesecake and she isn't a big fan of cheesecake, it's light and airy. I have to say that I was expecting to be unimpressed by the toasted apple, but it's like what a caramel apple dreams of growing up to be, nom^3! The service was very good both times. We will be back I am sure.

Categories: Cuban, Restaurants, Bars, Sandwiches, Nightlife | Review: I have now eaten here twice and each time I have had the Ropa Nuevo, no rice, double salad. (Keto).
Each time, it has been consistently delicious. I do feel like the amount of meat could be more generous, it is really only 3 small pieces of meat. I don't believe the value is there for the amount you get, whether it's rice or salad. You are paying for the meat in this dish. 
The service is fairly quick, it is a walk up and order establishment. I usually get a weird look when I ask to remove the rice from my dish, but I don't eat rice and they don't offer much foe a keto diet so I have to improvise. 
I will definitely be back, I love the vibe, the decorations and the attached Modern Times brewery. I like being able to take my food to the brewery to eat.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: A quaint little cafe in the heart of Carpinteria where one can enjoy what appears to a home cooked meal. Staff is personable and quick. Kids are handed a book when seated. They have homemade kumbucha that is brewed in house as well as Twice around the sun beer w/ hints of chocolate. If you love cinnamon rolls, they have ones that are to die for where every bite melts in your mouth.

Categories: Restaurants, Bakeries, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Coffee Roasteries, Coffee & Tea | Review: Don't be fooled. Kids aloof about serving mediocre coffee. Maybe the somewhat empty shop on Saturday am should have tipped me off. 
Don't get me wrong, the coffee's ok. But they sell the experience online as a specialty shop. 
I drink decaf because I react adversely to caffeine. I asked what kind of decaf they had today, and the guy said "decaf coffee". Ok. Not a bad answer, but nothing different. Again, not a bad answer, but nothing special. When I ordered a decaf americano I asked if i drank it here if I could get a refill from the decaf refill station, and I was told I couldn't because I wasn't ordering coffee. My other cafe choice nearby lets me do that, so that was disappointing. 
Really, Starbucks is same same. My other local specialty coffee shop is superior (I don't want to be tacky and name them).

Categories: Steakhouses, Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: Love love love this place. We order so many dishes and all of them were so delicious. Service is just amazing. Our server suggest me plum wine which was good. I highly recommend this place.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: This place has shown up frequently on my instagram feed, so McConnell's was a must try when we came down to Santa Barbara for the weekend. They had a variety of flavors, but I had quickly decided on the Dark Chocolate Chip & Nibs. I was going to go all out and get a Mocha, but that much dairy... and I'd probably break the toilet by the time we got back to our hotel. 

There's definitely a lot of crunching involved with this ice cream, but the flavor is very yummy. The dark chocolate still holds that bitter yet sweet flavor. It has a creamy texture , so you can bet that you'll have a good ice cream experience with any of their other flavors.

Categories: Specialty Food, Chocolatiers & Shops, Food, Desserts | Review: This review is related to an online order I placed through Zen by Cat (for this company's chocolates) for Valentine's Day. The chocolates I received were presented in a simple, but elegant box and were well packed. Nothing was broken and the box traveled from California to Oklahoma. The chocolates we received were soooo delicious and very high quality. Beautiful designs - definitely worth the cost. My husband was very impressed with his gift and made sure to savor each one. I will definitely be buying from this chocolatier again.
Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: The thing that makes this place special are the fresh cones made on site and the location.   The price is expensive.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Good authentic Mexican food IN SB! My only complaint was that they couldn't keep their reservation time and I waited abut 1 hour longer than we should have. Their seating system does not integrate with Yelp therefore Yelp was feeding us false information regarding our place in line.

We had the guacamole (just ok), queso fundido (good taste!) chicken with mole and their drinks were really good and creative. Try the mezcal ones!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: I am only posting this now, but it was pretty good. I got Milk Buddy (cookies and cream). I got it with condensed milk and chocolate sprinkles (don't forget whipped cream!) but for some reason it tasted a little funny. I know it was the condensed milk or something, but they at least make everything blend. I know what your thinking: then just don't get condensed milk! But I wanted all of my free toppings! I didn't really want anything else on there, but I'll take what I can I like the designs and how they make it in front of you, but it tasted a little funny.

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: Love it. Best owner ever.  Delicious for that is full of flavor and their lunch buffet is to die for.  I usually eat here once or twice a week. Great vegan choices
Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: Great fish & chips, side of fried pickles... Shared a wee haggis & pickled egg - I'd come back!

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), French, Restaurants | Review: This is not the best place for vegans or even vegetarians, but the chef was able to specially prepare a pasta dish for us.

It had tomatoes, goat cheese, walnuts, a pink sauce?, and fresh pasta. The dish was amazing and my friend and I couldn't stop raving over the quality of our dish.

The chocolate dessert was the perfect cherry on top. Although it may be considered "small" for American standards, the chocolate piece was wonderful.

I only have great things to say about this place! The interior makes you feel like you are in the French countryside and their sunroom eating terrace is just gorgeous!

If I'm back in SB, I will definitely come back here!

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Lovely lunch. We ordered the shrimp pasta, and thai curry salmon. Both delicious. Our server was awesome!

Categories: Breweries, Nightlife, Food, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Restaurants, Brewpubs, Gastropubs | Review: Just had the most amazing dinner at the cruisery with delicious drinks!! The owner was so sweet, he came to our table and introduced himself and turns out we both share the same hometown in Turkey!

Categories: Wineries, Food, Breweries, Nightlife, Bars, Arts & Entertainment, Champagne Bars, Pubs, American (New), Brewpubs, Restaurants | Review: This place was great! We loved the lager and the bar tenders were so friendly! They even made my boyfriend and I a grilled cheese with half ham and half prosciutto half ham because we couldn't decide !!  we will definitely be back.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Sandwiches, Food Trucks | Review: I was so excited for this place since i loved grilled cheeses and it's at the corner of where I live but it was so overpriced for such tiny portions that they give, I paid $9 for a very thin grilled cheese with barely any other ingredients in which I paid extra for. So disappointed :(

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: I love coming here for the clam chowder. It has an awesome view if you choose to sit outside. The drinks are good and strong which you can't really complain about. They are generally always busy so the wait can sometimes be really long but what we usually do while we wait is walk down toward the beach and watch people jet ski or the boats that are out there and pretty much just enjoy the view.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: I have eaten here twice in the last week and was not impressed with the service on either occasion. Both times I had to request chips and salsa, neither of which ended up coming before the meal, and on the second visit they never showed up at all. After the original order was taken on my second visit, the waiter never returned to the table. We had to have another waiter go find him so we could pay the bill. The food is good but not consistently hot when it reaches the table, even when it arrives quickly. If I had to wait for a table AND was ignored by waitstaff through the entire meal, the review would have been worse. There didn't appear to be a single female working the floor either time I visited. Maybe that explains a few things, just sayin".......

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Very hip ice cream place located on State Street. They have all sorts of flavors to choose from and you can sample if you're unsure. 

Smooth and silky texture. My peach sorbet didn't melt too fast despite the heat wave. It had just the right amount of sweetness.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Food, Specialty Food, Japanese | Review: I moved to SB right when Shintori opened. My mom and I have been going here ever since and it has been nice to watch the place flourish and do well! I've had sushi in SF, NY, LA, and I have yet to have a spicy tuna roll that tastes better than Shintori's. The sushi is very well priced for it's quality, and I've never had salmon nigiri that was more fresh! Great service and cute atmosphere, one thing is I wish it were bigger in there! But I kinda don't want them to expand at the same time because it'll lose it's charm.

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Plow and Angel is one of the nicer places that I have dined at.  We happen to eat there on an evening when a wedding was taking place and as a result got to hear their entertainer which was a man with a wonderful voice singing beautiful songs.  The combination of sparkly lights of the outdoor eating area, the wonderful singer and the delicious food and drinks made our night.  Yes, this place is pricey.  The mixed drinks range from $13 to $160 dollars (yes, $160 per drink). I had the blood orange margarita which was unique and refreshing.   The appetizers, main dishes and desserts are also in the $20-$90 range.  I had the sashimi appetizer and it was fresh but tiny.  We loved the side of peppers (1 in 10 is extra spicy).  My dining companion also ordered the baked alaska for 2 which was $26 and huge.  I am not a fan of chocolate so this was not my thing but it was beautifully presented.  The service was attentive and not pretentious.  I would go back if given a chance any day and would love to stay at San Ysidro one day also.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Delis, Breakfast & Brunch, Tapas/Small Plates, Cafes | Review: Ordered an Easter Dinner package - both the quality and quantity exceeded expectations! It was the highlight of my quarantine! Will order from them again.

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), French, Restaurants | Review: One of the best meals I've ever had! This place is adorable and takes extra time to make your night special. The food is quite expensive, especially for a college student, but for the taste and the experience it is well worth it. I will be back!

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis | Review: Always nice people and the Roast Beef Hottie "88" is just awesomeness on a roll. Biggest problem with the place is it can get super busy during the lunch rush and there can be no parking. You just might have to wait in line for a bit but that just speaks to how good the food is.

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: Where do I begin? 
My girlfriend and I arrived at about 6pm on a Thursday evening. When we arrived there were plenty of open tables, so we asked for a small fire pit table. We were told by the hostess that she could not seat a party of two at a fire pit table. So we said, ok, and took our seats nearest the steps leading to the inside portion of the restaurant. This seat was special, because behind us was the hostess stand, the kitchen and a major area of congregation for all employees. I could not cross my legs at the table or they would have been knocked by the numerous people that constantly walked by our table. As we looked around we saw an 8 top fire pit table with 3 people at it, we saw 3 6 top tables with 2-3 people at them. Why we were not able to have 2 people at a 4 top table, I will never know as there were 2 available when we sat down. 
I have long legs and was becoming uncomfortable with our seating arrangement and asked our waitress for another table, anywhere in the restaurant that wasn't quite so busy with traffic. Our food had just arrived moments after she agreed to seat us somewhere else. She then asked the gentleman with our food to take our food back to the kitchen as we were changing seats. Another waitress? came and helped us to our new table. The table was great! It was near the music, out of the high traffic area and we were able to chat. There was one problem, we apparently didn't have a waiter. The woman that helped us to our seats asked us if we needed another drink when she sat us down and we agreed. Fifteen minutes later, our drinks arrived. Our food on the other hand had not. We waited another 20 minutes for our food to arrive. 35 minutes after our food was brought out the first time, a gentleman arrives with what tastes and appears to be the same food he brought us originally. No longer fresh, and quite cold. 
We ate the food anyway, at this point, we were starving. We then chatted with a table full of partially eaten food for 1 hour. Not a single person came to our table, not a waitress, not a busser, not anyone asking if we needed water, a drink, no one cleared a single plate from our table. No one even brought us a check. 
This was my first visit to Zaytoon, and I wish I had a better experience. The ambience was beautiful and romantic, the food smelled amazing and the music was a nice touch. 
We were either being punished for wanting another table or just completely slipped through the cracks. We must have seen 3 waitresses, 2 bussers and a manager walk by us with not a word. 
That to me is the worst part. The cold food, I may have sent back if I thought someone would have actually been by to check on us. By far the worst service experience I have ever had at a restaurant in Santa Barbara.

Categories: Japanese, Sushi Bars, Restaurants | Review: My husband and I LOVE this place. We have been coming here for years. The owner is super friendly and the service is great. We are huge fans of the dragin roll. We like to come here to celebrate special occasions. It is our favorite place in Goleta!

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese | Review: Each time I've been here, the wait has been 30-40 minutes even though I only order one dish, the hot pot. It's obvious the quality of food isn't too great, especially the meats because it tends to be weirdly chewy and full of fats. It doesn't help that the portions are small and the dishes are expensive. It's a quick fill for authentic Asian food in IV if you are craving Asian food but to be honest, the food isn't worth the price.
Categories: Chinese, Restaurants | Review: Kung Pao Chicken tasted pretty good until one of us bit down on a sharp bone fragment.  The fragment was sharp on both edges and almost 1/4 inch.  Be warned

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: My go to spot in town. The food is amazing, great ingredients, lots of options. I eat here more times a week than I'd like to admit. You can't go wrong with anything on the menu. To top it off the staff are always so nice.

Categories: Restaurants, Bakeries, Food | Review: Discovered this bakery when heading into vons for some pastries the other day. The staff was attentive and they had an excellent cookie, cake, tarts, pies, croissants , sandwiches and additional cafe items I was very excited when choosing something. I had the ham and cheese croissant which was delicious. The cookies all looked so delicious and everything is so clean and neat inside. Love it.

Categories: Venues & Event Spaces, Cafes, Wine Bars, Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Great food, good drinks, nice easy atmosphere, a bit on the pricey side but a nice treat.  We sat at the fireplace couch/table things and had a great meal after a long day of drinking.

I highly recommend it for anyone seeking a nice quality dinner with a laid back, casual attitude.

Categories: Specialty Food, Health Markets, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Food | Review: Did not sample any sweets; however, their bread and muffins are incredible! (much better than Udi's)  They must replace gluten with sorcery.

Excellent service - attentive and friendly.

Categories: Food, Specialty Food, Seafood, Cajun/Creole, Restaurants | Review: I wasn't too impressed with either the food or the service. Very similar style to boiling crab but more expensive and their house marinade is not as well developed (more like a pasta sauce). It was very difficult to get our server's attention for things like drinks and refills she came by twice during our dinner (to take our order and give us our check). Also they source their clams from a third party so our bag of clams was half empty shells, though management gave us an additional half pound once this was brought to their attention. With regards to overall experience, I would not recommend and would not return with this restaurant at their current level.

Categories: French, Cafes, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Sandwiches, Bakeries | Review: Hands down best bakery in Santa Barbara and might be one of the best in so cal. Croissants are superb, flaky, always fresh. The almond croissant is massive and tons of marzapane, if you want a hefty pastry, get this!

Their regular food is also quite good, healthy simple fair with great service. Peerless Coffee also offered, along with beer and wine. For dessert the cookies are amazing! Literally named after the founder Renaud, legit Frenchman.

Categories: Restaurants, Burgers, Sandwiches, Pizza, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Good food for a hidden little gem. The recommendations on Yelp weren't the greatest but definitely worth trying if you're in the area!

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Sushi Bars | Review: Wow, this isn't a regular sushi restaurant. I know my Japanese food--be prepared for some creative and delicious food! Atmosphere is nice, food is incredible.  I will return here whenever in the area.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: If you don't know, now you know. Bring extra cash and not because it is expensive but because you are gonna want seconds. Everything is great, the speed of service is fast, so do not let the line keep you from this amazing place. 

I recommend one of everything, the salsa is great too.

Categories: Food, Coffee Roasteries, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Salad, Sandwiches | Review: Tasty coffee is a very nice clean space. The grab and go salads looked amazing!  Very large patio to practice social distancing.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: So this place made top 10 must eats in Santa Barbara. I believe that list should be revised. I'll start with the positives; knowledgeable and friendly staff, fresh local ingredients, generous appetizers, and good drinks.

Alas, the negatives outweigh positives. Salt and sweetness are being overused. It's the first time I had brussel sprouts smothered in sweet sauce which just totally killed the flavor for me. Leave them savory, sometimes Simplicity works much better. We could not finish the plate and had to rely on free popcorn to cleanse the palate; perhaps that's the reason they serve it. In addition, leaving them in the trunk for an hour gave birth to the most horrible smell I have yet experienced. I really thought I would need detailing afterwards and had flashback of that Seinfeld episode. 

I did enjoy the salads and was curious enough to try the $50 prime rib dish. Although the dish was good and creative I can't say it was shareable unless you fill up an appetizers prior. It just left me feeling underwhelmed and prices are not worth the attempt at creativity and the quality of the meal. 

The Lark may serve better as I happy hour with shareable appetizers among a large group. But this is not a dining experience.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Japanese, Restaurants | Review: Though the cuts of sashimi were large, the price was a little more than I would usually pay. The fell is totally Santa Barbra. Because I didn't do any of their specially rolls, I can't really comment on the sushi. But the fish is fresh!

Categories: Korean, Restaurants, American (New), Mexican, Tacos, Nightlife, Fast Food, Bars | Review: Awesome tacos.  Cool location right next to batting cages if you're into that.  Good prices too. Will be coming back soon.

Categories: Bars, Karaoke, Nightlife | Review: came here with my boyfriend, cool atmosphere, and friendly staff. Tanya, the owner is awesome. Big plus to having the sunday ticket. will definitely be back to handout and watch some football.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Japanese, Restaurants | Review: Sakana is one of the better sushi restaurants in town but nothing here in SB compares to SF or LA. The service is good but the tables are very cramped and it can get warm in there pretty quick. Well-priced compared to Arigato with less noise...

Categories: Organic Stores, Caterers, Grocery, Specialty Food, Event Planning & Services, Health Markets, Food | Review: Many of the people that work in this store seem totally miserable.  Terrible customer service and rude.  A  reflection  of management?  Even the "manager" is rude. I come here as little as possible because it's already so extremely over priced.  It never feels positive and there is virtually no eye contact or warmth from anyone.  You feel like you're bothering anyone that is "helping" you.  Of all the WF stores I have shopped, this is by far the worst.  It causes me to wonder if these people are being paid properly or if they are taken care of properly in anyway.  Thank God for Trader Joe's and other wonderful stores we have access  to.  I don't need Whole Foods! Especially not the Santa Barbara store.

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Nightlife, Seafood, Bars | Review: Great food...had the calamari and hubby had the top sirloin...both were very good..service was outstanding...mila was great...drinks were awesome...try the beach Bloody Mary!! Also beautiful view..

Categories: Dive Bars, Nightlife, Bars | Review: The ultimate dive bar.  Cheap stiff drinks, pool tables, and an awesome juke box.  Don't come here to be wowed, but to relax at the place where you know everyones name.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Nightlife | Review: Excellent Salsa! And, they're happy to give you extra. The crab cakes Benedict with Chipotle hollandaise and chorizo burrito were very tasty. The outdoor patio is dog friendly. The staff is great too. The Coffee is only so-so, on the darker/burnt side.

Categories: Pakistani, Restaurants, Buffets, Seafood, Halal, Indian, Vegetarian, Vegan, Food, Salad, Desserts | Review: By far the best Indian in Santa Barbara! I love this place and am always sad when I go somewhere else. They are very friendly and helpful. Even when it is the buffet,  a cheap all you can eat quick lunch, they continue to bring fresh naan and give great table service. I dream of their aloo gobhi and bengan bharta! Their mint/cilantro spicy sauce is to die for!
Huge fan!

Categories: Nightlife, Wine Bars, Bars, American (Traditional), Tapas/Small Plates, Tapas Bars, Food, Arts & Entertainment, Wine Tasting Room, Wineries, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Worth finding, and actually on the way to EVERYWHERE.  Everything here is better than it needs to be.  
The space is cute and sincere.  Wine selections are lovely, perhaps more significantly:  all available at Tasting, Half Glass, and Glass pours. 
Okay.  The food.  So I am both enamored with, and skeptical of, small plates wine bars.  
Get the grilled cheese that's struck you fancy.  Secchetti was a stand out.  
I wouldn't have minded one more green salad.  But if you're leaving the funk zone for la, or places unknown, you are doing yourself a diservice not to stop here.
Categories: Sandwiches, Specialty Food, Food, Health Markets, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants | Review: Best smoothies in the whole world. And fresh bread is amazing. Very knowledgeable staff.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: This place is fantastic! I'm from a heavily populated area of Hispanic culture, so Mexican food is pretty common and very traditional. 
This was great. Everything was great. The ambience, the time of service, delivery of food, the taste. Its all excellent. 
Highly recommend. I got the enchiladas and the others at our table enjoyed their burritos. I imagine anything you order here would be excellent.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: Came here because of the Axxess card and was very happy with the food and service. I would go around lunch time because the dinner menu is more expensive but the portions arent that much bigger.

The Japanese Pumpkin Ravioli w/ reduced balsamic sauce is the best!

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars | Review: 5 stars if you are between 18-21.
1 star if you are over 21.
3 stars is the compromise.

It's all the kind of skeevy, strong drink fun with nasty dancing that you could ever want as a college student, but if you are beyond that......it's skeevy with bad-tasting drinks and nasty dancing from horny little boys.

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis | Review: Asked cashier about the romaine gorgonzola salad. He didnt know but asked his coworker. Coworker said it was his favorite but it felt that recommendation was a bit fake. Salad ended up being unenjoyable. The tabasco sauce dressing is weird but more importantly the salad was ice cold. The avocado was cold,  the lettuce was iced, and the whole thing was just too cold. I guess they prepare them ahead of time and put in fridge i dunno. I wanted to take it back but i did order it so i tried to eat it.  Also the turkey or whatever the meat was tasted like sandwich thin deli meat from supermarket. Well sorry for the harsh review but the place has 4.5 stars and i came because of yelp

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Tiki Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: Cool decor and vibe. The mixologists are friendly and accommodating. The smoothies pack a nice punch. I've tried the Mango and the Pain Killer. Both strong and enjoyable. I appreciate that they don't want to make super sugary drinks. 

However, the cocktails that come in the tiki mugs are cute but super small and not strong. I like the lime garnish with the absinthe foam that comes with one of the cocktails (can't remember name). My niece got the Oaxacan in the tiki mug last week and she complained that it was gone immediately. 

When I brought this feedback up to the bartender, he said they didn't want to make strong cocktails so you wouldn't only drink 1 or 2. He tried to make it sound like this was a policy in the best interest of the customers but I made a joke that the reason folks love Joe's and Harry's are the strong drinks. Not to mention the prices at those 2 places are several dollars cheaper per drink. 

So I like this place but they need to amp up the alcohol in their drinks (smoothies are fine). To me, it comes off as a lot of bs without much return for paying $11 for a drink.

Categories: Bars, Pubs, Breweries, Nightlife, Food | Review: Lovely atmosphere. Whenever my husband and I want to escape the hustle and bustle of LA we drive up to Carpinteria and always stop by Island Brewing and have a few drinks before making our way to the beach. Staff is friendly and the thing I love the most is it's very family friendly here. We have 2 small kids and they always make their way to the kids play room. Wish it was closer to home so we could visit more often!

Categories: Chinese, Restaurants, Szechuan | Review: Still new, but looks promising.  I stopped by for lunch with my dad only about a week after the restaurant opened.  The interior has been completely renovated and looks quite nice.  Service was friendly and attentive; you can tell these people are looking to win over customers.

As we waited for our food, we saw a group of half a dozen people or so at a round table across the room.  Soon a guy came out with a cart on which lay a whole Peking duck.  He proceeded to carve it up for the group.  Very cool.  Also, one of the chefs brought us a small plate of noodles and vegetables as a complimentary appetizer, bowed wordlessly, then disappeared.

I ordered the pork with garlic sauce and my dad got fried rice with Shrimp.  Both were pretty good.  The portion size was ample.
