The only downside is that at this point they don't have a lunch menu as such, so most items are a bit on the pricey side for lunch.  (My garlic pork was $12, which I think was the cheapest meat entree available.)  As I said, the portions are fairly large, so it's probably a decent deal for dinner, but at lunch I'm usually looking to pay a bit less and get a bit less. Hopefully they will soon add lunch specials to the menu as Chinese restaurants often do.  (We suggested this to them as we were leaving.)  I may go back to try them for dinner, where I'm more prepared to pay more and get more.

Worth a look!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: Food food so hip so fresh so yum yum happy to eat here every time!

Yum yum Indonesian and yum yum Brussels sprouts!!!

Good vibes yum yum ^_^

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: The owners are so cute and nice !! I completely support local shops!! It kinda sucks that yogurt land is right across from this place. Their yogurt is a little bit more expensive than yogurt land's but the service and crepes make up for it !!! They have the best crepes !! Usually it's wrapped up in a nice cone shape for you to hold while you eat it!! If you want to share their delicious crepes on a plate, you can ask them to do so with the frozen yogurt on top of the crepe rather than inside.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Food, Specialty Food, Japanese | Review: This place sucked. Sushi is mediocre and expensive, chefs take FOREVER to make your food. Rolls fell apart as we ate them. To add insult to injury, had to endure some weirdo talking to my husband and I at the sushi bar the entire time. Bad experience from start to finish. Left unsatiated.

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Peruvian | Review: Food was delicious but the service was poor. Took a long time to get our order placed and never got a refill on our drinks until we got our check.
Categories: Pizza, Restaurants, Italian, Vegetarian | Review: Great little bistro with eclectic pizzas and beautiful decor. Over 50 italian wines and great cocktails.  Must try right on State Street.

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Meh.  Sucktastic. Underwhelming.

Wished we walked a little bit further up the street for breakfast.  But we ended up here - go figure.

It was pretty packed so we figured it must be good.  I think, because it was raining, people just wanted to get out of the rain and found this place.

Food was not good.  At best, mediocre.  Ordered the harvati cheese omelette and the french toast.  Yikes.  I could make a better a tastier omelette and french toast at home.  Besides the food being eh, the time it took to get our food royally SUCKED.  We waited about 30 minutes for our food!?!?!?  What the fu@#? And during this time, there were only about 5 people in the restaurant.

Waitstaff was friendly, but slow.  Would not venture here again.  Be patient and walk up a few more stores and you will find a better selection of restaurants.

Categories: Latin American, Salad, Food, Desserts, Restaurants | Review: Great service. They've changed the menu few months back so you'll find less exptc food. Still good though. We tried the scallops and bonemarrow. Both very good. I did't really like that chocolate ham main dish. They were nice enough to remove it from the bill. We tried few other dishes. All good. Very friendly chef and the server.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Nightlife, Restaurants, Comfort Food, American (New), Burgers, Salad | Review: Beautiful restaurant, friendly staff, fast service and most importantly lots of lovely food. This menu has a ton of flavor and enough variety for everyone! I had the turkey chili with SB Grilled Cheese and my fiance had the Chicken Tikka Masala. While we'd definitely order both of these item again, I'll probably try something new because everything on the menu looks so damn good!

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: Loovin Spoonful is better than any frozen yogurt place I have been to. The unique flavors taste so accurate its insane. It literally feels like you are eating whatever the indicated flavor is. Go try it out and be blown away. Very affordable as well.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: My girlfriend and I were fairly dissapointed with our dinner this evening especially after reading it was voted best seafood restaurant of 2009 on SantaBarbara.com. We sat on the patio which was nice but the service was seriously lacking. Our waitress took our order and never came back until after we were done eating. We both got some clam chowder which was OK. (For real clam chowder go to "The Cannery" in Newport Beach). I got the fried shrimp plate and my girlfriend got the scampi. Both were just OK. I don't think we'll be back.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, American (Traditional), Venues & Event Spaces | Review: After going to Harry's for only my second time in my 8 years of living in SB last night, I've decided that it was my last. My friend and I were quickly seated, and enjoyed a cadillac margarita filled to the brim. I ordered a full meal, my friend got a side, and we enjoyed our drinks. Our waitress definitely let us know she was not impressed he was only getting a side. (Awkward) My food arrived and it was probably the worst corned beef and cabbage I've ever had, so disappointing! (I will never stray from Dargan's delicious corned beef and cabbage again). I ate maybe half because it was expensive and I certainly would not be taking it home to eat later. The waitress returned to the table, took everything off and gave the bill. $60 bill for 2 drinks, corned beef and a side. It seemed a little rushed, as we were still having drinks, and planning to order more. Her attitude was starting to turn my experience sour, so we decided we'd go elsewhere for a second drink, since we definitely did not need to pay more for this awful service. Apparently, they do not accept Discover cards, and if you try to pay with one, you will have your card thrown back at you. The waitress then stood at the table as I, now flustered and even more unhappy, am digging through my wallet to find an acceptable card. I made eye contact with another patron who witnessed this event, and we shared a shocked look! My goal was to give a good tip because we were mainly there for drinks and I understand that people often do not tip an ideal amount when they get less food, but after she ruined my evening and I was ready to storm out, I left a subpar tip and noted the service was the issue. Awful service, terrible corned beef and cabbage - we will not be returning.

Categories: Restaurants, Soup, Salad, Thai | Review: I've been coming to Mesa Thai for years (that's what us locals call this place). The service is good and food is excellent! My favorite dish is the spicy Thai basil chicken-unbelievably good! Definitely worth a visit or just get it to go :)

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Desserts, Turkish, Restaurants, Bakeries | Review: This place is amazing...after entering I had that feeling like to be in a fairytale. I found out that all materials for the interior decoration where brought from Turkey, even the tiles. On top of it, you can ask for the furnitures or all equipments, they can provide it for you (next to Lokum you'll find Seven Hills, that their "Grand Bazaar", you have to take a look).

Let's talk about the sweets...well, if you ever been in Turkey then you'll know that this place has the original taste. I'm amazed about the variety they provide. My favorite is the Baklava with Turkish ice cream. 
The Turkish coffee is the usual original one, but they do have also different flavors like hazelnut, cardamom and more, just ask. If you never taste Turkish Coffee before, don't be surprised about the coffee ground..it's not like espresso. 

Last but not least, the employees. They are well trained and very friendly. 

At Lokum you'll find the Turkish hospitality in all details, I highly recommend this place.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: Best happy hour! And phenomenal food always, regardless of happy hour. We love the Pigs in a Blanket!!!

Categories: Bakeries, Food, Patisserie/Cake Shop, Cafes, Restaurants | Review: This place had really good options for food and pastries. The pastries were beautiful. I like that they had verve coffee here. My latte was great. The dining area was also nice. Looked like there was some outside seating but it was pretty busy. I still didn't have a long wait,

Categories: Korean, Restaurants | Review: I don't know why this place is getting such poor reviews. I went here for dinner and ordered the spicy pork off the barbecue specials, and shared a chicken bulgogi, Everything was well seasoned and marinated perfectly. The portion size for the bbq was what to be expected, and the bulgogi was enough to be an entire meal, and delicious in and of itself.

The sides were varied and quite delicious; they kept the sides refilled whenever we needed them. The service was quite good, refills of drinks, sides, etc. I would definitely go back to this place for dinner and would love to try it for its lunch time deal!

Categories: Seafood, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: We always get a steak on our last night of vacation. We've been on the search on where to get one. While walking out the pier - we stopped and looked at the menu and saw they had a "happy hour" from 3:30-6 that had a deal for two. 
We both got the sirloin steak - with a baked potato and vegetables. 
Right after we ordered - I decided to look at the reviews for this place. My heart sank as I was sitting there reading all the terrible reviews!! 
Shortly after - our steaks arrived. They looked picture perfect! 

So! Give this place a shot. The hostess was very kind - the waitress was nice and kept our drinks filled - and delivered everything we asked for in a matter of seconds. 

Our steaks were cooked properly - with great flavor. The potato was piping hot - and the veggies were delicious and well seasoned. 

The view is fantastic and can't be beat. 

Glad we stopped in today!!

Categories: Pizza, Restaurants, Italian, Vegetarian | Review: During our trip to Santa Barbara, we wanted to dine at a Italian place. This place safely distanced the tables so there were no overcrowding unlike many restaurants on State St. so we were open to dine in. The service was friendly and they provide you with waters and bread. 

We ordered the Mediterranean pizza and a lunch special. All of it was filling and delicious. The pizza had a great crunch and crisp. The price was at a medium point. Not cheap but not expensive. 

COVID:
Outdoor seating. Hand sanitizer and bathrooms provided. Socially distanced tables. Workers have masks.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Don't come here if you want them to get your order correct or if you are in a hurry...empty place and it took 20 minutes for them to get my order wrong, and 10 more to fix it...it was just 6 tacos.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (New), Food | Review: Such a cute breakfast spot! I had the egg white frittata which came with a half avocado covered in seeds and strawberries. The frittata had potatoes inside which added a nice different texture :-) My BF got a salmon omelette which he really enjoyed too!
Great seating inside and out. Very convenient to the beachfront hotels on State Street!

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Good ice cream expensive especially prepackaged pints at Albertsons like $8.50. $10 milkshake is ridiculous I understand premium ingredients but it's just cream,milk and sugar base. Understand capitalism and profit also have to live on $900 a mo. So it's a luxury for me or settle for Hagen Daz on sale.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: The pad see ew tastes quite good! Taking away one star for the small issue that it doesn't taste like Thai food at all

Categories: Restaurants, Delis, Food, Specialty Food, Grocery, Sandwiches | Review: Sandwiches are pretty GREAT and they make them super quickly. If you want to be a part of a zoo, then go during lunch hours. Other than that, you shouldn't have a long wait. 

You can't go wrong with any sandwich so just pick one and go for it! I highly suggest getting a cookie on your way out. 

SHOUTOUT to the lady that's always there in the front handling business and getting your order to you

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Desserts, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Seafood | Review: FisHouse has always been a favorite dinner and happy hour spot. Tried the brunch with the Axxess deal today and it was to die for! Also their pancake breakfast shot taste literally like a pancake breakfast

Categories: Tuscan, Seafood, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife, Italian | Review: An amazing start to a food tour in Santa Barbara. This small Italian restaurant has a lot of character and style. 

I sampled the Ravioli Al Burro Salvia which was delicate but delicious. Filled with spinach and ricotta. Yum!

Then, I tried the Tagliatelle Alla Bolognese. Spinach pasta in a complex but flavorful Bolognese sauce. Wow!

Worth a return trip.
Categories: Restaurants, Bakeries, Food | Review: Worst bread and challa I have ever eaten, cookies sit in the case for days...to top it all off, it's ridiculously expensive and rude.

Categories: Food, American (New), Thai, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Desserts, Cafes, Sandwiches, Caterers, Event Planning & Services | Review: What a fantastic find! The service, food and ambience is wonderful. The pork bahn mi is easily now my favorite sandwich in Santa Barbara. The sweet and spicy balance is perfect, it tastes very fresh. The pork is tender and flavorful, the bread is soft, delicate, and perfectly toasted. The beet salad also blew me away by how much delicious flavor is disguised in a seemingly simple dish, definitely recommended. Jordan (if I remember her name correctly) was awesome! She was passionate and knowledgeable about food, and chatted with my wife and I for a while. Great service! We can hardly wait to try their late night menu!

Categories: Food, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants | Review: I loved the orange banana with total!  It was healthy and an amazing snack.  I love how I can choose a extra ingredient to go with it for free.  It was an awesome experience and I will always go back for more.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Not often am I surprised in a good way, this place did. The food was pretty darn decent and the salsas were above average. I would definitely come back again.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, American (Traditional), Venues & Event Spaces | Review: Went in with my cousin who's a california resident I'm visiting from Florida food was good only complaint I ordered a mean tai which Harry's I hear is famous for drinks and I never got it so after 20 min I gave up then after dinner I said something to the server and all's he did was say I'm sorry and said that happens sometimes so I was disappointed and then he forgot to sit the check which he corrected had they remembered my drink or not been apathetic when we told him would have been a higher rating

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Cafes, Coffee Roasteries, Coffee & Tea | Review: Friendly staff nice atmosphere and reasonably priced! Would be a nice place to study except the music is a little loud for my taste. Also have an environmentally conscious "for here" option where they provide silverware and mugs which I appreciate!! Vanilla latte and bagel were both delicious. Sat here and had breakfast while I waiting for my Laundry to be done next store! Super convenient.

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: We go here frequently for lunch because it's close to our office. They serve a healthy meal and always have a catch of the day. The service is usually pretty good and the people are friendly. They own the restaurant down the way so if you want go to the restroom you walk down a few stores. They have handled Covid nicely and I would recommend this place to anyone who wants a healthy lunch at a reasonable price. Oh and finally, they don't over cook the fish!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: Glad we stopped here for Sat Brunch on our way up the coast!  Saw it on yelp, but as we were getting out of our car to do some window shopping we heard a group comment on Tupelo saying how good it always is...
            We agree, from the 5 min wait(11pm) to the quick service everything allowed us to enjoy our meal and our company!  We look forward to visiting again to explore the menu!

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Excellent food. Excellent service. Owners are amazing and go out of their way for you. Soup, popcorn, sandwiches were excellent. Our fave was the Pickles and Swiss sandi. We will definitely be back

Categories: Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Worst meal in years... we had a reservation and when we arrived they were clearly overwhelmed.  Why didn't they warn us that on Thanksgiving they were out of bread, mashed potatoes and half their desserts?  Poor management continued to accept takeout orders when people with reservations weren't getting the correct food.  Worst part: they ran out of creme anglais to go with the teeny tiny pumpkin pie slice so they served it with HORSERADISH SAUCE WITH NO WARNING!  Totally surprised one of our party who took a big bite of the "creme anglais". What is wrong with them? Then they charged us full price for the meal even though they were out of half of the items and substituted w/o warning.  Horrible.  DO NOT EAT HERE.  Taco Bell would have been better and I hate Taco Bell.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: OMG. Dude, you need to go here. I mean this is some of the best Mexican I've ever had. First, as you may have read they have a salsa bar. Cool. The point is that the salsa is DELICIOUS. There are about 8 choices, I really loved the one that has a BBQ pepper kind of taste. The food comes FAST. I got the tacos Del mar with grilled shrimp. It was everything you want. Cabbage, shrimp,mango. The BEST rice I have had in my life as a side. And the salad had some sort of sweet dressing which was yummy and sweet. We made this our pit stop driving up to SLO, and it was soooo worth it. When we drive back down to LA, we will be stopping here again. Your belly will be happy after this. Prepare to unbutton your pants.

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese | Review: Sushi Teri is pretty decent. I typically get some kind of special roll- I love the Orange Dragon roll.  I always get the seaweed salad, it's my favorite around town. Their udon soups are also pretty good. The chicken curry udon is my go to. 

I think that the sushi/ sashimi combinations are too expensive to be worth it. You can get sushi combination platters of similar quality elsewhere. 

I there like the service is somewhat slow, and I'm disappointed almost every time I go.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Lost its SOUL! Overpriced crappy bowl! I used to love this place-yesterday I was very disappointed! I got the same soup I always get-only all of the cooked and fresh vegetables used to be carefully cut and measured and ripe/fresh-now it's just a pile of poorly chopped "crap" tossed carelessly in the bowl-big chunks of sad cilantro-long mis matched celery-wrong amounts of everything-NO SOUL-NO CARE! What happened to the care and precision that was once put into all the dishes? They made a tune of money and got cocky? Too expensive for careless mass produced food!!! Maybe the cat was away so the mice played? No chef present-the staff was kind-but all very distracted with heavy flirting with each other- female employee was showing the 2 young male employees pictures of bra/panty sets on her phone and hugging touching them....very cute and actually entertaining /genuinely fun to watch- but "can I order"!?! Are the choppers underpaid machines? Looks and tastes that way. I'll never go back-sad because I was devoted. If you must go here-call ahead of time and see if the chef is there-I think the quality might be better if she's there!?

Categories: Burgers, Salad, Restaurants, Desserts, Food, Chicken Shop | Review: Healthy delicious burgers/sandwiches/salads/soups, and proceeds benefit local charities.

My first visit there, I had the California Club (grilled chicken, bacon, avo) and tortilla soup which both had good flavor and were of good size. I'm eager to try more of their tempting menu. You can choose two items for around $10, which is a good deal for lunch or dinner!

Service is quick and friendly, order at counter and food is brought to your table. Self serve drinks.

There's a Kyle's downtown and a new one just opened up near Costco in Goleta.

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: I love Zaytoon, the owner does a great job of constantly re-innovating this place and constantly making progress towards being the best it can possibly be. Most businesses go the cheap route and it shows. The drink and food menu's are constantly updated and need I say more about the ambiance? From flowers and lights to the fire pits, this is an amazing place to take a date, or a great night out with the boys!

Categories: Noodles, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Creperies, Chinese, Asian Fusion | Review: I was a little skeptical about the Asian food in IV, so I was pleasantly surprised with my amazing experience at Lao Wang. I love how they've accepted the college town vibes. Also, the girl who took my order was super friendly and helpful.

Definitely recommend the bao buns, they were really good!

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food | Review: I watched him make my drink and he used normal milk when I ordered soy, and then when I asked him about it he told me he had used soy milk. I didn't want to cause a scene but my drink went directly in the trash after having my friend taste it and confirm it was indeed dairy milk. He had also warmed up the milk for an iced drink and then poured it over ice causing the ice to melt and the beverage to be lukewarm?? He also never smiled and stared at us until we felt uncomfortable. It was also 77 degrees (very warm) in the shop and no outlets to charge. Absolutely terrible. Worst coffee shop I've ever been to (and I've been to many) and wish I was able to drink my drink. Biggest waste of $5 due to careless, lying employee.

Categories: Wine Bars, Restaurants, American (New), Nightlife, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: My first time in Santa Barbara we stopped in here for a drink. I had the Diablo chili fusion cocktail and it was perfect on every level.  

We didn't get any food here but it smelled amazing. COme to find out there is another hungry cat in LA. I ate there and will be reviewing the food for that location at a later time.

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Nightlife, Seafood, Bars | Review: Left a $10-tip; they charged a $20-tip. When I called, they said they will return the extra $10 they have charged, but I am still thinking about the scenario if I had not checked my bank statement and had not seen this particular charge... P.S. I have no concern regarding their food; only about the way they apply charges to client accounts.

Categories: Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Bars | Review: I had a fun time here! I like the different places you can go and the music they played. My only complaints are that the bathroom can be a bit of a wait and sometimes the fog was a bit too much to see. Other than that though this is a fun night club =D

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Coffee Roasteries | Review: Love the French Press! Came here everyday while in Santa Barbara. The staff was kind and energized. They seemed to be enjoying themselves and never became stressed when there was a long line of people.

It gets a little squishy inside when there is a long line but the line seems to move fast. AND the patio seating is superb. Comfortable chairs with pretty plants make for a good camp out.

The pastries are all made by the French Press at their location on Anacapa, where they also roast the coffee. Get a muffin and some macarons (75¢ each!).

For coffee try a cappuccino or iced americano. If your in a sweet mood go for the iced tres leches latte - not overly sweet and definitely delicious!

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis | Review: I can't believe it's taken me this long to write a review of South Coast Deli. Finally breaking out of the house and going to get some food. I'm tired of my cooking :D

Before COVID-19 I would go there probably 3 days a week. They have a lot of great salad options. I'm partial to the romaieand gorgonzola but I prefer to have kale instead of romaine. The other one I go for is the Asian green salad with chicken. I usually get it to go but I ALWAYS ask for dressing on the side and put it on myself and give it a shake. That's the only thing I would ding them on is that if you forget it will be HEAVY on the dressing.  When I do sandwiches, I usually get wither the Dars sky high (it's basically a reuben sandwich but with coleslaw), get it on RYE though! My other favorite is the Meatball sandwich, ADD PESTO! I don't get the panins much anymore after they switched the bread to sourdough. They do need to find a good panini bread again! Lastly the staff is always awesome, up for a chat when it's not slammed, most of them remember my name now, and my order quirks :)

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: This is a real deal bakery.  I haven't appreciated a bakery's goods this much since Give Thanks Bakery where I'm from.  Their danishes are really good, and their chocolate cake is ridiculous.  They offered a sample of their almond butter danish, and I was tempted to get that, as well.
This is real dessert, people.  I've seen comments from some people about liking Costco danishes more than these authentic ones and, well...that's just wrong.  These danishes have the crispy, fluffy layers of real buttery dough, with real raspberry/apricot/cream cheese filling.  I can eat a Costco danish like the best of 'em, but those are slimy mass-production messes compared to these real ones.  These are also 4 times the price, so you get what you pay for :)
The chocolate cake was real chocolate, too.  I mean cocao, not hydrogenated oil with chocolate flavoring.  Very rich, moist cake -- and there was some alcohol-infused simple syrup added, likely to keep it moist longer.  Whatever the reason for it, it only added to the taste.
Simply nothing bad to say about this place.  And when I walked in with my jeans and T-shirt and talkative 2.5 year old in a stroller, I was treated just as courteously as everyone else.  Sometimes you're frowned upon in nicer establishments by not catering to the high-end image.
Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Arts & Entertainment, Food, Wineries | Review: Had a great time, didn't try a wine I didnt like and I'm a picky. Great vide! Good music, great for a sunny day

Categories: Restaurants, Chicken Wings, Sandwiches, Breakfast & Brunch, Pizza, Burgers | Review: passion in every bite,cozy lil place 
the owners are over the top hospitable 
all in all  awesome

Categories: Bubble Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food | Review: Love this spot. I've had boba previously but nothing of this quality. There is a large selection of flavors and combinations that are sure to please. My favorites are the lychee green tea, lavender taro milk tea, and spice chai tea. A simple cantaloupe slushy is great ad well. There is always something new on the menu and the drinks get seem to exceed my expectations everytime. Mike the owner is delightful and welcoming. Making my 805 Boba experience special. Thanks Mike!

Categories: Ethnic Food, Restaurants, Mexican, Specialty Food, Imported Food, Food | Review: The best veggie burrito (and salsa) you'll find anywhere. The service is atrocious and they know it, but who cares. I guarantee your belly won't regret it. If you're ordering to-go and don't speak Spanish, prepare for a whole lot of confusion. And don't be deterred by the terrifying woman who mans the register. Yes, she hates you. No, she's not poisoning your rice, as I was convinced my first time there.

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Mediterranean | Review: Visited here after me and my fiancé got engaged when it was Somersert. Absolutely amazing food, even better drinks and best of all was the service!

Categories: Seafood, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, Farmers Market, Restaurants, Food | Review: I left a message for santa barbara fish market on Friday and they called me back today. The customer service is great. No complaints. The oil spill in Santa Barbara has not affected their local fish supply in regards to quality and quantity. They are doing well and they're still up and running which was really good news for me.

I have lived all over California. But I always call santa barbara fish market when I need premium grade fish. I've never had a bad experience with their sushi a.k.a. premium grade fish.you can always call them and ask them if the local Santa Barbara fishes any good and they will give you an honest answer for you purchase.

Over the last six years I've been here four times every time I visited here it has always been really good and I find something new that is in season that I may not of known about that is not on their website as well. So I always recommend going in just to see what they have and to see if they have anything new. It's also nice to talk to the people in the front because they're really nice . And if you're into people watching it is nice to watch how skillfully the workers in the back prepare the  fish. 


They are also the only place that I know of in Southern California and that sells Spanish marinated anchovies.these are my favorite. I would imagine they would be one of the highlights of any dinner party that they are served at.

Scottish salmon fresh to death 

The are you tonight is a beautiful color and a nice shade of pink

The Yellow tail taste very fresh and I am not sure if they have young yellow tail or not. But the hamachi is good.

The swordfish that I've cooked there has turned out very well I am pretty sure it's the ingredients and not the chef.

Chilean Seabass was a little expensive but I think it was worth it when my friend prepared it for us it was really nice and buttery.

 The local halibut is so good I usually buy half a pound and dip it in soy sauce and eat it on the spot. And by another pound or two to take home for later. (Yes I'm a fatty.)

Another thing is, I come here in person and I purchase two trays of uni and then I realize that it was so good that I go back and get another two to share with whichever friend I came up to visit Santa Barbara with it. Always really good and really sweet and well prepared. No complaints.

Seaweed salad is good. The ahi pokey is really good. I got suckered into it when they gave me a sample of it. I have not been able to replicate the deliciousness. The ceviche is pretty good I'm always a little wary of ceviche if I don't make myselfbut no problems when I've eaten it.

They have a wide variety of local fish that they bring in and you can call them on a day-to-day basis to see if they have anything I just made a call today and they had Rockfish and local halibut which was  just caught that day.

Thankfully I do not have to drive to santa barbara fish market every time that I have a craving for their high quality fish. There overnight deal is pretty nice and it works out well for me most the time. The packages don't get lost and I am usually very happy every time. I hope you enjoy your experience at santa barbara fish market bon appétit.

Categories: Bubble Tea, Restaurants, Food, Asian Fusion, Vegan, Acai Bowls | Review: This place is so delicious and amazing. I go for the vegan options! The vegan mini is inexpensive and filling! There is extra teriyaki sauce on the side so I always add more of it, along with hot sauce.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Burgers, American (New) | Review: Although Eureka! may be considered a chain restaurant, the one in Paseo Nuevo feels more like a local Santa Barbara staple. My last visit was particularly great due to our waiter, Christopher. He made sure we were well-informed about the menu and went out of his way to check up on us despite it being a busy Saturday night. I can tell Eureka does a good job hiring their employees based off the service I received tonight, grateful to have such a great place to recommend to others when they visit SB.

Categories: Nightlife, Restaurants, Steakhouses, Bars, American (Traditional) | Review: Long story short, my food took forever to come out. My friend and I waited like 35 minutes to get our food served . We looked around and it was not as busy. I don't blame the server because it was clear the she was bar tending AND serving. Management should have more employees work the floor ESPECIALLY if its during the lunch hour. Service and the quality of food is everything.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Overall the restaurant was an awesome find. The salsa and chips were delicious. I ordered the "Tacos del Mar" with halibut. It didn't take long for the food to arrive and it look very good. I took one bite of the taco and was surprised. There was no overpowering fish taste but there was really no taste to the taco. There was sauce under the fish that had plenty of flavor when I tried it alone, but I don't think there was enough for the whole taco. The rice on the other hand was amazing, it had the perfect amount of saltiness. The best part of the meal was the salad. Everything about the salad was perfect. I highly recommend this restaurant as I'm sure it was a fluke about the fish. Last thing, I don't recommend sitting by the window facing the main road during sunset, it can get pretty hot.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: This is a solid Thai place for Santa Barbara which doesn't seem to have a variety of cuisines to choose from. 

The thai iced tea was nice and large, and was made nicely - not too sweet, not too tea-y. The fried chicken wings were very delicious. It only comes with 4 pieces, but their the wing/drum pieces, so they were 4 large pieces. It was cooked perfectly to a golden brown crisp and tasted very good by themselves or with the sauce/cilantro. I also had the ribs, which were a little strange at first. They were saucy, but the sauce it was cooked in would seep into the other parts of the dish like the rice and the salad. After the first impression, the ribs actually tasted good - especially with the fish sauce that comes in the caddy on the table.

(Date of visit: 09/08/17)

Categories: Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Bars | Review: My friends and I had a good time here.  We did not know what to expect, after reading some of the reviews here, on yelp.  It is true that some of the guys did not have a clue, when we were trying to get away from them, but I think that that is true everywhere.  We had a great bartender, Jeremiah.  He was very good to us and mixed some great drinks.  If I ever go back to Santa Barbara, I might check this place out again.

I suppose, it is all who you go out with, that contributes to your experience in a place, as well.

Categories: Steakhouses, Restaurants | Review: Everything was ok except my steak.  Ordered the Ribeye and was able to eat about a third of it.  What looked like a steak ended up being a steak of fat with small pieces of meat scattered about. Also it was not seasoned I had to put salt in it.  My daughters sirloin on the other hand was good but also needed to be seasoned.  It's a shame that there is a cook working there that does not know how to make the beef shine through proper seasoning.

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: Sadly, really bad.  We had a bartender that clearly had very little experience, if any at all. Took forever to get served and the drinks weren't even right. Why throw a beginner behind a bar during peak hours on a Saturday night? Doesn't make sense. The crowd was also pretty horrible. I've had many great times at Sharkeez in Newport Beach, so this was a let down.

Categories: Specialty Food, Cheese Shops, Cafes, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Wine Bars, Sandwiches, Food, Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Best roast beef sandwich in all of SB.  It's seriously so good.  They are known for their grilled cheese sandwiches which are also excellent, but the roast beef sandwich leaves me unable to remember the last time I ordered a grilled cheese.

FYI - The roast beef is a big sandwich, filling

They make a pretty good espresso too, which is incredibly rare for any restaurant to do!

Categories: French, Cafes, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Sandwiches, Bakeries | Review: How could anybody rate this less than 5 stars??? This place is unbelievable. We literally ordered one of everything, and nothing disappointed us!

The feel of this place as well as how amazing everything was prepared is as close to Paris as you'll get in California. I would even say the quality of the pastries  is Better than most Parisian pâtisseries we've been to.

We thought the French press was pretty good which is where we went the day before, but the lattes here were perfect!!!

Thanks for an amazing breakfast and coffee!

Categories: Irish Pub, Nightlife, Bars, Pubs | Review: My days hanging out on State Street back in my 20s were legendary but now that I am in my early 40s I can't really do the nightclub scene anymore because I just feel silly being around the kiddos even though I'm still a kid at heart. Now when I am itching at the seams to get out and hear some live music I had to the James Joyce where I know I will always get some good live music to soothe my soul well I crack peanuts at the bar and chat with locals. Long live the Joyce! The bartenders are the same bartenders you were there when I was in my 20s! That is so crazy wicked cool!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, American (Traditional) | Review: Good food. But the space is small yet quaint. I had the Eggs Benedict which was the best I have had in a very long time. Fast service and clean. It's obviously a local spot and if in town worth the visit.

Categories: Restaurants, Salad, Sandwiches, Chicken Wings, Pizza | Review: Great local place, the pizza was great , we had both thin crust and thick crust pizza. They even have an old school salad bar. The staff was really nice and helpful. The biggest plus Is the  dining room is even dog friendly, we will be back .

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: I have a special place in my heart for McConnells I am not vegan by any means but the dark chocolate ice cream was amazing! Also the waffle cones are delicious. When it gets too hot they do offer people umbrellas if you have to stand outside since their storefront is on the smaller side. Their service is very fast and welcoming which is helpful since it is a popular location in the Funk zone.

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services | Review: This review is a bit delayed but I wanted to make sure that I publicly raved about the catering that Renato and Via Maestra 42 do.  I got married in October and they did an AMAZING job!  I actually worked for Rentao about 10 years ago and so I knew that his food and service would be top notch but I have to say, he exceeded my already high expectations.  

From the planning to the day of, Renato was personally there for every step.  You can really tell how much pride he has in the produce he puts out and know from the very beginning that you are in good hands. 

The food was so so good.  Everyone at my wedding was so pleased with everything; the passed appetizers were particularly delicious.  The service was amazing as well.  He had great servers that always made sure people had what they needed and that no one's drinks were empty.

Not only would I hire him again for any event but I regularly go to the restaurant which is also amazing.  

Take it from me (someone who worked at a high end catering company in San Francisco for 5 years), that you will not be sorry if you hire Via Maestra 42 for your party (or if you just go there for a delicious meal).

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: Tried it for the first time during a trip to SB. The food was amazing and the place looks great inside! Really enjoyed the fettuccine Alfredo with shrimp and garlic bread. Not expensive yet super tasty!

Categories: Caterers, Restaurants, Spanish, Personal Chefs, Event Planning & Services | Review: The Paella was awesome! They did both a traditional and a vegan paella for our organization's major donor event and it was incredible. Guests raved about the food. they were professional, on time, friendly, and made great food. Such a fun way to have catering at your event, it felt really fun and welcoming and smelled amazing while it was cooking. So glad we connected with them and will use them again for sure!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: So crazy! We made reservations but there is no host or seating. There is a line to order and a number for the table so you fight to get a table. Not a real restaurant

Categories: Food, Bagels, Donuts | Review: best bagels in town !! they have so many options, and always whip out your order super quick. I've been coming here for 3 years and have never been disappointed..not sure why their yelp page doesn't have 5 stars, but will gladly continue to support this great local business!

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Really good food! Fun place! Also with Dart  Coffee and Monkeyshine. Cute fun place and good food, all of it. Of course it's pricey but it's in the funk zone so it has to be.

Categories: Chicken Wings, Restaurants, Italian, Pizza | Review: Sal's has recently become my go-to delivery place (all thanks to my roommates who got me hooked). For $9 plus delivery charge, a satisfying amount of food comes right to you. There's definitely enough for two meals, but if you're a pigster like me, you'll scrape up every bite and wonder where it all went. 

I recommend the Cajun Chicken Sandwich with Provolone cheese (hold the mayo. Blech.) It comes with fries or salad too. I tend to switch off between the Caesar salad and Greek salad, which are pretty yummy. I've ordered a side of fries too, which are addicting. Hence why I try not to order them much. What can I say, fries are one of my guilty pleasures!

If you're on a budget and looking for a good meal that's worth your $$, Sal's is the place.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: Um can I ever remember a time I ate at a French restaurant?  No.... So with that, this was an elegant affair, with a small cozy dining experience and exquisite meals all around.  It is next Olio e Limone (sp?) and they have a smaller room next to it for private parties.  If you don't get a reservation here, especially on the weekends, forget about getting seated.  Great atmosphere and I ordered the Venison - amazingly done, not one ounce of fat and my GF had the lamb.  Great restaurant because everything is so well cooked and seasoned not to take over your tastebuds but to really give them an entertaining experience.  I've never remembered meat being cooked to perfection like that in years.  I would eat here again.  The blackberry sorbet is rediculous!  The Tartin dessert is great too.  Something about the smooth delivery of flavors here that will always leave me wondering how the French do it.

Categories: Specialty Food, Health Markets, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Food | Review: I can't give 5 stars because I've only been once. BUT the tart I got here was ABSOLUTELY AMAZING. Since I HAD (notice the had there.... not decided) to go GF, I've missed a lot of things.... and it being summer I REALLY missed fruit tarts. But man, this did NOT disappoint. It had the right texture, taste, and appeal. I want to drive to Santa Barbara on a regular basis now JUST to get a fruit tart. No joke. 
The cafe is nice and very State St. I had no issues with the staff and the seating was lovely. The prices were a touch high but again....State St. 
I WILL be back again. I want to try other amazing things.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: Excellent dinner....definitely add to your list of places to visit! We showed up with no reservation but they got us in within an hour. (Would recommend reservations) Service was good but could have been better as far as hostess and wait staff is concerned. Management seem to be top notch. Amazing chefs in the kitchen and it showed in food presentation and delishishness! Thank you for a great evening!

Categories: Breweries, Bars, Burgers, Mexican, Tacos, Cocktail Bars, Club Crawl, Breakfast & Brunch, Beer Bar, Nightlife, Restaurants, Food | Review: This was my first visit and it was very relaxing.  I had just arrived in Santa Barbara stopped here to grab some quick food.  We sat in the front part of the restaurant near the bar.  I loved the design of the bar, it was clean and decorated nicely.  Our server Brandon was friendly and talkative he out shined our waitress who was pleasant overall.  I ordered the Pollo Al Pastor: great flavors, generous portion and a little spicy along with the Guava Margarita.  I would love to visit this restaurant again and try more of the menu items. They have a patio and inside dining area as well.  The patio appears to be pet friendly.  Overall nice experience, good customer service, tasty food.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: So much fun here! You get drinks and appetizers down stairs while you wait for your table then head upstairs for a dining room overlooking the harbor. Oysters Rockefeller were awesome and so was the Bloody Mary! Yummmm I'll be back

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: My wife and I were in town and look for a nice place to have dinner and some drinks everywhere we called required reservation, Santo Mezcal took us as a walk in and we got seated within 10min. Food and ambiance was amazing but best of all was our waiter Omar, his timing and service was extraordinary he was a large component to our overall marvelous experience.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Food, Desserts, Japanese | Review: Five stars of yumminess. We also appreciated the restaurant layout. The tables are spaced graciously throughout and gave us a sense of privacy. We will definitely be back when we're in town.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Not very special and too expensive : (.  The only thing going for them is that they are on a touristy spot and they don't have competition on the pier.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars | Review: This place was an awesome find. Right in the middle of everything on State Street in Santa Barbara. Loved the warm lighting and ambiance. Had a shrub that was like nothing I've ever tasted. One flavor after another all in one sip - it was divine. Tapas were excellent and hit the spot. The owners were fun and friendly and obviously cared a great deal about our over all experience. Fresh ingredients, CREATIVE drinks and great atmosphere! Can't wait to try something else!

Categories: Health & Medical, Restaurants, Mexican, Cannabis Clinics, Barbeque | Review: This was outrageously awesome chicken!!! The chicken is 10-stars....it is THAT GOOD!

It has knocked off the perch my previous #1 Roast  chicken spot: Michoacan Pollo Al Carbon in Redwood City. Both offer reasonable prices for a whole roast chicken with sides, and to be fair, both are very different cooking styles and spices. Michoacan uses more Mexican spices and cooking styles straight from Michoacan--and it is killer. My kids love it (even my picky eaters)! This Santa Barbara Chicken place however was just a roast chicken with incredibly tasty spices, cooked to perfection. Amazing flavor. We kept asking: did we eat it all??

The whole chicken plate comes with a small cup of rice and small cup of beans and jalapeños on the side for about $23. Sorry didn't take pics...but the ones online at Yelp are perfect.

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Cafes, Desserts, Restaurants, Creperies | Review: The cafe is a very small space right next to the IV Deli Mart.

The owner is very nice though and the crepes are just fantastic. They can be a bit pricey, especially if you like to add more goodies like chicken or ice cream.

I've tried (so far):

1. Chicken Pesto Crepe = 3.5 stars; the chicken was delicious but I felt like it was missing an ingredient to really make it a "damn that's good" factor.

2. Margarita Crepe = 5 stars; this is absolutely my favorite! I add chicken to it and usually the person who makes the crepe is quite nice and adds extra pesto for free. It's the combination of chicken, pesto, cheese and marinara sauce that makes it so good. Plus, it's a bit cheaper than the Chicken Pesto Crepe.

3. Nutella Crepe = 4 stars; I tried this with a scoop of ice cream and it was delicious. Indeed, it's very chocolate-y and sweet. I would recommend this if you're absolutely craving something sweet.

The lattes and frappes are also really good and can be cheaper then Starbucks.

The prices can be pricey for the portion you are getting but the quality is superb for a small cafe and it is SO worth it.

Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Food, Wineries, Arts & Entertainment | Review: Enjoyed a flight on the patio. Price was right and they were very nice about making substitutions.
I didn't really love any of the wines I tasted. As we were on our last taste a bachlorette party came in and our party soon became ignored. We had to ask multiple times to pay for our flights so we could leave. Would have made more sense to have us pay before starting the bachlorette party's flights.

Categories: Korean, Barbeque, Restaurants | Review: *Pros: Quality meat and I love how they don't cater to the Asian-fusion-loving hipster crowd. They actually encourages you to have your ALL YOU CAN FREAKING EAT game face on by putting a price on failure. So eat with a furious passion!

The most tolerant dish for the people who say they want to try new things but end up ordering some bullshit fluffer dish is the "sweet and spicy fried chicken". Order all things meat and eat all the banchan! Their banchan is ON POINT and I always have my kimchi refilled at least 5 times. 

*Cons: Greasy table and floor. I can't exactly blame them since it IS a Korean BBQ and meat grease gets everywhere in a small space like that. I wouldn't want them to douse 10 gallons of Windex to get rid of the grease like some places in LA do. Maybe it's a blessing in disguise, I have enough cultural relativity in my heart to embrace all of Kimchi Korean BBQ's flaws and beauty.

tl;dr: It's good, don't be posy and vegan or you should go to Miso Hungry (read: Miso Bourgeoisie and Morally Hip).

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: Salted Caramel Chip. Enough Said.
Ok ok, maybe thats not enough. Honestly, every flavor is dynamite. Their seasonal flavors are fun to try as well. While its hard for me to ever get anything besides the salted caramel chip, I will sometimes change it up and get their coconut. Its so fresh and rich at the same time. 
Fun fact - they now have a stall at GCM in downtown LA. I was so happy to find this out. 
Oh! Don't forget to try their homemade graham crackers!

Categories: Pizza, Restaurants, Sandwiches, Italian | Review: Just had a pie from here last night.  Last time I checked a pizza's primary ingredients constitute cheese, bread, and sauce.  The latter of which there was virtually none.  If I wanted cheesy bread I would have ordered that instead of a pizza pie.  The crust was also much too doughy and thick.  I think the best pizza's are NY style with thin slightly crispy crust and a perfect balance of cheese, bread, and sauce.  I'm sure one could have it made that way but what's the point when there are a number of other pizza spots that are far superior

Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Food, Arts & Entertainment, Wineries | Review: ABC is a quality wine/winery that is smooth, like velvet, , supple, and delicious! Emily explains the wines with class and dignity! You can tell she really cares for her product..hooray for Au Bon Climat, I've been enjoying your wines for years, and will continue doing so!

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: Celebrated parents 50th Wedding Anniversary and had a wonderful experience.  Food was excellent, service outstanding.  The staff made the evening very special, thank you to the Bouchon team.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: Casa Blanca is a very fun place to have a drink and some food. I have been here several times and have got to say it's always a good atmosphere whether it is bustling with people or a relaxed rainy day. The staff is very friendly and the food & drinks are very tasty while being reasonably priced and their happy hour goes from 3 to close most days! I would personally recommend the nachos and the house margarita.

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: So tasty! I haven't had a single mediocre or poor item on their menu, everything is delicious! Plus, the service is fantastic and not the least bit snobby. (You don't need to be dressed up to come here either.)

I originally visited to try their French dip on a recommendation from a friend. It is delicious! Worth a try even if you're not a French dip fan. Their salads are also killer!

They're open late and serve food until closing! BIG plus in SB!

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Beer Bar, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Food Delivery Services, Food, Restaurants | Review: Beautiful dining area! Tons of delicious gluten free options which we shared, and gluten free desserts too. Excellent service! We'll definitely be back

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Desserts, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Seafood | Review: Came here for lunch after a visit from Stearns Wharf.. Due to COVID times, they do not offer paper menus - you will need to scan QR code to view the menu. 

As we got seated, we love how the staff were so friendly and offered our puppy water. We came here because this place was pet friendly and across the street, there's a huge grass area. 

We ordered the following dishes: 
- Raw Oysters (Half Dozen) : 4/5 (oysters were really fresh and you can tell they were prepared right - we really enjoyed the accompaniment (the 
vinaigrette and cocktail sauce pairs very well with the oyster) 
- Macadamia Nut Sea Bass - 4.5/5- I love how it doesn't sit too heavy. The fish was cooked to perfection!! 
-Lobster Tacos -5/5 This was one of our favorite dish!! We decided to order this because we rarely see lobster taco at other restaurants. I would say this is a must try! 

I love how a lot of these dishes from their menu reminds me a lot of Hawaii! Would definitely come back again!!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: We were visiting Santa Barbara and did some wine tasting at Jamie Sloans. Were then looking for dinner options and had a taste for Mexican.  Our server recommended La Playa Azul and so glad she did. 

Got a nice table outside even when told making the reservation likely inside dining.  Glad something opened up! Service and food were all outstanding.  Just what we were looking for. Great experience.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I took a friend from out of the area to this restaurant. Despite some of the negative reviews, I was hoping it would be a good meal since the setting is so classically Santa Babara. There were only three other tables occupied at 6:30 PM on this particular Saturday....not a good sign. In any case, the service was attentive and friendly--no problem there. However, the kitchen used no seasoning on any of the items we were served. If you have tried to eat a full meal completely void of salt, then you know it's not enjoyable.  Perhaps all those empty Saturday night tables at El Paseo are a message to the chef.

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: great drinks!! the moscow mule was so good and the chicken n waffle appetizer was yummy! also had a really fresh cesar salad

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Salted caramel chip ice cream = 5 stars
Cookie dough ice cream = 3.5 stars
Service = 5 stars
Environment = 5 stars ( very clean and organized )

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese | Review: Decent fish quality for a very good price.  There are a good variety of $10-$15 dollar entrées, or you can eat your heart out with the $20 Sushi & Sashimi combo.  If you love sushi but are on a budget, this place will do nicely.  Also, the mochi icecream for desert is fantastic.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: Poor experience and not customer friendly. My wife and I arrived at 7:30 without a reservation and asked for a table- we were told it was at least an hour wait and they were booked solid. I counted 15 open tables out of approximately 25 total tables. We put our name in and also put our name in at 'Jane' down the street. We returned to Milk and Honey and were told to sit at the bar (despite 9 tables open). We had a drink and got a text that our table at Jane was ready 20 minutes later. The hostess here never followed up with us even with all of the tables open. 

I wasn't impressed by Milk and Honey and absolutely loved 'Jane.' Save your money and go to one of the many lovely other restaurants Santa Barbara has to offer.

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Burgers, American (Traditional), American (New), Fast Food, Sandwiches, Food Delivery Services, Food | Review: I grabbed a chicken Alfredo pasta and a burger..... WOW. That's all I gotta say, they were both delicious. By far a spot to get food in Santa Barbara for a late night snack.

Categories: Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: First time dining at Corktree on 1/10/18. My brother in law suggested a wine. We asked the waiter for that wine and the price. He said that they had it and that it was $35-38. We were good with that. He promptly served our wine and we never saw him again for at least 20 min. In the meantime a couple was seated across from us. The couple across from us had their wine served and order taken before our waiter returned. In fact, they had eaten their dinner, paid their check and left before our food was served. When we paid our check, to our surprise, there was a corkage fee of $20!  The wine was $25 w/o corkage. Now mention by the waiter of the corkage fee.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Szechuan, Cantonese, Restaurants, Chinese | Review: Big fun at the sushi bar with my family, early on a Tuesday. AYCE x 3 gave us plenty, and we sampled all sorts of rolls. A good idea since they have many offerings. Super friendly staff, and John the sushi chef and his assistant Alvin rocked us with food and conversation for an hour and a half. My daughter even sang for them, since they begged. We will be back, and glad to have a neighborhood place to get our sushi fix.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: So, mezcal has been my jam for awhile now! We were bored one weekend and figured we'd drive up to SB and see what we could find for lunch. So when we ran across this place and they were all set up for some outdoor Covid dining I was elated! The cocktails are all on point. We had a La Flor Ahumada and it was scrumptious. I always love when a drink is served in a nice little cocktail glass. We tried the Margarita Pepino ... a spa in a glass :) The Sant Old Fashioned is a twist on a classic and I have to say that it is one of the top 5 cocktails that I've ever had (and that's saying a LOT lol). The server sold us on a tasting of Yola Mezcal. The presentation was lovely and it was really good. Our party had a couple others that were really good too although I can't recall the names. The food was good as well. We enjoyed some tacos, a poblano chicken dish and a seafood dish. Then our DD drove us home :)

Categories: Thai, Restaurants | Review: Great date spot because it is cute and small and the service is always really nice. 

I love the food and the fact that it is a locally owned family restaurant makes it that much better.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: Great views, but the food was not good. Very expensive and just not that good. Wait staff was not very attentive and it was a very SLOW evening on our way home from SB. Wouldn't stop again.

Categories: Restaurants, Pizza, American (New) | Review: This is my new favorite place!! Went there with a group of friends to celebrate a birthday and it was a really special celebration because of the warm, welcoming atmosphere and the DELICIOUS food. We ordered a few salads and each ordered an entree and every single bite of food was like a party in your mouth. The owner came over to deliver a few of our entrees and each time chatted with us. He is charming and clearly very talented, as is his partner/chef. The food is all farm to table, everything was super fresh and tasty. Can't wait to plan our next adventure at The Middle Child!
Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: I had it for breakfast.  Coffee shakes are kindof like breakfast aren't they?  They make good ice cream!  They make killer shakes!

Categories: American (Traditional), Vegan, Restaurants | Review: Wow!! Love this restaurant. . The lights in the tree are fantastic and create a really nice ambiance. All of the food was good. Highlights include crab cakes, ceviche , and artichokes! Overall super impressed and will definitely be back.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: After a long day if site-seeing, we sought out a fairly well regarded sushi establishment and weren't disappointed here. We had a chance to get seated outside while the place was jammed on a Saturday night and it's a year later and finally writing a review. 

The sushi pleased all 4 and happy to share our experience with everyone in yelp-land.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Burgers, American (New) | Review: My go-to place to eat, grab a beer and have a great time with friends. It's affordable and delicious and I have probably tried everything on their menu 3x and still love it. Brussels sprouts are the best in town (besides The Lark), nachos, truffle fries, veggie burger, and tacos are some of my favorites. Their happy hour is also incredible, 2-6p and 9p- close. Who can beat that? Their service is also always great.

Enjoy!

Categories: Caribbean, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Cajun/Creole, Vegan | Review: Great space, great food, super friendly and involved owner. Definitely recommend this! As a vegan I enjoyed having options, and the other three in my party enjoyed the awesome authentic cuisine as well!

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: NO uni, on a regular Tues night! Chef said "we couldn't get any today". WTF, this is Santa Barbara, supplier of uni for the entire US!! Seriously this was very disappointing, and would seem to reflect poor, ineffective management. Also, NO omakase now?? What kind of purportedly high end sushi place lacks this option? We got the highest end sushi and sashimi dishes, which were mostly fairly pedestrian, actually. Fake crab in your best sushi combi? Really?? And the rice, something really good sushi chefs really make sure is the right consistency and temperature, was cold and too mushy. Chu toro and yellowtail were very good though. I mean the whole meal wasn't terrible, and it wasn't expensive, but I really expected far better based on the hype. This is not a serious sushi place.

Categories: Steakhouses, Bars, Brewpubs, Breweries, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Sports Bars, Restaurants, Food, Burgers, Comfort Food, Seafood | Review: As a long-time resident of Santa Barbara I thought I had experienced most hidden gems for food and beer, but after seeing the impressive article about The Brewhouse in the SB NewsPress, we realized we hadn't been there yet, so we high-tailed it over for their Happy Hour and dinner.

Most my friends know that I am a Stroganoff-kinda-they/them, and having tasted it at other restaurants in SB and Goleta, their Beef Stroganoff with Filet Mignon is now my new favorite. Wild mushrooms and onions, and served over fettuccine noodles . . . simply delicious. My dining companion had the Pork Chop, and they/them swore that it's the best one they/them have had in a long time. Also have to note that their Fish and Chips are really good.

Serves Dan and Harmony always make sure we have everything we needed throughout our visits, and were spot-on with their suggestions.

And of course being there for their Happy Hour we just had to try some of the many appetizer choices. Too numerous to name here, but there was something delicious for everyone (my favorite being their calamari, prawns, and fire roasted artichoke). We would have tried the other choices but was committed to leaving room for that Stroganoff.

And also thoroughly enjoyed their beer selections. IPA's, Wheat Ales, and that Porter they have were all delicious.

Conveniently located on Montecito Street between Castillo and the Morton Bay Fig tree, and next to The Neighborhood Sports Bar.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Worth the drive from LA! I had the pancakes, eggs and breakfast potatoes! Delicious! The view of the beach from the patio is so relaxing. Our server Lisa made great suggestions and made sure we were taken care of!

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars | Review: I love shabu shabu food... so, when I was visiting SB, I searched Yelp for restaurants w/in walking distance from hotel and saw this restaurant, I decided to try it despite the ratings. And I am VERY HAPPY that I did! The portions are generous, the beef, seafood, noodles and vegetables where fresh and out standing. Thebservice was friensky and prompt and the cost was very reasonable.  While i did bot try the sushi, the Tonkatsu appetizer and the the shabu shabu dinner are HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!!

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Food Delivery Services, Caterers, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Wedding Planning, Nightlife, Food, Event Planning & Services | Review: We got married at the Santa Barbara Zoo, which Rincon Catering also does. Angie, our planner, was really really great! She was super responsive and just generally a pleasant person. The venue was set up just the way we wanted it, and it looked great. Plus, the food is seriously delicious. People kept telling me how it was the best wedding food they'd ever had! 
Thank you so much Angie and team!

Categories: Food, Bubble Tea, Chinese, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Hot Pot | Review: Great hot pot place. Prices were good and the quality of meat was fantastic. Ordered the Tom Yum with udon and was surprised with the amount of different ingredients and flavors coming from the soup. The boba place next door (which you can order from your table) is great as well. We will definitely be back.

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: The baos were delicious. KFC chicken bao had crispy breaded chicken, quite authentic, with nice sauce & other toppings that went well together and added a touch of exotic fusion taste to it. The Shrimp bao was even better for my taste, not as strong as the chicken but very well-balanced.
Service was meh - the waitress wasn't very attentive, and I had to tell her that I want to make an order, but she didn't help me out so I had to ask her for a menu.
Still, would definitely be returning to try more of their menus. Great ambiance too - I'm like to dine in next time.

Categories: Food, Custom Cakes, Bakeries, Cupcakes, Desserts, Caterers, Event Planning & Services | Review: We had our beautiful custom wedding cake and full dessert table from Lele this past weekend. They did a fantastic job. Beyond the fact that the cake was gorgeous and tasty (we had a Gluten free banana flavor which was fabulous, a double chocolate flavor (also great!) and a carrot cake which our guests loved!) the service was top knot notch! I changed my mind on flavors, frostings, which layer would be which flavor and what separate desserts I wanted a million times! I was a bridezilla when it came to my cake... and Signe (their pastry chef) was SO accommodating. She replied in full detail to each and every one of my million emails with details and caveats for me to consider. She went above and beyond in terms of customer service. She was always so thoughtful about every aspect...I.e., what color and style serving platters I wanted (and included photos), what kinds of desserts paired well with each other etc.. She was truly fantastic. I highly recommend them for any special occasion.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Reviews make this shop seem a little pricey, but the pier experience makes it worth it. The local wine tasting balcony is open to the public, and eating ice cream up there with my girlfriend was an amazing experience. I definitely recommend the trip to the pier got the ice cream alone!

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: I have been to Zaytoon for takeout 5 or 6 times since COVID-19.  The food is incredible.  It is delicious and perfectly prepared.  The lentil soup is filled with subtle tastes.  The chicken kabob is tender and flavored with just the right seasonings.  Every dish my wife and I have tried has been incredible.  The portions are generous.  I have had Mediterranean food in many countries.  The tastes and quality of the food at Zaytoon reminds me of the best I had in Turkey.  I can't wait until Santa Barbara opens up so I can have dinner on Zaytoon's patio.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (New), Food | Review: Food was decent, service was nonfunctional. I was charged for an item I didn't even order and when I asked the server, he acted as if it was my fault I was charged for the extra item. It's a order upfront and seat yourself type of spot which makes for strange seating arrangements. Food was nothing special, the dawn patrol combo does the job and the French toast with which they use their house Hawaiian bread was decent overall. Would have given a better rating if the service was upgraded. 2.5 stars overall

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife, Barbeque, Sandwiches | Review: Yum!  Shared the amazing smoked meat platter and a skillet of Mac & Cheese last night with a friend.  Delicious BBQ sauces and the meat was wonderfully tender - the best smoked meat I've ever had actually: pork ribs, pulled pork & brisket.  LOVED it.  The only reason I'm not giving it five stars is because there is this super clunky issue with bringing beverages you started during your wait next door into the restaurant to finish.  It just doesn't work.  Wine from next door is 15/glass so I'm bummed to chug it down.  If it's a legal issue I wish they would set up a system where they just look the other way and be a good host to their guests.  Food & service were good.  And it's gorgeous.  Inside is very loud but outside with the heaters is super cozy.  Definitely check it out! Their desserts looked AMAZING even though I didn't get one.

Categories: Soup, Thai, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: Great service! The gentleman serving was very eager to please and very accommodating! 

The food was good.   I ordered the pad see ewe (spell?) and the curried fried rice.  The pad see ewe was really Good! Always a favorite dish if mine. The curry fried rice was not bad.  A little bland. 
It also was a little expensive.  
Overall I would go back is I was really craving some Thai but it's not my new favorite restaurant in SB.

Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Wineries, Food, Arts & Entertainment | Review: Rejoice Goleta! We finally have it all :-) after several years of enjoying multiple craft brewers and even now a cidery. Goletians ( and visitors) can finally enjoy our very own full service winery and tasting room. Located on Calle Real a few spaces away from one of our excellent breweries ( captain  fattys) Samsara has opened up its main tasting room and winery facility. ( there's the original out in Los Olivos too). Having  a full winery with tasting room and production facility is a true godsend for Goodland wine lovers and a bold first for owners David and Joan as well as veteran wine maker Matt Brady. Yes THAT Matt Brady, the guy who  put the "Kick Ass" into Jaffurs and Granddaddy wines for the past 10 years or so. That's an unbeatable combination IMHO :-). I've been following this project since I heard rumors about it over a year ago and I'm privileged and lucky to get to experience the soft opening this past weekend. I can't encourage a visit to Samsara any more strongly. The wines are great now and they will only get better as Matt shepherds ever more vintages from vineyard to bottle. Beyond that the crew from owner and winemaker on down is top notch. Everyone knows everything about the wines being poured and the story behind the brand. We couldn't have had a better group decide to set up shop out here in Goleta. Viva Samsara

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Terrible customer service/awareness. So I have been going to Lilly's for years, long before the Goleta location opened. I used to stand in line outside the Chapala store and watch the freeway cars whiz by. Today that all changes. I order 3 tacos and start to wait. I then remembered that I wanted peppers and onions. I very politely wait until the cashier moves to the next person and quickly ask. She says sure. Then while I'm moving back to where I was waiting, she informs me it's a dollar. I decided to get in the back of the line and wait my turn. When I get there I noticed I spent the cash I had on my first order, so I grab my debit card. I'm then informed there will be a 50¢ charge for purchases less than $5. It's not worth it at this point. I then go to pick up my tacos and they try to hand me the extras but I decline because I didn't pay and the looked at me like I'm the biggest problem ever. I just don't understand it when people in service jobs refuse to give good service. I looked very disappointed and I feel they couldn't care less. I will avoid them now and you should too, at least until they look up the words good, service, and customer.
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Hands down the best place to get tacos in Santa Barbara. Need i say more?

Categories: Restaurants, Seafood, Mexican | Review: Everything here is so fresh and delicious!  I believe Chef Ramon doesn't set his menu until after he's gone to the farmer's market to see what local produce is best that week.  Everything is paired wonderfully and it'll be unlike any taco you've had before!  The ceviche and farmers market taco are my current favorites.   I'm normally not into juice and almost always have water with my meals... but you have to try their agua frescas!  Last week I had the jamaica-lemongrass combo and it was refreshing and tasty without being too sweet.  I've also has the La Rana which is pineapple, mint, cucumber and lime --- equally  amazing!  My husband and I have been trying to make it most Sunday's (currently the only day they're open) to for a relaxing, healthy, tasty, afternoon experience.

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Nightlife, Bars, Beer Bar, Restaurants, Burgers | Review: We actually really liked Zookers. It's located in a shopping center not far from the beach (but you'll want to drive). The food was really quite solid. 

We had the spring rolls to start, which my wife went crazy over. They had nice tropical undertones with the shrimp, mango and cilantro. For the entrees, my memory is fuzzy, but I think my wife and I may have both gotten specials. I think she got a lamb shank and I got fish. And they were both really, really good. I also got a beer. Their beer list is decent, as there were a few on there I haven't had and others I've had that are good. 

The bill, however, was a touch higher than I would have expected from a place in a shopping center. Even given the fact that its food was good, the following night, for example, we ate at a relatively more upscale restaurant in Santa Barbara across the street from the beach, sitting with a view of the water, and ordered approximately the same amount of food -- but our bill was lower. It wasn't a huge difference. But I'd say, given the casual (but pleasant) ambiance and non-thrilling location, I would have expected its (admittedly) excellent food to fetch a slightly lower price point. 

So we definitely liked the place, but thought it could have been priced a touch lower.

Categories: Restaurants, Bagels, Coffee & Tea, Food, Bubble Tea, Donuts | Review: This is my second review of Spudnuts. I haven't missed a day in seven years (except for a few vacations). That's right, I have gone every day for seven years to eat my garlic bagel, muffin and coffee. 

This Spudnuts is the only place in Santa Barbara where you can get an authentic garlic (with plenty of garlic) bagel. They are delicious. The homemade muffins, donuts, juices and coffee are also excellent. Although I have not had the bagel or croissant sandwiches or boba drinks, all the regular customers tell me they're delicious, too. 

I highly recommend this Spudnuts on Upper State St (different ownership of the Spudnuts downtown). Don't be fooled by the understated look of the premises, the food is as good as any ritzy restaurant in town. If you're looking for a great place to go for breakfast or lunch, you won't be disappointed with Spudnuts.

Categories: Szechuan, Chinese, Restaurants | Review: My boyfriend and I order take-out here a few times a month... it's definitely our go-to comfort food!
