Like other reviewers have said, Szechuan is on the expensive side. We usually get two entrees (including rice) and it comes out to about $25. I am a vegetarian and they have a pretty decent menu for that - the Kung Pao Tofu is great!! It's spicy and saucy and I love it. 

The fried rice is a bit bland and sorta flavorless. It's a good side-dish to balance out something spicier, but I wouldn't recommend it as a main dish! My boyfriend loves the chicken chow-mein and sweet and sour chicken. The staff is VERY sweet and always quick and efficient. I definitely recommend their food!

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: Im rating this based on my yogurt and mcconell's ice cream.

i had the cookies and cream mcconell's ice cream and it was delish! i couldn't get enough! the creaminess and taste was wow!! they have a good selection of their ice cream and i would definitely recc ppl to try the strawberry boudeux but i thought that was too sweet for my taste. 

I didn't like their yogurt because it was not good, it was overly sweet and their chocolate, well you can get it  at costco foodcourt for a better price! it melts in your cup, their toppings look like it's been out for awhile. It seems family owned. =) 

sweet alley is ok for yogurt, but i think you should come here for their ice cream (mcconells).  

its kinda pricey but its one of the few places in goleta.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: Two stars b/c our server was super sweet. She might have been the only waitress on that night so it took a long time to get drinks, water, apps, etc. Too expensive for the quality of food.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, American (Traditional), Venues & Event Spaces | Review: Beware the drinks are stronger than the rumors claim them to be and the police are waiting . . . the food is beyond disappointing in fact that's another chance you're taking . . . the way to handle Harry's is to take a cab or walk have two or three drinks and then your meal could seem delicious . . . well ?

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches | Review: We made a pitstop for lunch here last week and it was really nice.   We ordered a cuban and a BLT and loved the bucket of pickles to add to our order as a side.   We were able to sit outside which was nice, except for quite a bit of construction going on next door, but that's not Three Pickle's fault.

Categories: Food, Grocery | Review: I live close to them, and honestly for my purposes they are pretty convenient. They came in handy especially when I was short on time. For that, I give them 4 stars. With that said, 

Cons
- Their produce is not fresh. 
- They are on the expensive side, but I do not agree with other people saying "way too overpriced" (maybe if you're looking for produce... but please at least go to co-op or Albertsons for produce.)
- Customer service is sometimes good, but usually the employees are just doing their basic tasks.
- Open hours could be better.
- Interior design could be better.
- They should clean more frequently!

Pros
- Satisfactory range of products. They have produce, meat, dairy, already-made food, frozen food, alcohol, and pretty much everything that would be in 7-11. 
- Location of the store. 
- Again, sometimes they are nice! I had some nice experiences here. 

If you live near heart of IV you gotta love it

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Wineries, Food | Review: As a Gaucho who has both taken the class and been to the tasting room I am ashamed to read some of these reviews.

Not only was the class and tasting room an opportunity to try a multitude of fine small batch wines, but it was an opportunity to taste them across from the man who made them.

Edouard is a very, very knowledgeable man with more degrees than you can fit on a resume. 

However; if you were expecting a bag of wine and a blackout, wine tasting might be the wrong venue for you.

As far as value, there was always opportunities to drink more of your favorites after class, and I can assure you I never left that room having only one glass of wine.

Try the Cab and the late harvest Chardonnay!

-Jay

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Burgers, American (Traditional), American (New), Fast Food, Sandwiches, Food Delivery Services, Food | Review: Such good food and service here! Genuine people on the phone too!! Fast delivery and the BEST munchies! Ordered BBQ Fries & they were top notch. Try this place out!

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: Love this new spot in the funk zone. Great menu, lots of options to choose from. Great ambiance and service was very good. Will be back.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: What a MEAL!!

My boyfriend took me here because he tried their signature Agaves Burrito a couple years back when he visited and it was one of the most memorable meals from that trip. Let me say, the food here did not disappoint. We decided to treat ourselves and ordered 3 entrees, and our order came with the usual complimentary chips with 4 different types of sauces (red sauce, green sauce, pico de gallo, and a darker salty sauce I'm not familiar with!)

Agaves Burrito: They drenched that thing in their amazing chipotle sauce. It came with a side of rice and salad. You never usually expect much out of a side salad, but this one was actually pretty good! It had dried cranberries, tangerines, corn- and all the flavors worked very well together. As for the burrito itself, the fillings definitely were not distributed very well (one end of the burrito was all lettuce, and the other end was all the good stuff like shrimp/fish/guac,) but altogether it was very delicious.

Mole Enchiladas: All their sauces are a hit, and the mole is no exception. It has that beautiful smoky sweetness, and came with a side of rice and beans. 

Chile Poblano Campestre: Looks-wise, not really sure what it is the way they presented it, but eating it was delicious! The description lists it as 2 corn tortillas with a poblano chile stuffed with meat and cheese, but in reality it was more like they cut the chile in half and flattened it, then put a layer of meat and cheese on top.

Categories: Food, Specialty Food, Seafood, Cajun/Creole, Restaurants | Review: Place was really nice, atmosphere has good staff, food is just good nothing too special. However im pretty sure the crawfish was frozen and they said it was fresh but overall this is an ok place to eat if you just want some seafood in a nice place. Additionally, they need to boil seafood with seasoning. Flavor is lacking a bit.

Categories: Steakhouses, American (Traditional), Desserts, Food, Restaurants | Review: The atmosphere at Clementines is charming, the old purple house is quite inviting. We were a party of nine on a Friday evening. We were nicely accommodated, even filing a request for a quieter spot for the hard of hearing in our party. 
The service was fantastic, we were closely attended to, water and drinks refilled constantly, and inquiries to our needs and satisfaction were made regularly.
I had prime rib, which was fabulous. I heard similar comments from the others at our table. And the pie included with dinner was a real treat. 
The only down side was a lack of air conditioning. It did get a little warm, but that was a small price to pay for our lovely evening.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Best tasting ice cream we've had in a while! We had the french vanilla, blueberry, salted caramel chip, and rocky road. Definitely a good place to stop by to fulfill your sweet tooth while in SB! Great customer service as well!

Categories: Gelato, Food, Restaurants, Italian, Vegetarian, Gluten-Free | Review: bad service
had ravioli sampler so-so
eggplant overcooked bad flavor and hard stale edge
my daughter and I both had stomach cramps an hour afterwards!
no joke! that is why i am here reviewing!
very disappointing when we were wanting upscale italian. Presto pasta is better than this!!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: They serve Santa Barbara's local McConnell ice cream! Ice cream always tasty and freshly made! My favorite are mint chip, pistachio amaretto, coconut toasted almond chip and Turkish coffee! Must visit when in Santa Barbara!

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: This place is so much more delicious than I anticipated. I've been to another similar sushi bowl place in the La Cumbre area that left Miso Disappointed...
I was pleasantly surprised at this new place. The quality of the ahi tuna was outstanding. Toppings were fresh and yummy. The friendliness of the staff, and the care with which my bowl was assembled surely added to its tastiness. The boba tea-I want to replace my blood with it.  I'm not sure what sorcery went into making this liquid heaven... I am certain that someone sold their soul to someone else for the recipe...I imagine this is what laughter tears of the happiest unicorns on planet earth taste like. Two huge thumbs up.

Categories: Bars, Coffee & Tea, Food, Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, Pubs, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Caterers | Review: Great place for high quality and unique coffee drinks and bites with quite a number of options for a small place. The skinny mocha frappe was delicious as was the breakfast burrito. I'm only passing through on a trip but wish we had an extra morning to come here again.
Categories: Tacos, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Very good. Carnitas burrito, triple fajitas, caldos are some of my favorites. Pretty good salsas. Friendly people

Categories: Vegan, Vegetarian, Restaurants, Indian | Review: This restaurant has *fantastic* quality and is a sorely needed addition to Goleta, which had no real Indian food before. Have been here twice already and everything was delicious (fig walnut kofta and paneer elaichi pasada), with plenty of vegetarian options. It is on the more expensive side; however in my view the quality is *absolutely* worth it. Not to mention they have Dosas as well, which is rare for most Indian restaurants in the area. I simply cannot understand the low ratings some are giving--I would give this restaurant six stars if I could!! Will definitely be coming back regularly.

Categories: Japanese, Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Vegetarian | Review: This Kyoto is not as epic as the "capital city" but it offers some cool stuff

Pros:

Happy hour is 50% off of sushi from the bar. That's pretty incredible. 
Friendly service
Secluded eating areas 

Cons:

It's a bit too unkempt for me. They could use a major deep cleaning. 
When you use the private eating areas, you have to remove your shoes. So imagine what this place smells like with college students on a weekend night. It's gross. Come early. 
You have to really make an effort to flag someone down so you can pay and leave.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, Party & Event Planning, Food, Venues & Event Spaces, Arts & Entertainment, Social Clubs, Specialty Food | Review: Our daughter was recently married at the Riviera Mansion and it was perfect!  The entire planning process was painless and simple, as Breanna makes sure of that.   She is wonderful to work with, as was the whole staff.  Breanna was always quick to reply if we had any questions at all (and we had plenty!).  She was a delight and made us feel that we were her priority.   Breanna was completely awesome in every way!                                We had so many compliments on the venue.  The outdoor setting for the ceremony is stunning, yet simple.  The indoors has a charm of it's own.  The food was delicious, the drinks were awesome!    The night ran beautifully and couldn't have been any better.  I would highly  recommend this venue.   Everything was absolutely amazing!

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Restaurants, Food | Review: Good local coffee spot. Coffee, ambiance are very good, muffins about average. Perfect spot for people watching on State st.

I felt welcomed when at Jitters
they don't rush the long-term sitters.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Pro: Aesthetically pleasing... Pink Chopsticks, cute lights, just cute. Service was quick and nice! 

Con: Very pricey. Including tip, my meal was probably $20 in total when it was not worth $20. The food was average, I had to ask for extra sauce for more flavor. I was expecting a lot more :(

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: Last night we thought we would try Giannfranco's Trattoria, having had a friend make the suggestion.  It's was fabulous, I had the roast half chicken, it was so well seasoned and the sauce under the chicken was heaven, roast potatoes where perfect the greens great contrast.  My husband got the Delmonico streak, fabulous as well!  We couldn't stop sharing and if we could have we would have eaten the bones.  Really great experience!  Great service, family owned and operated, How nice to see real Italians making the food, and boy did it show.  One of the most Italian eating experience I have had in a very long time.
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Excellent Mexican food with an even better salsa bar! Definitely worth trying if you are in Santa Barbara.
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Great location, good staff, decent food.  Good for families.  Indoor and outdoor seating.  Recommended.

Categories: Restaurants, Vietnamese, Vegetarian | Review: Crispy chicken on rice. Gak. No flavor. The fresh spring rolls. No flavor. And the peanut sauce it was served with?  Out of a bottle. Tasted like vinegar. I'll drop $30 elsewhere.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Came here on suggestion from a local
Wanted some comfort food, drinks and to watch the World Series game.
Met all three criteria very well.
Super friendly bartenders.

Note that we are downstairs with limited menu (but does have a TV)

Categories: Restaurants, Tacos, Mexican | Review: Total random encounter with this spot but we needed a quick snack and popped in for a taco. Veggie taco is massive and tasty, crispy Asada taco was happily devoured-leaving only beard crumbs as evidence. Perfect for a pit stop; way better than standard fast food.

Categories: American (New), Tapas/Small Plates, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants, Burgers | Review: The concept of bbq after wine tasting in the funk zone was a much needed design and happy it is here! Food was pretty scarce in the funk zone after a wine day so helping drunkies have their alcohol soaked up while also within walking distance makes perfect sense. We tried the tri tip sandwich, fish tacos, and two types of wings. All were good, but next time I'll just do wings wings and more wings. Yum. The wings had me at hello. The tacos were ehh. 

Also, on tap was a tangerine ale. Yes, try one or two of those!!!! 

Plus, there is parking if you are a lucky Parker as not too many spots.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican, American (New) | Review: Whether you're sitting at the bar or one of the cozier nooks upstairs, this place will not disappoint. The bar menu--a range of eclectic dim sum items--is served until 11:30PM, making it one of the few late night eats options in Santa Barbara. But why order food when you can have dessert? The espresso and ice cream (which is served in a martini glass with little dark chocolate espresso beans on top) is a delicious indulgence for your next midnight snack. Our server was fantastic--attentive, personable, efficient. I'll be back for brunch sometime.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: Words can not express how much I love this business. Visit almost weekly for their frozen yogurt unlike anywhere I've ever tried! My advice? Go on an empty stomach! You will be coming back for more and more.

Categories: Beer Bar, Burgers, Belgian, Restaurants, Nightlife, German, Bars | Review: Great food, big selection, nice location and good service. Sit outdoors for good people-watching! Went there this weekend and was really happy with the whole thing.

I'm not a big fan of fries in general, but their garlic fries are fabulous--thick-cut and covered in garlic and parsley, with a delicious selection of dipping sauces. I especially liked the rosemary aioli.

Categories: Mexican, Vegan, Restaurants, Vegetarian | Review: Very good vegan Mexican options for who don't eat meat at all, so don't have to worry about meat contamination.
It's as same concept as chipotle. Service and food was quick and good! They do extra charge $3.00 for guac except one day
However, they don't have high chairs or customer restrooms that's the only minus point

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: Partnering with insomniac is AWESOOOOME! Drinks are on the pricier side especially since you already had to buy a ticket to get inside:/

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, French, Vegan, Seafood, Asian Fusion, Gluten-Free, American (New) | Review: Local, organic, meat or not -- hands down my fav restaurant in SB. As a vegetarian, it is a luxury to have a wide selection from the menu to choose from. Food is excellent, and check out their delicious bento boxes for lunch.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: Like stepping into a fish shop in England.  The food was delicious.  I knew I wanted the fish, but not the chips.  He offered "mushy peas"!  This is a side always ordered when in England and was delighted they offered it.  We found this restaurant in Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives.  Would definitely go back when in the area again.  I just wish they were closer to us!  They have an interesting menu of British fare and would like to try some of their other offerings.

Categories: Nightlife, Restaurants, Steakhouses, Bars | Review: Double eagle medium rare or go home! Best prime-rib I've had. Better than Lawry's, I'm serious. Would definitely drive 1 hour just to eat here.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: I love this family owned business. Many years ago my husband and child used to eat breakfast here on a regular. The owner was always kind and took the time to speak with us. The food was always so delish and I loved the corn beef & hash. So sad when we moved. I truly miss eating breakfast here.

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Really good baos, apps, and the soju is a solid deal. The paella is solid, but split with a larger group since it's super rich and hard to eat more than a small portion of it.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: It takes real guts to open a fish and chips shop in the same town as Brophys, but Mac's is better by far, in taste, quality of ingredients and value. BTW - they have a sample of haggas - something I have always heard of but been afraid to try - but for only $2.50 why not?? OMG - even my kid s loved it. THE BEST AUTHENTIC FISH & CHIPS and deep fried haggas, what can be better than that??

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: I'm a Thai food snob. I moved to SB from Boston where the Thai food is top notch and plentiful. I've struggled to find a Thai place that fills the noodle bowl shaped void within me and it hasn't been easy. I judge a Thai restaurant by their pad see ewe. If they can get this seemingly simple dish right, I know I can trust them. But most of the pad see ewe I've had in SB have been WAY too sweet. Like spoonfuls of sugars sweet. Then I found the Wednesday special at Empty Bowl. The sauce is just the right balance of sweet and savory with a healthy sprinkle of white pepper on top, just the way I like it! If it weren't for the price, I'd be here every Wednesday. But to be fair, I'm sure rent in the Public Market isn't exactly cheap so I'll pay the $13.95 and savor my pad see ewe. My husband's gotten their other dishes and is always pretty pleased with them. Service is friendly and fast. It's usually easy to get a spot at the the counter on weekday evenings but if there's a long line, get your noodles to go and find one of the little tables scattered throughout the Public market.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, Tex-Mex, Sandwiches | Review: I think I'm over Natural Cafe. The other day I ordered my usual SB Veggie Grill, and realized it no longer comes with the tahini sauce. It's just grilled veggies on brown rice. When I asked what happened to the sauce the employee said it now costs $2 extra.  It's already $11.50 for the plate (grilled veggies and tofu on a bed of brown rice). Unless there's something about tahini being super expensive that I don't know about, I don't get why you would take away a sauce from a menu item and then charge for it. I think that's bad business.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: I learned about the existence of this place right in my own neighborhood from Yelp, and decided to take a new friend here for dinner.
The ambience is somewhere between family dinner house and starched French upscale; quite comfortable. The food quality is also in that range, and the prices match that.
The Asian fusion theme made a nice diversion from the more common "American family", "Chinese" or Thai themes that are more prevalent in the neighborhood in this price range (Max's, Madam Lu and Your Place), so it was a good choice for a first date.
The service was good. We had no reservations, and they gave us the table of honor at the front window (clearly, they found my date as attractive as I did), but when we found the guests at the next table a bit loud and asked if they could move us to a quieter corner, they immediately obliged.
I'm sure I will be back soon.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: WORST place to dine at. For the $$$ I'd rather go someplace where I will be treated fairly and nicely. Wait staff were HORRIBLE, especially the white guy with the beard, he was completely racist and rude. We were brushed off and told since we do not have reservations, he'll "see if he has a table for us," and proceeded to ignore us. Once we were seated, they practically rushed us to eat and leave. Unpleasant dining experience, and to top it off, both my bf and I got food poisoning for food that was mediocre and salty.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: We're 12 years into the 21st century and there's STILL a restaurant named Sambo's?  Unfortunately for race relations AND my stomach, yes.

Two stars:  1 1/2 for the view, and 1/2 for the food.

Sitting across the street from the blue calming swirl that is the Pacific Ocean, your mind is completely cleared... until the sad moment when your food arrives.  

Making the first landing were the complimentary muffins, who lured me with their siren song of fresh blueberry.  One bite dashed my hopes upon the rocks, as the bounty turned out to be microwaved.  Definitely not a treasure to add to my booty.

Sambo's believes that It's not good enough for their customers to merely SEE the water; they should experience it for themselves.  Or at least, that's what I told myself when my tasteless coffee arrived. 

Between my coffee cup and the view, I was surrounded by so much water, it wasn't surprising that I ordered the baja fish tacos.  About ten minutes later, my server informed me that they were out, but I could still order the regular fish tacos.  Turns out, they were out of those too, so I settled on a club sandwich.

'Settled' is putting it lightly.  Cold toasted bread, black spotted lettuce; government cheesed and over mayo-ed, it was pitiful.  

The food at Sambo's is basically just a distraction; an afterthought.  The scenery is the real attraction.

If I didn't think he'd enjoy the view, I'd suggest the 'chef' walk the plank.

Categories: Tapas Bars, Restaurants | Review: I have recently gotten into eaten food this way and I must say I like it. When the food is this tasty it's hard not to eat everything but being that it's tapas I find myself eating jut the right amount. the drinks are tasty as well. makes Santa Barbara a better place in the world.

Categories: Soup, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Bars, Italian | Review: Terrible food, bad service, ignorant wait staff.

We ordered the risotto and veal limone. 

The risotto was dry and inedible, as well as undercooked. It was served piled high in a bowl, with the top dry and the bottom floating in broth. Clearly, they did not cook the risotto properly, and it had not absorbed the stock.

The waitress rudely claimed that "there are two kinds of risotto, the creamy kind and the dry kind that they served". What nonsense! As Wolfgang Puck says in his web site, "Remember, the risotto should be creamy, not runny". I guess I can live with the waitress being ignorant, but at least don't be rude!

The veal limone came 10 minutes later (they can't even bring all the entrees together). Another disaster. Some dry, sorry-looking pieces of veal, with no sauce on them. When I called the manager over, he said that this dish is not supposed to have sauce, and that his customers like it that way. Maybe he should read some of the reviews here on yelp.

To finish the evening, the waitress came by and placed the check in front of me without a word. No apology, no "I am sorry you didn't enjoy your food", nothing.

Let's see... High prices, bad food, ignorant staff: I would not go back to this place if you paid me.
Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Ramen | Review: Nice trendy ramen place. The food is good and the portions are generous.  Haven't been since Covid lockdowns started though.

Categories: Gelato, Food, Restaurants, Italian, Vegetarian, Gluten-Free | Review: I was told come try this place because it was suppose to be good and inexpensive. It was! It was all really tasty and the prices were fair. My only dislike was how close together all the seating was. I tried their Palermo pasta dish and it was heavenly!

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: This place has an amazing staff, great atmosphere and unquestionably the best coffee in Santa Barbara. Every week, I bypass tons of coffee shops to make my way there. Try it for yourself and you'll agree with me.

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: The first two times we visited this place, it seemed wonderful. But today, we visited it and the Sam exact dishes we got the last two times were now ....just .....plain....old...TERRIBLE. THE BAKED OYSTERS were full of sand and shell flakes. The tuna tartare was tasteless. The muscles in broth tasted like mop water.  ULTIMATE FAIL.

Categories: Food, Breweries, Beer Gardens, Mediterranean, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: John is absolutely amazing. My boyfriend and I stopped by here in search of a beer on my birthday and we stumbled upon this place. Highly recommend

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Karaoke, Beer Bar, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Man, I'm pretty upset. I ordered a couple stuff. I ordered fries with cheese, legit put only fries with cheese. They even called me to confirm it, and I explained just fries with cheese, and they were like okay. They still messed up my order LOL... How is that even possible!? They even messed up my quesadilla order too. Honestly the food is mediocre, they messed up my order even after calling to confirm, and it's overpriced as well. Go anywhere else but here lol.
Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: The food was delicious and Ernesto was very knowledgeable and had great suggestions on the menu. I'm definitely coming back.

Categories: Wine Bars, Restaurants, American (New), Nightlife, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: Wow really? We have eaten at the Hungry Cat in LA many times before a performance. Love the food and experience. Sadly I cannot say that about the Santa Barbara location. Upon arriving we were asked if we had reservation. It was Tuesday night and half of the main dining area as well as the back part of the restaurant was empty.  We were greeted (I use that term loosely) by the rudest server ever. I wish I could write a review regarding the food, but we were so put off by the attitude we left never to return.

Categories: Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food, Restaurants, Vegetarian | Review: I went to blenders and ordered 2 drinks.  One was a blue pomegranate and it was made short even after using the spatula to pour any excess smoothie into the cup. I know the smoothie is supposed to reach the top of the cup because I used to be a supervisor there. This is not the first time this has happened, it seems to be a reoccurring problem.

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: This place is wayyy better than Flavor of India, if you like your curry to be creamy and flavorful then India Club is the way to go. Seriously I tried both flavor of India (again) and India club (first time) in the same week. Flavor of India made me want to cry the food was so bland but India Club was delicious, reminds me of a place back up North. I loved the paneer tikka masala here and the saag paneer! I cannot wait to come back to try their other curries. Though they are on the expensive side, I would probably just come here for lunch. The food's delicious, best Indian food I've had in SB.

And take a look at the pretty picture I posted of the curry, such a nice design!

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Nightlife | Review: It is so sad to me how incredibly disappointing this restaurant is. I was really hoping they would find a rhythm. I have given it a few tries because I thought they needed time to work out the kinks after opening. Nope. Food is SO inconsistent! One time lunch was good but that was in between visits where it was way under cooked or way overbooked- same dish. Definitely avoid salads or chicken dishes. Service is more often than not unbelievably rude and very slow. Too many restaurants to choose from to risk another bad experience at Union Ale.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: We eat out a lot -- in fact, my partner and I pretty much go out every week while we're at home in Los Angeles. However, the outdoor dining on State Street in Santa Barbara couldn't be any more romantic. We recommend reserving one of the outdoor tables at local- favorite Opal. It's removed from the boisterous bar scene found on lower State Street. Yet, still has plenty of action to watch from your table.

On one of our very first visits to Santa Barbara, over a decade ago, we came to Opal for my special birthday dinner. The place hasn't changed much over the years, but that's a good thing. It was the comfort that we needed. Oh, and maybe some creme brulee and cheesecake for dessert.

Sometimes it's just the simple things in life -- time away from the kids to reconnect and talk. The food is pretty delicious, too. Reservations are highly recommended, especially if dining over a holiday or the weekend. 

Reservations can be made on OpenTable or by calling the restaurant directly.

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services | Review: From out of town- I ordered the OSO BUCCO. It was mentioned as a special, so I didn't see the price. It came out and did not taste like the oso BUCCO I had known...crazy but the elderly home I worked for cooked it way better.
The rice with saffron wasn't nearly as good as my father's... Theirs was way to mushy with loads of olive oil. 
Their starter bread was the best part of my meal. 
Check came out and my dish was $32!!! I sank in my seat as I could have made that dish for a family of 5-6 for that price.
We normally change up our places for our ladies trip anyhow so we won't go there again.
I thought the service was nice!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars | Review: COVID review: came here a few times during my visit and will definitely visit this cocktail bar again. Their current setup is all on the street with maybe 12-15 tables. In order to drink, you have to order a food item. That said, the food provided from their neighbors, Sama Sama, hits the spot. Personally, I love this rule because it gives me a chance to try new things and partake in my favorite hobby - eating. Back to drinks...you can tell there was a lot of thought put into these. I tried the potentially spicy tequila cocktail and the cow got into the onion patch. I wasn't sure if I liked them, so I had to order 2-3 of each. The cow got into the garden patch is a Gibson style martini. My favorite parts are the house made pickle (gives it a subtle flavor twist) and the cocktail onions that come with it. The spicy tequila cocktail is totally different and can punch you In the throat (the best way possible.) I ordered mine spicy 8/10 (love spice) and it was awesome. It is definitely a sipper and left a nice tingling in the back of throat - not so burny I was asking for a pitcher of water to dump on my head. I haven't tried any other cocktails because I like these two so much, but may try something else when I go back for their fast and friendly service tonight! Cheers!
Categories: Asian Fusion, Seafood, Restaurants, Poke, Food | Review: Yummy fresh fish with lots of great toopings! My favorite is the sushi bowl :)

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: After a visit to Super-Rica,  I'll never be able to eat at Taco Bell again!
The line went waaaay out the door, finally, we make our way in, and watch the handmade tortillas.  hmm.... this is Julia Childs fave Mexican taqueria.
Wo! this was well worth the wait!  We shared from the regular menu:
--Tacos de Adobado (marinated pork) - good to the last bite!
--Tacos de Chuleta (grilled pork) - average
from the Daily Special:
--Vegetable Tamale - lots of flavor
--Chile Relleno de Queso con Crema - first time I've ever had crema on relleno and... it works!
We could only finish half, but reheated in the hotel microwave, it was still yummy!

Categories: Seafood, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: I went to Moby Dick several times, and this time is the first disappointing one!  The price went up and the quality went down.  The lobster is smaller and not as good as before.  The clam chowder is still the best though. Anyway, I will not go back for awhile....  good luck!

Categories: Japanese, Sushi Bars, Restaurants | Review: Ordered sushi.   A bit overpriced for the value.  They had large portions however the quality of the fish was suspect.   Nice people but I will probably not go back.  I only ate half of my order because the worst thing is low quality raw fish.

Categories: Salad, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Nightlife, Seafood | Review: Food; excellent, service OK, atmosphere OK. 
I found this place while passing thru Santa Barbra.  It was just what I was looking for.  I ordered a steak and it was tender and cooked to perfection! My wife got the crab legs - I think it was one of their "specials".  She said it was delicious. a little on the pricey side, but worth it.  Both selections came with a salad bar which had very fresh ingredients and some bread.   Service was slightly lacking but very friendly.  Dimly lit. We'll definitely go back!

Categories: Food, Vietnamese, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: Noodle City is the only place I go to get pho in Santa Barbara.  Nowhere else can compare to how great their pho is.  I've been going here for the past 6-7 months once or twice a week! It's that great.  They also have an amazing thai tea with boba (only served on the weekends).  Their service is always fast and never disappoints.  It does get fairly busy sometimes, but I've never had to wait very long for a table.
Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: The owner has got a great sense of humor... pleasant experience! Get the green tea flavor!

Categories: Restaurants, Barbeque | Review: I ordered the tri-tip sandwich lunch special.  Didn't taste BBQ'd, it tasted boiled.  No Juices or seasoning.  The cow it came from must be rolling in its grave. Fries were OK.  I was really hoping to get WOWed.  Too bad :(

Categories: Food, American (New), Thai, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Desserts, Cafes, Sandwiches, Caterers, Event Planning & Services | Review: If you go for lunch order the salad. They are little sleeper items under the big chalkboard menu. One sweet and one savory. Both options are 1. huge and 2. super-tasty. Seasonal, quality ingredients every time. 

The sandwiches are good too, but right now I'm hooked on the salads.Someday maybe I'll make it in to try out the late-night food, but it's one of my top lunch spots to grab something yummy and healthy.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: We've been here 3 times.  We've never been disappointed.  Will always come back whenever we're in Santa Barbara.  The clam chowder is one of the best we have ever had.  Top two lifetime at least.  Excellent fish and chips.  Second or equal to Humpys in Anchorage, Alaska.  Outstanding views of the harbor with impressive, stunning mountain backdrop.

Categories: Breweries, Nightlife, Food, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Restaurants, Brewpubs, Gastropubs | Review: The food was great, the service was great (it was a Wednesday) and the drinks were great! I wished I lived here so I could come all the time

Categories: Cupcakes, Bakeries, Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea | Review: If you are going to order from crushcakes for a wedding or party read this first!!!!!!

This place is absolutely horrible!!!! We ordered 100 cupcakes and a 6 inch round cake cake for my daughters sweet 16 this weekend. I tried to place the order 2 weeks ago but no one would get back to me, it took a week for anyone to even respond to me after trying to reach them several times. This should have been my first indicator of the horrible customer service. Since there was no where else to go to get "fancy cupcakes" in Santa Barbara County we still placed the order. 

We picked up our order on Friday afternoon, we loaded them in our car in the boxes and bags THEY provided. When we arrived at our house EVERY SINGLE cupcake was squished, falling apart and frosting everywhere... They were an absolute mess. EVERY SINGLE ONE. This is over 400 dollars in cupcakes and cake mind you... 

I called them to tell then what had happened and they put me on the phone with the owner Shannon Noormand. After telling her what had happened she proceeded to yell at me on the phone in front of her co-workers and customers (I know this because she told me just before) that it's not her problem and that she would not give me any type of refund for these destroyed cupcakes, that was completely their fault because they packaged them in flimsy boxes and stacked them on top of each other. 

Who conducts business like this!?!? CRUSHCAKES AND SHANNON NOORMAND. 

This entire thing was such a catastrophe that we ended up having to THROW AWAY every last cupcake and buy new ones at Von's last minute because there was no other options. 

Let me tell you... The ones we purchased at Von's tasted just as good and they 1/16 of the price of Crushcakes. 

I am in the process of disputing this purchase with the credit card company. 

NEVER IN MY LIFE HAVE I HAD SUCH HORRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE ESPECIALLY FOR THE OWNER TO ACT THIS WAY.

I will never return there and will NEVER recommend them to anyone. 

HORRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE, THE OWNER IS A TERRIBLE PERSON AND THEIR CUPCAKES ARE NO WHERE NEAR WORTH THE PRICE.

DO NOT GO TO CRUSHCAKES!!!!'
Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: The staff is not the friendliest and even the customers that were there were not very nice.  But it is conveniently located off the freeway

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Restaurants, Cafes | Review: To say I was excited to try H&G Coffee would be an understatement. I saw they were opening and got in there opening weekend. They share a location with ParadICE (which is phenomenal) in Paseo Nuevo. While the shop is quaint in size, there is lots of space outside to enjoy your order. They are adhering to COVID protocols so make sure to wear a mask and give space to others. 

I went with my go-to drink and ordered a vanilla latte with whole milk. I don't know how else to describe my drink other than a smooth cup of comfort. The coffee wasn't too acidic and the vanilla flavor was just right. It was seriously delicious! The staff were also super friendly and engaged with my kids while we waited for the order. Love everything about this place and plan on going back!

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Pickles and Swiss is the best sandwich shop in town and I make a habit of visiting whenever I am in the area.  The quality of the ingredients makes their sandwiches stand out and there is something for everyone on the menu.  My go-tos are the Chipotle Turkey Club and the Brunch Dunch but you can't go wrong with any of the sandwiches.  I recently tried one of their cookies for the first time and instantly regretted not doing it sooner.  The staff are really friendly and have on numerous occasions offered some great suggestions such as adding Sriracha to my Eggplant Parma - try it, you won't regret it!

Categories: Restaurants, Thai, Food, Chicken Wings, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Great food and portions / service staff is exceptional as well. Lunch menu is best value but nothing is to expensive

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: This place can be a hit and a miss.
The happy hour is really good I can't complain. My favorite drink is the Paloma. It's a Margarita with grape fruit! OMG to die for!! Happy Hour all day Sunday is great!!!
The service and the food is where i only give this a 3 star. Service is only ok when sitting at the bar. There is a awesome bartender named Dominic so hope you get him!  The food is ehhhh.....not that great but not that bad. In the middle. I would go there for the happy hour and drinks :)
Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Among the best in Santa Barbara. Great tacos and nachos, good prices for the area. Seating is mostly outside.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: funk zone location. winter limited flavors include winter pear crisp, blood orange, and vanilla grapefruit.  nice strong flavor and smooth texture
classics like eureka lemon and marionberries

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Food needs work.
Dont have the Dungeness crab .It is  not prepared properly and the shell is fully on and its a long gruelling fight to get to the crab.I sent it back and they suggested the Crab Louis salad but that crab tasted like  canned and was super salty.
I have been here about 6 times as its in my area  and is open later than some restaurants but ive never been impressed with the food which is mediocre. 
View of harbour is very nice.
Staff is polite but not well trained.

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants | Review: Pros - great, great view off the pier! 
Great, strong drinks!
Good service 

 Cons- food isn't great! Had their fish taco (not impressed!)

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: What a cool hidden treasure of the 154. If your into California history and good food this is your place. Save room for dessert.. the pecan pie is off the charts!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: Excellent ice cream but wildly expensive for small portions. Terrific if you feel like splurging on a treat. Very little parking. Lot is tiny and you may want to be sure your collision insurance is current. Well advised to try the street.

Categories: Bars, American (New), Tapas Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife | Review: My girlfriend and I both SB locals have been hesitant to come here since the change but after visiting we where not disappointed one bit. After looking over the drink and food menu we asked the bartender, Raul, to order us his favorite appetizer and suprise us with the best drinks. Everything was amazing. The staff here made our ordinary date night extra ordinary

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: The ambience is excellent amongst the greenery and no spot is a bad one.  Excellent cuts of meat and exotic things to mix with.  Craft cocktails and good local beer on tap.  Can't recommend this place more for a dress up date night or even a casual night out.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: We looked forward to our 3rd anniversary weekend in SB and dinner at Bouchon.  I'm a hobby chef and constantly search for top-shelf restaurant experiences. 

6:15 reservations on Saturday night May 17th   Reservations were honored and we were seated immediately.

We have only 2 "dings", but unfortunately the second one pretty much spoiled the experience.  Everything else was fine, however .  .  .

Our waiter was friendly and helpful, but unfortunately disappeared for long stretches. This meant no wine for the first few minutes after our entree was served.  The water glasses were empty as well.  Wine arrived quickly when we asked the bus person to get our waiter for us and he realized his mistake.

The main issue; My wife's rack of lamb, which was ordered medium rare, arrived essentially raw.  I prefer my meat very rare (carpaccio is a favorite), but this was obviously "blue" rare.  It was sent back, which is not always the worst thing to happen and doesn't necessarily spoil the experience.  Again, unfortunately, the lamb was returned to my wife now completely overdone.  Obviously no one cared enough to fix the problem properly.  Sad and too late now . . . so she picked at it, and finally ate some of my pork chop (which was excellent)

This is an expensive place, and this wasn't a subtle problem.  Either the chef, the expediter or the waiter should have noticed the obviously underdone lamb - and even though it was not addressed at the correct time, there's no excuse for the lack of care taken to ensure the mistake was handled.

It's a shame that all we could remember was a poorly prepared and handled rack of lamb.  It's nice that my wife complimented the fact that I'm a better chef than the one at Bouchon, but that's certainly not what I look for in an high-end, special occasion spot like this.  I expect a chef that leaves my home skills in the dust.

Why is it so difficult to get a true 5 star meal these days?

Categories: Steakhouses, Local Flavor, Nightlife, Fish & Chips, Restaurants, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: Went to Arch Rock Fish after reading the reviews. Liked the place for its ambience and food. Our waiter was a cool guy, very accommodating. 
The menu is short but has some good dishes. 
We ordered the calamari, the mussels spagetti and the vegetable pasta. 

The calamari was ok. 3/5
the mussels spagetti was great. it went very well with the bread too. 4/5
the vegetable pasta was ok. 3/5

In all this was a good experience just not a exceptional one. the ambience of the place is cool.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: Best poke I've had, very nice place to eat at and love the atmosphere. The food tastes very fresh and cold. The boba drinks are also one of the best drinks I've had. Totally recommend this place

Categories: Soup, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Bars, Italian | Review: Excellent location but bad food and dirty dishes. My wife and I went there on a weekend a couple of weeks ago; we ordered  a Lasagna and Pasta al Pesto: $40+ for something we could have cooked at home. The dishes and silverware looked really old and used, the plate had been used so many times that the scratches in it were noticeable from a distance.

Improve food quality, lower prices and buy new tableware and you'll be set for success.
Categories: Food Trucks, Tacos, Mexican, Salad, Food, Restaurants | Review: Plain and simple: I waited over 30 minutes and paid $5 for half of a corn tortilla stuffed with some cheese.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: I was so excited to try Tupelo Junction Café on our recent visit to Santa Barbara - I had heard good things from multiple sources. It did not disappoint. We had a perfect, homestyle, chill Saturday brunch.  We came in about 1 and they would close at 2 (until reopening for dinner), so it wasn't super packed. We had lovely service and ordered a bunch of yummy delicious, mostly fried ;), things, and some drinks.

Favorite was the fried chicken and waffle. The waffle had cornmeal in it and the batter on the chicken did too. The chicken was very well seasoned and cooked perfectly. It was great. Next favorite were the cheddar cheese hush puppies with spicy pepper jam. Fried balls of cornmeal - awesome. Add a gooey cheesy center - even better. Add some pepper jam to dip them in - perfection! The fried green tomatoes were good. But they were served on a corn and tomato sort of salsa. That was all good, but it made the bottom side of the "fry" a little soggy. Great flavor, just need a better presentation, I think, to avoid the sog factor. Finally, the cinnamon apple beignets. They were good but not really beignets, which I think of as more "pillowy", these were more dough-y, like taking some monkey bread, pulling off a piece and deep frying it. Can't really go wrong, but not really a beignet. And the crème anglais was great. 

We topped off the meal with a couple of drinks in mason jars - a margarita and the savannah Georgia. Both quite tasty. Long story short: we were stuffed and happy at the end of our meal. :) Add me to the fan club!

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Active Life, Golf | Review: Service that exceeds expectations.  I lost my 5 iron at the course on a busy Saturday morning.  I called later in the day, and sure enough the staff found it and put it in a safe spot.  Since I live over two hours away, the staff member (Randy) agreed to ship it to me.  Randy was both kind and professional.  Thank you to the whole Sandpiper staff for continuing to provide excellent service.  I'll be back.

Categories: Nightlife, Wine Bars, Bars, American (Traditional), Tapas/Small Plates, Tapas Bars, Food, Arts & Entertainment, Wine Tasting Room, Wineries, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Love supporting this small business especially during the current Covid-19 crisis. They have nightly takeout specials during this crazy time so i decided to give it a shot with their $18 Fish & Chips takeout special including salad & cheesecake. The fish was a very generous portion with three big pieces. I could've easily shared this with someone. I liked the crispy batter too.

I always love coming here because their food is great and their wine dispenser system is a huge hit. 

Please support our local, small businesses!

Thanks Michael for your hardwork and great food & service.

Categories: Vegan, Vegetarian, Restaurants, Indian | Review: I came here a few more times since my first review and like the changes they made.  First, the naan is so much fluffier and fresher than the first time we dined here.  The price is a few dollars cheaper and each entree also includes basmati rice.  We usually order the fig walnut kofta.  I also like the chicken chettinad, made with tender chicken thigh, savory, greasy, and full of mustard seeds and curry leaves.  I tried the Sri Lankan eggplant dish and it kind of reminded me of Chinese eggplant, a little sour, a little sweet, lots of wok hei all mixed with pulpy soft eggplants.  Overall, I have really grown to like this place.  The most recent visit consisted of 6 adults and 2 kids and everyone enjoyed the food with the two vegetarians proclaim that it is the best Indian food they ever had.

Categories: Breweries, Food | Review: The grand opening weekend at M Special was a blast! Outdoor seating and games, food trucks, live music, I had an amazing time.

I love the idea of this place; located in a warehouse with the feel of a true brewery. The warehouse is very close to the marketplace, but located in a more secluded area. I actually prefer that because the place is in a huge open space and the parking lot is huge. Everyone who works there is super friendly and kids were running everywhere that day dressed in M Special attire and selling these adorable hats. 

Growlers were 16/20 (16 for fill and 20 for first time purchase, I believe). And the standard cups were going at $5, typical for a brewery. 

Although options were limited, they had a good variety of beers. They had an IPA, a Hoppy Blonde, Traditional Blonde, American Brown, and more. I loved the American Brown. There are a couple more beers opening this week, so I'm definitely excited to try those! 

Free pretzels and water are a definite plus. :)

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, American (New), Restaurants | Review: for cleanliness & safety 
 for service speed & friendliness 
 for fresh ingredients and a great menu
 for returning customer and would recommend 

I think I've only ordered 2 different things on the menu I loved them so much lol. The Turkey Chipotle Club is my go to. Although the name is misleading because it's not a club sandwich with 3 slices of bread, it's made on a ciabatta. 

I've also had their pesto chicken salad sandwich, bomb.com! The have a variety of cold and hot sandwiches or you can build your own. It's a bit on the pricey side depending on what you order but you definitely get your $ worth and don't leave hungry.

Categories: Dance Clubs, Nightlife | Review: One of the most low key gems on this block!! Best bar hands down! Mike the owner is the funniest guy you'll ever meet. Great drinks, great DJ, will definitely be back!!

Categories: American (New), Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Staying in Santa Barbara for a mini vacation. Was craving Mexican and a hotel employee highly recommended this restaurant. Ordered the Burrito Mojado for $11, which could feed two people. Tasted AMAZING. Great flavor and presentation.
Place is busy and can get loud. Parking is limited (We walked.) Salsa bar had several options. Ordered at the desk, find a seat and your order is delivered to you. Fresh chips are also offered.

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: It was my first time going to Santa Barbara and a couple of friends told me the place to go was Sharkeez. When I got there the bartender Alex was super helpful making the night extremely enjoyable. 10/10 would recommend and ask for the bartender Alex he would make sure your night goes smoothly.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Steakhouses | Review: Checked the place out on a Sat night. Originally they gave us a wait time of 30-35 mins but it only ended up being about 10! So great start. It seems that you need a reservation to sit outside, which i will definitely do next time, but the inside setting wasnt bad. The drinks were a little expensive...but strong enough to make it worth it. I recommend the mai tai. Dinner was, again, a little expensive..most seafood entrees were between 22 and 27...BUT you get a ton of food and I got no where close to finishing.
My only complaint, isnt really even a complaint...but the waiters were a little awkward, which made the night a little more entertaining for my sister and I...but if thats something that bothers you than maybe try another place.
And they take Axxess so dont forget!

Categories: Food, Vietnamese, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: My family and I drove down from Arroyo Grande to see my nephew and his gal, and to have some Vietnamese grub.  This is a good little mom and pop type noodle house.  The flavors and presentation are simple yet adequate.  I did not expect hardcore vietnamese food that you get in the bigger population centers...  everything was tasty and prepared well... go and enjoy!  This is a basic, all purpose Vietnamese cafe and does the triick nicely.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Great diner for an excellent breakfast. They can't do espresso drinks but you can get those next door. The eggs are excellently done and they are a little picky with substitutions on their menu (as in they don't allow subs) but I'm confident you will find something you like regardless.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Desserts, Turkish, Restaurants, Bakeries | Review: This place is crazy, you HAVE to try the pistachio coffee. And try the desserts -crazy. A lot of them are really chewy. The staff is sooooo nice. Everyone was offering samples. The seating area was cute. The kids loved it.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Delicious Hangover noodles wow spicy! If you like heat then those one is for you. If your sensitive to spice steer clear. Organic tofu was eh ok but the fresh veggies and noodles with sauces was the best.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, Bakeries | Review: The food here was phenomenal. We ordered the green breakfast bowl, which was unique and the sauce added a delicious flavor. Loved it. The turkey panini had jalapeños baked into the bread and it was very crunchy and delicious. The atmosphere both inside and on the patio was very nice and cute. Loved this place for a casual brunch.

Categories: Pizza, Restaurants, Italian, Vegan, Gluten-Free, Sandwiches | Review: Mesa pizza has always been a solid choice when I have a craving for pizza in sb. I have never had a single issue ordering through them, and they are always very friendly and courteous.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, American (Traditional) | Review: Tried this breakfast spot by our Uber drivers recommendation and we were not disappointed! Great service, great food-it reminds me of good ma and pa home cooking. I highly recommend this to anyone who likes classic hearty breakfast food.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: Our favorite Spanish restaurant! We had another amazing dining experience, last night. Incredible food and ambience; my husband was partially raised in Spain and vouches for its authenticity.  The staff goes above and beyond; definitely try the gambas, seafood paella, and sangria. Perfection all around with a dog friendly patio!

Categories: Asian Fusion, Seafood, Restaurants, Poke, Food | Review: Everything is so flavorful .
I had ceviche and the miso soup! Sooo delish!!!! 

The crew is so nice and they work fast! 
Super friendly!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Latin American, Mexican, Seafood | Review: Chef Ramon is back in business! Corazon now has a full menu with some of the old favorites from their pop-up shop and lots of new creative dishes. Clearly this restaurant is about creating fresh, quality Mexican food and they do not cut corners. I ordered the pork belly taco and after one bite I immediately regretted not ordering a few more! The pork is tender and juicy and the other ingredients (salsa, peaches, & others) just created an outstanding medley of flavors. The homemade blue corn tortillas pull everything together and is one of many things that makes this place stand out. I also ordered the Norteño which was a flour tortilla with cheese, guacamole and delicious smoked steak asada, and a few other ingredients. This is also a must try. The other people in my group also ordered the Chicken Mole taco, shrimp taco & the beer batteries Lingcod taco. All were amazing and  fresh!

Corazon's new location is in the Santa Barbara public market. It is clear that they will bring in the foot traffic that the market has been lacking since its opening. This restaurant is a must try for anyone visiting or living in Santa Barbara. I'm already planning my next meal here. Might have to cancel a utility bill to make room for a bigger taco budget.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Nightlife, Restaurants, Comfort Food, American (New), Burgers, Salad | Review: Delicious food! And very good service! Good! Good! Good! Amazing meatballs! Not like in ikea! Really perfect!!!:)

Categories: Steakhouses, Local Flavor, Nightlife, Fish & Chips, Restaurants, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: Happy hour ALL day Sunday, Can't beat that! $6 Sushi roll, $6 bloody Mary and $12 fish and chips....score. The ambiance is cozy and the bartenders are not only friendly but super cute ;) A must visit when in Santa Barbara.  It's off State street so its lets crowded. There is city parking off Ortega and Anacapa. Can't wait to come back!

Categories: Pakistani, Restaurants, Indian | Review: A mixed experience. This restaurant located on trendy State Street has a nice visual appeal when you first see it. The back half is indoor seating and in the front half is open air exposed, sort of like a covered courtyard that faces the street. For colder nights, there are heaters for the front area, but unless you are close enough, you may find it a bit chilly as my kids discovered, and they started migrating to hanging out under a nearby umbrella heater during the overly long wait for our food to arrive. 

The menu, which is just plain printed computer produced paper sheets encased in clear plastic covers with no graphics was lacking much detail, unprofessional looking and did not have the appropriate visual appeal for an upscale restaurant experience. An even further detraction was the statement emblazed on the top front of the menu that stated you must order your food all at the same time upfront and that additional orders would not be accommodated in no ambiguous terms. This clearly sets the tone that service level is secondary to their desires of how they think patrons should behave. It's almost embarrassing to see such attitudes displayed on the menu and clearly they don't understand that they are in a service business where accommodating patrons is what sets you apart from the stiff competition. 

The food choices are your basic typical Indian fare and they do not have anything particularly innovative or creative. The food, when it finally did arrive after a long wait was tasty and well prepared, but the experience was marred by the somewhat inattentive service up to that point. The pricing is on the expensive side for typical Indian restaurants, but given the high rents that I assume come with the location, is not outrageous. 

Overall this place has a lot of positive elements but a few key flaws detracts, which is unfortunate.
Categories: Dance Clubs, Nightlife, Bars | Review: I have been coming here for years, never fails. I always have the best time. I recommend coming any chance you can.

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Wow! I thought this place was gonna be a gimmick. Best gluten free bread ever. The pickle was crisp and flavorful. Everything was home made from a scratch. What a difference. I ate my egg-avocado sandwich and wanted at least 2 more. So tasty. Great kids menu too! Friendly owner. Treat yourself!

Categories: Food Trucks, Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: Absolutely amazing I stop by on my way after work to try these beautiful crepes, very quick and extremely delicious I told all my coworkers and they went and got some as well and love them!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: You walk up, order, and the food is brought to a table of your choosing. 
The ambiance is funky and trendy. 
Laid-back. The signature dish is served in an elevated bowl, and can burn your hands, if one isn't careful. 
Side dishes are served in cute tortilla bowls. 
Fresh delish Mexican!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: We made a reservation here the day of the SBCC graduation. To our surprise, the restaurant wasn't busy at all (most restaurants in SB were full). We arrived and were greeted by a nice hostess and she seated us right away. Our waiter didn't take our drink order till about 20 minutes after sitting down, and our food order about 25 minutes after that. To make things worse, our food took a little over 30 minutes to receive. When we got the food, the majority of our party didn't like it and our cadillac margaritas tasted like regular house margarita. 
Waiter seemed nice but VERY slow with everything. Took well over an hour to just get our food. Maybe this place was nearly empty because people know the service is slow and food is way below average. 
Do yourself a favor and go elsewhere!

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Nightlife, Seafood, Bars | Review: Looking for some nice dining not too far from the UCSB campus? Look no further. 
Luckily, you don't taste the convenience in the food.
While I've only been here twice, I'll definitely be coming back.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Seafood, Sushi Bars, Japanese | Review: Mexican roll is the best here and I most definitely would recommend getting it! The crunchy roll is really good too!

I always get chicken katsu here. They give a good amount  of chicken. I opt out on getting the curry sauce and just stick to eel sauce and siracha. 

It is a bit pricey but the food is good and worth it.

Categories: Venues & Event Spaces, Cafes, Wine Bars, Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: The Wine Cask restaurant is incredible.  I recently held a large party event there and everything went smoothly.  It's hard to pick just one thing when it comes to "What stands out the most", so here's my short list.

Service - Their service is the best; professional, responsive, and accommodating.  From planning the event through service on the night of the event, they always went the extra mile to keep me informed and to make sure that the event was going as planned.  Every detail was covered (they think of everything) and the service exceeded my expectations.

Food - Hats off to the chef. Our menu had something for everyone and the food was amazing.  From presentation to taste it was perfection on a plate.  

I look forward to holding future events at the Wine Cask - thank you to everyone who made the evening as memorable.

Categories: American (New), Wraps, Chinese, Sushi Bars, Barbeque, Dim Sum, Asian Fusion, Ramen, Restaurants, Noodles, Soup, Japanese, Hot Pot | Review: I'm kind of a sucker for dumplings, so when I saw this place had opened I had to try it.  I've been back a few times.  Despite being a sucker for dumplings I'm not exactly a dumpling conoisseur, so I can't speak much to the authenticity of these dumplings, but overall they scratch my dumpling itch.  I've also tried a few non-dumpling items like the chicken skewers and those were pretty good too.

The dim sum items are perhaps slightly more expensive than they ought to be.  Depending on the size and type of dumpling or bun, you get 2-4 dumplings for somewhere around $5 or $6,  When I've gone for dinner I usually get three items and it comes about to about $17 before tip.  More than I'd like, but there really isn't much competition for dumplings or dim sum in this area so I take what I can get.  They also have lunch specials and supposedly hot pot (they still have the induction grills in the tables) although I haven't tried any of that yet.

Service is generally quiet and efficient.  No complaints there.

I'll probably be back whenever I crave dumplings.  Although it's more expensive than I'd like, this is still a decent place and I hope it sticks around.

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Peruvian | Review: From the drinks to the service to the food, I was impressed. The ceviche (very interesting, non-traditional), delicious diver scallop salad, and prosciutto thin crust pizza were all very well prepared with attention to detail. Molten chocolate cake with passion fruit center, not too sweet but yummy. Very creative and well mixed drinks, were thoughtfully created. The sauces were thoughtfully prepared and well executed and the flavors worked well together and throughout the meal. This restaurant is a departure from the local scene and I like it. Its not inexpensive, but is well priced and i think you get what you pay for. I will definitely be back (soon!).

Categories: Restaurants, Vietnamese | Review: [To Go Review]

I was skeptical.  The lady who answered my phone call said, "I'm sorry, what's Pho Tai in English?  I should know this."   I feel like most people in large cities could tell you that Pho Tai is rare beef pho.  It's as common as ordering Pad Thai at a Thai restaurant, it doesn't need an English translation and you can see the Viet words on the menu.  What am I getting myself into?  Is this going to be bland and watered down?

To my surprise, the Pho Tai (12.50) was amazing.  Solid broth, generous amounts of meat and very comforting.  It was fragrant and perfect.

My partner ordered the Bun Thit Nuong Cha Gio (v3) (12).  He proclaimed that the meat was the perfect texture and char and had amazing flavor.

Though, a lot more expensive than I'm used, I appreciated that it was a good authentic meal in this humble little town.  It was worth it despite the language obstacle over the phone.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Great Pacific Ice Cream has a great location in Santa Barbara's Stearns Wharf. Perfect for a Sunday afternoon stroll, where you can enjoy the amazing views and grab an ice cream cone. 

The quality of the ice cream itself is only OK. I would prefer more natural flavors and colors, to be honest. 

The line is also pretty long, but it does move quickly.

Categories: Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Food | Review: I absolutely love this place when I am in SB.  The people are super nice and I love the atmosphere.  Now I do have a lot of vices :) but luckily caffeine is not one of them so I only drink decaf and I would not say that is the highlight of this place.  What I love is the spices they sell here.  They are so fresh and aromatic and I always stock up on turmeric, the hot and mild curries, and other spicy offerings.  It's a little old school how they work here were you need to lug the glass containers to the counter and then they hand weigh each item out and seal them in little baggies.  But I don't care because it makes it all the more an interesting experience where you can hang and chat with the local folks.  The other highlight is the tea section here.  I bought a tea that had interesting dried fruits in it that was phenomenal as an iced tea I made.  It would blow away anything you would get at Pete's or Starbucks.  The chai is also quite good.  This place is great for gifts also because they hand pack everything in bags  and you can make a nice little gift box for a reasonable price.  Yes this is the place for your vices and spices bar none.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: Great service, which was probably the highlight of the night. Do not try to go here on a Saturday, but it was easier to get in on Sunday. Gluten free options are plenty, but no GF breads. The server wasn't clear about soy versus soy sauce as far as GF dishes go, so the paella came with soy sauce soaked mushrooms, which was my misunderstanding. 

Highlights: cana de Cabra cheese, pate and tortilla Espanola. 

The Korean paella was lacking pork belly and much else besides rice, and had a good amount of heat to it, which would have been nice to know ahead of time.

Overall, good vibe, hip place, but expensive.

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese | Review: The food was so delicious, the portions huge and the service so friendly. Covid-friendly, as no-one can enter and loiter around, the service desk is at the entrance of the restaurant.

Categories: Noodles, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Creperies, Chinese, Asian Fusion | Review: They have very bad service. We ordered two buns and a noodle with a fountain drink. At first, they forgot to give us the soda cup. Later, they missed our bun and then the waiter gave our bun to other table. The promised us to make another bun, but that took us forever to get it. Do not recommend this restaurant.
Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Sugar free frozen yogurt, PERFECT temperature.  And under $2 for what elsewhere would be like a regular size serving.  ME LIKEY.

Categories: Fast Food, Burgers, Restaurants | Review: Update: after reading my review the management contacted me to apologize and make things right. I love habit and the staff has always been great. It's my top choice of all the burger places for fast food. It keeps me coming back for more. 

I've been coming to habit for years and I enjoy being able to get it in many cities now. Nothing is quite like the original though. They have a great set up for handling orders during Covid 19. However, even though they have someone confirming your order when you place it and a again upon pick up, they still left items out of my order. I should know better and check for myself when I get to my car, but when you have hungry kids to feed your mind isn't always on double checking others work. Driving back just isn't an option, so check your orders.

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Can't think of one negative thing about our visit there.....

Went there for lunch on the Saturday of Old Spanish Days Festival. Got in without a problem as apparently they've just started serving lunch. The greeting was nice and the service overall was very good...professional and polite and friendly.  They look sharp in their white jackets.

We started with a rum punch and the hurricane martini...served in pint sized mason jars which is a fun twist. Lots of flavor, and packed a good punch.
For an app we had the battered and flash fried artichoke hearts...delicious and not greasy at all, served with a nice dipping sauce.  Very good...wanted another order of them.

For lunch I had the bronzed chicken Po'boy served on a perfect French roll, crispy and chewy, pickles, shredded cabbage, and a nice light mayo.  It too...delicious.  Came with a side of nice roasted potatoes with a bit of Cajun or old bay seasoning.

My girlfriend had the vegetarian mardi gras pasta primavera...which she loved...a little creamier than what she typically likes, but she loved it.  Came with two mini muffins: jalapeno corn and a walnut.

We were checked on a few times to see how we were doing which is always nice.

For dessert...beignets...we probably didn't need them but we polished them off. A huge dusting of powdered sugar topped our order of 3. They were a little denser than I recall in New Orleans, but they sure were tasty.

We will certainly recommend Palace Grill and would certainly go back again.
It was a peaceful oasis on an insane day of crowded festival sidewalks....and we were able to walk it all off during the rest of the day.
Kudos to the chef...and the servers.

Categories: Bakeries, Cafes, Coffee & Tea, Food, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Yummy red velvet pancakes and cupcakes.  

Dog friendly!

Categories: Caterers, Restaurants, Desserts, Bakeries, Event Planning & Services, Gluten-Free, Food, Cupcakes | Review: What can I say about Carole that hasn't already been said? My fiancé and I came down for some wedding planning and on a whim I decided to call Delicious Expressions for a cupcake tasting. We ordered 6 flavors, and to my delight she delivered them to our hotel at no extra cost! And they were so good! We couldn't decide which we liked more: the red velvet, Nutella surprise, lemon raspberry, chocolate delight, vanilla caramel, and bacon maple (yes, BACON!). We decided we'll have a variety of all of them at our reception. Who knew picking a wedding cake would be so easy? Carole is so friendly and professional, and I can't wait to share our find with all our guests!
Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Crappy service, crappy decor and crappy food.  We left this restaurant with my husband hungry.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Sunday brunch 7am no wait. Excellent service! Flight of mimosa, in order of favorites; grapefruit, peach, cranberry, orange. Husband got corn beef hash and said it was amazing. Large portion of beef. He got a side of pancakes. The pancakes tasted amazing!! I got the tiger special. The French toast was great, the scrambled was awesome, and the bacon was good. Complimentary basket of chocolate chip and blueberry muffins were good. Chad's sit of on of the only beaches you can see the sunrise. Overall a great place to eat. I would highly recommend it!!!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Fantastic food and great service! We ordered the Pad Thai and Beef Panang Curry (Tuesday Special) and both were very tasty. Love this place and can't wait to be back!

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants | Review: The location is ideal but the food and drinks are not spectacular.  Additoinally, the waitress messed up our bill pretty badly -- actually giving us someone else's credit card at the end.  There were some cross over charges, too.  I will say however, their oysters were fresh.  But I don't think I'll be back.

Categories: Food, Vietnamese, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: Best pho in Goleta. I'm from LA, so I have high standards for pho, and there have been times when Noodle City is on par with where I'm from! However, there might be times when it's a bit off. Probably depends on the chef!
Overall attentive and quick service + good pho!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Seafood | Review: Tasty food! Even my picky 4 year old liked it. Restaurant staff could be more welcoming and friendly. The restaurant was a little warm inside. We would go back for the food, but wasn't crazy about the atmosphere.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Fish & Chips | Review: Saturday afternoon - no wait time

Service: waitress was very attentive at first and also apologized for the delay in food but after serving our food she was no where to be found. After asking another waitress for our check she comes back stating she had been on lunch and she will bring us the check. After another long wait we got up and asked for our check. 

Food: ordered agua chiles, shrimp enchilada a and gorditas. All pretty good but not amazing. 

Micheladas are also pretty good. Would give 8/10 

Overall I'd come back but would hope the service would remain consistent throughout the meal.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: I agree with the Yelpers Joaquin, D and Annebel G.

Cute place but the food didn't cut it with me, lack of flavor and that was a general consensus with everyone at out table.

It needed a pinch of salt or something. Oh well, I can say I've been here but that's about it.
