Categories: Food, American (New), Thai, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Desserts, Cafes, Sandwiches, Caterers, Event Planning & Services | Review: The service was fast and prompt and no wait on a Saturday night.  I had the noodle bowl with tofu and the flavors in this dish were delicious.  I will definitely be back for more!

Categories: Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, Japanese, Nightlife, Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Cocktail Bars, Asian Fusion | Review: We had an excellent meal while we were in town for Thanksgiving weekend.I had the short ribs, my husband had the steak. Both were fabulous. Highly recommend for the food.
Categories: Restaurants, Mexican, Burgers | Review: Stopped by here today on the drive south...great carne Asada tacos! Good beef, large in size/generous portions!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Food, Desserts, Japanese | Review: I love trying new sushi places when I'm traveling and this one was a hit! Fairly priced and the size of the rolls will not disappoint! I went with when spicy garlic edamame and the mexi-Cali roll from another review and it was on point!
Categories: Thai, Restaurants | Review: Decent food but holy smokes so expensive! $16 for a fried rice?!? I dont think id come back just due to that alone, highly overpriced!!

Categories: American (New), Burgers, Restaurants, Hawaiian | Review: One of my favorite burger places in town! I love the layout of the restaurant, and big kahuna Wednesdays with buy one meal, get the other half off!  Any sandwich you get is going to be delicious. 

My only critic is they mix up the fries. Sometimes thin shoestrings other times steak fries. I'm not a fan of the shoestring fries.

Categories: Chicken Shop, Desserts, Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Barbeque, Mexican, Bars, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants | Review: What a lovely, classic restaurant!

I had the privilege of ordering takeout from this great new place tonight. I found the place to look much like its prior tenant, Paradise Cafe (which was a favorite of mine), but was definitely refreshed and modern. The owner did a great job restoring the place to its original charm. It looked super authentic, I was impressed. 

Service was friendly and I felt the ladies whom had assisted me did a great job. I ordered a bowl of the vegetable pozole along with a side of garlic bread and a margarita. All items were exceptional. The bread had a Tex Mex-meets-Santa Maria flavor, so good. The pozole was incredibly flavorful and certainly rivaled my grandma's recipe. The margarita was absolutely delicious and totally hit the spot after a long workday. 

I hope to come back here, soon, and dine on their awesome patio. Thanks for the wonderful service and food, La Paloma!

Categories: Garage Door Services, Restaurants, Live/Raw Food, Food, Home Services, Mediterranean, Keys & Locksmiths, Burgers, Vegetarian, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Vegan, Salad | Review: LOVE THIS PLACE!!

It's getting harder and harder to find great vegetarian/vegan food in Santa Barbara. This place has a great vibe, excellent customer service, and the food is delicious as well as filling! 

This not your average vegan restaurant where you leave hungry. Great portions and a great value. I try and take as many people as I can to this place. Plus they usually pretty hip, current music which adds to the experience.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: So I must say that this cafe is great. My boyfriend and I were looking for a cute little breakfast joint to end our Santa Barbara trip with and upon looking through many yelp reviews and google searches we came across this little cafe. It is quite small. And upon arriving we got sat down on a table right in front of the door which was a little awkward and after asking if we could switch tables, the waiter was polite and super friendly and allowed us too. The food is very homey. It does not have that artificial Denny's and IHOP taste which is exactly what we were looking for. My favorite thing of it all was the pancakes. One bite into it and I felt like I was sitting in my own kitchen eating. GREAT SERVICE. They are very friendly and attentive. Would come here again. And everyone in Santa Barbara should stop by and try it (:

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: I've gotta say, my experience was probably the worst I've ever had, service wise. I put my name down for a table of two at 550. They said about an hour. It ended up being 750 before we got a table and we begged at the end... Mind you it was very cold and windy for those 2 hours. We got a drink order in after about 5 minutes and then NOTHING for half an hour. The guy who was waiting for the tables in our area completely ignored us like we weren't even there. He kept going to the table next to us that sat down after us, at least 4 times. I tried to get his attention and he said it hold on. Left for 10 minutes and comes back and literally says " what do you need " ( so rude) and I said oh sorry are you not our waiter? We haven't seen anyone since we placed our drink order so we haven't ordered food and he says " haha really???" I've been right here this whole time...." and I said oh well you never came by or looked at us so I wasn't sure that you were our waiter and he said "well.... I'm in a black shirt aren't I?!" I said I'm not sure what that means and then he says again... WHAT DO YOU NEED. So I said I'm not sure why you are being so rude to me, at this point, I feel really uncomfortable and never been treated like this by a waiter and I'm ready to leave. He then says "WOW so I'm a waiter now?!? IM ACTUALLY A SERVER!!! IM GETTING MY MANAGER!!! So I said please do!!! By this point, I'm boiling and can't wait to talk to a manager. One never comes. Another waiter comes by and we talked to him, nice as could be and convinces us to stay. We order lobster tacos and enchiladas. They were fine, probably 3 stars. Drinks were a 2, But overall the experience was so horrible that I would say it averaged a 1. Stay far away if you see a young tall lanky waiter with slick back hair. Obviously don't know his name because he never gave it to me.

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants | Review: Went for Lunch on Saturday with the wife and kids and I must say I was very impressed with the service. Our waitress Aubrey was very tentative, informative and polite. The Bloody Mary's she recommended was beyond what we expected. A neighboring customer recommended the Lobster Tacos or the Lobster sandwich so we had both and they were so good. We are definitely coming back and recommending this place. Well worth the hour drive. The ocean view outside and the shaded area was perfect for the kids as well. 
Great job Aubrey... Thanks for making our visit a great one.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: The food here saved me from a hangover the next day. 

Late night Mexican food is always a good idea when it tastes this good. 

Got an asada burrito and a ceviche. Both tasted great. Service was friendly, and the food came out quickly for how extremely busy it was late night.
Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Cool place overlooking the Ventura harbor.Great service and great food. Cant beat lunch or dinner with a view

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, American (New), Restaurants | Review: By far one of the highest quality sandwich shops in Goleta. Can't get enough of the chicken orzo and tomato bisque soups! Everything ive had in my handful of visits has been consistently amazing, also super friendly staff, and great music/atmosphere!

Only complaint is its pricy, but you get what you pay for!

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: I love this place!  I have been a couple times over the years and, when we went last week for a company Christmas dinner, I was reminded of how much I love it! We had 10 people for dinner so I called ahead of time to make a reservation & it was made with no problem.  We went on a Wednesday night so it was easy to get a reservation, but I would definitely call way in advance for large groups on the weekends as it's always packed when I walk by. 
 The breakdown:

Waitstaff: Large groups are always hard, but the waitstaff handled our group like the pros that they are! They were very attentive, kind, and efficient. Everyone's orders were correct & there is a part of the evening where they pass out song sheets & you all sing What A Wonderful World together.  

Food: Come hungry! We started with the coconut shrimp & Cajun Popcorn (crawfish) for appetizers & they also give you mini muffins (the kind varies from night to night). Both appetizers were fried & delicious! The Cajun Popcorn is what they're known for so this is definitely a must!  I got the jambalaya over dirty rice & I am still thinking about how good it was! I ended up taking half of it home & it was still just as good the next day. I would recommend going with one of their tried & true Cajun dishes. People at the table who did not get one of these traditional dishes were not as big of fans of their meal. We ended dinner with the bread pudding (another Palace specialty) & it was also SO good! Definitely a great place to go with a group (small or large) so you can share!

Ambiance: The place is decorated with a Mardi Gras theme & it has a very Louisiana feel.  For those not easily offended, make sure to check out the art in the bathroom.   For those who are easily offended, you've been warned. 

Enjoy!

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: They have absolutely amazing food here, but they are consistently not staying true to their hours. I have came in here several times 30 minutes to an hour before scheduled closing and I was turned away from being served. Why have you scheduled hours shown to close at 9pm if you close at 8? I don't see how that's fair to your customers. I saw about a dozen other people get turned away from being served at 8:30 in the parking lot when it clearly shows that they should be closing at 9.

Categories: Salad, Sandwiches, Vegetarian, Restaurants, Chicken Wings | Review: I ordered for the first time today and it was really good. We got lemon pepper wings, Rodeo parm wings and Sichuan thingy rub chicken tenders. It was a flavor experience for sure we all really liked everything but my favorite was the lemon pepper wings. They had good ranch too which is important there's nothing worse than good wings and terrible store bought tasting dip. I will order again.

Categories: Tapas Bars, Nightlife, Salad, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Wine Bars, Cafes, Pizza, American (New) | Review: My hubby and I come here almost every anniversary and other occasions. Every time, we get the same server who has been there for years! Aside from the great service and wine selection, the food is bomb. We love the cauliflower, the sliders, the calamari, and flat bread...basically everything. Plus it's within walking distance of State Street for a lovely after-date stroll.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Wish I had taken a minute to check yelp before spending over $21 for three single scoops of ice cream! If it had at least been good ice cream, I could have understood the premium price. But, it was so bad (no flavor and dry, gritty texture) that we ended up tossing it in the trash after a few bites. What a waste.

Categories: Venues & Event Spaces, Cafes, Wine Bars, Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: So glad to see that this longtime and legendary restaurant has risen from the dead.  It's divided into a formal dining room and a less formal cafe area.  

Because we were celebrating a special occasion, we were seated in the private events dining area and were allowed to select our own music on the soundsystem (even though other diners were in the same room).

The food was excellent all-around and extremely reasonably priced.  The drinks were excellent, too.  But the standout was the service which was gracious, professional and beyond friendly.

Highest recommendation for the cafe and the great servers who work there.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: We had dinner here during our visit to Santa Barbara. There was a 15 min wait even though we went late. Great ambience. Plus they had inexpensive Margaritas, which I loved apart from the food. Portions were huge and overall everything we tried was delicious.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: If you want to go to the only bar in SB that has a cover and sit outside with drunk people smoking while sipping on your expensive mixed drink or dancing to extremely loud music, then this is the place for you. If you are over the age of 25, I would think twice unless you are looking for "hot singles in your area."

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Jazz & Blues, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: What a great little find while staying in Goleta. Old school speakeasy feel. Great drinks, cool vibe, friendly people.  What more can you ask for?

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: Nice fresh sandwiches. Cookies were still hot. I would've given 4 stars but as a previous reviewer mentioned...they are pricey. I would recommend this place over subway which is right next door. So if you want fresh tasty food eat here. Plus employees were super nice and patient while it took me a few minutes to order.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Seafood, Steakhouses | Review: The atmosphere here cannot be topped. The ultimate romantic date night with impressive scenery. Very accommodating for celiacs/ gluten-free options, such as the dark chocolate soufflé (absolutely divine) for dessert and the famous tortilla soup. We both ordered the Steak Diane Table-side option for our main entree and were not disappointed. The waitress was unable to bring it directly table side due to COVID-19 restrictions, but she did the best she could to cook it where we could still see and experience it. Everything we ordered was amazing. The wine list is extensive but the staff gives great recommendations to help narrow it down for you. 

10/10 would recommend to a friend.

Categories: Restaurants, Barbeque, Food, Caterers, Event Planning & Services | Review: I actually ended up not using this caterer, but I certainly would in the future. They were very friendly, attentive and flexible and the food we tasted was absolutely excellent. 

We were in contract with another caterer in town, that was unfortunately not able to meet our needs. We tried Santa Barbara BBQ and one other caterer and were very impressed by both. Due to no fault of their own it just made more sense for us to go with the other caterer. It was a very difficult decision and I still wonder if we chose the right one. Our event is in 2 weeks. 

In any case, if you are considering SBBBQ for your event, go in and talk with them and have them do a tasting menu for you. You will not be disappointed with the food or the people.

Categories: Caribbean, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Cajun/Creole, Vegan | Review: Stopped by here with my girlfriend about 2 weeks ago.  We had a couple drinks and some appetizers. I cant remember the servers name but she was very friendly and attentive and she was the only one there. We had the roasted curry cauliflower and the fried plantains with guava reduction, both were very good. Definitely gonna stop by again soon.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: What a lovely dinner!   Elegant yet comfortable place.  And the food was just delicious.  We had the special artichoke and burrata appetizer that was yummy followed by the most wonderful veal.   We needed with a decadent chocolate and banana pudding with warm center.  Absolutely wonderful!   We will be back.

Categories: Asian Fusion, Chinese, Restaurants | Review: after reading the few reviewS, we decided to try this place. we ordered from their Chinese menu and ask the Waiter which  dishes he recommended. We ordered a tofu fish Was very good. The other dishes was also excellent. I recommend Mama Lu on the chinese menu.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: The food is EXCELLENT!!! Super clean inside and out.  They have a beautiful outdoor patio and the music is entertaining!  It's frequented by locals, celebrities and tourists.  People from France, Mexico - all over come here for the food.  Great customer service.  We always refer this place to friends and family.

Categories: Bars, Mediterranean, Restaurants, Nightlife, Moroccan, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Italian | Review: Loved everything about this restaurant-- great cocktails and appetizers,  delicious pappardelle with veal ragout, yummy pana cotta for desert.  Nice setting too!

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Food Delivery Services, Caterers, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Wedding Planning, Nightlife, Food, Event Planning & Services | Review: I can't say enough good things about the Rincon Beach Club and the staff at Events by Rincon. Every time I work an event that Events by Rincon has anything to do with the end result is always a successful event with satisfied customers. The owners of this business, Marc & Carol, are two of the most humble humans I have met, they have clearly poured their entire life savings & hard work into creating this amazing wedding event service. It is a must that you go with them when in need of catering and or a beautiful venue.

Categories: Food, Grocery | Review: Great selection of meats, vegetables, dairy and snacks. I really love that Ralph's is open late. I usually come late at night, because during the day, the drivers around the complex are very irritating.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breweries, American (Traditional), Brewpubs | Review: Absolutely the best spot to come . The are so kind and  considerate. We miscounted the amount of people  we made a reservation for (our mistake don't do it!) but things happen to people and they are prepared. The owner should be proud as the staff handled it perfectly . Gratuity shall be served as these people and this place is my favorite in the area hands down. Cheers to those who see this and much appreciation and love given !

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: 4 starts for location. 1 star for service. Went to the bar, bunch of kids having fun. Bartender Jim and a brunette girl, work hard at not making eye contact, even after consistently tipping them above 20% for drinks.  Whatever, the location is worth it in and of itself, even if the bar staff think they are celebrities.  It's always going to be packed because of the terrific location.

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Nightlife | Review: Spent 45mins here to wat h the Kentucky derby. Not one waiter walked up to ask what we wanted to drink and several tables in my sight never got visited either. There was waiters just not really doing their job at all. I considered walking up to the bar to get drinks and food ordered but noticed everyone else doing the same thing and it looked hard to access a bartender from what the other people were dealing with that I didn't bother trying. I'll go elsewhere to get a drink since there's plenty of places within walking distance

Categories: Food, Mexican, Restaurants, Bakeries | Review: I'm a Soup person, & their Caldo de Albóndigas (Meatball soup) perfect has a nice chipotle kick, & their handmade tortillas are delicious .Definitely hits the spot and their portions are for 2, soup anyways.

Categories: Cafes, Restaurants, Sandwiches, Chicken Shop, Salad, American (New), Burgers, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Don't come here for the after 3pm $6 burger deal. The burger was overcooked, dry and tasteless. If I had more ketchup, it may have tasted better. The beer was nice though.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: Asian fusion food
Small plates for sharing 
Nice service 
Signature wings n chicken bao were pretty amazing!
Drinks menu has asian liquors

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Seafood, French | Review: My partner and I travel to Santa Barbara each year from San Francisco.  We love the water, the walks, the lovely evenings.  We have found, however, that the restaurants, while providing generally good service, offer largely unimpressive food -- certainly edible though not often memorable.  I am pleased to report that Julienne stands out as the grand exception.  We had a reservation on our first night and so enjoyed dinner that we returned on our last.  I had "duck three ways" on the first night and goat the next time.  Both were hits, especially the goat.  The wine list, while not extensive, is well varied and I appreciated that our server did not try to sell us up on a more expensive wine.  Instead, she reviewed our dinner choices and suggested a reasonable option.  The vibe is definitely laid back and the restaurant draws an interesting cross section of guests.  By all means go to Julienne.

Categories: Restaurants, Barbeque | Review: Walked in around 4:30 today with a friend to ask to use the bathroom, since we were waiting for a wedding to start next door.  A man greeted us inside. We asked to use the bathroom.. He said no, we had to purchase food.  I asked if we could sit on the outside tables and buy drinks. He said no.. We had to buy food!!  Couldn't believe it!!  He lost my business and several other people since they were also going to buy some drinks  there.. Before the wedding started..   We went to another restaurants near by and they were very nice and let us use their bathroom and we all gave them our business!!!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Mexican, American (Traditional) | Review: Bottom Line:  Best breakfast in Old Town?  You decide.  Lemme email you a burrito....

      Due to a disagreement with my toddler at home, he went ahead and had a bowel movement during the period in time when I normally eat breakfast.  This was unfortunate as I typically enjoy eating food before going to work.
       So I did the good guy thing and changed the diaper instead of playing it off to my wife "..what do you mean he pooped....?" *door slams and screeching tires*

Anyways, I called the good people of Domingos and asked them if they did order for pickup and not only did they take my order but they blind-walked me through the menu as I had no idea what they even sold for breakfast.
    they didnt care - they basically just said 'what do you want - doenst matter what its called, lets get this burrito thing done for you'

So I ordered and when I got there, everyone was super nice, loaded me down with Salsa, told me to have a good day etc.

The burrito was great, salsa was great.... everything was great.

This place is seriously flirting with 5 stars!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: I love their combo burritos Soo Much! Yes they are a little over priced but I have to treat myself sometimes. But they get 2 1/2- 3 stars because they Always get something wrong. I always ask for no beans and somehow they always mess that up. Tip - always check your order!

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Totally loved this new Mexcal style restaurant located below the upscale Indigo Hotel. Terrace seating which accommodated our group plus 1 canine. Passillo stuffed peppers grilled to perfection. Ahi ceviche presented and combined beautifully. The mole enchilada favoloso! stiff drinks get the Mexican mule...strong bite! of course the sea breeze from the coast

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: Amazing flavors, and unique dishes that are superb!!! Definitely one of my favorite places in Santa Barbara. Service is great, as is the interior design!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Great!fresh delicious food,water with limes or oranges,nice seating !we ordered marinated halibut in lime juice,tortilla chips,chicken enchilada with rice and beans.everything was fresh And very tasty!! Highly recommend!!!!!

Categories: Szechuan, Chinese, Nightlife, Karaoke, Asian Fusion, Restaurants | Review: What a shame, but another failed restaurant in the making. Service was good and friendly. Some of the dishes were very good. We had:

Bok Choy with King Mushrooms- Excellent, off to a good start.

General Tsao chicken-tasty chicken nuggets in a sweet sauce, batting 2 for 2 so far.

Calamari fried in chili-A disaster. It was chili all right, the kind we use with beans and hamburger. Whole garlic cloves. Simply awful.

Moo shu chicken-the filling was good, but no pancakes. 

Napkins were paper towels. No lunch menu. No serving spoons.  Expensive.

 On the plus side, water glasses are beautiful, plenty of parking and the restrooms were spotless.
 
Sue, we miss you.
Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Basically the perfect thing for your trip to the pier. It's rich and smooth and the scoops are pretty hefty. Chocolate chip cookie dough!!

Categories: Wineries, Food, Wine Tasting Room, Arts & Entertainment | Review: This is a quaint tasting room in the El Paseo courtyard and is a great place to go for an easy, quiet glass of wine.  My understanding is that Barden is another label in the Margerum wine family and I have especially enjoyed their Fonte.  I've been a couple times with friends and it is very pleasant with a great line up of wines.

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese, Szechuan, Soup, Seafood | Review: A tidy little resturant with nice decor.  We ordered from the lunch menu, Tofu and string beans.  The dishes come in a Bento box, very nicely laid out.   The food was fresh and tasty.   

Very friendly staff.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Pro: Outside seating, good food, friendly service, right on the beach.

Con: Parking - it's Santa Barbara. 

Stop by SB to visit a friend on a trip up to San Luis Obispo and was led to Sambo's by her when we wanted breakfast food. Sure it is not around the downtown area, but the beach front view is a good break from any indoor eatery. 

Try their omelets, one of the best I ever had.

Categories: Breweries, Restaurants, Sports Bars, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Food, Bars | Review: Stopped in for some great beer and good pizza and garlic fries... Got the great beer but fries we not great at all. Not too much flavor and I sure didn't taste the garlic. I ordered the veggie pizza with light sauce and I got light cheese as well :( when I asked the server if they put light cheese too she stated that I could see the receipt and it only said light sauce instead of asking me what was wrong with the pizza or If there was anything she could do. Beer was good that is why I gave two stars instead of one. Sorry SBC

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Lounges, Seafood, Spanish, Italian, Nightlife, Mediterranean | Review: My Goddaughter got married at The historical Santa Barbara Courthouse and for the reception, her new husband and she hosted the reception at Cadiz, We had four entree options to choose from and all three dishes had generous portions and exceptionally delicious. We had a large party, probably around 45 and the service was really good too. 

First Course was A Romano Caesar Salad, then for the main course either Seared Salmon, Steak with Papas Bravas, Pesto Fettuccine with Shrmp or Margarita Flatbread . I sampled three of the four and they were delicious.

Next time I am in Santa Barbara, I will definitely go back and order off the regular menu. I recommend staying at The Montecito Inn 5 minutes away.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Understated elegance.  It's a classy beautiful restaurant that is still relaxed, friendly and chill.  The food is creative and delicious.  The service is great - accommodating, friendly and fun.  Great drinks, great food, great place!

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: I've seen these types of poke establishments popping up all over the world. I love the concept and super happy it's finally in Goleta. The fish was very fresh, as was the ingredients. Definitely loved the meal. For sure. Super friendly people working there too! It's probably a 4 to 4.5 star place, but totally willing to bump them up to the 5, as this is a gem to have in Goleta. Seriously.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional) | Review: I called them and ask if they can take 17 people.  They asked me when, and I said now.  They said yes and had everything set up for us when we got there in 5 minutes.  The service was great and the food was great.  The hugo's omelet taste amazing, the place definitely deserves more recognition.  The owners are also really nice.  Best experience I've had in a while.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches, Bars, Steakhouses, Delis, Nightlife, Burgers, Cafes, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: My favorite BLT sandwich in Santa Barbara can be found here at Jill's Place, also known as Shalhoob's back in the day. The bread is usually day fresh and soft, the bacon is thick, crispy and plentiful, and the L and the T just right. Add some avocado if you wish, I do. Perfection.

Elsewhere on the menu, the burger is one of the best in town, and it should be because Shalhoob's butchers goes back years and years. Also I recommend the Southwest Chicken sandwich, with its chipotle mayo and grilled peppers and onions.

I know Jill's place more as a lunch stop, but the dinners are fine as well. I just haven't had anything past their burgers for dinner to speak on any authority.

So: highly recommended!

Categories: Cafes, Diners, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I've been to the other Rose Cafe in SB, which is delicious, but this Rose has a homey feeling to it, with superb food. The staff is very kind and welcoming to the Cafe, and the chef himself comes out to see how the food is, and told a joke out loud to the entire cafe. Very good experience here! Order the chilaquilles!

Categories: Bakeries, Sandwiches, Food, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: The food and service was great! Salmon toast did not disappoint and the BLTE was amazing as well. The Bloody Mary was knock out. This definitely is a place we will go to again!

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: This place is a gem.  Off the beaten path with views of the SB courthouse.  Very nice.  We only had the appetite for the Bao.  It was amazing and did not disappoint.  I wish I had a bigger appetite when we went because I would've looked at the entire menu and said yes.   Awesome place.  Super good, period.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Was visiting the area tried this place out with the hobby! Horrible. I can't even say the salsa was good! It tasted like pace picante sauce! I mean I know everyone has different taste but the chili verde I read was in red sauce so i was expecting that but the meat was super tough and hard could tap my fork on it! Not good! No spice but hey not everyone likes spice so that's just me the only think I ate was chips and my rice! Beans were not even good! It was all bad don't waste your time with the chili verde and don't expect much for salsa and ya might be ok

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: I've been here numerous times for bdays, social gatherings, and family gatherings. Oysters are very fresh, drinks are great, seafood entrees are amazing! Just found out they are also in Santa Monica!! Will definitely check it out :) Service is quick and staff very friendly.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: One of the worst dining experiences I have had.  We had reservations for 8pm,  we didn't get seated until 9:45. Once we finally did get seated, I will say that our waitress was wonderful.  She was smiling and lovely and literally the only reason this restaurant gets two stars.  The food was beyond sub-par, I got a chicken burrito and the chicken was so burnt it overwhelmed the taste of everything. In addition,  the dish didn't come with rice or beans. If I'm spending $15 on a mediocre burrito, at least give some rice and beans.  My $12 drink was so much mixer I was questioning if it contained alcohol at all. 
I should've gone to Taco Bell,  would've gotten the same quality of food for a lot cheaper.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: After our horseback ridding trip in Goleta we decided to head to into Downtown Santa Barbara for a late lunch. After reading some positive reviews we decided to try Carlitos.

Staff: They are very welcoming and friendly, they were very helpful when asking for suggestions. I had the carne asada plate with a red enchilada, beans and rice. The meat was grilled to perfection and the green sauce that comes on the plate was fabulous. Beans were great and the rice was perfect. The homemade corn tortillas the best. I highly recommend this place, the drinks are great as well. The outdoor patio is great. Great place all around!

Categories: Nightlife, Soul Food, Restaurants, Comfort Food, Local Flavor | Review: OK, now I don't want to be a buzz kill, but really everyone? I am at a loss for all the great reviews. My husband and I came by excited to try based on all the great reviews - what? It was essentially velveeta-like cheese with a bunch of random toppings. Aside from the fact it looks like...Let's just say unappealing...It simply was mediocre at best.  I applaud the effort, nice staff, innovative business model, straightforward menu and efficient use of space and resources, but that is all I can offer in way of compliments.  Perhaps the problem was that we were sober and past college-age? Not food snobs, but we were hungry and still gave our order to a homeless person a few bites in. Bright side: we lost our appetites, so in a round about way, we got what we paid for.

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis | Review: this is the only location ive been to:

if i'm in the area again, i always make a special trip here.  south coast is one of those places where you crave it when you're not in town...even though there are a gaziliion sandwich places on every corner.  and sandwiches are usually craved once a week, just like your sushi, pho, steak, in and out, fish taco...etc... fixes.

their sandwiches are packed full of flavor, but theres something unique about this place that i can't pinpoint.  i don't know if its the family-owned feel, decor, customer service...tasty sandwiches but i can come here and eat by myself- whereas i can't go watch a movie by myself.

i always order the roast beef with horseradish cream cheese, red onions, avocado...and then i add swiss cheese and sprouts.  genius of a sandwich, yet so simple and accessible ingredients.

theres a reason why a family owned business has opened two more locations.  congrats and bravo!

Categories: American (New), Wraps, Chinese, Sushi Bars, Barbeque, Dim Sum, Asian Fusion, Ramen, Restaurants, Noodles, Soup, Japanese, Hot Pot | Review: Such bad food holy crap. Go here for the drinks you'll have a fun time. Good vibes. Do not eat the food though ew ew ew

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: Hands down, best experience from my SB trip.

Husband found this place for me since he knows how much i love trying coffee houses when I travel. I personally was hesitant at first, their drinks didn't sound too good and the price is steep. But I'm so grateful he convinced me! 

Ambiance - love how it's all really a big courtyard. There is so much seating available for small groups, couples and individuals.

Drinks - Husband got the burnout and I got the Jupiter signature drink. Wow! It's so beautiful, it was difficult to drink, at first. It was a wonderful combinations of coffee and flavor without being overly sweet. 

I would recommend this place to anyone traveling here.

Categories: Specialty Food, Food, Event Planning & Services, Personal Chefs, Food Delivery Services | Review: Hard to express, let alone contain, my love for Aspyn's amazing dishes. Her menu is adventurous not only in its locally-sourced ingredients but in her fine presentation.
You can taste her passion in every morsel.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: Reading previous reviews, I was expecting the food and drinks (bloody mary) to be amazing! I ordered the steak and eggs or should I say the rubber chewy dry steak!! My boyfriend had eggs and bacon which wasn't too bad but I mean how can you ruin scrambled eggs and bacon??? His dish came with a choice of banana nut bread, toast, or baguette.. sadly he chose the dry banana nut whatever that was... for a not so good brunch, it was overly priced!! Oh and I can't forget to add, the bloody mary was disgusting, you might as well order a cup of tomato juice!!!  I must say, I would have given them 5 stars based off the service!!  The girls are awesome and very friendly!

Categories: Caterers, Event Planning & Services, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts, Food | Review: I was walking a I saw this place, I am looked at there flavors and they had Nutella, which I love but is very hard to find. The Nutella flavor was so good. I also love that they have a lot if cool flavors. It was a bit pricey$ but overall very good ice cream. Who recommended.

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: Dive bar, cheap drinks, enough said. Chill. Videos on replay get a bit mesmerizing. That's not a good thing.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: Definitely a 5!

We went for breakfast - everything was amazing! The bloody mary's are not only delicious, they come with a crab claw in them that you can actually eat. I reccommend the dungenous crab cakes with avocado salsa and poached eggs - it is no doubt the best breakfast I've had all year. I can't wait to go back for lunch or dinner to try what else they have to offer!

Service? amazing
Prices? not bad for gourmet food
Seating, Decorations? great!
The food? awesome.

You should go!

Categories: Cajun/Creole, Breakfast & Brunch, Caterers, Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, Burgers | Review: I ordered the seafood gumbo and it was very bland, I added salt to give it flavor but it didn't improve and I couldn't finish it.  My husband ordered the fish & chips but the fish was greasy and also lacking flavor.  My in laws liked what they ordered which is the reason I gave it two stars but I personally regret having come here.

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: Great place to visit whether it be on a Monday afternoon or a Friday night! If you're looking for somewhere more quiet to eat, there may be better option. 
That being said...
My girlfriend won one of the weekly raffles, which entails by 1 get 1 free drinks. What we didn't know what we were getting was great service from attentive, friendly staff! Definitely worth a visit.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Nightlife, Bars | Review: Came in with my girlfriend to eat for my birthday. In the past, the service was kind of ehh but it's gotten better for sure because it seems like they hired a bunch of new people. We still had to wait a bit to be seated and to receive our food but our server (whose name I think was Andres) was HILARIOUS and really friendly. 
Pretty good enchiladas.However, the deep fried ice cream was SO delicious. I recommend seating out in the patio. 
I was kind of iffy about going to this place because of the reviews but I think the restaurant is to improve. I would definitely go again!

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Do-It-Yourself Food, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Bars | Review: Cool place to get a bite and a drink with the friendly locals.

Drinks can be stiff so bring a designated driver :)

Categories: American (Traditional), Vegan, Restaurants | Review: First of all , the fact that there's an upscale restaurant with an all vegan menu is something to celebrate in and of itself. This place gets full points on ambiance and service. Very friendly and hospitable staff ! The food is not too bad  but unfortunately not elevated. The crab cakes were well executed and quite tasty. The shishito peppers were extremely tangy - I get that there's lemon tamari in it , but it was far too pronounced in it's expression. The cauliflower was not the star in the kung pao cauliflower dish. And whoa, was it saucy ! Maybe it was on me to eat it last , but once it was on the plate , it pretty much imparted it's overpowering essence on every other dish we ate after . Maybe dial down the sauce so that there can still be harmony between all the dishes ordered? (And also spare the poor cauliflower from having an identity crisis ?) And the truffle potatoes were mediocre - nothing to write home about and not very " truffley ". The wild mushroom tostada was quite good , but not good enough to atone for the sins of the dishes that came before sadly ... Is this place worth a visit ? Yes absolutely but if you're looking for culinary artistry , you will be disappointed. I would want to rate them a 3.5 but since that's not an option here , I will have to go with a 3. I will end on the note that they did show promise and I sincerely wish them the best and hope they can attain the superlative level of excellence that's still rare for plant based cuisine.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I don't get this. It shows as one dollar sign $ but I felt what we got was quite expensive for what it was:
- A plate of mole, without very much rice nor beans
- A sope de nopal, granted it was double sized, but nothing else, no sides.
- A small papaya water
$29. $29!!! And many plates were $14. And you order at a counter. And it's a total hole in the wall (nice enough but not deluxe). Jeez, maybe I'm too used to L.A. prices? 
My friend said the sope was good. The mole was OK, I've had better, the rice somewhat dry, the beans typical refried probably with lard and nothing special. The food was quick, we went there due to running close to concert time and long lines at other places. It wasn't bad but no reason to go back especially at those prices.

Categories: French, Cafes, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Sandwiches, Bakeries | Review: Ok, so I just moved to SB, and am wondering why there are so few reviews here!? (this is why I set up my account). 

Anyway, wow, this place was delicious. Went yesterday right before closing, so their selection wasn't the best. But I wasn't disappointed at all. I had the apple brioche. My dad had the lemon tart, and mom had the choco eclair. The lemon tart has to be the best I have ever had. I can't wait to try everything else they offer.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: This place was a fantastic all around experience. Stopped in for lunch after a 45 minute wait; but that's ok, it happens. Our server was very friendly and fun and reflected the great atmosphere of the restaurant. The crash of the waves and stunning view was impeccable. 

Now to the food: fantastic. Fresh salads, healthy club sandwiches, a nice array of drinks as well. There's not much else to say except that I wish I knew about this place way earlier.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: Love this place, one of my fav brunch spots in sb!  The french toast is amazing, as is everything else!  Although I haven't tried them, the bloody mary's look unreal, coming complete with a crab claw--definitely will return soon to give one a try!

Categories: Pakistani, Restaurants, Buffets, Seafood, Halal, Indian, Vegetarian, Vegan, Food, Salad, Desserts | Review: I'm really not sure why none of the India food places in the Bay Area or even ones I've visited in India can beat Flavor of India in SB.  I mean, SB is not very diverse.  There are a lot of Indians in the Bay Area and of course in India but I just haven't found a winner..except for F of I.

My (ex) roommate and I used to hit this place up minimum twice a week.  Major plus: they deliver.  My regular was the Chicken Korma but the lamb dishes are excellent.  Ask for chef's choice of the moment for the lamb.  Chicken Korma is rich and flavorful..but my tummy didn't hurt after eating it unlike most creamy things.  I really wish I could order some right now. MMM.

Categories: Cajun/Creole, Breakfast & Brunch, Caterers, Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, Burgers | Review: I have eaten at Cajun Kitchen for 30 years!
It is always delicious today I had the Huevos Rancheros. 
It was awesome very tasty and very filling!!!

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches, Bars, Steakhouses, Delis, Nightlife, Burgers, Cafes, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: My new favorite place in town. Friendly service, reasonable prices, incredible food. We just got pick up from Jill's and I am beyond impressed. It's debate night, I'm TIRED, and just didn't want to cook, but needed something comforting- it's debate night (again) after all. Best fried chicken in SB, the jalapeño poppers are delish, and the steak bites- yummmmmm. I wish we would have ordered dessert- our only regret with this experience. I was so impressed that I even called them to let them know how much I loved our meal.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breweries, American (Traditional), Brewpubs | Review: I had the jerked chicken sandwich and it was good.  they put some pickled veggies on it that didn't really go well with the flavors.  I ate it separately and it was much better.  I still won't order a burger from here though...

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Cafes, Coffee Roasteries, Coffee & Tea | Review: I was staying nearby and saw a sign for this place. I turned to Yelp for a review and more info. I don't normally like "stuff" in my coffee, but the fact that they make their own vanilla syrup and don't load up their vanilla latte with syrupy sweetness had me intrigued. I ordered online and it was ready when promised. I picked it up along with a hot chocolate for my wife. My coffee was excellent and she liked her hot chocolate -- it got a, "it was really good" review from her. 

If you don't like sweet, maybe go with a regular latte, but if you want something more than a regular latte, and still want to taste the beans, the vanilla is a solid choice.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches | Review: I came in today and got an XL Presidio - pastrami on a hoagie roll. The pastrami was very tasty and tender because it was sliced very thin. Nice, but was it really a $12.99 sandwich? $15 with tip, and I took it "to go" or I would have said something at the time.

I'm originally from NY, so maybe my standards are unreasonably high for a sandwich, but I was just at Canters on Fairfax, and IMO they did a much better job, and piled on TWICE as much pastrami for the same money. 

The guy working there said they had been busy making 400 sandwiches for an order. I got there a little after 11am, so maybe they went stingy on mine to try and make sure they had enough for the rest of the day? I'd like to give them the benefit of the doubt in some way that might excuse a sandwich billed as "XL" being stingy on the meat. 

The pickles were good, with my preference to the flavor of the spears over the sliced ones, although both were good.

I may give them another try at some point, but there are other places in town yet to try and based on the previous Yelper reviews, they really let me down.

Categories: Bars, Tapas Bars, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Speakeasies | Review: Really good spot. Very low key and the drinks are great. Very fun and everyone is very nice and have a a cool spot in the back.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Popped in here for quick margaritas whilst waiting for a very delayed train....  and we were pleasantly surprised!  A great find!  The margaritas tasted very fresh.  Not sure if they make their own mix but it sure tasted as if they did!  Perfectly refreshing on a very hot day.  And the fresh, homemade chips came with three different delicious salsas.  Will definitely be back to try the food.  Could see dishes as they were coming out of the kitchen and each one looked amazing!  Also, the service was perfect - drinks came quickly along with the chips/salsa.
Categories: Pizza, Sandwiches, Chicken Wings, Restaurants | Review: Love the pizza lots it topping . Great service.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Venues & Event Spaces, Peruvian, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Nightlife, Latin American, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, Mexican | Review: Visited Cielito with my family. I had the market enchiladas. My food was fantastic. My husband had the Filet which was over cooked but also delicious. Service was ok, everyone was very nice just a little slow. We all had margaritas and they were also delicious! Prices were a bit high considering the portions were pretty small. Overall good experience!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: Excellent breakfast Plenty of choices
love Peet's coffee. 
Amazing crab cakes corn bread and green fried tomatoes, and much more
Lunch available
service was just right

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Tiki Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: This place is A-OK. 
My issues are with the extremely dim lighting, uncomfortable bar stools, disgusting bathroom and overwhelmed bartenders. the bartenders themselves though have a cocktails and dreams kind of dynamic and relationship which is fun and I think if you went during an off time or were a local, would be even more enjoyable. I was extremely impressed with their cocktails. They were very well crafted and flavorful. I must admit they're some of the best I've had but they do take a really long time to be made. Also it took a while for my husband to get a water... There were two gents working the bar and the place wasn't crazy busy but it was during the Solstice Parade weekend.  

When we went, the light from outside was blasting through the front area and so when we were in the back by the bar where there are now sources of natural light, my eyes couldn't adjust. Granted, I'm light sensitive so this was more jarring for me than most people I think. It was just so bright up front and so dark in the back, it was a glaring distraction to my right eye and made the back seem all the darker because my eyes couldn't adjust. I wish they'd had progressive lighting or just more lighting in the back so while you are sitting in the back, it's not so noticeable. Anyway, moving on....

The bar stools. They are really cute so points for that, they definitely fit the scene but a failure for whoever designed this space, aesthetics aren't the only important factor for furniture, it needs to be functional too and these are not. It was partially the guy next to me and partially the small seats, but as a result he would not stop bumping into me. He initially kept apologizing but then just continued regardless. It was driving me nuts so we moved to a table. It took away from the experience because I felt so cramped. The problem is they are just really small seats without a lot of space around them . There's also limited amounts of them at the bar so you feel really close to your neighbors. The table was a lot better. 

The bathroom... the bathroom was so disgusting I had to drop off a star. I'm not sure if it was just the day I went but it's one bathroom for the place and it wreaked so strongly of mildew, it was overpowering. I was hoping the employees had a separate sink behind the bar they were using because it was gross in there. Gas station bathroom gross.  

Much like Danah, I feel conflicted giving 3 stars. I wish I could give a .5 more. I'm sure this is locally owned and without a doubt, the drinks are fantastic but maybe it's more of a 20-something spot... I definitely think it's worth a try but not sure if I'll be back.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: We came here for brunch on a Saturday and waited only 30 mins which was great! The staff was awesome and the people were very friendly who dined there. We ordered the mimosa flight and it was very GOOD! There was one with OJ, cranberry, peach and grapefruit and they were all so delicious. Our favorite was the peach! They also serve you little mini muffins while you wait for your food and they were hot and fresh. I ordered the corned beef hash benny and my boyfriend had the florentine omelette. Both were fantastic! The omelette was big and had fresh ingredients in it -- it was light and very tasty. The corned beef hash was to die for! I am a huge corned beef hash fan and this one was the BEST I ever had. The meat was juicy and there was so much of it! The eggs were perfectly poached and the hollandaise sauce married the whole meal together! I would for sure come back just for the corned beef hash. I am sad we don't live closer because this place is killing it with breakfast!

Categories: Asian Fusion, Poke, Seafood, Food, Restaurants | Review: Really didn't enjoy my meal from this place. Here are my reservations: too much rice, not enough protein, bland and flavorless sauces, the ceviche and guacamole looks and tastes store bought, and the imitation crab meat is extra heavy on the mayonnaise. The restaurant is a cool concept but it delivery is just a failure for me. I don't think the owners are passionate about the food they're serving. Don't eat here issa trap.

Categories: Mediterranean, Restaurants, Salad, Greek | Review: Great Mediterranean food! Free range meats, great vegetarian choices. Look at the menu on line, it can get 
confusing.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: OK, I love ice cream!  I am a Virgo aka super particular and analytical.  I hve tried almost every type of ice cream.  I have even tried gelato in Italy, icecream in Brazil, and home made ice cream!  So when I say this is the best ice cream ever, it is the best ice cream ever!
Also, I am very conscientious of my weight and try to eat extremely healthy.  I live frozen yogurt and have tried in the past to substitute for actual ice cream cravings but McConnell's real ice cream is worth having the real thing.  I would rather have one scoop 2 times a month then frozen yogurt 1 time per month!
Ok, now you know how good the ice cream is and so here are my recommendations.
Mint and Chip!  Mint and Chip!  Mint and Chip!  It is not too creamy, not too heavy, not too sweet.  It is refreshing but I get my chocolate fix in there too.    
Then there is the chocolate raspberry truffle, chocolate burnt almond (can you tell I am a chocoholic?), and cookies n cream!
Try your ice cream in a sugar cone.  Also, you can have 1/2 a scoop of a couple flavors.  One scoop is plenty for one person.
Oh and feel free to try the others as the staff will gladly help you become familiar with all of their flavors.
So here is my confession: I am addicted to McConnell's ice cream and I may go more than 2 times a month!
I scream, you scream, we all scream for icecream!

Categories: Restaurants, Food, American (New), Wine Bars, Nightlife, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Event Planning & Services | Review: Jane was amazing. The perfect place for a nice dinner in Santa Barbara. A friend reconnected it to us, and we couldn't have been more pleased. We ordered clams for an appetizer, and my sweetie and I both ordered entrees off the specials menu--I ordered the lamb and he got the halibut. We were too full for dessert, which was disappointing because other reviewers said the coconut cake is great. We will have to go back for dessert and to try other entrees, too!

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: I had the much acclaimed pumpkin curry with chicken, a spicy green curry with big chunks of red peppers, potatoes, pumpkin, and basil. It was delicious. I also got to try a little of each of my friend's dishes: Vegetable Pad Thai, Poorman's Noodles, and Tofu Red Curry. All were delicious. I'm from Philadelphia where we have our choice of several amazing Thai restaurants. Zen Yai easily compares to the best in Philly.

Categories: Noodles, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Creperies, Chinese, Asian Fusion | Review: First of all, the reviews here are manipulated. There's a sign in front that says give us a 5 star Yelp review and we'll give you a half off pint. 
Very expensive for what it is, and very low quality food. We got the only veg option, a noodle soup, and it was served boiling hot so we had to wait about 40 min to be able to eat it without burning ourselves. All of the vegetables looked and tasted like they were bought frozen, little flavor and weird texture. There was no actual cooking involved to make it, just frozen vegetables thrown into broth with noodles. Not worth the $20 when you can get cheaper and higher quality food and many places nearby

Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: Was told it would be 50 minutes to pickup my order been waiting outside for 45 minutes and still no to go order. This better be the best Thai food ever. Must only be one chef for 75 people

Categories: Food, Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood, Desserts | Review: Ordered online which was super easy to pick up quick on way to LA.  Service was exceptional  and the breakfast burritos were very tasty.  We ordered the "small" breakfast burritos and they were SO big!

Categories: Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Food | Review: Iced coffee with sugar free vanilla syrup and a heathly dallop of heavy whippng cream.

win.

Categories: Tex-Mex, Restaurants, Vegetarian, Sandwiches | Review: Would be 4 stars, except all the girls working here are way rude - I don't think I've ever gotten so many simultaneous dirty looks in my life. My salad was also served in a wooden bowl that was missing two huge chunks around the edges. Everyone else says "very friendly service" though, so maybe it's me?

But hey, at least the food is awesome!! We were happy to find healthy, delicious vegetarian food on the road. Nice selection of dressings & the jicama in the botanical salad is great. My travel companion got the veggie quesadilla which he enjoyed. We also liked the bulletin board, advertising a show we had just seen back home in NYC. We'd come back next time we're in the area but please, ladies - smile!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: This tiny little restaurant overlooking the harbor is definitely worth a visit!  The service was great and the food was even better.  We all had the clam chowder which was amazing.  I had the salmon salad which was one of the best I've had!

Categories: Fast Food, Food, Acai Bowls, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants | Review: i'm weird and got the spirutein? coffee shake. Dont know what the hell i was thinking. The weirdest taste i've ever had. But it definetly had the beginnings of an awesome shake. Its obvious they know how to blend. haha. but seriously i hate when i get a shake and i get back this clumpy ice-y thing. I mean seriously blend the freakin shake already. you know. So the consistency of the shake was fab. and i would def try something else.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: I really liked this spot and the drink I had, a gin concoction - was EXCELLENT. The food and the service were good.

Very much reminds me of a spot I would have gone to in college, the idea is to share. We had 3 or so dishes and none were memorable. The age/food quality/energy is on the college side. Location is great and the ambiance is cute. Great place to go get drinks.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: So we can up to Santa Barbara to run an errand, and since there's nothing else to do there.... We decided to eat. Seafood was out of the question because of the oil spill from the past week, so we decided to eat Mexican (figured it was the easiest thing and you can't mess up on Mexican food.....WRONG!) we found this place on Yelp and it had over 800 reviews with 4 stars.... Looks legit, right? WRONG again! Those stars would only apply to people that have never tried Mexican food before or have no taste buds. 

First off, the parking was horrible. Adjacent street is not SUV friendly and there's a long line, idk why... Cause the food SUCKS. 

We ordered two items off the "specials"-the vegetable tamale and the green chili, asada quesadilla, and horchata. Everything came out to be $21(way too expensive for Mexican food)
Vegetable tamale: worst tamale ever had. Very underwhelming and over rated. Definitely does not live up to the hype of the reviews 
Green chili: very bland and tiny AF. In any other Mexican restaurant, these plates come with rice and beans, this did not. As pictured, it looked like a sad green chili in a box. The chili had no natural flavor to it. They didn't grill it right. 

Lastly, the wait does not justify how horrible their food is. On one good note, my daughter did enjoy her asada quesadilla, however if they failed to succeed at making a quesadilla, they really should reconsider changing their cuisine. Take it from a Mexican food lover, avoid this place, just go eat seafood, and if that's unavailable, just go home.

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), French, Restaurants | Review: I had reservations here for over a month and when I arrived they were closed! Waste of my time ruined my bday dinner!! Poor management

Categories: Food, Desserts, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: Great service, open for takeout during covid. Ice cream was pretty good, great texture, the mint flavor wasn't very strong. Pretty expensive for what you get.

Categories: Restaurants, Soup, Japanese, Sushi Bars | Review: I miss their old location, it was much more personal. The food here is still good, but the service is very slow and on a busy night you will wait a long time for your rolls..

Categories: Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis | Review: Love this place. Not only is the food really good, and honestly you can find anything between hot sandwiches, cold ones, salads, small meal/big meal.  If you're really hungry go for the Chicken Chipotle Club-  it's a three-layer sandwich that is amazing-just be prepared to save half for later. Really though, the food is good but the people really make the place. The staff is young, friendly and energetic and they are really efficient in getting my order out quickly as I am mostly there on my lunch break for work.
Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Always a favorite when we take our yearly trip to Santa Barbara! Excellent food and service.

Categories: Food, American (New), Thai, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Desserts, Cafes, Sandwiches, Caterers, Event Planning & Services | Review: Just finished the Nom Tac Burrito. It was delicious. I love the vibe of this place. It's going to be one of my go to places in Santa Barbara.

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: This place might have a really good happy hour but the regular seafood dinner I had was disappointing. The lobster bisque and the clam chowder that a lot of people seem to like were just average to me. I have had much better ones at many other places. 

The crab cake wasn't good at all. The crust was too hard and the filling was lacking flavors. The live Maine lobster was nice but not really worth it. It was fresh but not particularity tasty. Could really be cooked better than that.

The drinks were good though. I enjoyed my Mai Tai. It was strong.

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: We were pretty disappointed at wasting our time and money here. On our trip to SB for Christmas, we stopped and had a drink on Christmas Day before our later reservations at Finch and Fork (excellent btw). 

This restaurant doesn't deserve to be in SB! The place is a sticky disgusting mess. Every empty table was waiting to be bussed from the moment we walked in and sat down- to over 30 min later. The bar staff and waitress in the front weren't THAT busy they couldn't clean the tables. We ordered a house frozen marg and a cucumber jalapeño on the rocks. Cucumber jalapeño was good --burns a lil at first! However the house margarita, (which if it's your house beverage it should be perfected, no?)--was awful! No alcohol. Flavorless. It was just eating blended ice with some lime thrown in. Gross. We have both worked in the restaurant industry for several years and hate complaining, but we had to send it back! Waitress didn't apologize or anything but curtly brought us another cucumber jalapeño. Ok, fine don't say sorry but thanks for the replacement...

We were ready to go and give the gal the card to pay the check. It took forever. Update your system guys; it's not that hard! It's 2014. I didn't read the check till after we were charged (my bad sorry) but my drinks were overcharged by $1 each? What the...??? I read it and the menu says the cuc jal Marg is $8.25. The drink is charged for $9 each, and then an extra .25 for "HH" which usually means happy hour. Did I really just get charged .25 extra for each margarita at happy hour? Absolutely disgusting. 

Just please Don't go here.

Categories: Steakhouses, Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: Let's talk sushi! I personally love the Mexican Roll. 1/2 price sushi is ALL DAY Sun-Thurs and "All-you-can-eat" sushi @ the bar ONLY is ALL day Friday & Saturday. If you want Hibachi (the tables where they cook for you)  I would HIGHLY recommend calling in and making reservations ahead of time to ensure your seats; it gets pretty busy. And unfortunately they don't take Sushi bar reservations because there's only what? 26 seats? I mean if your dumb ass reserves 8 seats and you don't come in on time the restaurant looses money by holding your seats for someone who isn't there when there's people that could be sat =( So it makes sense that it's a "first come- first serve" policy. Anyways, the filet mignon is to die for @ the Hibachi grill and if you're looking for more bang for the buck try the Dinner Box or the Lunch Box at the sushi bar. It's orgasmic. OH, and don't forget dessert. If you've never had a tempura ice cream you need to try it! DEEP FRIED YUMMINESS. By the way, if you're 21 and up, don't forget to Sake-Bomb it. :) And if you're not into beer like me, try the plum wine. Absolutely love it.

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: Hummus, baba ghannouj, falafel, and tabbouli right by the warm fire. And don't forget to try one of their signature cocktails! I got a pomegranate margarita they also offer blankets during the winter!

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Breakfast & Brunch, Seafood, American (New) | Review: We took advantage of happy hour. Bar is very clean, good music, and as Hockey fans, we were pleased to see the Playoffs on the big screen. Service was excellent (Nicole was a pleasure) and the drinks were A+. We were on a road trip and we had our share of bad drinks along the way.  It's nice when someone actually knows the right ingredients and mixes them perfectly. Thanks for making our stop in Santa Barbara that much better, Nicole.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: God, I love Brophy's.

if you're going for dinner any night of the week, get there EARLY. they're usually packed by 5:30. Best views, great seafood, & the bar is fun. plus you really can't beat the view. 

The service is always crazy fast, they definitely want you in & out because it gets so busy! Come here for drinks or a cup of clam chowder & some seafood.

Also, the salmon salad with whatever dressing they use is out of this world.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Loved everything about this place, Food was delicious, location was beautiful (beachfront view)and the staff was very nice. 

And the cherry on top was that you can order drinks from the bar and take them out to the beach while you wait for your table !

Categories: Wineries, Food, Breweries, Nightlife, Bars, Arts & Entertainment, Champagne Bars, Pubs, American (New), Brewpubs, Restaurants | Review: This is a great place!!! Awesome menu!  Amazing potato pancakes!!! If I go to the funk zone- I must stop here!!  

Love the burger!  Love the service!  Great ipa!  Great service- fun atmosphere.  Nice patio.

Categories: Lounges, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants, Hookah Bars, American (New) | Review: The atmosphere was great! We went for my friends bachelorette party. There were three other bachelorette parties at the same time on the outside patio. I wasn't that hungry so I just got the fried artichoke. It was hard to eat. The place has very small portions.

Categories: Szechuan, Chinese, Nightlife, Karaoke, Asian Fusion, Restaurants | Review: Authentic Szechuan food , had mapo tofu , it was so good! Highly recommend! They also have this cool little robot

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Food was excellent. Very friendly staff,  they gave us free carnitas to sample.  The "rajas de queso" were superb. Also very good: pozole, chile verde burrito, and burrrito ahogada de adobada.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: This place is delicious I am a soup guy So this is the place for me. The service was amazing the ambience is gray and the soup was divine. I would go back in a heartbeat and I will every time I come to the area.

Categories: Organic Stores, Caterers, Grocery, Specialty Food, Event Planning & Services, Health Markets, Food | Review: Best customer service, beer, and food selection at the Buzz Hive inside of Whole Foods! Regular there and do my normal shopping in Whole Foods as well... you won't be disappointed!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Nightlife, Bars | Review: The Wife and I consider this place to be the Slowest El Torito in the Country. The place is filthy and humid all year long. Food is bad, too. I hate Taco Bell, but would go there rather than waste my time and health at this location.

Categories: Caterers, Restaurants, Spanish, Personal Chefs, Event Planning & Services | Review: A+++++++We were thoroughly impressed with the total experience. We have had many paellas and will put Chef Ben's up against any of them. The apps and desert were delicious as well. Everything was presented as if we were in a 5 star restaurant. Not only were we served but it was also nice not to have to clean up after. We will definitely hire Chef Ben next time we are back in SB. He truly went above and beyond expectations to make our night amazing. Best dinner of the trip!!! Thanks for everything.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Seafood, French | Review: Had the duck dumplings to start, with 6oz Top Sirloin for the main course. The duck dumplings were good, but I loved the sirloin. Melt in your mouth soft. Flavor was excellent. We went on a Saturday night memorial day weekend and it was fairly busy. A little on the expensive side, but I was a fan.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: A quaint, semi-upscale, casual restaurant with outdoor seating. They have an extensive menu, and the best time to go for the best prices would be happy hour, between 3 - 6pm. The food is relatively clean if you order something grilled. The service is a tad slow, because the servers are overworked and busy. I appreciate the hand sanitizer on every table, and along the corridor. They take germs and the pandemic seriously here. I like their wine and alcohol list, and it's a peaceful spot for conversation. Because the restaurant looks quaint, sits up on a hill away from the sidewalk and busy street, and there's little tables in different little spots, it feels kind of romantic. I would come here again for a peaceful and easy afternoon.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breweries, American (Traditional), Brewpubs | Review: First time here, and the wife and I are already looking forward to our next visit. Our server was fantastic, I only wish I remembered his name. He helped us select our drinks and his suggestions were perfect. My wife had the doner kebab and I had the burger, and both of us were very happy with our selections. We will be back!

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, American (New), Restaurants | Review: I come here with my gf every time she craves pickle popcorn on lunch break.  I think she's addicted.  The sandwiches here are always good and the service is great.  Kind and courteous staff. The Reuben iis my go to and the chipotle turkey club is my alternate.  Can't really go wrong at this place.  Little pricey but very tasty.
Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food | Review: Apparently, no more challah bread supply on Fridays (or at all) .. :(     Disappointing.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: It was just under average. The flavors of yogurt are not the best, there was only one non dairy option which seems like it would be more prevalent as fro-yo is often touted as a healthier alternative to ice cream? The toppings were not consistently stocked over the couple different occasions I had been. I wish that they had a pineapple flavor (dole whip).

Categories: Steakhouses, Burgers, Bars, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife | Review: The ambience is nice as well as the staff. But the food is terrible. We got the coconut prawns, baked Seabass and fillet mignon. They come with soup or salad and a side. Almost everything except the fillet was microwaved. The salad was old, The plates were not clean. Disappointing for any restaurant specially for a $$$ one. Anything would've been better than this dinner.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Great tacos of all varieties and fast friendly service.  The only set back is that they are closed on Tuesdays .......I guess business gets lop sided when they get the      taco-Tuesday" trend  , other than that I would give them five stars as far as tacos go

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: Great ice cream and frozen yogurt shop. Definitely try the horchata fro yo, cookies and cream, double peanut butter chip and their churro de leche ice cream!!!!All are good and great customer service!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Great place.  Good food and drinks, nice staff and a lovely outside patio area.  Good location at top of State Street.  Prices are very reasonable and portions are fairly generous.

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), French, Restaurants | Review: Best Happy Hour in SB/Montecito! Food and drinks are great. Favorite drink is the meyer lemon margarita(I think that's what it's called). The location is great and secluded. The greenhouse is so beautiful and my favorite place to be seated. Been here several times and never disappoints.
Categories: Food, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches | Review: Fun little cafe. Great Coffee, a lot of options. Good food options for quick bite.

Categories: Gelato, Food, Specialty Food, Desserts, Chocolatiers & Shops | Review: Absolute perfect place for gifting! 
The truffles and chocolates look so pretty ( some shaped like pineapple, buddhas, & red lips...some look painted) and they  taste divine. 
Charmingly decorated.  Excellent service.
Gelato is fabulous and rich. 
Very sweet spots to sit. 
I just wish they were open later in the evening sometimes.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Barbeque, Food, Sandwiches, Wine Bars, Diners, Bars, Specialty Food, American (Traditional), Nightlife | Review: Amazing food! Amazing atmosphere! Menu is small but they have perfected each of them wow 

I would skip the avocado. It was pricy for literally a grilled avocado. 
Highly highly recommend the especial. It was cooked to perfection with so many flavors coming together 

For the mains, the meats were all cooked amazingly and it was nice to have the pasta to mix up the palate!! 

Must come here! Call and get a reservation early

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: I wrote a review of Casa Blanca a few months ago...  As I recall they opened the Friday of Fiesta last year so this would be their one year anniversary!!!   Congratulations CBR!

So glad to see that the Yelp reviews have been consistantly better than when they first opened.   

Anyway,  I went again yesterday for Fiesta (once you have seen 12 horses, do you really need to see the other 788?)  What a wonderful meeting place!   I had a coupon from Night Out (All Santa Barbara peeps should have Night Out App on their phone) for a $3 margarita.   I am always  impressed by the margaritas at Casa Blanca.   Even when the place is swamped, the margies are wonderful.   When you have them at happy hour, they are just as good!   

Didn't have food this time but the people sitting at the bar next to me sure seemed to be enjoying theirs.    

Casa Blanca you are the MOST welcome addition to Santa Barbara!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (New), French | Review: I took my 9 year old son, Justin, to dinner at Downey's the other night and as he was finishing his main course he declared, "This is the best meal I have eaten in my entire life."' When I remarked that it was an impressive statement to make he said, "Yes, it's definitely a 10 out of a 10." I shared his remarks with Liz, the owner/hostess who seemed appreciative and amused. Justin then told her he couldn't believe they found a way to make broccoli taste good. She explained it was the freshness of local ingredients, which they purchase from the farmers market for all their dishes. Liz actually took my son back to the kitchen so that he could tell the chef in person how he felt about his dinner. Justin thought that was pretty cool. One final note, when we were reviewing the menus Justin wanted some kind of beef. The waitress overheard him and went back to the kitchen to see what they could do. They were able to prepare beef short ribs for Justin - an item not on the menu - which he clearly loved. Bravo to Downey's for being such a fantastic dining experience for diners of all ages! We'll be back!

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Italian, Breakfast & Brunch, Seafood | Review: The view is lovely and I thought the food was fantastic!  The appetizer bread with cheese crisp, truffle fries, cauliflower, and Pappardelle were all very yummy!  I have 3 minor complaints to mention though.
