First, my husband had the spaghetti and he was not nearly as impressed with his food as I was (cold and not flavorful) - he would give it only 2 stars.  Second, the price of tea was kind of ridiculous: $6.00 for iced tea and $8.00 for one small pot of hot tea.  And third, our wait time to get our check actually took an exceptionally long time.  Besides those minor things - I would definitely go back : ).

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: This spot looked like a great place for some drinks and food in downtown Santa Barbara, however, the service I received was less than satisfactory.

We came on a Tuesday for dinner and it was busy! I had a big party of about 10 people and so we understood it might take a while. We were told that there is a large table in the front of the restaurant that is a self seating table that we can claim, so 5 of us sat there next to three other people seated on the big table. Some 4-seat tables opened up next to us and we asked if we could claim those tables as well, since we were under the impression that it was a self-seating deal. The waiter replied with "absolutely not!" which was unnecessary. Nevertheless, we waited and they said they would have to split our party up. 

I ordered the impossible burger ($15) which did live up to my expectations. It's completely plant-based and tasted just like a burger patty when you eat the burger as a whole. If you just take a bite of the patty, you can tell the texture is a little off, but overall it was great. I also ordered the carne nachos which were tasty and is a nice appetizer to share. 

The waitress never came to refill our waters and never checked on us at all. There was a bit of attitude when we ordered and paid.

Categories: Nightlife, Soul Food, Restaurants, Comfort Food, Local Flavor | Review: Looking for a positively hearty meal after a night on the town? You've found your match. 

I decided to try this spot out for the first time recently after hearing rave reviews for what felt like the last several years. They operate from a pick-up window at a closed nightclub (which I hear is supposed to reopen with a new name and concept sometime soon) and you might miss it if you blink. There are a few tables out in the patio area and it's rather secluded from the craze of State Street even though it's in the heart of where the nightlife is. 

The macaroni dishes come in several variations with two different sizes - huge and really huge (or so it felt like to me!). The dish I ordered was cranked out pretty quickly and was very good, although I could not finish even half in one sitting. I will say that it certainly helped soak up what would have been a bad hangover that following morning.

Not sure if they are open at the moment with the whole coronavirus scare. If they are, though, I hope to try them out again.

Categories: Wine Bars, Restaurants, American (New), Nightlife, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: Came here on a random daycation to SB. Of course, I didn't just go to for The Hungry Cat, I came to just hang out. My bf and I wanted to get some seafood and we both wanted a variety of things throughout the day. I wanted uni and he wanted crab legs. After yelping uni and crab legs all day, we decided on The Hungry Cat. Now, I'm not one to go for these Yelp locations that have $$$ on a whim but somehow, this place caught my attention. Well, the photos caught my attention. We made reservations online for a later time and decided to go through with the reservation. Boy, am I glad I went through with that plan! We ordered the 1 tier seafood platter and uni (a la carte). The platter came with 2 giant clams, a fourth of a crab and lobster tail, 4 pieces of oysters, mussels, and shrimp. The uni was buttery and everything an uni lover can ask for. The highlight of the platter was the lobster tail. It was cold and still amazing. All of the food on the platter came out to $45 which I think is not bad at all and the uni was $14 with 5 pieces inside. I will definitely be coming back...maybe be adventurous and get the 2 tier....

Categories: Korean, Restaurants, American (New), Mexican, Tacos, Nightlife, Fast Food, Bars | Review: this place is amazing. i visit for lunch here about once a week. reasonable prices and great food, great service. i highly recommend the triple play cause you can get any 3 tacos for around 9$!. they also have a very good chicken sandwich and you can substitute the fries for tater tots (if your into that)!! only thing i would say is the lunch rush can be a pain to deal with parking if you are on a time limit. i like to go around 1130 to beat the crowd or after 1245 cause parking frees up.

Categories: Bakeries, Restaurants, Food, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Coffee & Tea | Review: Solid eggs Benedict.  Quickly served super friendly staff. Coffee and ok on point and really good potatoes.  This is a new favorite

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Long wait and over packed, meaning sitting on your neighbors lap, very loud. Not our particular scene. 

Served me a raw seabass. Both my wife and I lost our appetites after that.  My wife loves crab cakes and she only ate about a quarter of hers after seeing the raw fish.  

The servers are mostly young and don't have basic food knowledge.  Even when the table next to me asked the server how long can she can keep her fish, to eat later without refrigeration.  The servers reply was "I'm sure you can keep it out over night, but I'm not a fish expert."

Categories: Breweries, Food | Review: This is hands down my favorite local brewery! Between the amazing beers, spacious patio, and family (and dog) friendly vibe, it can't get much better. The staff is also super awesome! 

I love coming here on the weekends for a couple beers and a game of corn hole with friends! My personal favorite beer is the sabado tarde (tangerine beer). You really can't go wrong with any of their beers though- they're all super good!

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Went on a Friday night, was told a 45min-1hr wait, but ended up waiting over 1.5 hours.

Food was not bad, but definitely a little expensive. I guess you pay for the ambiance and location (it's near the beach). Food was kind of pretty/instagrammable (rice comes in a little pyramid), but that's about it, for me, personally. I got the chicken enchiladas. 

All in all, probably not worth the hype or wait. I think maybe the prolonged wait made me judge the place on a lower start, but the long wait also means it's a very popular restaurant.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Italian, Breakfast & Brunch, Seafood | Review: Was so disappointed with their food. Would not recommend coming here if you're looking for some good food. Now if you just want a glass of wine and a view then this is an option

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Best breakfast I have ever had. Homemade amazing mini muffins, cali eggs benny... fantastic! Get your name on the wait list before you get there. Worth the wait though :)

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: Chipotle and Crab Bloody Mary - delicious, could've been a little spicier, but it came with a crab claw! Yum.

Fried Green Tomatoes - I can see why yelpers recommend it, and now I am! I could've eaten the sauce (a ranch?) by the spoonful!

Crab Cake and Potato Hash - smaller portion, but it really was perfect with the egg and avocado.

Mushroom, Asparagus Omelette - yummy, but nothing super special about it!

It's a cute little restaurant, we called ahead to put our name on the list, which I would recommend doing if you are going on a holiday or weekend because it seems like the wait for others was long!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Do not know why people are hating on this place, perhaps my objectivity melted away with the river of hot fudge on my heavenly $9.95 banana split. Regardless, service was good, the desert was delicious.  People write about this being an expensive tourist trap, yeah, you're in CA on a wharf.  Everything is a tourist trap and everything is expensive.   Its why there are tourists here.  If you want a non expensive, non tourist trap prices head to Ottumwa Iowa. I am sure the ice cream shops there are delicious and reasonably priced

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Food, Desserts, Japanese | Review: Best Takoyaki I've had! Came for an early dinner and shared takoyaki, the green dragon roll (spicy tuna, seared albacore), katsu curry, and tempura ice cream.  Everything was great but the roll was a little bit bland. I did, however, love the katsu and the takoyaki is the best I've had anywhere. Also, the iced matcha tea is delish. Can't wait to come back for the great service

Categories: Health Markets, Food, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Specialty Food, Grocery, Restaurants, Delis, Meat Shops | Review: The service is amazing here!  I'm still new to the vegan world but they carry a wide variety of products! They even packed ice for me so I can keep my food cold on my way home to Santa Maria!

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: Was in the area for debris work. Walked in, and you order at the counter. Then have a seat. 

Ordered the seafood hot pot. Delicious broth. Plenty of shrimp and shellfish. Just right touch of spice. Cioppino was also on the menu.  Fish looked fresh that was for sale. 

Would definitely come again.

Categories: Vegetarian, Gluten-Free, Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, American (New), Vegan, Cafes | Review: Such a great vegan restaurant. The food was delicious, the coffee was yummy, and the restaurant itself was on a roof top, so great views. We loved it!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Great food, great vibes, great drinks. Got Agaves enchilada and guayava margarita (that tajin rim made my day I'm not going to lie). This was one of my partner's favorites that he wanted me to try when we were in Santa Barbara for the day--definitely worth a visit!! 

You order at the front and they give you a number to take to your table, which you can pick anywhere inside or outside. We chose outside and they had booths which I was pretty excited about haha, food came out quick and we made a couple trips back inside to check out the mini salsa bar!

Categories: Pizza, Bars, Sports Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife | Review: Eh the wait staff didn't even know their own menu. Maybe she was new but having an employee disinterested or lcking understanding of their own menu puts me off. That being said she brought us the wrong food and argued it but the chef remade us the correct order. None of the amazing root beer they usually offer on tap if you go here is suggest trying it if it's in stock. The calzone was small in my opinion but was good to eat not raw dough which you find sometimes with calzones.the chef was really nice he said not too many are ordered so I understand. There's a bunch of pizza places in area this isn't the worst but this isn't the best either. Late night it's cool to check out you can get a slice to go as you wait for your uber to arrive.

Categories: American (Traditional), Bakeries, Restaurants, Italian, Breakfast & Brunch, Food | Review: The Ahi Club sandwich on sourdough was nice with melted pepper jack. Side salad and large pickle slices complemented nicely.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches, Bars, Steakhouses, Delis, Nightlife, Burgers, Cafes, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Such a great place to eat the food is amazing and the customer service is just as good this place is surely a hidden gem

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: Sometimes the food is fantastic, sometimes it's not. It's always a gamble coming here. Could use more consistency in the kitchen. Service has always been good when we've dined here though.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Mexican, American (Traditional) | Review: Huge portions! Excellent salsa. Our waiter was super nice. It's a family run place and we could tell they get repeat customers.

Categories: Cafes, Diners, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: This review gives five stars for the continuity of the Rose Cafe into 2018. It is motivated by the review by M J. on 4/1/2018. We have been coming to the Rose Cafe for a number of years. The cook is the same and the waitress that comes in at 9:00 is the same. The building has been freshened, and the credit payment system modernized. The breakfast meals are the same as before. My in-laws know all the people and know Mexican food, and they see no substantial change or any degradation in quality of the food. As I stated the cook has not changed, so if you liked the meals before, you should like them now. 

To many of us the tradition is important, and to repeat myself I give five stars for that.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: A nice little seafood spot on the beach in Santa Barbara, this restaurant seems to have a devoted following.  The food was delicious and the service was great.  

The restaurant could improve the service by putting up a sign in the downstairs bar stating the restaurant is upstairs since I and other patrons were confused initially.  But the Bartender was excellent and made us drinks on the spot.  We enjoyed chatting with a Swiss family visiting America with their kids for the first time.  There was an excitement to the restaurant that suggested a destination dining experience was to be had.  One family said they go every time they visit Santa Barbara.  

We would return but would appreciate a full service restaurant both upstairs and downstairs and MUCH more seating since we had a 1.5 hour wait

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Such a great Sunday football spot! Watch out for signature cocktails...they are AMAZE! My girlfriend and I sat at the bar so we could chitchat with the bartender and have a nice view of the football games. Within a half hour of us getting there (right when it opened), it was a full house! LOTS of GF options...swoon! Show fun and positive experience!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Oh my Goddess!
The most tasty, fresh, mouthwatering Thai food I have had in years. 
A little twist on mainstay dishes, however, their dishes have multilayered explosions of flavors! 
I have not yet been to Thailand, so
hands down, for me, I claim this as the BEST Thai Food I have ever had!
The atmosphere is hip and groovy. 
Worth a drive up to Santa Barbara!
Categories: Tex-Mex, Restaurants | Review: Service was kinda slow, but the staff was friendly.  Portions are solid, but I thought the food was just okay.  The chips and salsa are pretty good.

Categories: Lounges, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants, Hookah Bars, American (New) | Review: Where do I begin...the restaurant was open when we walked in but there wasn't a hostess to greet us so we had to ask if they were even open when we got a servers attention. We were seated promptly outside and asked if we wanted anything to drink. We asked for waters and two cocktails. We were hungry because we had just drove into town so we ordered the beet burger and the veggie flatbread right away. We never got our waters, the drinks took forever to arrive and the mule I ordered was terrible. I hate to be a snob but mules are not that hard to make and shouldn't cost $12 when  there's not anything super special about it. The beet burger was super good and well priced but the flatbread was the size of a kids meal and did not hold its value. All in all, Santa Barbara has plenty of other eateries that cost the same as we paid with much better food and drinks.

Categories: Nutritionists, Acupuncture, Health & Medical, Traditional Chinese Medicine | Review: I have seen Caroline for acupuncture for a number of years for various medical conditions such as headaches, musculoskeletal problems and GI problems. I have found her to be an experienced, thoughtful and caring provider. I have experienced relief and improvement under her care. The appointment process is easy and efficient. I highly recommend her without reservation.
Mary J.

Categories: Food, Nightlife, Mexican, Restaurants, Bars | Review: I tend to forget about this place since it's totally off the beaten path. If you're familiar with the ever so popular Los Agaves restaurant then you will notice some similarities here such as the salsa, the rice served in the shape of a pyramid, and a side of beans served in a mini tostada shell. As rumor has it one of the chefs that works at this restaurant use to work for Los Agaves.  

Is the food just as great? It depends on what you order. So far I haven't experienced any meals to complain about; although today I had a burrito and it was overstuffed with beans and less meat. So just a tip you may want to ask them to cut back on the beans. Menu prices are pretty reasonable and you get complimentary chips served with 3 different types of salsa (red mild salsa, roasted tomatillo, and a spicy avocado). All of these are delicious!

The service here can be pretty slow from what I've experienced. Even when they aren't so busy the waiters just seem to wander around and walk by you without checking on you. So just a bit of warning you may have to flag them down. Very annoying! 

They have a small bar area but offer a reasonable happy hour Mon. - Fri. from 4-6:30 p.m.

You can expect $5 margaritas, well drinks, $3.50 premium draft beer, and appetizer specials.

I've been here for taco Tuesday and it's very good! For $2.50 you can get chicken/beef crispy tacos or soft tacos with meat of choice. The only thing is you must eat them at the restaurant and they do not allow you to take them to go. The tacos are the same as the old Rudy's restaurant if you're familiar with them.

They also take the Axxess Card - Buy one meal get one Free

Give this place a try if you haven't been here yet!

Categories: Restaurants, Pizza, Italian | Review: Two words: Garlic knots...mmm pure garlic goodness in hot steamy bread..does life get any better?

We visit Santa Barbara almost every year, and this is a great lunch spot.
Good size lunch portions. We've never tried dinner, but I can imagine it gets pretty busy.
This place is always a pleasure to visit

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: We were in town for the night and decided to have dinner at this beautiful restaurant. Sadly we were so disappointed with the quality of the food. The salsa was the sad. The chips were hard. The order was wrong. There was a cockroach walking across our table. The waitress saw it and her response was it happens all the time they fly down from the tree. WTF that is disgusting and to say that it happens all the time was so sad. Probably will not ever go back. This ain't no authentic Mexican food.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Love this place! Always feels welcoming. Love the art installments that change. Favorites: mole enchiladas and any of their soups. Try the agua fresca OR the micheladas. Yum!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Waited over an hour for food to arrive after ordering.  Server argued when we suggested the wait was long.  Food arrived mashed together on too small a plate so eggs were soaked in blueberry juice for examplr.  Quality was average.  Don't go here if you want atmosphere ,service, and a good breakfast.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New) | Review: I liked this joint! Traveling alone and needing a late dinner in SB can be tough!  They hooked me up 2 or 3 times. Cool, modern interior.

Great soups, strong drinks, and the petit fillet mignon was pretty darn good!

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: This place will not disappoint. Delicious food and very nice service. 

I would call in advance to reserve a table. We called a few hours in advance to reserve a table for 5 and were greeted and seated soon after entering the door. 

Everything we ordered was delicious. I ordered the catfish and highly recommend it. It was super flavorful and came with veggies and fried potatoes. I also ordered some wine and our server kindly gave me a taste of a few before, and a generous pour. Other delicious items we ordered: red snapper (mild), cappolinio (shrimp pasta- very tasty), jambalaya (can't go wrong), and filet mignon (perfectly cooked).

Wish we had room for their famous desserts- chocolate soufflé and bread pudding. We'll have to get it next time when we're in Santa Barbara!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: I was soo excited to see that a rolled ice cream place finally opened in our area. First thing I noticed was that the layout is really confusing. It seems flipped? and it makes the area cluttered and it takes a minute to figure out where to go. I do appreciate how many ice cream stations they had. The price was higher than any other rolled ice cream I've tried, but I guess that's to be expected in Goleta. Still... $7.50 after tax for ice cream?? I got the thai tea ice cream and I loveed it! Overall it was a bit hectic and took a little while, and I would give 3 stars because of layout and price, but their customer service makes them at least 4 stars. Everyone was very friendly, especially the cashier, his happiness was infectious.
Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Coffee Roasteries | Review: Great coffee! And oat milk option is perf for lactose intolerant people. Friendly staff and great environment

Categories: American (New), Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea | Review: Yummy! Friendly staff and delicious food! Have to order then sit but they have a wonderful system of assigning you a table so no hunting one down.
Definitely will go back

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Loved it loved it loved it, as if we were children we would go every day to eat their delicious yogurt!! Try not to go every day so I won't get bored. Afordable delicious yogurt. Yummmm... ;-))

Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: The first few times I came here, the food was good enough, (I ordered the drunken noodles) but the service was always a little slow. However, when I came here a few nights ago the service was absolutely terrible and the food wasn't that great either. 

Long story short, I ordered my food awhile before everyone else I was with did and it still came out last, about an hour and twenty minutes after I placed my order. The waitress lied to me the entire time about how long it was going to take, and eventually admit that she put the order in with the rest of my party even though I specifically told her I was ordering early because I was in a rush. On top of that, I asked for the drunken noodles to be "as spicy as possible" but they came out completely bland and tasteless. Aside from not being spicy, the food still tasted like crap.

I was very disappointed that I had to wait an hour and twenty minutes to deal with the absolute WORST service of my entire life for flavorless noodles. Needless to say, I will no longer be coming here.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Tex-Mex, American (Traditional), Burgers, Food, Sandwiches, Mexican, Juice Bars & Smoothies | Review: I love the food here! It's healthy and tastes oh so goooood! 

The blue corn tortilla chips and salsa are very good. The portebello mushroom sandwich or Mt. Fiji is my top favorite things to order. The food is always fresh and flavorful. I have tried all of the smoothies and they are the best!

The staff is attentaive and always sweet. Totally would recommend trying this place out and I assure you that you'll become a regular :)
Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Fantastic view. Decent food and good drinks... Definitely a fun place to go.

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Food Delivery Services, American (New), Event Planning & Services, Restaurants, Food | Review: A small cockroach was on our table so we moved tables. Our plate was dirty it looked like someone had dirty/muddy fingers and grabbed our plate. They also forgot to serve our garlic bread which was included with our pasta. On top of all that the cooks started giving us bad looks. They only serve cheap wine like Mark West and other 5-10 dollar bottles. There was also no restroom.

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services | Review: I stopped by for dinner last night and had the best experience! I decided to have the Farfalle Al Pesto and it's the best pesto I've tried in any Italian restaurant in SB. It was very authentic. Additionally, the customer service was incredible! Teryhus provided very welcoming service - he was very familiar with the menu, he was charismatic, and made me feel comfortable dining at the restaurant. 
 
I highly recommend visiting this restaurant with the outdoor dinning. It is very cozy, there is delicious food and wine, staff is accommodating and professional. I will definitely return soon! 

Thank you to Teryhus and the rest of the staff for being so helpful and kind.
Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis | Review: Roast Beef HOTTIE- OMG delicious. And I dont usually like roast beef. But I went by the reviews and man is it worth it. Tobasco Mayo YUM!!!!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Phenomenal atmosphere, outstanding drinks, and excellent service (thanks, Kerri!).  I came on a winter weeknight and really got to enjoy the space... highly recommended as I imaging this place would be packed on a summer weekend. We tried many drinks (and broke the bank) but have no regrets. Golden Globe was our favorite!

Categories: Restaurants, French, Bars, Nightlife, Wine Bars, Mediterranean | Review: Our reservation was for Mother's Day brunch. The food was excellent - really superb. The service was also good - though limited on this occasion. Rating would have been 5 stars but I reduced it based on the "value" factor because I was disappointed that the $65/pp rate for the brunch also applied to our children, who clearly were not enjoying the mimosas. (I contacted the restaurant about this in advance and did not receive a response.) I also thought that the "suggested gratuities" of 18%-22% were a bit presumptuous and aggressive, particularly considering this was a buffet situation -- in other words, "service" amounted to topping off drinks and clearing plates between trips to the food line. Yes, the gratuity is a choice - and I did choose to tip generously because the two employees taking care of us were attentive and polite - but I don't like to see the expectation that tips be ~20% in a limited-service scenario. Overall, we enjoyed it and everyone in my family agreed we'd go back on another day. I would just avoid the fixed-price dates since they don't offer a discount for the kids.

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Jazz & Blues, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: By FAR my favorite bar/lounge on the Central Coast. It's quite difficult to find (and not well marked) if you've never been (it's even hard to find on the internet!) but that makes it all the more rewarding when you do find it, and as another reviewer said, once you're in, you kind of hope no one else finds it so it doesn't get crazy crowded or trashy. 

I'm obsessed with the decor and vibe, our drinks were great (if pricey, but that seems to be the norm now), including a free one for the bride-to-be I was with. I didn't even go past the first room, where there are booths for a slightly more private experience than standing at tables on the main floor. No one bothered us, which was a plus. 

I can't wait to take all my friends here, I can't shut up about it!

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), American (New), Salad, Pizza, Gluten-Free | Review: Took the family out... Great service and food, Tyler was the bomb...!  Note to self, order the smaller Asian salad.  The large/regular is enough for 2... bummer the Samuel Adams (draft) wasn't available.

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services | Review: I spent a large part of last year working in Rome. Although we have some amazing artisan foods in and around SB it was a slightly rough transition coming back to the low quality and high cost of American food. Thank goodness for Via Maestra! Their food is so Italian. Not Italian-American. Italian Italian and it does not disappoint.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Latin American, Caterers, Food, Restaurants, Empanadas, Argentine | Review: Delicious!  I was in town visiting a friend who recommended we try Buena Onda.  It didn't disappoint!  The empanadas were delicious and I loved the unique flavor combinations.  What a fun, modern take on the traditional empanada.  We picked up the empanadas and brought them with us on a boat cruise.  It was the perfect snack!  Definitely give them a try!

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Came to try out the restaurant with the boyfriend and his parents and was pleasantly surprised by this small, dark, intimate spot. He'd been really eager to try it out since we'd heard so many good things about this place, and I'm glad we came. They have excellent food, fascinating atmosphere. The stuffed animals, mounted heads, and antlers were a little unsettling at first, but once you get used to them (after the initial "Gah!") they kind of grow on you. 

The restaurant is really small (reservations definitely recommended), and the service can be slow (when we came there was a single woman serving all the tables), but it's all part of the charm. You come for a slow leisurely dinner, and the food, setting, and pace are all part of that experience. We ordered the appetizer platter, which came with charbroiled shrimp, venison sausage, and artichoke hearts. Sooo delicious. For an entreeI got the rabbit medallions, which came in a lovely savory sauce, mashed potatoes, and vegetables. BF and his mom both got the venison, which they raved about. Exquisite! Drinks are tall and strong. Almost dangerously so. 

Be careful with the drinks, the drive back to town in treacherous! And take the time to read the entire menu. Lots of comedic gems. And the bathroom is outside. There is plumbing though, but still kind of creepy!

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: Being a Gemini, I love variety.  So when someone says "Tapas" I jump at the chance to have little dishes scattered on the table in front of me so I can aimlessly satisfy my craving.  

The edibles at Milk & Honey are among no other.  There is an abundance of savory flavor without being over powering.  The selection is great, something for everybody.  I personally can't get enough of Aaron's salad.  The drinks are among the best in SB, if you like Mojito's then this is the place.  They have many flavors with mango being by top pick Mojito.  But my all time favorite drink is the habanero passion-fruit margarita!

M&H is one of my SB top picks.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: Wow and wow!! What an amazing experience!
We booked a reservation on 12/1/2020 ( I mentioned the day because it was my wife's birthday)for 8 pm on a Tuesday. My wife had the Butter Poached lobster , I had the Roasted White Sea bass and my son being vegetarian was told to get a surprise plate by the chef. Wow wow and wow my son was amazed with his maitake mushrooms w sweet potatoes. And our plates were out if the continent. Wow wow wow !!!!

Categories: Korean, Restaurants | Review: This is as Korean as Taco Bell is Mexican. 
The owner isn't Korean (not that it matters).
But the meat is marinated with a sauce that is Asian, but not Korean. 
I went once, and I instantly regretting dropping all that money for "Korean BBQ"
Their Kimchi is awful. I mean, I know lots of non-Koreans would think so regardless -- but calling that kimchi is an offense to my people. 

It's not worth it. Save your money and head out to Oxnard. There's a Korean BBQ in the Collection shopping center. Or -- if you really want, head out to LA and you'll find plenty of Korean BBQ All you can eat lunch for 9.99 that is hands and feet down better than this "Korean" restaurant. 

If you love Korean food and are so desperate for it, iGrill still isn't the place for you.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Delis, Breakfast & Brunch, Tapas/Small Plates, Cafes | Review: After 4 years I was able to find my way back to this amazing restaurant. The fam decided we needed a quick weekend getaway, so off to Santa Barbara we went! My one must stop place to eat was State and Fig. I'm am sooooo glad this place has been able to stay in business during covid!!! We were kindly greeted by a young gentleman who gave us a menu and told us to go inside to the counter when we were ready to order and then we would be seated outside afterwards. I ordered the French Toast stuffed fig cream cheese. OMG it was so good. The toast had a nice crunch to it, and then when you hit the cream cheese filling...IT'S HEAVEN. With big smiles and full bellies we went off on our day. As long as State and Fig is open, they can count me in as a loyal customer! thanks so much for always making great food and providing a great dining experience!

Categories: Bakeries, Food, Delis, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Local Flavor, Italian, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: We found ourselves in Santa Barbara on a very cold and very wet California night.  After picking up movie tickets, we walked to this bistro (having seen their sign outside advertising happy hour drinks).  The waiter was fantastic and super knowledgeable about wines.  He recommended a fantastic merlot (Flora) which wasn't on the happy hour menu, but was SO worth the extra price.  It was also great to have warm food on such a cold March night.  Highly recommend this place.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Got the fried calamari, fried tofu, pad thai, and fried rice. All were pretty good and the fried foods weren't greasy or felt heavy. The service was exemplary -- honestly I'd recommend them just from our experience with the waitress who seemed to be the only one working at the front.

Categories: Seafood, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: I have been to Stern's Wharf many times and I always have passed this restaurant many times. I only have taken pictures outside with Moby Dick (the whale that is outside) pretending that it is biting my head off. 

This year one of my co-workers told me that on Valentine's Day they have a pretty good dinner for two. She told me to make a reservation since it is a holiday and pretty much every restaurant is busy. I went to their website and saw the menu for Valentine's Special and made a reservation and within an hour I got an email confirming the reservation. 

When I got there with my significant other, we did not wait that long to be seated. Here is what we got for $99.96 (plus tax and tip):

1 Bottle of Listle Cuveé Pink (Champagne)
Salad or a cup of clam chowder
2 Prime Top Sirloins with a baked potato and fresh sautéed vegetables
1 Main Lobster (split in two, one half for me and the other for my significant other)
2 velvet cupcake (one for each)

Everything was great and the service was amazing. I guess their busy time was earlier than our reservation at 7:30 p.m. The server had more time for us.

 I hear that they also have a great breakfast so one of these days I would make my way to Moby Dick (not to take a picture outside), but to enjoy a breakfast with an amazing view.

P.S. we also had margaritas and they were fantastic. They served them in a glass that had a stem in the shape of a cactus.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: Classiest sushi spot in town... No, dare I say, in all of CA. I only come for their brilliantly crafted sushi rolls, but for all you wimpy non-raw food eating people, my mother, a cook, had the Kobe beef and she approves!

Here are fun rolls to try:
The yellow submarine, the rock n' roll, swinging roll, and their Sea eel goes to Hollywood!

As far as the atmosphere goes it's groovy wherever you're at - if you choose outside, it's a little romantic tho could be chilly, the sushi bar is always happening, and upstairs is a little more private, works for a big party as well, point being, you can't go wrong with this place. Go there any day of the week, but try to beat the dinner rush around 7!

Categories: Breweries, Food, Arts & Entertainment, Cideries, Wineries, Wine Tasting Room, Kombucha | Review: First time ever trying mead and it was so great at Apiary! Such a hip spot with great flavor, I'm SO happy we made it out there. A few people in our group even bought growlers which is such a great take away. Hand crafted heaven in a beautifully decorated space. We even had a silly piano duet which sounded awful, but was so fun! Thank you for putting up with our silly group on Saturday! We all loved it

Categories: Bakeries, Food, Delis, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Local Flavor, Italian, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: We had lunch here recently, and really enjoyed it. I had the salmon bacon club sandwich and it was so delicious. The whole grain bread was almost like cake it was so good.  Hubby had the Cobb Salad. Their version comes with green olives and there were way too many on this serving, but other than that, the salad was delicious. We sat outside and the people watching was fun and the view of State Street was nice.  I would like to come back soon and sample some of their wines as well as some of the other menu offerings. Service was really good, too!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: GREAT FOOD, Fresh fish and beautiful beach view in the daytime! 

Must trys:
-oysters
-cajun seabass
-ceviche
-fish n chips
-swordfish

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Food Delivery Services, Caterers, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Wedding Planning, Nightlife, Food, Event Planning & Services | Review: We were married at Rincon Beach Club on November 18, 2006.  This is ABSOLUTELY a top notch facility. From Melissa down to the servers, the QUALITY is surpassed by none.  After 5 years, our guests are STILL talking about how amazing Rincon Beachclub was....the food, the atmosphere, the attention to detail.  You will NOT be disappointed.  I'm SOOOO excited to be attending another wedding at Rincon this month because I can't wait to eat the food again and experience the magic of another couple's wedding at this lovely venue.

 I read Patty H's review and I honestly just DO NOT agree.  Perhaps there was a mis-communication or something, but Rincon Beach Club and Melissa are completely UNDESERVING of a one star. Melissa was voted by the people of Santa Barbara as the BEST wedding coordinator and that distinction, in my mind, has more weight that one person's review.  That is a collective vote by hundreds of people.These kind of reviews on Yelp can make or RUIN businesses. If you'd like more information from a happy customer from Rincon Beach Club, feel free to email me.   The venue is beautiful and so unique compared to all of these "hotel banquet room style weddings".     LOVE.RINCON.BEACHCLUB.
Categories: Food, Grocery | Review: Definitely more expensive than shopping at Vons or Albertsons but when I need to make a quick stop for groceries this place is convenient.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: We are visiting from NY and chose to spend our last evening here at The Lark, at the recommendation of a friend who grew up here. What a mistake. Menu is quite limited and very overpriced. My boyfriend had his heart set on the lamb ribs only to have our waitress tell us the kitchen was out of them. She offered no suggestion of what else he should order so he went with the fried quail. That is not enough food for anyone to make a meal out of! We also ordered the pappardelle, which took well over 40 minutes to come out. Even more offensive is that the tables on either side of us received their food quickly. How long does it take to make pasta?? We had to ask her to check on it twice. We were left starving, disgusted and infuriated we wasted time and money here. Do better.

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: The service is outstanding and they have the best green salsa I've ever had. I put it on everything. The drinks are really good too.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: The best meals I've had are so good that they provoke me to wax poetically about the nuances and wonders of food. Milk & Honey fed me one of those rhetoric-spewing meals.

To be sure, this is tapas, so I don't expect anything to be affordable. When you're talking about small plates of food you're paying for the work involved in creating such small works of art as much as you're paying for the food.

For instance, the Nice Rack! is three pieces of lamb covered in a delicious honey sauce so indulgent you'd want to dip bread into it and spoon it in your mouth. Our server was kind enough to predict this reaction so we had bread readily available. By the way, when we finished all the lamb I topped the bread with a slice of tomato and the honey sauce. Easily as good as the  tender lamb, if not better.

The Paellita is not tapas-sized however and could be a savory meal on its own. The Nutty Goat--goat cheese with an orange & cranberry basil sauce with honey and pecans--makes me want to stick a straw in it and drink it as fast as I can. The Tropical Pig, while good, was not as stellar as the other plates.

Our meal ended with a "custom" dessert. Noticing the flan but also the orange caramel sauce in another dessert we thought we'd create a hybrid. Our server, who was immensely helpful, had no problems obliging. The combination, to our expectations, was delicious and the orange flavor really complimented the flan well.

And I would be remiss if I didn't mention the drinks, which seems to draw everyone to this place initially. They are indeed good...to the point that I ordered two mojitos.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: I have only eaten the food from here 2 times, and both times I was not disappointed. I ordered for delivery and the order receiver was really nice, although not as knowledgeable about the pricing, probably due to fact that it's still new, but still, very friendly. The pricing is amazingly affordable, especially for the mountain of food they give you! 

I have eaten the pizza with pepperoni, pineapple, and bacon, my usual order, try it, it's BOMB!!! The meatballs are pretty OK too, nothing too amazing but much better than some and they give you 4 meatballs for about 2 bucks! 

I have never eaten the food on site at the restaurant, due to the fact that the ex-IVFP building has contained a lot of...contaminants. Anyway, all-in-all, it's the best bang for your buck, especially in IV! Plus, if you order for delivery and are lucky enough to score the cute soccer player, it makes your meal that much better!!!
Categories: Soup, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Bars, Italian | Review: Good food, good ambience, good service -- what else do you need on a lazy Sunday evening ?

Categories: Restaurants, Food, American (New), Wine Bars, Nightlife, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Event Planning & Services | Review: This place is delicious! Such great food and really reasonable prices. The service has always been great! Perfect at lunch with a huge selection of creative salads. I want to try every one on the menu! Dinner choices are excellent and the wine is really reasonable. Coconut cake at the end is a must if you want to indulge!

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: Great service and good food. I went here on a Tuesday night at 6pm for a friend's 40th birthday. The table they have is wasn't the best, but we dealt with it. We started off with one server who was nice, but I think he had to go help behind the bar and we got what seemed to be a manager taking care of us the rest of the time. He was very fast, knowledgeable, and fun. The two salsa dips were absolutely phenomenal and slightly spicy. The green sauce was my favorite thing I had! We ordrred the Octopus, Mahi Mahi with green mole sauce, tacos, and a cheesy shrimp dish. The food was good. Fish and octopus cooked prefect. Drinks were fabulous as well. Only thing I would change is the really low seated banquette at our table   that just was not comfortable and I felt like a child with how low it was.
Categories: Vegan, Vegetarian, Restaurants, Indian | Review: The food service and atmosphere was all great!  I would recommend to anyone and will be coming back for their amazing masala chicken!

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: I really want to give them 4 stars because for the most part the food is fresh and quite tasty...

Areas' they can improve on:
1.Ask how spicy the patrons want their food. My favorite place asks you 1-3 Hot, Medium, Mild. It's a bit bland unless you ask for some heat. 

2.Train your servers, have enough people on hand on the weekends.

3.Take reservations on the weekends- for groups 6 or more.

4. Parking-No idea how to make that better, maybe put a cross walk in  so if you have to park across the street you will make it across safely.

 I do hope they succeed.

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: While the ambiance is great and the wait staff is friendly the hostess needs to go - we had a party of five - we called ahead twice to confirm they did not take reservations only to find out that they DO take reservations for the patio and without them you can't sit there so they them crammed us into this table where I sat with the leg of the table between my legs and paid outrageous prices for good food but certainly not the best food I've ever had...but seriously the hostess was beyond rude and thoroughly unaccommodating!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: I've only been to Casa Blanca twice so far for Happy Hour so I cannot say much about the quality of food..

But, the Happy Hour Standard margarita for $5 seems like a good deal and tastes great and doesn't skimp on the tequila.    The view from the outside patio is also a great way to sit back and watch the tourist cruise on by and get some sun.  The chips and salsa were a bit of  a disappointment though.  The two times I went the chips tasted old, stale or just off and the salsa is average at best.

I think they're still refining their service because both times I was there I had the feeling they forgot about us...

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Decided to try a new taco place today in the Funk Zone with some friends. My fiancé said "I don't even need salsa on this burrito it's so good!" And it filled him up good. Well worth the money. The fish taco was DELICIOUS!!! The Chicken Mole taco was DELICIOUS PART 2, and the carnitas taco was delicious part 3. Not the mention the chips were excellent.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: I'm so thankful for reviews that sent me here...LOVED IT! Went for breakfast. Service was friendly and fast. I ordered the mushroom and asparagus scramble and it was divine. The salad and biscuit were excellent sides. This place was incredible!

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Wow. What an amazing place. The most incredible and helpful service and truly wonderful, fresh food. I had the Pulpo Ceviche (octopus) and Chorizo and Crab tacos. Both were incredible. I love their chips and salsa, too. What a find!!

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese | Review: Doesn't get more authentic for there regional dishes. If you are expecting sesame chicken from Panda Express this is not the place for you. American Chinese food is very sweet while traditional Chinese varies from region to region. I recommend any of their hot pots which will have a numbing affect from the chiles. The owner is very nice but doesn't speak much, But she will try always to smile and remember you. The 3 chefs in the back are great guys they really care about the food and are always smiling. I always go right before they open so I can talk to them about food. They always make sure to make my dishes extra spicy. I haven't tried there combos but the seem very popular with the Asian students.

Categories: Restaurants, Seafood, American (New), Steakhouses | Review: I very rarely write reviews and I very rarely, perhaps never, give 1 star, however this place is terrible. From the host service, bar service to the chef, everything was terrible which ultimately feels like poor management. The only redeeming quality is that they perhaps know they're terrible as they kept trying to give us free stuff to make up for the terrible-ness. However, I would imagine that most do not go to $$$ restaurants hoping for a terrible experience so they ultimately receive free food. 

Long story short, waited 30 minutes for a drink at the bar, 1 hour for a table when we had a reservation and there were tons of tables and then 60 min later hostess tries to seat us in an abandoned part of restaurant that was available the entire time. Drinks at bar were good but not even a "hello" from the bartender for 25 minutes is rude. Crab cake at bar was mediocre at best. Once seated, and 45 minutes after ordering, husband's medium steak was served rare and my halibut was raw in the middle. No asparagus. They dropped mashed potatoes to make up for asparagus not being ready. Fine but we didn't order those. Sent our uncooked food back. 5 minutes later they try to give us asparagus. We say we're done with the whole thing, and they offer dessert as you'd expect. Ordered baked Alaska, they poured booze over it and kinda lit in on fire but we had a puddle of booze in a bowl. 

Whole experience was weird. The hotel is nice but do not eat dinner there.

Categories: Food, Vietnamese, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: I'm really hoping that after I leave ucsb, someone will open up a good Vietnamese restaurant around here. But for now, Noodle City is the only decent Vietnamese restaurant around here.

I ordered the bun thit nuong cha gao and it was a little less than ok, actually. What made the dish so disappointing was that the noodles weren't even cooked all the way. I feel like this problem shouldn't even be the main issue at an Asian restaurant. Most of the times, when people complain about Asian food, it's usually that it doesn't taste the same as the authentic dish. But something as simple as cooking the noodles all the way shouldn't be an issue.

I've also had their pho and that definitely isn't up to par, either. The soup tastes watered down and, if you are a true pho lover, you know that their broth isn't cooked as long as it should be. 

Also, service is terrible. The lady is frightening and I am too scared to talk back to her, lol. 

Pray that Goleta gets a good Asian restaurant!!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Excellent Thai food. Definitely upscale. Loved my meal, the curry noodle soup, and my friends noodle dishes were excellent as well. Only downside is the price ($14 dishes compared to the standard $8 Pad Thai elsewhere), but it is very good. If you don't mind paying a premium for good food, check this place out.

Categories: Vietnamese, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, Wine Bars, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Noodles | Review: Food was okay. Didn't become a fan of Vietnamese food. Not sure about the non-vegetarian dishes because I ate only vegetarian - something with rice noodles, turmeric curry. I forgot the name of the dish.

Staff were courteous. Lovely ambience.

Loved the iced tea for sure!!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Bakeries, Desserts, Food Delivery Services, Patisserie/Cake Shop | Review: So far we've only had really great experiences with Insomnia Cookies. The cookies are absolutely amazing, and if you sign up to their program you get a lot of good deals. I ordered on the app and they were delivered very quickly. Looking forward to satisfying our future cookie urges with this place!

Categories: Mexican, American (Traditional), Burgers, Restaurants | Review: I first went to the Spot in the early 70's as a wee lad.  I know the management has changed since, but it has left an impression on me after all of these years.  I take my family up there probably 6-8 times a year. I think they make a great buger, with all of the requisite juicyness and fresh ingredients you will need. Better than In N out in my opinion.  Probably Shake Shack in NYC is the best of all, but The Spot has a sentimental "Spot" in this yelper's heart.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Hubs and I were in Santa Barbara celebrating our wedding anniversary. Upon recommendation from the B&B we were staying at, hubs made reservations for Opal. After carefully considering all attributes, hubs and I both came to the conclusion of a three star review.

First the good. Opal is a great place to celebrate an event. The service we received was impeccable. Our server was very nice and helpful. We were complimented on our dressy attire upon arrival and promptly seated. We both ordered a cocktail.

Unfortunately Hub's cocktail was served in a cracked glass that after two sips he realized a piece of glass was missing from the cup. Argh. Dangerous faux pas. A cut would have been inconvenient, but a swallowed piece of glass could be worse. His drink was promptly replaced with minimal fuss and luckily was noted in time.

Our entrees came and this is where the three star rating comes into play. We both ordered off the specials, based on recommendations from our server. I ordered the pork loin and he ordered the halibut. Both were described as having exquisite sauces that really set the dish's tone.  Our food arrived beautifully plated. Upon taking a bite, I noted that my meat was very bland and under seasoned. I then took a bit of the meat with sauce. The sauce lacked flavor. So did my green beans. My mashed potatoes were quite cold. The only flavorful component of the dish were a few onion slivers and a piece of artichoke on the meat. I understood that the dish was meant to work together and it would have if it had the benefit of salt.

Yes, the salt, About that, there were no salt shakers ANYWHERE to be found. Hubs later mentioned that the menu strictly stated no substitutions were to be provided as the executive chef was creating a dish with components that complement each other. I imagine that also means don't you dare add salt as the dish is perfect to begin with. Hence the lack of salt shakers on every table. To be fair, I didn't ask our server for salt. I'm sure I would have been provided with it upon request. But that is not the point. I am not a salt needy person; most dishes are too salty for me. If I say the dish needed salt, it's because it did. And the fact that it's not automatically provided speaks volumes. 

Hub's fish was seasoned adequately, but the exquisite sauce that was promised with his meal was incredibly meager and lacked flavor. At least my dish had an adequate amount of sauce, despite the lack of 'truffle asparagus" flavor.

Dessert was a brown butter tart. Hallelujah, it was bursting with flavor and provided a band aid to our disappointing dinners. I highly recommend the tart. It is one of the most delicious, satisfying desserts I ever had. 

Based on the above, I place our overall experience at 3 stars. If only one of our dinners were disappointing, I would have chalked it up as bad luck, but both dinners is a bit much. Service is very good and they take care of you for special occasions.

On another note, do diners no longer have any self-respect? Please don't go to dinner in a nice restaurant wearing shorts and flip flops. I'm looking at the men in their forties. That is attire reserved for the beach, McDonalds or shopping at Walmart. Way to keep it classy.

Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Local Flavor, Food, Wineries, Arts & Entertainment | Review: We did our wine reception (1 hr) and THEN our wedding ceremony at the SBWC. This was followed by dinner at The Lark Private Room. A wedding ceremony was a first for the Wine Collective, but they took it in stride. The place was perfectly prepared, 4 dozen glasses of pink bubbly ready for guests as they arrived, and a spectacular selection of wines chosen by Alejandro, general manager. The place has a chic, urban hip vibe that is unequaled in Santa Barbara - tall ceilings, beautiful lighting, elegant bar and the overall experience was out-of-this world. Thank you for making our special day incredible -- to the whole team there!

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Today we found our 'go to' when we are in town: Santo Mezcal. 

After a few days of wine tasting we were done with wine and ready for some great food and beverages that weren't wine.   When we arrived about 4:30 the restaurant was hopping. We had a party of 5 and the bar looked full but they have a high too parallel to the bar we walked up to. The bar tender, Marcos, saw us come and and asked how many were in the party. He had a few customers that were getting ready to leave and helped us all belly up to the bar. We told him we were ready for some tequilita and he asked us what we liked. He made us all drinks based on our pallet. I had told him I wasn't a fan of mezcal and he took that as a challenge. He made me a margarita, that while a little sweet, was the best thing I have had with mezcal. Ever. 

Now I know why they have won three awards for their drinks! 

The chips and salsa were phenomenal. The chips were fresh and came out at the right temperature, lightly salted and perfectly crunchy. They came with three salsas that were all great but my favorite was the green. 

After we were about half way through our first round, Marcos recommended some ceviche. I thought it would be good because we were right on the coast. I was wrong. It was the best ceviche I have ever had!  I ordered the Ahi Tuna Ceviche. When it came out, Marcos also brought us a small plate of the Halibut Ceviche. That was the true crowd winner. 

After a second round of drinks and some great conversation with Marcos, we headed off for the next stop but this restaurant was by far the best stop of the day. It didn't try hard to be trendy or elevated (and drop flat like the Nook did around the corner - don't go there, their food is bland and overthought), it just did what it did best and it shined.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: This place is great! Second time here and even better than the first. Destination spot for us. Excellent food, dog friendly and tasty beers!

Categories: Bars, Nightlife, German, Restaurants | Review: Tiny establishment serving up hearty German eats in a rustic and homey setting. 

Despite having lived in SB a combined 60 years, my friend Dee and I had never dined here before. Our curiosity about the place, combined with favorable Yelp reviews, led us here for lunch recently. 

The small dining room was about half full
while we ate on a weekday. The clientele skewed older/retiree and Anglo Saxon, and I could see why they might enjoy what Dutch Garden has to offer: meaty, stick to your ribs fare that will transport you to your German grandma's kitchen table or a hofbrau in Munich.

I ate pork schnitzel ($13) which were under seasoned and slightly oily crisp breaded cutlets which perked up nicely with salt and dabs of the delicious house made mustards which came in a little tray of assorted jars that also included store bought ones. 
German potato salad and sauerkraut were subtly tart but also under seasoned. The sourdough bread that accompanied the meal came from D'Angelo's and was delicious!

Dee enjoyed her hot pastrami sandwich ($10) that did not include any sides unlike my schnitzel platter but she didn't complain at all and was happy to tote her remaining 1/2 sandwich home.

We shared a draft blonde beer ($6) that was both refreshing and flavorful. Our server gave us each a small wine glass full of the golden suds.

For dessert we barely dented a foot long slice of German chocolate cake ($6) that felt like another entree-it was so heavy and dense with cake, coconut filling and chocolate icing. It was fridge-cold and I think if it had been served room-temp, both flavor and texture would have benefitted. The cake may have gone down more easily with coffee or tea but we'd both already over-caffeinated that day so we declined the server's offer of a warm drink. 

All in all I'd say I'm interested in returning to try more of their menu, as I'm undereducated in Germanic cuisine, but liked what I had enough to explore more. While I think the prices are a bit high, the quality of the ingredients seems to justify the pricing. The service was pleasant, and the quaint dining room decorated with a Christmas tree and items from the Old Country added to our experience.

Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Local Flavor, Food, Wineries, Arts & Entertainment | Review: By far my favorite place to spend an afternoon wine tasting with my lover. The variety offered here is like none other, my favorite is Jonata. If you work up an appetite they have a neighboring bakery which is convenient. The employees here are also great and have extensive information to offer about the wine your guzzling :) If you are a wine drinker make sure to make some space for the collective. 

-They also host private events if you are interested it would make a beautiful location to host any event.

Categories: Health Markets, Grocery, Food, Specialty Food, Farmers Market, Organic Stores | Review: So nice to have an option besides TJs or SmartnFinal. Welcome to the neighborhood. 2 things: be certain your veggies are always full and always have brilliant check out.  Too many stores have come and then failed their customers here. You can make $$$ here if you keep it sharp and smile! Eye on the ball.

Categories: Dance Clubs, Nightlife, Bars | Review: Over priced drinks, bad service, extremely rude bartenders and severs, overall had nothing to offer. Small lounge and mediocre drink and service. Go here if there's literally nowhere else!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Where to start :/
I made a reservation 3 weeks in advance for Christmas Dinner, I specifically asked if they had regular menu on top of the buffet and was told YES.  Upon our arrival the 2 hostess we're so clearly frazzled and unprepared for the Christmas rush, rudely said it is only buffet and whoever I spoke to 3 weeks ago was wrong.
There were 3 different parties that had the same last name so they got the tables mixed up.  We gave our table away and left.  I was super bummed especially because the view inside seemed awesome. 
*Tip*
If you can't handle the rush of being open on Christmas Day, don't be open.  Your hostesses are the patrons first encounter at your establishment; being polite and courteous goes a long way.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Went there for our anniversary.  Excellent service (Lyric) and great food.  They even accented our dessert with happy anniversary arooud it.  You have to try this place.

Signed,

Extremely Satisfied

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Disappointed with my pad Thai order - it was very bland . I have three stars because when I called they were knowledgable and answered my allergy questions and got the order right when I got there . 

I wanted to try the pho like soup tomorrow but I'm not so sure as it might also be a waste of money :/

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Latin American, Mexican, Seafood | Review: Some of the best tacos I've ever had. Corazon Cocina is a modern looking taco booth inside a food market called the Public Market. Though there can be a line, service is fairly quick and friendly. The owner personally comes out to help you with recommendations when ordering. We were given samples of their unique, tasty juices when ordering.

Now to the delicious food: We had the Norteno, La Corriente, and the Sal de Mar. All were so good. The Norteno had a flaky flour tortilla that was just the right amount of gooey inside matched with the hearty steak. The Corriente was also fantastic. The Sal de Mar came with large amounts of tasty sashimi grade fish. There are many salsas to pair with your food. Prices are very fair. I love the no-frills, focus on food atmosphere they have here. Great taco!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Event Planning & Services, Food, Caterers | Review: Someone once asked me if I thought Rori's was better than McConnell's, which could be sacrilege in Santa Barbara. Both are amazing, but I'd say that in my experience, the former's scoop shop and wide variety of flavors edges the latter out.

Rori's takes up a small corner of the giant space that is the Santa Barbara Public Market. They'll give you free samples of ice cream on adorable little spoons, and they have a couple of good vegan options for people who avoid dairy, like their pineapple whip. And speaking of small, they have an awesome deal for people who don't like too much ice cream called the 'itty bitty cone,' which contains about half a normal-sized scoop of ice cream on a fresh waffle cone. If you're a sampler person like me, they do three itty bitty scoops in one cup, and you can get the cone on top.

If you have a hard time finding ice creams that aren't heavy but have almost all the richness of excellent ice cream, I highly recommend the flavors they have. Some of my personal favorites are the pineapple whip (vegan), passionfruit, banana brown sugar, and peanut butter chocolate candy swirl. You can also bring home some of their flavors in pints for nearly $9, which is standard for artisanal creameries.

They're buy one, get one free on Axxess, so make sure you spend it on something expensive (which I didn't think to do.) I'd say their artisanal ice cream is worth it at all costs, though!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: The best, most authentic, spiciest, and friendliest place to eat in the SB Public Market (and maybe even the entire Central Coast). Cannot rave enough about the service, quality, and unique flare that this restaurant offers.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Wait for six is a long wait 4 or under is perfect.  They have breakfast burritos but they are not under breakfast section.  Maybe because there a hell of a lot cheaper. The place had a bathroom only problem toilet handle was broken and the paper towel dispenser was empty.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Come here if you want to try interesting and out-of-the-box ice cream flavors!! 
I got a single scoop of "sea salt cream and cookies" and "wild sweet clover." They are delicious imo, but my dad was not a great fan of them. The sea salt flavor is extremely salty, but it was a perfect match for the sweet and refreshing flavor of the clover flavor! 
They're flavors are great, warm and modern atmosphere is great, and how they use stainless steel spoons for testers is great! I love seeing businesses becoming more eco-conscious! 
I think the price is completely worth it! Definitely would go again just to try the different flavors and sauces!! They had strawberry jam as a sauce option and I realized too late :(

Categories: Ramen, Japanese, Restaurants | Review: Best ramen we've ever experienced, and right on Main Street. 
Try the SB Spicy Red Ramen. 
They do take reservations two hours in advance.
This food is incredIble and authentic..

Categories: Nightlife, Soul Food, Restaurants, Comfort Food, Local Flavor | Review: The food is awesome. The hours are even better. I do not understand why other businesses are not catching on. Mac N Cheese is every UCSB students best friend.

Categories: Dive Bars, Bars, Nightlife, Sports Bars | Review: Roundin' Third is awesome. It's a local bar where you can have decent conversations with people you either know, or know someone else who that person is related to. The bartenders are very friendly and people can actually carry a conversation here. There is a pool table here so you can shoot some pool with a few quarters. 

Since this is a Goleta bar the prices aren't so bad. I was shocked when I ordered two beers and I had to put money back in my wallet because I took out too much. This made me proud to grow up in Goleta. I will definitely be back sometime the next time I am in town. Thanks Roundin' Third!

-Tej

Categories: Asian Fusion, Seafood, Restaurants, Poke, Food | Review: What a great place to get a quick poke bowl! It's located in the Paseo Nuevo shopping center. You walk in to a familiar type of order line and tell them the size, the type of rice/base and the proteins,and add ons you want. They also have Miso soup and other side options.
The inside is a little sterile for a relaxed experience,but they do have a gaming screen to use. 
I loved the quality of the ingredients! They have protiens, from tuna to shrimp,ceviches, salmon and even marinated octopus. The add-ons are extensive!  I ordered a chefs bowl B and a side of miso soup.  It was very good! I think we will go back tomorrow and customize some bowls to take home.

Categories: Vietnamese, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: Food was really good. A small group of us popped in after a bit of shopping and everyone enjoyed their dish. Place was clean. Not upscale, but more of a hole in the wall. I'll eat there again.

Categories: American (New), Bars, Restaurants, Lounges, Nightlife | Review: I love this place, especially the back patio. Perfect venue for treating yourself and sig other. Food is as delicious as it is beautiful. Clever cocktail list and locally sourced menu. Private rooms look great, sadly the Club Room cannot be booked yet because the City is taking their sweet time issuing their permit. Nonetheless, the newest place to be and be seen!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Food, Desserts, Japanese | Review: I could eat their Donburi bowl with steamed tofu everyday and I'd be happy. Very, very happy. Their light teriyaki sauce is just right - not too savory, not too sweet and complements the bowl nicely. About the only downside is the lack of decor in the restaurant (plain white walls with one small light in the ceiling don't add to ambience and something on the walls would help with the sound levels) and the location of the place - I didn't feel that safe walking around the neighborhood at night. Not the fault of the restaurant, but of Santa Barbara which needs to decide if they want to protect tourists in the downtown area or just let the area "go," which is what seems to be happening at the moment.

Categories: Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, Japanese, Nightlife, Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Cocktail Bars, Asian Fusion | Review: Really unbelievable how good Oku sushi is! I haven't had better quality sushi outside of Japan. It's much better than Nobu, Tao or Sugar Fish in LA. They have on the website "the premiere sushi restaurant in Santa Barbara" and they weren't lying. I'm very excited to have high quality Asian cuisine in SB to join the amazing Latin and California cuisine we already have! The upper deck and bar is beautiful too, with unobstructed views of the water and pier. Can't wait to come back!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Unfortunately, the food wasn't worth the sixty dollars that I paid... They charged my two year old twelve dollars for a bowl of pasta and butter. The shrimp was not cleaned or devained and the outside was so cluttered that I couldn't move my chair back to go use the restroom. The clam chowder was decent... All and all... Not a family friendly place and really not worth the money.

Categories: Delis, Gluten-Free, Fast Food, Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: Tucked off State Street, is the best small deli in the west, and maybe the east. Having enjoyed Katz's Deli in NYC two weeks earlier we needed a pastrami fix, and London transplant owner Mark Keen and his staff exceeded our need, with high quality meat, perfectly baked fresh bread on premises daily, tasty melted cheese, crunchy dills, coupled with the tomato basil soup...ah, fix satisfied. The cold beer on a hot humid day didn't hurt the cause either. Cheers!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: I got the Northern Thai curry noodles and they were so delicious! The noodles were perfectly al dente and the broth was enjoyable. I wish the crispy noodles stayed crispy on the side but they quickly became soggy the moment they hit the broth. It was a weird texture eating the grainy soggy noodles with the regular delicious noodles. My boyfriend got the Bangkok Street noodles. He asked for non-spicy and they only put a dash of spice which was nice for him because he can't really handle spicy foods. The potstickers weren't bad either.

The business is located inside of an indoor market. There's parking in a lot a small ways down the street. It was free for the first 75 minutes. The workers were very nice

Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: NEVER disappoints
LARGE selection of menu items 
AMAZING staff 
Food to DIE FOR 
OUTSIDE AND INSIDE dining 
Take out is just as yummy as eating in 
Seafood is always fresh 
Good amount of food for the price 
Sticky rice and mango for dessert is the best

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Lounges, Seafood, Spanish, Italian, Nightlife, Mediterranean | Review: This place truly caught my fiancee and I by surprise, we expected a nice meal but were truly caught off guard by how amazing everything was.  Our waitress Natalie was truly an incredible asset to their team, her knowledge and descriptions of the offerings made us want to order everything!!!  All of the dishes were deliciously presented and tasted even better!!  The bean salad, scallops, beat salad are a must try and definitely opt for the fried egg with the bean salad.....delicious!!  The halibut was a bit bland, but the kobe steak was unbelievable.  Thank you all for such a wonderful night.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: Beautiful outdoor seating, great location by the funk zone. Service is very personable and attentive, Mark was great!! A bit on the pricey side about $12 per drink and app. Also sometimes slow to bring drinks outside. We got the omelette and seafood paella. The omelette was great. Paella was good too, big enough to share between three
Categories: Tex-Mex, Restaurants, Bars, American (Traditional), Nightlife | Review: I come to Chili's quite often, I love the honey chipotle bbq wings. The service is always  great and so is the food

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: I crave the Northern Thai Noddle Curry on a weekly basis. Its soooooo delicious. It's become one of my guilty pleasures every time I go to visit my boyfriend on the weekends.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: The enchiladas were bomb! The halibut was tasty inside and the put plenty of it in the enchiladas.  The sauce is so yummy, the pastor tacos are also good.  To drink I had the horchata and it was delicious.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: Came here few days ago, had a great experience. The food tasted like fresh ingredients. the fish and chips that I ordered seemed pretty authentic British fish and chips style. Staff and decor was inviting and friendly. Definitely will be coming back regularly.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Carlitos has cameras to keep an eye on their customers, too bad they don't keep an eye on the quality of their food. Food is expensive for what it is, and lacks depth of flavor. Sauces are oily and thin and beans and rice are slopped under the main entrees making everything taste the same. When brought to the managers attention, their hands are tied and do not take ownership of the situation, passing it off to the owner who will berate and belittle you for any constructive criticism.

Categories: Vietnamese, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, Wine Bars, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Noodles | Review: The chicken pho was amazing! I got it for take out and was able to eat it for two separate meals because the portions are huge. There is great flavor in the sauces as well. I also ordered the spring rolls which had an amazing peanut sauce! I will definitely be ordering from here again.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: So sad again, we waited for 20min for a server, had several walk by numerous times, we love to come in and get some drinks and an appetizer however we had to walk out... The kitchen and bar make such good food and drink... It's such a shame and disappointment the floor staff/servers can't keep up, it wasn't that busy.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: Went to this restaurant for a bachelorette party and they were very accommodating. The previous restaurant booked had a fire and we were able to book a large reservation last minute.
 
With several orders and drinks to be made our waitress was extremely accommodating. She was able to take orders and amend them when needed. 

Food was delicious and there was very easy access to State Street and tons of cabs and Lyfts. I would recommend this restaurant for a quick snack of large parties.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: First time trying the sushi here and must say I wasn't disappointed. The prices were reasonable and the food tasted good and fresh. I tried the California shrimp tempura roll and it was soooo good. I was expecting a small roll but it was big compared to other places I've gone. My husband got the ca roll and teriyaki bowl special and that was a deal for the price. The workers were friendly and the place was clean. Will definately be back again when in the area.
