Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Great food. Great atmosphere.  Always busy.  A great dining experience. We eat here as our "go to" Mexican Restaurant in Santa Barbara.  Tacos Del Mar, Mole Enchiladas, Fajitas are exceptional.  Very good seafood selection as well.

Categories: Steakhouses, Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: Came here for dinner after the urban wine trail on Saturday night. The place was packed but we managed to make a reservation the same day. We got the steak and the shrimp which was really good. 

Service was also good as well. I'm not sure why the low stars but I lived in SB for 4 years and this is one of its more famous places. I would definitely check it out if in the area.

Categories: Restaurants, Desserts, Sandwiches, Food, Italian | Review: Really yummy italian food takeout at very reasonable prices! Got spaghetti with meatballs, lasagna, and a salad. Everything was delicious. I had leftovers of the spaghetti & meatballs and they were great a couple days later.

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: Really nice food and great craft beer selection. They didn't bring out our sides with the mains though and forgot about them. However they gave us 2 free desserts to compensate.

Categories: Salad, Restaurants, Pizza, Sandwiches, Italian | Review: Great spot on State Street. Called ahead and pizza was ready when we arrived since deep dish took 45 minutes to cook. They didn't even ask for a credit card, which was surprising but nice. We ate on the patio which is big and looks into the bar/restaurant or the street so good view either way. The pizza was a bit disappointing but I am from Chicago so maybe my expectations were high. Not somewhere I care to try again although the service was great.

Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Local Flavor, Food, Wineries, Arts & Entertainment | Review: This was a solid 5 star place for me. I must agree with other reviews, that this is one of the best spots in the funk zone. I was super impressed with their tasting menus! Yes, multiple menus! We decided to go for the "Notary Public" tasting, $12. We started with the chilled Chenin Blanc, and another white with a buttery finish. I savored these as the day was hot and we sat directly in front of a blasting fan. Our help was friendly and outgoing, plus he gave great remarks about each wine he poured. Next, we tried a few Cabernet Sauvignons - which are so difficult to enjoy when its warm out, even though we were indoors.

This stunning, rustic and trendy industrial tasting room was also equipped with not only bar seating, it had a communal table, smaller tables, a pastry case and open kitchen to order from and outside seating. For those that need water during your tasting (like us), there is a large self-serve container with mason jars by the entrance! I loved that we weren't rushed, and most of the large-bachelorette-type crowds had left so the environment was extremely enjoyable. I will definitely be coming back!

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Sushi Bars | Review: Absolutely amazing experience here at Sun Sushi. Quality cuts and fantastic service. Get the six step salmon and uni. You will not be disappointed.

Categories: Pizza, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: I finally gave this place a try  after walking by a few dozen times.  Nothing from this place stands out.  The decor: bland.  Food: even more mediocore.  I had the tex-mex pizza, supposedly their speciality.        The grilled chicken sandwich was also lackluster.  Sure, it maybe healthy,  but there are lots of options around with better taste and atmosphere.  Like Silvergreens. My reccomendation:  walk on by.

Categories: Wine Bars, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, French, Bars, Nightlife, Creperies | Review: Rating this restaurant with 5 stars is a total no-brainer.  The food is fresh, delicate, and perhaps has crack in it because I've wanted to go back there every day since my lunch visit.

OK, let's get something straight.  This is not some trendy, swanky, jazz-playing creperie.  Quite the opposite, in fact.  This place is charming, quaint, and simple.  The walls are decorated with personal keepsakes and antiques, not blown-up posters or French sayings.  Think of it more as your grandma's restaurant.  If you do that, you'll be delighted by the atmosphere.

People have complained about the servers speaking French.  Really?  Seriously?  In my opinion, it only adds to the experience (and isn't that what dining out is all about?).  Unfortunately, I think these ladies may have read these reviews because they spoke little French during my visit.  Too bad, really, I was looking forward to it.

The food was downright scrumptious.  If you're craving crepes, this is the place to go.  Look no further.  We ordered two crepes during our visit--the lemon squeeze and a custom crepe I requested (because I can be difficult sometimes).  The lemon squeeze has lemon juice, butter, and sugar on it.  It was slightly tart, sweet, and had a bit of a crispy texture.  So good (and only $4!).  My custom crepe contained strawberries, bananas, chocolate, and whipped cream.  So, so tasty.  It was so good, in fact, that I would only let my husband have one bite.

Husband loved his turkey/cheese/avo sandwich on a French baguette.  He couldn't get over how soft the bread was.  (I tasted it--He wasn't lying!)

Oh, how I wish this creperie was local to me.  Instead, I have the over-the-top trendy swankster.  So unoriginal!

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Caterers, Mexican, Salad, Event Planning & Services, Desserts, Food, Nightlife | Review: Fundamentals like black beans and homemade tortillas are a substantial cut above at Los Arroyos, in comparison with more mass market Mexican establishments.  However the price point is just too high for me to make frequent revisits--The tacos a mano dish includes two small soft tacos, beans, and rice.  With the chicken option, this will set you back $11.50.  No extra vegetables included, and only a very meager amount of cheese.  A more economical option is to takeaway order a few sides--such as a single taco and a side of beans--and supplement it with some of Santa Barbara's finest produce at home!

Categories: Nightlife, American (Traditional), Cafes, Restaurants, Wine Bars, Breakfast & Brunch, Bars | Review: Kim Cameron, Valencia, CA
I have eaten here countless times. I kept trying to find something I really liked because everyone else loved to eat here. I just never really liked it. I LOVE Breakfast Burritos & Phili Cheese Steak sandwiches, but I don't love theirs. The Breakfast Burrito is kind of boring & I always needed to add things to enjoy it (which increases the price quite a bit). It's huge, but instead of making it sooo big, I think they should make it smaller & add more ingredients (add extra cheese, potatoes, meat- steak/bacon/sausage, & remove some of the beans), & also start making a fresh, more traditional-Mexican style salsa (they use a store-bought-like salsa, which really ruins it for me...). Also, for the Phili sandwich, they use processed steak, & processed cheese (the kind where slices come individually wrapped). For me, it ruins the sandwich (I can make my own, easily, in such a way...). The breakfast Cottage Fries are really yummy, but sometimes they are inconsistent. One time my friend got hers & they were perfect, then I was served mine & they were black & overdone (same table!). Also, the other Mexican food I've tried is too Americanized. Anyway. The food is OK, but I just don't like it as much as everyone else, I guess...

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Cafes, Cajun/Creole | Review: Cajun brunch on the central coast? I was excited to try some Jambalaya here, and it's always nice to take a break from the drive along the ocean. 

I tried the Jambalaya and eggs, and the dish wasn't as hearty and seasoned as jambalaya should be. The dish did have a lot of shrimp and a decent quantity of meat, but overall lacked flavoring. The cornbread was also lackluster. The dish could've use more cajun seasoning and also put more on the eggs.

Props for serving novelty food, just needs to kick up the flavor a notch.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, French, Salad, Sandwiches, Desserts, Bakeries, Food | Review: Almost croissant was delicious - flakey and soft. They didn't skimp on the almond paste filling; also for almond lovers, topped with tons of almonds.

Walked in at 10 am. I was ordering to go but noticed all tables were full. Took a leisure walk on the Main Street and after over an hour later noticed that the tables were still full. Word must get around.  

I'd definitely visit again!
Categories: Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Bars | Review: So. Trashy. It was like a frat party for high school kids.

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Ramen | Review: This place is always delicious!  I've been here too many times to count and I'm always happy with my food. My favorite is the Spicy aka ramen with thick noodle and pork belly. Though all their ramen is bomb.  A+

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Love the food and ambiance. Downside we waited 30 minutes for our table despite having a reservation. They told us it was due to the people not leaving even though they had already paid. If that's the case they should put a time limit on reservations like other restaurant do. It's not acceptable to make people wait that long. Not impressed.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I have to honestly say, the worst customer service I have ever experienced. Long wait time, expensive plates for not enough portion. Even management skills are ridiculous. The owners son runs the restaurant while the father runs the food truck and his son has no management skills whatsoever. If I could give zero starts.. I would

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (New), French | Review: Amazing, unique, delicious meals.  Great service.  Wonderful appetizers,  dessert and wine. Had the duck. Best I've ever tasted. They took great care of our party of 14 on 1/13/17.. Husband is the chef and wife runs the front with a great staff ... and makes all the beautiful art gracing the walls.  Very kind, gracious people. What an awesome evening dinner experience!
Categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: I love handrolls Mondays! Selected handrolls are only $1.99, my favorites are the salmon skin, fish tempura, and California roll!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Expensive, and the food doesnt match the rates.  That being said, the food was still good and seeing as it kind of is a tourist trap, I accepted it!
Salsa with the chips was amazing.  We also got seated right away at 730 on a saturday night for 2 people.  Pretty good service as well.

Categories: Cuban, Restaurants, Bars, Sandwiches, Nightlife | Review: The food was good but for the price it seemed pretty expensive. It's walk up ordering and pickup and they don't serve drinks except water. Self serve everything. So premium prices for rice, beans, and chicken doesn't make sense.
I enjoyed my food but not sure I'll be back on purpose.

Categories: Vegan, Vegetarian, Restaurants, Indian | Review: We have been here twice, once during their soft opening and once this past week for takeout.  We were big fans of India Club so were happy to hear Indian is coming back in to the neighborhood.  I love the idea of sit down Indian and especially the fact that the chef tries to use all fresh local ingredients.  So on the positive side the food is really good from my opinion, we've had the Tikka Masala, Garbanzo bean dish, the South Indian chicken, lentils, rice, naan and samosas.  ALL really super delicious.  I love the way the dinner combos come on the trays when you eat in, and the servings when you do take out are nice size.  We have also found (contrary to some reviews here) that the wait staff are all VERY friendly and helpful.  The only con for us is the price.  When we did takeout for just my husband and I, the cost was about $60.  Which is not cost-prohibitive for a once in a while splurge on takeout (we have young kids so take out is nice.)  But it's not at a price point where we could work it into our normal takeout routine and go regularly, which is unfortunate.  In comparison, India Club would run us about half that when we did take out, and even Tamira (which the food is not nearly as good as Masala Spice) is much less expensive.  I understand they are not the same exact type of restaurants, but when comparing local Indian, this is on the higher end and probably comparable to Bibi Ji's on State Street which I am not sure is going to work on Calle Real in Goleta over the long term.  Anyway, we are glad they're here, the food is great, service has been very Goodland-like and friendly, and I hope they last.

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese, Szechuan, Soup, Seafood | Review: Awesome Chinese food in Goleta. Everything we tried was really tasty, and they have a pretty solid menu with lots of options. The prices were a bit more expensive than we normally look for for dinner, but the portions are pretty large so it's not too big of a deal.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: DO NOT EAT HERE.  We've been going here for years and lately it is horrible.  Maybe the worst dining experiences I've ever had horrible.  I never write reviews, I really don't care that much, but our experience was that bad.  The few patrons in the restaurant were actually speaking with each other and describing their awful food and how long they'd been waiting.  We sent the "cheeseburger" back.  It looked like a microwaved school lunch or frozen food that would come with a frozen dinner.  I had an enchilada and taco combo that was cold and tasteless.  When I say tasteless I mean nothing...like it actually took the flavor out of my mouth.  How can beans and rice taste like terrible nothingness?!?!  We were there for over an hour and a half even though there were only 2 other customers.  The two waitresses were walking around and rubbing walls/counters/salt shakers with a dirty rag.  I don't know what the "cook" was up to.  It was strange and terrible and I implore you not to bother with this place.  What a shame?! Where is the owner?!

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: Service was top notch, and the hostess was very sweet to us.  For an expensive Italian restaurant I would say our main courses were just average but not necessarily worth the money.  I can confirm the bread was some kind of joke, tasted like day old super market bread.  Probably wont be back for a long time.

Categories: Delis, Gluten-Free, Fast Food, Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: My friend and I were on a mission to find the best soup in SB. We walked in 5 minutes before closing on a cold, wet, rainy day and Owen was really nice and recommended the chicken orzo. Even though they were closing he let us sit inside and eat it. It was so good we had to order seconds, and then, he recommended the vegetarian chili and tomato bisque. We decided we just had to try them all. They were all amazing and so was the customer service. I will definitely go again.

Categories: Venues & Event Spaces, Cafes, Wine Bars, Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: I rarely give five stars but we had a very positive experience at the chef's counter at the wine cask. The chef counter is a special prix fixe three - course menu with wine pairings designed, presented and introduced by the chef, and built around seasonal ingredients. The night we had our reservation the chef was not there but his sous chef, Nick,  was an able understudy. This young sous chef impressed us with his creativity, cooking skills and poise.

We started with a perfectly cooked seared scallop in a parsnip sabayon dotted with dill oil and topped with a strip of fried parsnip that looked like bacon but tasted like sweet earthy crunchy perfection. There was also a swirl of angel hair pasta on the plate that at first I thought was superfluous but quickly realized was a wonderful textural foil to the scallop and a perfect medium to soak up the  juices and sauce. The dish was paired with an elegant Melville Chardonnay.

The second course was a fascinating and inspired dish of sea bass in black olive oil with mustard greens and caviar infused with star anise. The unexpectedly harmonious pairing of olive and star anise leads me to conclude that the young chefs in this kitchen are talents to watch and I hope the management continues to give them the creative room they need to grow.  My only quibble with the dish is that I wanted more of the black olive oil to balance the star anise. The Grenache was a perfect match.

Dessert by pastry chef Rosie was a moist and homey gingerbread redolent of apples and allspice served with a cinnamon ice cream, poached pears and a pear purée. The dessert wine pairing offered was a perfect compliment to this autumnal treat. The portion of gingerbread was a bit large I thought, although my companion had no 
problem polishing off his serving and some of mine.

Service was professional and friendly. The ambiance was inviting. We will definitely return for the chef's counter next time we are in the area.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: I will write this review in two parts, one for this restaurant's service and the second for its food.

Firstly, I will give Sama Sama Kitchen zero stars for its poor service and attitude after my dinner engagement. This is what happened. I called to make dinner reservations for a party of nine people. The hostess explained that I need to complete my reservation online through a site called Tock and that there would be a $15/person fee which will be refunded back to me after I check in at the restaurant. We showed up for our dinner with our full party of nine and had a fine evening at the restaurant. A couple of days later, I received an email from Tock that Sama Sama Kitchen had issued my reservation refund. However, the refund that I received was short by one person ($15). I wasn't certain that I was reading the refund statement correctly and I checked my credit card transaction record. Sure enough, the restaurant had shorted my refund. I called back the restaurant in the next afternoon and spoke to a gentlemen who took down my message and said that the evening hostess will give me a call back about the refund status. However, the restaurant did not call me back. The next evening, I made a call again to the restaurant  and was able to speak to a hostess and explained to her once again about the mistake. After the hostess put me on hold to check her records, she got back on the call to ask if I was the person who had called the day before to inquire about this refund. She told me that they had checked their records and would not issue a full refund because only eight people showed up. I explained that that is a mistake and I had several pictures taken at the dinner clearly showing I had nine people in my party and I am ready to send her my pictures to prove that fact. After a second of awkward pause, she agreed to refund back the full reservation fee which I have now received the following day. 

My two complaints about this incident:
1. Ignoring your customer's inquiry. Why didn't the restaurant call me back to let me know that they will not issue a refund because they thought I didn't show up with my full party? Do they think their customer will forget about their inquiry if they think about returning to the restaurant next time? Do they care about getting repeat customers?
2. The unapologetic attitude of the hostess/restaurant during my second call. I understand that people make errors. But I would be perfectly okay if you fix your mistakes. $15 is a very small amount to fret over. If your customer takes the time to call you twice to explain about the mischarge, you should be a bit more apologetic instead of being slightly standoffish and saying just a very curt "thanks" to end the call.

Now for my review about the food and my experience at the restaurant.
We ordered these dishes along with a bottle of cab:
Signature chicken wings 
Crispy rice salad 
Eggplant and pork ballado  
Crispy duck salad 
Mie goren (noodles)
Nasi goren jawa (fried rice)
Grilled spare ribs 
Tofu and broccolini

The food was good and delicious but not the most amazing Southeast Asian fusion food that I've ever had. I thought the spare ribs with a side of fried yams and the chicken wings stood out most. The crispy duck salad was also excellent with a good portion of duck meat.  

For desserts, we ordered the Date Cake which was okay and the Matcha-Raspberry Semifreddo which was missing the raspberry in the actual serving without any explanation from the server. I thought dessert was underwhelming and somewhat overpriced to me. 

Overall the service we received at the restaurant was good and friendly. However as other reviewers have mentioned, the restaurant tacks on an automatic 20% gratuity. This may explain some rocky inattentive service at the start when no server explained to us that we needed to scan a QR code to look at the menu online which took forever to load for those of us with T-Mobile provider phones. Also the server did not tell us about any specials, or asked us if we had any questions about the menu.

Categories: Mediterranean, Restaurants, Salad, Greek | Review: We had the gyro signature plate and the halal lamb kabob. Both items tasted pretty good. I don't feel like they are worth the price. Particularly the lamb kabob is pretty expensive. There was about half the meat as what is shown on the menu picture on their website.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: The location definitely makes this place! It's right on the beach with great views, and away from noisy traffic. Went for a Sunday brunch with my parents and was seated right away. It was pretty chilly right on the water but the glass barriers on the outside patio did a great job coupled with the heaters. 
We all ordered breakfast food (huevos rancheros, chilaquiles, santa barbara omelette). All great but not amazing. Coffee was decent. Wait staff was great and checked up on us without being overbearing.
So you pay for the location but it's worth it, especially if you can manage to get a table right next to the beach.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I wish I did not have to write this review because its not about the food, but about the service. One night my fiance and i stopped in to get some food, and sat at a table (not a big place, maybe 10-15 tables). The waitress there went to every table to check on the other customers, chat with them, and won't even bring us menus. We sat there for almost 7 minutes before the chef in the back came out to give us menus, take our order, and give us our food. I couldn't believe it. We hadn't done anything for this woman to blatantly ignore us. They weren't super busy where we were over looked. She made the conscience decision not to serve us. The only reason I can think of is because I am Asian and my fiance is white and all the other customers were Hispanic. I won't ever go here again, I even if their food was the best freaking Mexican food in the world, I don't think any body else should.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: My Cordon Bleu trained chef husband and I ate here yesterday.  We give every restaurant we dine at a fair shot by ordering a variety of food to share and on the whole, we are pretty easy to please because we like to eat and both have a wide palate.  

I ordered the chef's special which was sausage and risotto as a main dish, he ordered the lasagna with lamb.  For appetizers he had the portabella mushroom with arugula and I had the eggplant and we shared a chocolate and banana bread pudding for dessert.  

Long story short, the food is marginal at best.  Overcooked, salty, inconsistent and an overwhelming taste of being canned and microwaved, we left this place less than impressed.  The chef's special is supposed to be one of the best things on the menu--it wasn't.  In fact, I couldn't even finish it (yes, it was that bad).  

How is it possible this place has such good reviews?  I don't get it.

Our bill was over $170.00.  Worth it?  Not by a long shot.

The physical plant and ambiance of the restaurant are pleasing, but the food and service leave much to be desired.  Definitely not worth the price.  

We won't be coming back. 

Save yourself the trip.

You're welcome.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Tiki Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: Love this spot! Adam is a conisseur of tiki drinks and the ambiance is cozy with a modern Hawaiian feel. Can't wait to come back :)) The new owners hit it out of the park!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Warning: I was really hungover and I waited in line for close to an hour. I'd never do that again here, but I would definitely return if I could forgo that line. 

To be honest, between two of us we ordered 6 things and they all tasted the same. All was definitely on the bland side and I needed to literally douse my food in hot sauce to get any flavor. To top it off, the meat was really dry. The fresh made tortillas are the star of the show and they know it. Pretty much everything you order is served with two. 

The pinto beans with bacon are a pass. Soupy, flavorless, this was the worst. The guac with tortillas wasn't anything special and the tacos were definitely more of the highlighted dish here, but Lillys blows them out of the water. This is a celebrated spot in SB so hit it if you can get here without waiting in line. If that's not possible I'd recommend moving on.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Lounges, Seafood, Spanish, Italian, Nightlife, Mediterranean | Review: Came here for a friend's birthday dinner. We were a large group but they accommodated us easily. The servers were professional, attentive, and friendly. The food was fantastic, served in perfect portions. To top it off they gave us all tequila shooters on the house to celebrate my friend's birthday! All in all a great experience, can't wait to return!

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Why are you even reading this! LITERALLY THE BEST sandwich place in the world. Service is amazing, goods are fresh and you'll leave with a smile. I promise. Stop reading. Start eating.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars | Review: This was an outstanding experience all around. We were quickly seated outside and given menus. The menu is extensive. The waiter/hostess/cashier was pleasant and attentive. Our order of a rainbow roll and gyoza arrived very quickly. It was one of the best rainbow rolls I have ever had. It left me wanting to order more.

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Food, Bars, Nightlife, Wine Bars, Wineries, Wine Tasting Room | Review: Nice wine-tasting spot with a fun funky theme! 10 bucks for 5 wine tastings which is decent. You get 2 whites and 3 reds. 

Service was great and I really enjoyed the first two white blends on the tasting menu. The girl at the counter was very informative and gave us great suggestions for touring SB. This is a super chill and fun place to just kick back at the bar and have a chat. YUM!

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis | Review: Please note this is my first ever negative review on SCD.  

An online order was placed today at 12:10pm.  One of the items ordered was the Chicken Chipotle club.  When the sandwich was opened, it was realized that half of the ingredients were missing!

After testing out the online order flow after the fact, when the Chicken Chipotle Club was selected, the online ordering system defaulted to "Without: smoked turkey; bacon; jack cheese; chipotle, mayo; lettuce; tomatoes; red onions". The person ordering the sandwich did so on my behalf, and was not familiar with the online ordering system, nor the ingredients included in the Chipotle Chicken Club. 

What was built for me was an extremely dry sandwich, without the listed items above.  Why would your online ordering system automatically default to not including the above listed items on the sandwich? They are staple ingredients in what I thought was literally an amazing sandwich.  

Not only was my lunch ruined by a glitch/kink in the online ordering system, and my money was not well spent, but my trust in SCD has now been diminished. Again, I've only ever had amazing things to say when verbally recommending SCD.  I'll know need to rethink my loyalty and search for a different sandwich shop that has a correctly working online ordering system. 

-Confused Sandwich Lover

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: Horrible front hostess management!!! 
Not the Amazing organization for the so called "Reservations" they take your name and then they call you when your table is ready..... And people basically go somewhere for "Drinks" while they wait for the table to be available, and honestly I think Well basically EVERYONE finds a place to eat here in downtown, so if they are hungry enough and tired of waiting.. While they have customers waiting for a table and when you see more than 4 tables open, and wondering why people are not being seated well .. " that's because we are calling the people on the list..." you can bet that when CUSTOMERS ARE WAITING TO EAT and are waiting AT THE restaurant not somewhere out there messing around... They want to make them wait the longest!!!??? What the F*^%#!!!!!???


Small portions. Decent sushi. Until next time. 
MAKE THEM SEAT YOU. DONT LET THEM MAKE YOU WAIT!!!!

Categories: Bakeries, Sandwiches, Food, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: With mediocre service ( at the take out side ) and food I couldn't help but give this place two stars. I had an arugula salad with salmon. It was just ok, tiny piece of overcooked salmon, the dressing was pretty good though. I'm
Not sure f they just think it's only locals who will come here but it's tourist season , they should be a little more friendly. When I approached the counter I said I would like the arugula salad to go please ... well we only do it to go ...... it was the tone.

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: I love this place so much, I don't even know where to begin. I went there for the 1st time a week ago. And as of today I've been there 4 times. The service is awesome. They answer questions and give great recommendations. I get an iced oat milk late, as well as an almond scone . So bomb.

Categories: Steakhouses, Local Flavor, Nightlife, Fish & Chips, Restaurants, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: Solid 3 1/2 stars...
we ordered:
clam chowder-4 stars. Lots of clams n not too salty
crab cakes-2 small crab cakes. 3 stars
seafood Cobb salad-big salad for $16.
salmon cedar-4 stars. Salmon was cooked to perfection
fish tacos-3 stars. The side corn was really good.
sea bass-3 stars. I am not sure if that was really sea bass we tasted.  Texture didn't taste like it.
Everything tasted good. Nothing really stands out tho.
Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Seafood, Steakhouses | Review: Go for the amazing views and beautiful setting. Food was just okay. And our waiter went missing for about 20 minutes.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: What a great Mexican restaurant with an authentic, yet vibrant and relaxing feel, right in the heart of Santa Barbara!

I dined here with a couple of friends on a Saturday evening before catching a movie across the street at the Arlington. The drink menu was pretty good, serving your usual Mexican beers, as well as some margaritas and mixed drinks. We sat in the outdoor patio, which had a great and relaxing ambience. Service was good and was checked up on often. The chips and salsa were a good start to a great meal. I had the burrito asado with grilled steak. They really pile it on with the guacamole and all the fixings on your burrito. The rice that came with it had great flavor. For about $17 (yes, pretty pricey for a burrito), you do get your money's worth, as they pack your burrito with a lot of meat and some sort of thin sauce/broth that had a really delicious, bold flavor without being too salty. It was all plated very elegantly.

For an elegant Mexican dinner in a relaxing atmosphere, come dine at Carlitos Cafe y Cantina!

Categories: Wine Bars, Restaurants, American (New), Nightlife, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: "4:30 empty restaurant on a Sat afternoon, it took 40 min to get 2 burgers, 6 oysters and some ceviche.  The burgers that supposedly were taking so long(cooked medium) came out shaped like baseballs, and one was overcooked(of course). Ceviche needed seasoning.  There's staff standing around in the kitchen and on the floor ; typical SB, no hustle.  Look, I know how it is, but when it costs this much and we were the only table in there service should be more attentive and food should come out faster.  Simply over-rated."

Categories: American (Traditional), Vegan, Restaurants | Review: Get ready to fall in love (or more in love) with vegan. This Montecito gem is perfection. To the tenth power. What a splendid experience. Every dish, drink, and every service touch was impeccable. Wish I lived in town because this would be a staple of my dining experiences!
Drinks: 5/5 (little miss sunshine and gold rush)
Appetizer: 5/5 (get the crab cakes...trust me)
Entrees: 5/5 (mushroom tostada) and 4/5 (polenta)
Dessert: 4/5 (apple crumble)
Service and ambiance: 5/5

Categories: Asian Fusion, Poke, Seafood, Food, Restaurants | Review: What the hell is this crap?  I paid $16 on Doordash for this?!?!  There's like 2 tiny pieces of octopus and 2 shrimps.. what ripoff!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: Delicious!! I had a double scoop in a waffle cone- island coconut and CA lemon zest. I HIGHLY recommend the CA lemon zest (if you like lemon-y things of course). It's soooooo good!! The coconut was good too but fairly standard as far as coconut ice creams go. I also tried the cinnamon which was really good too. In all, very creamy and full of flavor. Waffle cone too was crispy and flavorful, as it should be. 

It is off the beaten path (i.e. Not on State St) but thanks to the previous yelpers, we bought a Groupon ($5 for $10 worth of ice cream) and it was so worth it- and handy too, given that my double scoop in a waffle cone was over $7! Definitely on the more expensive side for ice cream but it's tasty :)

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Tentative service.
 Great food (and tasty options for vege- and pescatarians!).
 I had the palomita drink...sooo good and refreshing.
Chips and salsas were on point.
Will definitely be back when I'm in the area.

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: The service was amazing. Such nice people. They took care of our group of 17 very efficiently. And the food was great. Definitely recommend

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: I was not expecting to be so impressed and I was BLOWN away!!! I especially liked that I could get the Flight and not have to choose just one flavor but get 3 decent scoops of 3 different amazing flavors that I, of course, sampled ahead of time to make sure I chose wisely. I picked the squash gingery one, the lemony marionberry one and peanut butter chip one (those aren't the actual names but you get the idea) each was heavenly!!! I will be thinking back on this pleasurable experience for a very long time and looking forward to the next time that I get to go and I WILL GO AGAIN!!! We only visit SB on trips to visit our son at college and McConnell's is a bit out of the way but it is SO WORTH IT!!!!! Ice cream lovers will very much appreciate this sweet gem! Don't miss it!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Latin American, Mexican, Seafood | Review: Queso fundido was great, with mushrooms that gave it a fantastic texture. Loved the freshly-made in the kitchen tortillas. The mole con pollo or chicken mole taco is very tasty. The folks in the kitchen know how to make a good mole. The self serve salsas are very tasty, especially the smoke chile and the avocado salsa. The chips were a little dry and thicker than what I like but the pineapple salsa paired very nicely with them. Also enjoyed sitting in front of the kitchen and watching the food being made. Great job folks!

Categories: Soup, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Bars, Italian | Review: Great pizzas, wine and service! We had a wonderful dinner New Year's Day - heat lamps made it comfortable enough to eat outside. Can't recommend enough!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: To be fair, I don't eat here. My guys LOVE this place, but the line is usually so long that I just don't wan to wait. But my staff loves this place, my family loves this place and the super long line says others do to.

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: Such a cute name, I found this place through Yelp and wanted to try it out when we were in Santa Barbara. Early in the morning we saw bike riders stopping here to get their morning cup of joe. It was fun and exciting to see this since the name is called Handlebar. 

Overall the place has chairs on the outside so you are able to sit down and chat with one or two people. The inside is filled with goodies to buy from random wine, flour and even roasted coffee beans. Which I bought a bag of. 

I ordered a charcoal latte with a shot of espresso and it came with such a cute design. A little heart and I also got it with oat milk. This place is super adorable inside and out and even the name is super fitting. If I'm ever up here I wouldn't mind stopping by again to try out more of their other coffee creations! 

Just FYI Their store hours are from 7 AM till 1 PM currently.

Categories: Garage Door Services, Restaurants, Live/Raw Food, Food, Home Services, Mediterranean, Keys & Locksmiths, Burgers, Vegetarian, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Vegan, Salad | Review: It was our first and last time at this VERY casual (some diners weren't even wearing shoes) again the ambiance was very casual but the prices were high end...way to expensive for the execution of the food. The service was so slow, the food was inedible for me, the tacos had so much cumin I couldn't eat them.  The falafels had too much cumin and were hammered and so was the burger... w/ the strangest ketchup substitute... beet and clove purée, eeek!  what's wrong with straight up ketchup!  The polenta and sautéed veg was a good idea but it definitely needed salt and a cream substitute... oh and some flavor!  This place has a Michelin star??  It's hard to believe that.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: Nothing has changed since it was mikas sushi, they just changed the name and it brought in so much more business. Mondays! $1.99 handrolls: tuna, spicy tuna.spicy scallop, California, and salmon skin. My 3 that I get always? Spicy tuna, it is chock full of it! The spicy scallop has.scallop in every bite. And the salmon skin is so.crispy, it sometimes has chunks of fried salmon meat.still.attached...a yummy Monday treat!

Categories: Sushi Bars, Japanese, Restaurants | Review: No idea how this place is getting the good ratings it is. I knew it was going to be high priced sushi but I assumed then it would be good quality from all the reviews. We were wrong. 50 dollars for 2 rolls and edamame was so not worth it. My husbands roll was decent. He said it was worth more like 10 bucks though and it was generic. It also was not held together and the first piece completely feel apart when he tried to pick it up. Mine on the other hand came so sloppy, had a ridiculous amount of cream cheese in it and the avocado was all brown. I had to scrap some off because it tasted rotten. The tempura shrimp on the inside was rubbery, cold and tasted very old and fishy. I only had a couple pieces. My husband (who eats all my left overs) tried a piece but then said he didn't  want anymore cuz it tasted so gross and not fresh. We got a side of sauce with our Edamame that had too much of an over powering fishy aftertaste so we couldn't  eat it. And the 6 dollar edamame was extremely small for the price. Save your money and go get good sushi cheaper somewhere else.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Came here with a group of about 20+ and man can I just say service was incredible. Not to mention the food it was amazing. I am actually not the greatest fan of Mexican food however this proved to exceed my expectations. My favorite wasn't just the food but the "aguas frescas" which are traditional fruit drinks and the deserts filled with sweet goodness. If you are in the mood for anything super amazing order their burritos.

Categories: Restaurants, Hot Dogs | Review: Dave has come such a long way, from his hotdog cart on the East Side to his own place and now his Ice Cream shop! Dave's dogs are so unique and the best hot dogs by far!! His creations are not only delicious,  but a great way to change up the monotony of regular hot dogs wherever you go. I'm not much of a Mac & Cheese fan, but his Mac & Cheese dog is amazing! If you're looking for something unique, inexpensive and delicious, and are looking to support local businesses, then hit up Dave's dogs. You won't be disappointed, I promise! 
Three thumbs up Dave!!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: I came here for the first time last month when a friend and I took a road trip up the coast and decided to eat breakfast in Santa Barbara. I ordered the vanilla dipped French toast with berry sauce and it was delicious! However, the plate was rather big and I wasn't able to finish my meal. That's not a bad thing ;) So today, on the way back from a trip to Refugio State Beach, I decided to bring my dad here. This time, we only ordered one plate of the French toast, and it was quite enough. 
The waitresses are all very courteous and nice. I will definitely keep coming here every time I'm in Santa Barbara!

Categories: Restaurants, Chicken Wings, Sandwiches, Breakfast & Brunch, Pizza, Burgers | Review: Delicious! Stopped here on out way to San Diego. The bread is fantastic! We had the chipotle chicken sandwich and the buffalo blue cheese burger! Homemade sauces and chips were great. Wish we could find something like this in Texas!

Categories: Sandwiches, Vegan, Cafes, Vegetarian, Restaurants | Review: This is sorta my spot if I have a date with a woman who says that she is a vegetarian. It doesn't happen much but when it does this is usually where I take her cause this is about as far down the vegeterian wormhole I ever want to go! It's a meat veggy vegan mix and yummy ;)

Categories: Food, Grocery | Review: Literally, the most raggedy looking, pretentious, low class employees imaginable work here. The prices are terrible, no surprises there. I used EBT and a debit card to buy a few things. (I work at a food bank. We promote these programs because college is expensive and students are hungry, hello.) The girls couldn't figure out how to work the card and tried to blame/guilt/shame me for using EBT when they were the ones that couldn't use their own cash registers from 1985. Worst customer service ever. They have zero standards for their employees that come to their shifts looking like they're baked and just rolled out of bed after their one night stands before coming to work. Please get some standards IV Market. UCSB needs to do something about places like this.

Categories: Noodles, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Creperies, Chinese, Asian Fusion | Review: The buns were filled with mostly fat cuts of meat that weren't braised with the right braising liquids and the rice bowls were filled with corn kernels and Japanese pickled radish. Reading the name and the menu, I expected the restaurant to be serving Chinese, but was thoroughly disappointed by the fusion confusion. I'be cooked better braised pork belly than the chefs at Lao wang and i paid less for the ingredients. The noodle broth was not Ma or La and it was extremely under seasoned. The hot sauce at the bottom of the bun was filled with heart burn inducing taco sauce rather than traditional plum or duck sauce.

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants | Review: I live in the Santa Ynez Valley and headed over the hill to SB with a friend of mine.  We decided to stop at Longboards and catch the Fiesta Bowl Football Game and get some food.  The location is great and so are the views.  We sat at the Bar and saw an awesome sunset.  We both had Mojitos to drink and ordered some food.  Mojitos were great.  I had the Burnt Ends Sandwich which was awesome...loved it.  My friend had the Maui Burger which looked great but the Meat Patties were very thin....not what you would expect from a $12 burger.  All in all the food and drinks were great.  The Service is another story.....the place was not that busy and the 2 times we wanted refills on the cocktails we had to ask the bartender.  My glass sat on the edge of the bar empty for 20 minutes before he came over...and then I still had to ask him for another drink.  My friend and I are both in the Hospitality/Food Service Industry and we both agreed the service sucked.  Because of that, I probably would not go back...there are plenty of other places in SB who are more than happy to give there customers good, attentive service.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: So this place is a little small inside but they have a nice outside patio where you can take your drink (they have a full bar) and your food outside and watch the people, traffic etc. The food was good and everyone was helpful and friendly.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Bars, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Nightlife, Seafood, Cuban | Review: What a delicious treat. I went to the Shaker Mill with some friends from out of town last night and we had an amazing time. A great representation of the Santa Barbara nightlife. I really enjoyed their beer selection and my fiancé loved the mango lassi (a little too
Much haha). 

I really like how there are different food options that you can bring to the Shaker Mill area (both Cubaneo and modern times restaurant). 

Our friends visiting from LA had a great time.

Categories: American (New), Tapas/Small Plates, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants, Burgers | Review: Popped in here for a weekend lunch before hitting the tasting rooms.  The place is a nice outdoor space with lots of shade.  The large picnic tables are built for community dining, but they are so wide.  Too late now, but if they were just a bit narrower, you could get more folks on the patio and it wouldn't feel like my wife, who was sitting across the bench from me was in a different area code.

The sandwiches were good and filling.  We both ended up with the tri-tip, and we could have easily shared it and maybe added in a lite app.  They also end up being a tad bit pricey, especially if you add in the avocado (that's a profit item for the restaurant) and horseradish cream.  I enjoyed a beer, which was probably the best value on the menu, and my wife had a glass of wine which was a generous pour, but not the best wine.

Overall it was a nice spot with good food and a great option if you have a group and can get in before it gets too crowded.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Nice place. I loved shrimp fried rice and Tum kho. Service was fine requested a heater that was pulled next to me

Categories: Restaurants, Delis, Food, Specialty Food, Grocery, Sandwiches | Review: What can I say that the previous reviewer Angela didn't say. She nailed it. Best Gyro anywhere! Amazing selection of meats, cheeses and salads. Our favorite lunch destination in SB.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: This is my new go-to Thai food restaurant in the SB area!! SO SO SO good; every plate is absolutely delicious. The pad thai was the perfect texture and amount of peanut sauce, the yellow chicken curry (my favorite food) was flavorful and satisfying, the panang curry was hearty and a beautiful creamy orange color, the coconut drink that I ordered with the meal was fresh and delicious, the mango sticky rice was a perfectly sweet finish to the meal... Definitely coming here again soon! I'd say the only thing I wouldn't reorder would be the pineapple fried rice; it was super tasty, but felt more like something I could whip up at home, so I'd put my money towards more yellow chicken curry or mango sticky rice. 

Plus, the service was pretty quick, they've got a spacious outdoor seating area, and the decorations and paintings are beautiful. Thank you Zen Yai! Can't wait to be back.
Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Sooooo good.  Only reason why they got 4 stars and not 5 was because its a little expensive.  Other then that I loved it.

Categories: Restaurants, Bagels, Coffee & Tea, Food, Bubble Tea, Donuts | Review: I usually don't like stopping into places before closing but I ended up popping in 30 min before closing. I just got a thai tea boba and a white sprinkled donut. Both were pretty good! The nice lady at the counter even threw in some donut holes for free! Overall would definitely stop by again.

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: This is a review based on the service. We clearly ordered two meals and the cashier left out one of our orders and failed to charge/put in the second one. He claims that we did not correct him when he repeated our order, but we had initially clearly ordered two items. We waited 45 minutes before the cashier came out to let us know and then he proceeded to ask us to have the second item charged. He did not offer anything to correct his mistake.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: Everything was special about this place from the well informed bartender to the tasty tapas. Loquita has great food, drinks, and the service to match. The food here is as authentic as you can get without leaving the country. The diver scallops were amazing and the toast with tomato is just like you will find in Granada. I highly recommend and can't wait to go back and try the paella.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (New), Food | Review: Solid solid solid, everything it's reviewed to be. Don't miss this spot. Amazing grub, coffee etc. really terrific experience.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, French, Vegan, Seafood, Asian Fusion, Gluten-Free, American (New) | Review: im very honest with these reviews- look at my other ones. i'm not going to make excuses for places but im also not going to forget to appreciate the little things... and this was one of the best vegan meals i have ever had... EVER had... they are not even entirely vegan which is the shocker- as most non vegan places believe a plate full of vegetables is what us vegans eat.. this place was truly imaginative, and provided me with a perfect four course meal..  yes, i, Shirley, said perfect... a rare thing indeed.
i really wanted to have this place cater my wedding, the second i realized every bite i had was magical, i was truly obsessed with the idea of them catering my wedding- and now that i'm planning it... they're gone. so sad.

wish places like this stayed open.... this and pure luck in silverlake... COME BACK PLEASE!

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Bakeries | Review: Great bread, good pouched egg and homemade jam.  The waitress screwed up with the cheese choice on the omelette, but her attentiveness made up for it. Off the main street, looks like more of the locals hang around here. Would go back.

Categories: Noodles, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Creperies, Chinese, Asian Fusion | Review: the only place in IV to get good pork belly. Since it's the only place it's very good but can sometimes be a bit spotty. If I had to say this place had a food reliability scale it would be around 90% which is still an A

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (New), French | Review: This is and all ways has been the best restaurant in So Cal if you want real California Cuisine just go there and relax eat and enjoy because it does not get any better!  Also it is pricey because the chef only use the best products he can get that why the menu changes daily.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I passed by this restaurant on the way home to LA from Buelton a few weeks ago.  I had the Holy Ole Mole burrito.  It was the best burrito I have ever had.  It was a pretty good size and I finished the whole thing.  The basket of chips covered with mole sauce and queso at the beginning of the meal was a great warm up to the burrito.  You would think that it would be too much mole to eat in one meal but it was just right.  I can't wait to go back.  I am even tempted to drive up just to eat their burrito and drive straight back to LA.  Yes, its that good.

Categories: Korean, Restaurants, American (New), Mexican, Tacos, Nightlife, Fast Food, Bars | Review: Absolutely, hands down the best seafood tacos!!  This is a thousand times better than Nikka tacos from yesterday. Five stars 

Everyone this is the place to be. Definitely walking away with a smile on this face

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Nightlife | Review: Being a big fan of American ale, union ale, kill the keg, and your food, I was disappointed in the change of happy hour.  We went there today to get SEVERAL"well" drinks at the expected happy hour price of $3/each only to find out when the bill came that the happy hour had changed to $5.50 a drink and now is considered a premium well drink with the finnlandia vodka.  Calling a drink a "premium well drink" in the first place is misleading and a oxymoron.  We had no idea they changed the prices, and felt a bit scammed by the whole deal.  Finnlandia is hardly a premium vodka to begin with.  1.75 liter for 19.99 
http://www.bevmo.com/Shop/ProductDetail.aspx/Spirits/Vodka/Finlandia/Finlandia-Vodka/_/R-4-3582

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I had Chicken tortilla soup today. It was absolutely delicious!! Can't wait for Luz/executive chef of the restaurant. Kudos to the chef!! Love her sister Juanita. Nicest sever/waitress EVER!! LOVE BOTH OF THEM DEARLY!! Would definitely recommend this restaurant ALWAYS!!

Categories: Food, Desserts, Gelato, Caterers, Event Planning & Services, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: The gelato was perfect. Fresh, not too sweet and great texture.  The price for a 3 flavors cup was inexpensive. I wish they were in my town.

Categories: American (Traditional), Bakeries, Restaurants, Italian, Breakfast & Brunch, Food | Review: Fresco is a solid, casual, easy spot to get a sit down meal. They make good soups, salads, and wraps, and always have a pizza and pasta choice. The prices are reasonable and the service is usually pretty good, though it is an order at the front place. They have lots of patio seating, which is great. 

My go to order is the turkey club wrap, which is delicious. I get the raspberry walnut salad as my side and would suggest you do too.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food, Bubble Tea | Review: I have been coming here since their grand opening, and although their boba is not the best- it is still better than most places here. Recently, I feel like they have been watering down their drinks, esp the jasmine milk tea.

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: We had dessert for all 6 of us. The Danish Donut was outstanding - contender for best donut ever!

The Sara Berhard was 5 stars.

The Citroen was light and refreshing.

Strawberry Fragile was at least 6 thumbs up.

The butterings was excellent, very good and we got some to go.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Absolutely love this place! I rave about this place to everyone I know! I would travel once a year to different areas in Mexico and my favorite part would be the food. Since the pandemic I haven't traveled but this place fulfills the craving!! The food is AHHHHMAZINGGGG. The most recent visit was for my birthday in September and I felt so special. The staff were extremely polite. They play "las mañanitas" (the Mexican version of happy birthday) throughout the restaurant speakers. The song glitched and stopped and the staff kept singing. It was awesome! truly felt happy. I ordered their cucumber margarita with Mezcal. Mezcal is very smooth! Their enchiladas de mole! Chiefs kiss! 

So if you're looking for great food, drinks and service this is the place! 

I would advise to check their average waiting time chart to avoid the crowds! They do not have reservations. First come first serve!

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Wineries, Food | Review: A diamond in the rough when it comes to Petite Syrah and Grenache. And for the price point ($35-40/bottle), Jaffurs offers a great value.

The tasting room and bottling area are one in the same. It's quite humble, but don't let that fool you! Sit down and sample a few varietals, you will not be disappointed. 

Probably the favorite wine of the day was the 100% Grenache. Bought two bottles and can't wait to open 'em up with a nice dinner.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Good food, slow service..don't let the name fool ya;) lol really hip though there's an ultra chill vibe, you can definitely tell the coolio foolios congregate at this fine establishment.

Categories: Restaurants, Delis, Food, Specialty Food, Grocery, Sandwiches | Review: Let's just say if you want a good sandwich or a fresh salad some very unique kind of drinks then this is the place to go. With their thursday and I was not disappointed

Categories: American (New), Mexican, Restaurants | Review: So good! The salsa bar is perfect for a salsa addict like myself. Delicious tacos and quesadillas. Environment was clean sand service was fast!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Odd concept...  They have a very nice dining area that is fine dining quality, but they mix it with a fast food service atmosphere.  You have to wait in line to order food and hope you find a table because it's so busy.  The food was average.  I prefer more authentic Mexican and this feels more like fancy chain food Mexican.

Categories: Lounges, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants, Hookah Bars, American (New) | Review: Mike's sister had her rehearsal dinner here the night before her wedding and unfortunately at the time, I was on this super strict diet/exercise program and wasn't SUPPOSED to eat anything that wasn't delivered to me that morning in a cooler bag....however who am I to turn down free (well, not paid for by ME anyways) food and wine, especially when I didn't have to drive! 

So I ate and boozed to my heart's content. There were communal bowls of Lobster Mac and Cheese with Pancetta that were almost the cause of a couple of fork fights. This dish was ridiculous and I did not let myself feel guilty AT ALL for gobbling up more than my fair share (and sneaking bites off of Mike's plate when he was too busy yapping to notice my lightening quick fork skills). The Crab Crusted Hawaiian Walu was also out of this world (and not something you often see on menus). The meal was rounded out at the end with platters of fresh baked cookies and pitchers of "adult" milk that were spiked but I didn't taste much if any alcohol (am I that much of an alcoholic?).

Overall, I LOVED this place. LOVED. It has a sexy vibe and sultry decor and is located right in the middle of all the action on State Street. Combine that with crave-worthy dishes and I am head-over-heels in L-O-V-E.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Best ice cream I ever had.
I don't like it when they make desserts overly sweet.
This place you taste the real flavor because the sweetness don't overwhelm your moth. Lol

Categories: Restaurants, Indian, Himalayan/Nepalese | Review: Thought I'd give it one more chance...  same ABC gristly chicken-inedible!  Fried breaded Paneer- as dry as a shoe, It is such a shame because their sauce is good, actually very tasty.  I am so upset, so expensive for ickness...  I will stick to L.A. for Indian.  I have been eating and cooking Indian food for 42 years, I really wish they would just use better meat, white meat, or please at least no fat and gristle.

Categories: Dance Clubs, Nightlife, Bars | Review: The crowd isn't pretentious, but generally younger UCSB students (esp. on Thursdays- aka: College Night, no cover).  
Ever heard of "getting sh*tty at the kitty?" This may happen to you here, but it's so laid back that you needn't fear judgement, just get up on stage and work what you've got!

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Specialty Food, Farmers Market, Grocery, Health Markets | Review: Love Isabella Gourmet. I absolutely adore finidng new tasty treats to try out and old favorites. I especially love to grab some food and take it upstairs and enjoy some delicious Cebada Wine.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: We had the best experience here. Our waiter was extremely knowledgeable on every menu item and recommendations. Drinks and tapas were phenomenal and the service was exceptional. Would love to go back and order more off the menu - we didn't have a single thing we didn't like!

Categories: Food, Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood, Desserts | Review: My wife and I discovered Litos after we watched it on DDD. We decided to make a trip to Santa Barbara and try this place out.
Let me make something clear; if your standard for good Mexican food is Taco Bell, then don't bother coming here because you will simply not appreciate this place.
Litos is 100% authentic Mexican food, and it is delicious. From their pozole to their menudo, their carnitas to their beans, rice and tongue tacos. The food here is prepared with care and their recipes are amazing.  
The environment is very casual and you have to go up to the counter to order. As soon as you sit down they'll bring you some amazing chips and salsa.
With all this being said, this place is a solid 5 stars and a must for any Mexican food lover visiting Santa Barbara. You will not be disappointed.

Categories: Seafood, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Drove out on to the pier with a few friends for lunch.  Two of us remarked to each other how long it had been since we both had been there.  Over 35 years for both of us.  We sat outside on the patio, it was very nice!  The service was reasonable.  The menu was on the sparse side.  The prices were on the high side.  The food was underwhelming!  Nothing horrible, but nothing extraordinary.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Sooooo.... We had high hopes for this spot.  Here on Saturday wanting a good Bloody Mary and brunch food.  The signature Bloody Mary is trash.  They tried dressing it up with shrimp and a slice of bacon, but the flavor of the drink itself is LACKING.  Total bummer.  The lobster Mac n cheese was literally soup!  The flavor was bland and the sauce was SO watery.  They have the audacity to charge $24.95 for that garbage.  Unbelievable!!  Lastly, the cured salmon plate was yet another let down.  The dill cream was decent, but the entire entree was beyond basic with zero flavor.  The capers didn't even help and it isn't served with red onion, which it desperately needed.  This entire meal, including our drinks, were a waste of money.  Disappointing.

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: It's a nice dine in place located in downtown.
Food is good. As compared to other really good Indian restaurants that I have been to, the food here is average in taste. Price is affordable  Place is clean and hygienic and staff is friendly. I like the ambience around. I would recommend this place to everyone. Parking is easily available.

There is always a waiting line. SO, you might wana try  asking for reservations before you go.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: It was very tough deciding on either 3, or 4 stars. All the employees were very nice. They dont have duck which was the only reason why we came in...wanting duck...Thai place doesnt have duck SMFH. Since we were there we were like well lets try something new today. Ordered many plates, all were freakin delicious, the chicken wings had next to no meat on them though. Like the chickens must have been starved before they were killed because it was just skin over bone which was super weird. Yet what little meat was on there including the skin tasted awesome. Overall I'd give it 3.5 stars. And IMO 95% of Thai spots have, and very well should include duck on the menu. Its been a long ass time since I've come across one that doesn't.

Categories: Food, Arts & Entertainment, Wine Tasting Room, Local Flavor, Wineries | Review: Wow! I just got my Fall shipment and it included the Angelique 2011 Syrah!! LOVE it!! I have been working in a wine tasting room the past few months after leaving Los Angeles and moving to Seattle, Washington (inspired by a wine tasting trip to Santa Barbara). I have been tasting a lot of wine lately...some really delicious and complex, some just kind of not quite ready and missing the mark. I've been anxious to try some wine that slapped my attention and left a mark. This wine (beautifully bottled btw) had a late night love affair with my mouth.  Wow. This bottle of Syrah is a beautiful, thick, deep rich plum color, incredibly friendly, warm and jammy, (huge blackberries and/or raspberries). Had some with a small scoop of vanilla bean ice cream in front of the fire. Yeah. True story. This is truly a beautiful dessert wine. Very pleased and excited to share (and buy more...).

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: Love this spot. Jaime and Rudy take care of me quite well. The wings are on point and so are the drinks. I like my drinks like my women, strong and outspoken. The absol-fucking-lutly thing wrong is the fixed bar stools. For a big man, you can't fit. They don't spin correctly. From a operators stance, if its a banging night, how do you make more room for people to order? More walking space, raise occupancy during fiesta... blah blah blah, list goes on. but I went on a tangent. Try the wings with the habanero mango sauce ya'll!

Categories: Szechuan, Chinese, Restaurants | Review: Had the MooShoo pork for dinner; it was great; looking forward to my next meal there.
The reviews were helpful for my next choice to eat.
GREAT FOOD!

Categories: Delis, Gluten-Free, Fast Food, Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: Great sandos and great salad too. It's a bit over priced but it is in downtown. Ordered it on grubhub and it said that it would be ready at 4:40 but when I got there it wasn't ready. I had to wait 10 minutes extra.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: Worst service imaginable. The restaurant was very slow
 We were seated right away, then waited 50 minutes just to get our drinks served . Food served was mediocre . Will never go back. And it was underwhelming and over hyped. The only plus is they have nice lighting but you will starve staring at them.

Categories: Food, Nightlife, Mexican, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Excellent food!great bartender. Loved the El Diablo drink and seriously enjoyed the Paloma enchilada with the amazing chipotle sauce. The chili rellano was amazing as well. Will be back for sure.

Categories: Health Markets, Food, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Specialty Food, Grocery, Restaurants, Delis, Meat Shops | Review: I was tempted to give this place only 4 stars due to the price, but after stumbling onto the 8:30 half off fest it surely deserves 5 stars.  It is lovely store, has a great produce selection, and a delicious array of pre-made options.  A really great find for travelers from Berkeley used to the Bowl and Monterey Markets.

Categories: Juice Bars & Smoothies, Breakfast & Brunch, Brazilian, Restaurants, Food, Salad, Acai Bowls | Review: Finding culture in Santa Barbara can be a tricky task given that the population is predominantly Caucasian and Spanish-American/Mexican (the one exception). I am always stoked when I do find a place owned by immigrants who pour their heart and soul into their business. From the minute you step up to the entrance of Brasil Arts Cafe, you can tell that it is Brazilian given the flags, bright colors, art on the walls, and difficult to pronounce food items in Portuguese. 

I have visited several times and unfortunately, the availability of all their food items is hit or miss. If they have Coxinha ("Brazilian Chicken Fritters;" shredded chicken meat, covered in dough, breaded and fried) and Pão de Queijo ("Brazilian Cheese Bread;" baked cheese bun) in their display case, you've hit the golden prize! Pão de Queijo is a great gluten-free alternative to those with dietary restrictions. Both have spectacular flavors and make perfect fried snacks. They also offer fresh fruit smoothies and acai bowls.

If you want to jump in and try an authentic, larger Brazilian meal, they offer fish, chicken, and steak accompanied by rice, beans, farofa (toasted cassava or cornflour mixture placed on top of other foods for texture), vinaigrette, chimichurri, and fried plantains. I enjoy that you get a little taste of everything with the entrees. You can never go wrong with a zesty chimichurri or sweet plantains!

Be sure to peek in the back of the cafe near the restrooms where there is a gym that offers classes of Capoeira, an Afro-Brazilian martial art combining elements of dance, acrobatics, and music. I absolutely appreciate how Brasil Arts Cafe has become the center for Brazilian awareness, culture, and community in Santa Barbara. Check out their website for other events that involve classes, dances, exercise, concerts, and other activities. Stay strong and represent, Brazil Arts Cafe!

Categories: Restaurants, Vietnamese | Review: It is evident that the owner/chef takes great pride in the quality of his food.  The vegetables were bright, the basil fresh and the broth was free of any oil or grease. It was like eating a restorative soup.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: I love this place!!! Fresh, flavorful and cravalicious! The Pad Thai is the BEST I've ever had!!! Legit! The potstickers that the owners mom makes are a must have! Nothing like a homemade dumpling of love! Can't wait to return soon!

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: I might move back just to eat here anytime. Every dish was served perfectly. I had the mushroom risotto. Loved it. Quiet enough to talk comfortably with your dinner quests. It was my birthday. They came out with ice cream and the best chocolate sauce desert can buy.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: Horribly rude bouncer at the door, $20 cover and there was nothing going on inside.
I asked him if I could take a peek inside to see if we would like to stay, he was rude and told me no, when the door opened I saw not one person inside and it looked dead, also not a fan of the music.
His attitude was a total turn off and my husband and I went to State St to enjoy our night out. Not worth returning!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: We had a large group event in their back room. Even though we had people who joined us later than the reservation time, they were very attentive and continued to take drink orders when people arrived. The food was great and everyone was served together and in a timely manner. We stayed after lunch and talked and they never acted like they wanted us to leave. We brought a cake and they provided plates and a server.

I would highly recommend Del Pueblo for a small or large group-it was a really positive experience.

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Jazz & Blues, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: All I can say is WOW!!! This spot hidden way back in a business complex beats all expectations! 

Retro? Check! 
Great Drinks? Check!
Dive Bar?? Not even close! Looks like a Dive Bar on the outside, but a classy jazz lounge on the inside!!

So clean and meticulously put together! I was so impressed! So many try to get this vibe and fail, but owner Dawn knows how to do bars right! Mercury anyone? 

It is like walking through the door back in time! 

Don't miss the stellar patio which is amazing, with plenty of alcoves to get comfy!!

Check this spot out and say Hi to the bartender Kyle who takes pride in his cocktails and his craft!

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, Italian, Pizza, Restaurants | Review: Advertised Gluten Free pizza, but when you go to order, you have to order large. For lunch $22.00 was too much to spend. I had a Mediterranean Salad had to hunt for the few olive pieces and just a few dried tomatoes. $9.00 for lettuce. The waitress was very nice though.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: Awesome!! Lots of choices. My daughter got mint chip and cookie dough single with a waffle cone. I had a bananas and salted Carmel with a milk chocolate. 

All four were excellent. I love the history of this place and I'm so glad I stopped. It was perfect for a hot day. 

Parking can be a challenge here and there is no bathroom. You have to walk a half block down the street.

Categories: Food, Vegetarian, Pop-Up Restaurants, Burgers, Restaurants, Food Trucks | Review: Great burger and the spicy juju sauce is delicious on both burger and fries. Seems like the dang burger crew has been moving around to different pop up spots so be sure to check their Instagram to find them. We checked them out Sunday at corktree cellars,  perfect because you could enjoy a patio cocktail while you wait for your burger. Their veggie burger is super tastey too (tried a bite from my sister's). The crunchy corn meal outside is a great idea to keep the black beans and quinoa from being mush like alot of other places.  Might have to go veggie next time

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Seafood, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Venues & Event Spaces | Review: This was a very good casual place to eat.  I stuck with two appetizers (steamed muscles and fried pickles) and my wife had the buttermilk fried chicken.  She was absolutely amazed by the vegetables and the chicken itself was very good.  Our portions were quite large and the wait staff was very attentive.  We were there on a Monday night, so the MNF game was on the large, HD TV by the bar.  There was heated outdoor seating available, but we chose the cozy tables inside.  Overall, this was a great place to go.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: This restaurant delivers really consistent excellent customer service and is a delight to visit for both breakfast and lunch. Not to mention they have incredible salsa!

Categories: Food, Live/Raw Food, Restaurants, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Asian Fusion, Poke, Seafood, Food Delivery Services | Review: First time ordering food here and very satisfied! Ordered a small bowl and it filled me up pretty well. The avocado was only $1 extra and they gave you an entire avocado. Service and employees were very friendly, helpful, and quick. Tastes were fresh and delicious, will forsure come back.

Categories: Restaurants, Hot Dogs | Review: Although Daves story is that of a success, (Congratulations). This place is all hype Wah wah wah. . . que the trombone. 
  A friend whom is an implant to SB (alum) was more than eager to introduce a fellow Angelino to what is arguably the "best" hot dog spot in town. Let me tell you one thing if this is true for SB I feel bad for you (sorry not sorry). Maybe it's the spot for comfort foods&fried desserts & what not (IDK didn't have any) But by no means is this a place for a good ole loaded hot dog. 
        With a variety of options my selection was clear & as was my buddies . . Huge let down. It's as simple as cooking the hot dog Bc it was far too cool & mushy try cooking it for a few more minutes & the bun wasn't toasted or even warm!? Maybe even adding mayo to the bun?? It's was far less than sub par. The toppings weren't what I had imagined when hearing raving of these loaded dogs, not generousw/ the toppings. Dry, dry, dry (3xs so you know I mean it) In one of the photos below you can see the separation of hot dog& toppings. To get a visual of the dry.  As far as pricing I was surprised considering how much loaded dog spots are in LA.  

Could've just been a bad day but my friend said they taste that way. I really wanted to like this place Bc of the back story. 

A guest ahead of us in line was in for his 2nd serving of Elote w/ takis (something new to me at the time). good for business, but I wasn't a fan I got an elote w/ 1/2 takis/ regular and I wouldn't have either again. 

We did meet Dave my friend being an implant was familiar w/ him and Dave greeted him he was very sweet welcoming and appeared to be keeping things together.  One thing I was a fan of: the logo! 

Plentiful parking, indoor seating Mexican Coke's& beverages & little chips as well. Well priced.


FYI Dave I ACCIDENTALLY deleted the Dec review my goal wasn't to make it more amusing, I save all review drafts.
Categories: Chinese, Cafes, Taiwanese, Restaurants | Review: Very delicious and convinient! Especially the sesame cold noodle with fried chicken. It's my favorite!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: We were in SB for four days and didn't have great service in numerous places. I understand that with covid , restaurants are struggling and adjusting. Boathouse was beyond great!! Location, food and especially the employees. We can't wait to go back!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Bakeries, Desserts, Food Delivery Services, Patisserie/Cake Shop | Review: Insomnia Cookies - what a concept!

Firstly, I give major kudos to a business opening in the midst of Covid. That is not an easy feat and I wish them nothing but success. That said, I feel success will find them quick due to their location. 

The store is on the 400 block of State and is open late - the ideal recipe for victory. You've had a night out on the town and want to cap it with something sweet? Say no more, Insomnia has you covered. 

I went in and tried two of their cookies. They had a nice assortment and the cookies came in different sizes. As these confections are artisanal, you will see that they are priced accordingly. That said, pricing is still low enough to justify ordering one or two to snack on - or order by the dozen as well.
