The two cookies I had were fresh and delicious. I will definitely be back again when I'm in the mood for a sweet treat!

Categories: Korean, Barbeque, Restaurants | Review: Been to this restaurant several times and have never been disappointed with the service and quality. I want to really commend the manager in his or her selection of servers. In particular, servers Brian and Grace have really made my time eating so terrific. I have never had a bad experience with any of the staff here and they are more willing to provide drinks, meat, side dishes, etc. 

I used to go to Gen in Oxnard, but with such a great place to eat right here in Santa Barbara, there's no need to go anywhere else!! 

Keep up the great work!!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Food was good. Buffet was a little scary as you weren't really 100% what was on there. (I think there was a deep friend been burrito?). We checked it out but went with menu options because lord only know how many people have sneezed on that thin. Service was meh. 

We were seated and within about 5 minutes a lady came by to get our drink orders. That was the last we saw of anyone for about 20 minutes. Then our soft drinks came, but bar drinks still not there. We placed our dinner order at that point because we were starving. About another 15 minutes food comes out. Keep in mind, almost 40 minutes and we STIL haven't got the drinks from the bar. Waitress said 'They're backed up". How on earth do you get backed up THAT long? We could have walked to the ABC store and come back and still beat the bartender. Finally drinks came, but were made wrong. We didn't say anything because at the rate they come out, we'd be finished eating and in bed before they hit the table.

Took forever to check out and leave also. Server was nice but definitely not worth much more than a 15% tip.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Food Delivery Services, Caterers, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Wedding Planning, Nightlife, Food, Event Planning & Services | Review: Recently had our wedding here. The venue was gorgeous and the food was absolutely delicious! All of our guests had a great time! The score downgrade is attributed to the planning. I very much recommend planning an event at Rincon Beach Club however with the assistance of an outside planner who will make sure every detail is correct.
The communication among planners was inconsistent. One person would tell me one thing then I would receive different information a few days later from someone else. I would make an inquiry and often wait a week or so before a reply or have to call to get the issue/ concern addressed. It was a very stressful planning experience from the start up to 2 days prior to our wedding.

Categories: Salad, Restaurants, Pizza, Sandwiches, Italian | Review: Really great dinner.  Pizza awesome. And VEGAN cheese option will bring us back as they can accommodate a dairy allergy.  Big shout out to our server FED....overall great dinner in the patio.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Food has always been decent and huge portions. Salsa bar is tasty. Cashiers have always been very  aloof and not very friendly. Biggest complaint is how terribly uncomfortable the chairs have always been. Even the ones they've had in the past before thes

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: Amazing ice cream!! Must try the salted caramel chip. 

Be aware that parking is super limited so I suggest parking a block or two away. Always worth the walk!

Categories: Bubble Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food | Review: Boba here is good, but not great. My drink lacked flavor and the boba was hard to chew...it could've been cooked a little longer. 
I've had many boba drinks and this is just okay. I'll try again next time, in a few months, to see if there's any improvement. 

Other than that, the store is in a good location and is very clean/organized.

Categories: American (Traditional), Vegan, Restaurants | Review: Great vegan food, but too expensive for vegetables. Interesting menu items,  the wild mushroom tostada is very good, but $19 for a tostada is a bit ridiculous . Small restaurant so make a reservation and don't expect a lot of privacy when dining. Dressy casual attire, nice for a special occasion.  I think the food is very good vegan food, but it's a splurge spot in Montecito.
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Yum.  Delicious. Just had blood orange margarita. Guacamole is excellent.  Chips tasty.  And fast too!!!  I recommend.

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Burgers, Fish & Chips, Sandwiches | Review: Came here for a quick snack before driving home. My friends and I intended to eat light but we ended up ordering more food. The top pleasers were definitely the oysters and dinner rolls for me. We ordered salmon tacos with fries, house made chips, and clam chowders. The salmon tacos and clam chowder lacked seasoning. We were really happy with the rest of the meal so we didn't mind. Every staff member was welcoming and helpful as well. They all displayed positive attitude despite being busy. We appreciate that and we enjoyed our time here.

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: We drove 3 hours to come here! It worths every minute. Love the broiled salmon and Nikka Rockerfeller. Service is super fast, we didnt have to wait for the food at all!

Categories: Food, Italian, Pizza, Fast Food, Gelato, Chicken Wings, Sandwiches, Salad, Restaurants | Review: Ordered the Margherita Pizza to test out their pizzas. It was really great. The sauce(which is different than the sauce they use for other pizzas) was light but still flavorful. The Mozzarella tasted fresh too. Could use a little more Basil sprigs but the taste was awesome. Would definitely recommend it.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Los Agaves was listed as #16 on the just released "Top 100 Restaurants in the U.S. for 2016" by Yelp. 

The food here definitely makes the grade in my opinion.  This is one restaurant where you don't have to spend big bucks to get premier quality food.  Some of the most expensive restaurants in the U.S. (French Laundry in Napa, etc.) are on the list, but Los Agaves rated higher in customer satisfaction. 

Definitely worth a visit.
Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: The food was amazing-so unique and fresh. Service was also excellent! I definitely recommend the Northern Thai noodle bowl the monkeyshine ice cream!

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Located in downtown SB, Opal was the perfect place for my group of friends and I to end our day trip to Santa Barbara. The service at this place is FANTASTIC - very warm and accommodating even though this place was bustling on a Sunday evening. The interior is adorable - very European with tiled floors, red napkins, and French themed paintings. The prices are also very reasonable for what they serve. Entrees run from $14-$36. 

The menu offers a wide array of dishes, including many seafood items! All of the appetizers sounded amazing. There was a phyllo encrusted shrimp appetizer and an ahi tuna appetizer that both looked appealing to me, but alas, all I had room for was an entree and dessert.

Three girls in my group, including me, ordered the warm seafood salad ($14). I highly recommend this dish. Listed as a local favorite, this salad was huge (I couldn't finish it... and I always finish my plate)! I absolutely loved the creamy garlic dressing that was tossed into the mixed greens. The shrimp, salmon, and bits of scallop atop this salad were also delicious. 

Someone else ordered the lemongrass encrusted salmon with a coconut curry sauce, topped with julienned vegetables ($23?). This dish was also very good. The curry sauce actually had a strong coconut curry flavor which I loved. This entree is also listed as a local favorite and I definitely recommend it as well as the warm seafood salad.

The other girls in our group ordered the crab encrusted salmon ($29?) and the paella ($24). Both were good, but I wouldn't recommend them as highly as the seafood salad and the lemongrass salmon. 

For dessert, we informed our server that we had two birthday girls among us and I told him their names. The kitchen spelled out "Happy Birthday Amanda and Michelle" in chocolate on our dessert plate and topped the dessert with a candle. We ordered the "Like Water for Chocolate" Surprise ($9) which turned out to be a huge bonbon looking like thing. It was chocolate and vanilla mouse with a hard chocolate shell sitting in a small pool of raspberry sauce. I'm a chocoholic and this dessert was so good. The dessert used dark chocolate which I loved. I was licking my spoon :).

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, French, Salad, Sandwiches, Desserts, Bakeries, Food | Review: Love this place. Great coffee and fresh croissants! The crouqe madam is Amazing too. Highly recommend this place for breakfast or lunch. 
Just speed up the line.
Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: I like Starbucks but the bathroom here is filthy!  There are many other options so I won't be back here.

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Nightlife, Pubs, American (New), Wine Bars, Restaurants, Italian, Coffee & Tea, Bars, Food | Review: The location is great and the food was ok. Our service was good. The negative is the outdoor tables and chairs. We saw one chair break when the guy sat down. The outdoor furniture looks and feels like they bought them at the 99 cent store or Walmart. If you upgrade your furniture I will give a 4 star. 
Alan

Categories: Desserts, Restaurants, Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Delis, Food Trucks | Review: In celebration of Latinx Heritage Month, my sweet tooth couldn't help itself but to share quite the hidden treasure in Santa Barbara:
Fernando's Churros.
Off a Westside side street...
behind Rosale's Mexican Restaurant and Auto Zone...
in a vacant parking lot...
sits the bright orange Fernando's Churros truck. 

Bring those bills, henny!! 
Once you get a whiff of the fresh churro dough blended with cinnamon and sugar, you'll be crunching it like it's hot!

Fernando is super sweet and easy going. Once I didn't have cash and his card reader wasn't working--he said to just come back another day to pay him. Doesn't get more trustworthy and local than that! 

Their truck slogan reads: "PRUEBA--DISFRUTA--REPITE! (Try...Enjoy...Repeat!)". Just like that, you'll be an addict and regular like myself! 
Oh, heck, he knew I'd be back for my fix. 

Fernando also does catering for large events (quinceañeras, weddings, etc) with the toppings (chocolate and caramel sauce), plus offers local delivery!
Note: Keep in mind that they're there only in Santa Barbara Mondays and Thursdays but travel to Lompoc, Vandenberg and Lompoc the remaining days of the week.

Since I'm such a Churro fan, check out where else in Santa Barbara County to get your churro on--though none can triumph Fernando's!
https://www.yelp.com/collection/Y3ZRTDE_Iu9CUfDN3Dq4FA?utm_content=Collections&utm_source=ishare
Categories: Restaurants, Pizza, Italian | Review: Good value and large portions. Complementary garlic rolls are very good.  Pasta dish I had (bolognese) was not inspired.  Salad was good.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: Such a below average sushi restaurant, sadly. The service today was unfriendly & the girl seemed like she really couldn't be bothered to help me. My husbands sushi was also definitely gone off (dynamite/scallop) which we didn't realize until we were all the way back in Santa Barbara.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Cafes, Bakeries | Review: Quite possibly, the BEST Middle Eastern food I have had (it's my favorite cuisine and eat it often).
The menu is not extensive, and it takes a little longer than you might expect.
However, the amazing quality of the food totally makes it worthwhile.
Food lovingly prepared by a culinary artist.
The service is excellent and very personal.
Can't wait to come back!

Categories: French, Mediterranean, Restaurants | Review: There was only one waiter taking care of the entire restaurant.  It took me 15 - 20 minutes just to get water and order a drink.  About 15 minutes more to order food.  I ordered a "hamburger" from the "burger" section of the menu and psych it doesn't included a hamburger patty - that is extra?! WTF?  So by the time I actually ate my food I had been there 1.5 hrs.  I also noticed this on their menu - "As a courtesy to our Foreign guests a 15% gratuity will be added".  A business this poorly run isn't going to last long.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, Bakeries | Review: Friendly service. The blackberry panna cotta is a must try. It's soft, creamy, and smooth. These stores definitely embrace the open and shared space concept. I came expecting a full on bakery, instead it is more like a cafe with lots of inside and outdoor seating. It is nice and cool inside on this hot summer day. Perfect for sipping on our mochas or lattes while enjoying a pastry. A tour group came in and they were served the galette, which looks amazing. I should have ordered that instead of the donut. Although the donut was tougher than I thought (resembles more like the texture of a bagel), I still enjoyed it nonetheless.

Categories: Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Diners | Review: The only reason i keep coming here is because they are open late and I can usually get a table for a large bunch of friends.The service is usually great, They really try to accommodate our large groups of friends.  But the prices of the menu are too high. Its freaking Denny's! Meals should not cost $10.00 here. Ever!!  Plus i have issues with the inconsistent quality. i do like their specials menus as they sometimes have something good.but overall I always regret eating here the next day. Would someone please open up more late night dining spots in Goleta and Santa Barbara? i have a ton of friends who are looking for a change of venue.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Cafes, Restaurants | Review: It's ok to go to if you want something decent and easy. I notice it has a certain Hope Ranch housewife kind of clientele. The food has improved since they opened. The service is random, sometimes really great other times not so. Today our server was seriously MIA and we had to ask others for help.

Categories: Chinese, Cafes, Taiwanese, Restaurants | Review: What a surprise to find this yummy Taiwanese restaurant near UCSB. Very authentic braised pork rice bento box. Tofu hotpot is very tasty too. Will come back next time in town.

Categories: Pizza, Italian, Fast Food, Restaurants | Review: $4.33 for a slice of cheese pizza? Wife said it was "OK".  Bartender was nice. 

Just keep walking.  There are dozens of better places to eat along State Street.

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Going through a long drive and it was a great stop.  The staff their was very friendly and warm.  A mix of Mexican and Italian.  Don't forget to try the flan!

Categories: Restaurants, Pizza, Chicken Wings, American (New), Italian | Review: Sorry to see that the Calle Real location in Goleta has closed.  I liked this place.  Gods pizza.  Convenient location.  Lots of parking.  Fast service.

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese | Review: Not the best chinese restaurant ever, but pretty darn good!  Their onion pancakes and pot stickers are wonderful!  Their General and Orange Peel chicken are good, maybe not better than other places I've tried - but yummy all the same.  The owner is so sweet and makes you feel so special and welcomed.  Because of her I would give the place actually 3 and a half stars!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Some of these best Mexican food I have had. Authentic yet ahead of them game. They salad that accompanied my dish was so tasty. Recommended by then Wayfarer Inn Staff so thankful they did. Prices are a little high but worth it. I say it's a Def go if your in Santa Barbara!

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: Nice coffee shop. Overall, the Americano I ordered was good... but not mind-blowing. It had a lot of potential, but unfortunately wasn't roasted as well as I had hoped for. Had an unusually sharp and sour flavor - tasted a tad burnt.  It also lacked a certain richness and complexity in its taste. I would possibly return to try other drinks offered here - staff was really friendly and great atmosphere.

Categories: Food, Italian, Pizza, Fast Food, Gelato, Chicken Wings, Sandwiches, Salad, Restaurants | Review: This was the most delicious pizza I have ever experienced.  Try the fresh margarita and the pepperoni!  I'll be back!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: Ate here yesterday. Had a wonderful meal of fish 'n chips - batter was excellent, fish was tasty and the price was right. This is a very authentic chippy!

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Think elevated Mexican food that will make your tummy happy! Don't forget the delicious and fresh drinks. Start with the Ceviche Verde which is tangy and full of halibut, pineapple, jicama, basil and cilantro. There is also an Ahi Cerviche that is spicy and sweet at the same time. We love the octopus with adobo called, Pulpo a las Brasas. Another favorite is the Rib-eye with papilla chillies and a crab enchilada. You really cannot go wrong with the food here. Everything tastes great and be sure to make a reservation or get here early for a table. This place is very popular!

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: I have a theory--the better the location, the worse the food. I don't know why this seems to reign true so often for me, but it does! This location is awesome--right across the street from the Pacific Ocean. Our drinks were ok (Bloody Mary and Mojito). Bloody Mary was kinda garbage, and the Mojito was slightly better. The lobster roll was ok, clam chowder was ok, my daughter's steak (kid menu) sucked, son had plain pasta--lucky for him they didn't mess it up by adding their own sauce which they brought on the side (what's a better word for disgusting?), and finally-the old lady's salad (she said it sucked). If this location was trash--I'd give it 1 star simply because the value to food ratio is so off (food sucked, price was absolutely not cheap). Verdict--if you don't really care about the food quality...this is the spot! Just hit up Jack In The Box after, when you inevitability don't finish your food...just ask for to-go boxes to give to one of the many homeless people across the street...just be sure to apologize to them before hand! 

Part of my code--pay for the food, never ask for a discount, and if the server offers to take something off the bill...refuse it. I'll just never have to come back. Simple. 

I welcome a response from the owner!

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, Italian, Pizza, Restaurants | Review: Probably the worst service I ever received at a restaurant. Not only did it take them almost 20 mins to even acknowledge our existence but when they did it's only because we finally flagged them down like "we're ready to order". The waitress rolled her eyes at my mother in law when she dropped off our appetizer bc the pizza pan was hot on her hand and we didn't move stuff on our table quick enough. We got total attitude when asking for refills on our drinks. We had to ask for a box & our check twice & it took them almost 10 mins to get it to us. It was awful all around. The only reason I even gave them a star is because the food is actually good, but seriously need to work on your service Nutbelly, so disappointed.

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: HandleBar Coffee is great.
When I order drinks it's ready within seconds, its taste great. They are so fast I got worried but the quality is there. Affordable prices too. They do close a little early but for what you're getting you really can't complain.

Categories: Caribbean, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Cajun/Creole, Vegan | Review: This place is really cute, especially when the inside is open for seating. 
The food is incredible and the chef always delivers the best authentic Caribbean food I have had! Would recommend to anyone looking for something new and different on state street!

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Amazing ice cream! Found the shop while strolling TO dinner, and we promptly decided to change plans and eat 2 scoops of ice cream for dinner. Not our finest moment, but it was totally worth it. We loved our chosen flavors: olive oil with almonds, salted caramel chip, and (for something fresh and summery) the eureka lemon and marionberry. I also sampled the chocolate covered strawberries, which was delish.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Coffee & Tea, Bakeries, Desserts, Donuts, Food, Restaurants | Review: Wow! Surely the best donut of my life. Visiting SB and saw this place. Who thought of all these flavors?  And a well made cappuccino made my morning.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Desserts, Wine Bars, Bars, Italian, Nightlife | Review: The food was quite good; however the service was quite terrible. Our waitress forgot our caesar salads, serving our main course after a long wait. Our request for a second glass of wine fell on deaf ears. She remembered as she gave us the check and then charged us for 2 lattes. She was busy talking with a gentleman at another table. Thank goodness we had a great time; however will think twice before making another reservation.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: The noodle soup and broth are excellent. Great favor. The only down side is that if you want to eat there, seating is very limited.

Categories: Caribbean, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Cajun/Creole, Vegan | Review: Excellent food in a comfortable atmosphere. The plantain "nachos" were incredible! They are plantain chips topped with lobster, crab, avocado, green onions and a very mild caviar. 
The gumbo, red beans and rice, were top notch!
Service was friendly and the chef was kind enough to come talk with us.
This is good food and we will be back!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: Just had lunch here. Saw that they were on diners drive ins and dives. Tried the wee haggis for the first time. It was really good, not at all what I expected. The fish we well bettered. Good flavor, the fried pickles were another hit and the pickled egg toped it off. Will definitely come
Back again.
Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: Good candy selection. Good selection of frozen yogurt flavors. Open late hours. Definitely a fan!

Categories: Food, Breweries, Beer Gardens, Mediterranean, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: Amazing Beer!!! Try the Smores stout if they still have it! Delicious beer. Really kind owner, they give 10% of profits to environmental organizations and they put on some really cool biology talks too! 1$ off beers during happy hour 3pm-6pm. They have jenga, cards against humanity, books, dominos and other games too!!! The names of the beers are named after endangered species on the california coast! Super cool! Love this place!!

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Burgers, Fish & Chips, Sandwiches | Review: We celebrated my girlfriends birthday. Service was awesome, Our meals was executed perfectly. Thank you for a memorable evening! Highly recommend!!!! We'll be back for sure!!!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Tasty and unique soft serve ice cream flavors!! I loved the toppings options too. 

I popped over while waiting for my Tyger Tyger to-go order. I ordered the mango sticky rice ice cream and topped it with ginger pieces, peanuts and shredded coconut. It was lovely! Not too sweet, but delicious, natural flavors. 

I also tried the turmeric chocolate ice cream which was interesting, but not for my palette that day.  I'm sure in the right mood I really would have enjoyed it.

There's usually only three flavors at a time, but it seems that the options change from day to day.  

I had hoped for a cute little fish tail cone that they have splashed all over their Instagram page, but every time I pop by, they're out.

Categories: Nightlife, American (Traditional), Cafes, Restaurants, Wine Bars, Breakfast & Brunch, Bars | Review: I spent many hungover Sunday mornings here at Cody's Cafe. It's got a great family / coffee shop feel to it, and it's not too loud...thank god. There's a good mix of old people, young people, middle-aged people. I also noticed a lot of regulars on Sundays...especially the old guy reading Mien Kampf who is always sitting alone...he's a legend!

I love the country-fried steak; the blueberry muffins are BOMB!!! The servings are pretty huge, too. Each person comes out to ~10 bucks, but you are FULL.

There's sometimes a wait, but it usually only lasts 5-15 min.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Please please please read this. This is place is just not good period. Great atmosphere when I walked in and great service. The food just didn't cut it. First off Pad Thai wayyyyyy tooo sweet. If you decide to get it, I warned you. Ordered the Bangkok noodles and they were okay. Too sweet as well. It's basically pho soup that taste like really sweet pad Thai. Also ordered a side of Gyoza pork pot stickers that came with 6 Gyoza for 11.95!!!!! This place is just not worth it. It wasn't terrible but it's not good for what it's worth. Service and staff are great and amazing though. Pad Thai tasted like dessert. You have been warned.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Best Mexican food in SB. Get the Los Agaves (shrimp) enchiladas. Don't forget to get complimentary chips and salsas. It's a must-stop every time I swing by the 805.

Categories: Pizza, Burgers, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Everything was delicious, the Pizza and the fish tacos were superb.  The servers are adorable and the pastry chef came out to greet us as sweet as can be.  The cheese cake and the Rum cake were the best I have tasted in those two categories.  I am a Pastry Chef and just love eating at the Bourbon Room. The family photos on the wall, the three sisters working together make this restaurant the loveliest place to go.

Categories: Restaurants, Mongolian, Chinese | Review: Everyone that I have spoke to don't have many nice things to say about Ming Dynasty.  I can understand why, since most people that go there order the wrong things.  They don't show you the full menu on the English menu.  You have to ask for the Chinese version (this is where the good stuff is), but unfortunately there is no English translation.  So obviously most people wont know how to read it.  

However, if you can read Chinese and are experienced with Chinese cuisine.  Order food that pertains to Taiwanese dishes, these seem to be the specialty of a chef.  I noticed before that certain dishes were either hit or miss depending on the day I went (sometimes so bad I would get a stomach ache).  

I got fed up and wanted to know why.  So, on one particular day when everything tasted amazing.  I asked who the chef was, and I found out from the waitress that it was a chef named Frank.  He typically prepares the fish dishes the best.  So next time you go there, try to request him.  He prepares incredible fish and tofu just like the dishes I experienced in Taiwan!  

I must note that this only pertains to the menu.  I've never tried the Mongolian BBQ.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (New), French | Review: Best dinner I have ever had in SB. Great service, amazing food. The dessert we had was a seasonal one and I am speechless. It was strawberry ice cream sandwich. We went in without a reservation but it was late in the evening and we were seated right away. We were told they have a peach ice cream sandwich in the summer months and I am looking forward to returning and trying it.

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: we came here for dinner I. Santa Barbara and it's a hillstone franchise! So of course the food is delicious. We enjoy our usually favorites like the French dip and the smoked salmon. The wine and cocktail selection is also delicious. Walking distance of our hotel and with cute shops along the way

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I went here with a group of 7 on a Wednesday night so it wasn't crowded at all. The service was quick though I was annoyed about not receiving enough menus for everyone in the group. Despite that, I ordered chicken taquitos and they were delicious. The complimentary chips and salsa were yummy and consistently refilled. Definitely recommend trying this place out!

Categories: Japanese, Restaurants, Noodles, Sushi Bars | Review: Great Service. Wonderful sushi and rolls. We ordered maybe too much but spicy tuna and sushi 8 was a super way to start
A great gem on our adventure in Santa Barbara!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: This place was okay!  I had the fish and chips with the clam chowder.  The servings were generous (I got a small and was super full afterward) and the food looked yummy!  The fish seemed high quality and it was fried with a nice crispy batter.   It came on a cute bed of London themed newspaper.  I also tried  a bite of the chicken sandwich and that was pretty good.

However, I didn't think anything was very flavorful other than just tasting like it was fried.  And after the first few greasy bites - I was over it :(.  Also - the clam chowder was nothing special, in fact it was surprisingly runny.  I probably won't go back myself, but I'm not super in to fish & chips anyway!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Event Planning & Services, Food, Caterers | Review: It's located in Santa Barbara Public Market. I had a milk chocolate chunk on cone(itty pitty), which is one of the top rated flavors. 
I loved the chunky chocolate in it, and the cone was crispy, too. It's just way too sweet...

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Two stars for the food, which was good, not great. 

The service was the most basic I've ever experienced. That is to say, our order was taken (though not promptly), stale chips & decent salsa were proffered, drinks and food were deposited in front of us (both at the same time, no drinkie in advancie here!), the check, however, was promptly given, and our attendant suddenly sped up once it was time to process the credit card. No smile, no eye contact, no thank you and, thus, a miserly  tip from us.

I guess we were there at Siesta time on a Sunday (about 2 p.m.) and thus were being unbelievably rude to patronize this establishment when there were clearly more pressing matters at hand. It appeared to be the owner that attended to us, even! Seriously, if you don't want business and want to nap a couple hours, put up a CLOSED sign and go knock yourself out!

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: We enjoyed a tasty meal outdoors, with shrimp roll appetizer and crispy rice salad as two highlights. Also recommend pork bahn mi, chicken bahn mi and crab chips. Timbre Chardonnay, from a Santa Barbara winery, was excellent.

Categories: Caterers, Food, Event Planning & Services, Personal Chefs | Review: We did a tasting with Danny for our wedding and his food was absolutely amazing. He listened to what we were interested in and created some incredible dishes based on our needs. He was incredibly friendly and personable, and really accommodating. And again, the food was just spectacular. It was beautifully presented and far more gourmet than any other caterers we sampled. This is a guy who really knows gourmet food. We didn't end up working with Danny on our wedding for purely logistical reasons, but as soon as we have an opportunity to hire him we won't hesitate. Highly recommended!

Categories: Food, Specialty Food, Seafood, Cajun/Creole, Restaurants | Review: Love this place! The shrimp is so good and perfectly spiced. I always get the drunken mix in medium and its so damn good. I come here quite often because the food is great and it is relatively inexpensive being that its in downtown. The service is pretty good, but sometimes can be a little slow. All in all a great place.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: Our first time here, no reservations, got lucky. Went on a Thursday at 7:30 and got 2 seats at the sushi bar which I preferred. Place is small, loud and packed. Kevin was our Sushi Chef, all the Chef behind the bar were very entertaining.

We ordered Sake as we reviewed the menu. One thing I noticed is how extensive, maybe overwhelming the menu options were. We are pretty straight forward pure Sushi/Sashimi lovers. We ordered: Sweet Shrimp, Smelt Egg w/Quail Egg Sushi, Edamame (garlic soy flavor), Spicy Thruster (3 spicy handrolls of tuna, yellowtail, scallop), Jalapeno Yellowtail (red jalapeno. habanero sauce), Su-Miso Albacore (mustard su-miso, jalapeno, cilantro).

The sushi was OK, though they did not have Uni to our disappointment. The Spicy Thrusters, very OK. The Special Yellowtail and Albacore dishes were OK. Fish quality did not have a lot of taste, though presentation of everything and service was very good. 

With competition being so high in Santa Barbara, we will try a different place next visit.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Food, Specialty Food, Japanese | Review: Went in on Saturday night. You have to remember this is a college town, so not surprised when majority of the clientele are college kids! Very small place. Can get loud. Service was scattered. Maybe because our server seemed new (did not know the menu) had to look up what Tekka Maki was. Our first order was 2 orders of Tekka Maki & small warm Sake once we cleared up her confusion we recieved one order. We corrected her & she went off to correct order. What she delivered was not Tekka Maki the second time & never got the order of warm Sake (made sure not on tab). Thinking this frustrates the owners. Had to ask for water (but think that was just a central ca thing because none of the restaurants we visited served water unless you asked)

Categories: Soul Food, Wine Tasting Room, Food, Restaurants, Arts & Entertainment, Vegan, Mexican, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Wineries, Bars | Review: Nook, we love so much. If you were any sweeter we would all need a dentist. But honestly, food is incredible, the veggie burger is unreal and the turkey burger and garlic aioli are made with love.  We will come back again and again. 
Thank you Nook!

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Do-It-Yourself Food, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Bars | Review: Great weekend spot for drinks and live music , especially if ya wanna be low key

Really well priced cocktails 

I have never had the food here so can't comment on that or the 'do it yourself' action

I recommend !

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Zen Yai is a consistently delicious place to order Thai food. My favorites are their Yellow Curry, and their Pad See Ewe-- make sure and get the spice tray as no trip to a Thai place is complete without a little bit of spice!

My one complaint keeping this from a 5 star review is that the lunch specials are about the same price as the regular entrees for a similar amount of food (less of the entree, but you get a side salad)

Next time I'll order the full entree at lunch and skip the salad-- since I'll end up with more of the entree that way.

Categories: Caribbean, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Cajun/Creole, Vegan | Review: We had a frustrating experience here. 

We came for dinner without reservations and were seated immediately since the restaurant was fairly empty. 

The menu really looked spectacular and the food smelled wonderful from the street, which brought us in instead of going to Sama Sama across the street. 

The interior design is a little haphazard. Waking in, it almost looks like it used to be an ice cream or dessert shop, then you turn the corner and there's what looks like an open kitchen that wasn't in use. 
The dining room was nice enough with an open-air concept. 

Once we ordered, the food took forever to come out. It turns out that there is only one chef working in the kitchen, and they had decided to chit-chat with a table of young women for a very extended period. This entire period, literally no food was being prepared for us. So we just sat and sat until our food came out after at least 45 minutes. In that time, no appetizers came out. Again, no food was being prepared at all because the only chef working in the kitchen was not even in the kitchen!

This was extremely frustrating. We relayed our concerns to the owner, who also was the person who sat us at our table. They made similar comments of being frustrated with the chef. After all this, nothing was comped from the bill. Again, the owner agreed that the dinner service was subpar and needed improvement. And nothing was comped. 

It's even more frustrating because the food was actually very good, but everyone was so upset they could not enjoy it. 

Incredible.

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Great place for lunch. Forget subway, panera, Quiznos, or any other "deli meat chain" this place is fresher, tastier, and priced the same. A must for lunch.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Irish, Nightlife, Pubs, Gastropubs | Review: Now this is a true Irish bar.  The Shepherd's Pie was as good as it gets.  The drinks were fresh and just the right temperature.  This place is kid friendly as well a nice area for the younger crowd that doesn't want to be surrounded by families with kids with a pool area.  Nice way to end a stroll on State Street.  The only thing they could do is maybe add a few more modern TV's otherwise it was dang good for me.

Categories: Food, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches | Review: This place is just eh. It has good seating and a nice variety of options. Coffee was good, not great. It was too hot which lent to a burnt taste. 
I had the avocado toast which was beautiful but blah. It had little to no taste. I was disappointed, especially for the cost.

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: I loved coming here when I was in Santa Barbara. The drink prices are cheap and they have pretty good food. The staff is extremely friendly as well. Watch out for their Sunday Bloody Mary pitchers. It's a great concept, except I can't drink that much alcohol in one sitting. Definitely a favorite spot for my girlfriend and I. I highly recommend this place if you are looking for cheap drinks and a good vibe

Categories: Cafes, Diners, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: This was on Memorial Sunday weekend. There were 8 of us in our group. It took them over 1 hour to service us the food. There were other people coming after us, and they were getting their food. We did not know and understand what was the delay. We asked the waitress what was the delay 2 times, and she said, "I will check and never came back". We were about to leave when finally the food came. By that time, I was too annoyed to enjoy it. The rest of my group members were so hungry, it tasted good to them. In addition, there was only one waitress working the outside tables and she was overwhelmed. I felt sorry for her. The waitress gave us a plate to go because one of the orders was incorrect. So do not go there if you have a large group.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Restaurants, Nightlife, Caterers, Italian, Bars | Review: Amazing food and service. Visiting Santa Barbara for the weekend and stopped in for dinner on Saturday night with no reservation. We waited about 20 minutes, but got to meet Braden, the bartender. He recommended a bottle of wine that was amazing. We were concerned that it would be an expensive bottle, but no! One of the lowest on the wine list, it was just his favorite Pinot Noir. And we agreed. He was genuine and very friendly, not just trying to upsell.

When we were seated, the staff were so friendly and the food was to die for. Strongly consider getting the Ceasar salad Mediterranean style. And if you like anchovies, they've got them. The homemade made pasta melted in the mouth, al dente but so fresh! The sauces added so much flavor. The Green lipped muscles in the Cioppiono were a surprise! High quality food! If we lived here, we would return again and again!

Categories: Food, Specialty Food, Olive Oil | Review: This place is fantastic. The customer service was warm friendly and informative. The products they sell are... exquisite. Seriously the 25 year balsamic blows my mind every time I taste it. Considering how expensive olive oil tends to be anyway, I find the stuff they sell to be really worth it. Totally try stopping in and doing a tasting. If you love gourmet food then this is the place for you!
Categories: Dance Clubs, Nightlife, Bars | Review: Terrible service!! Security guard such an asshole and was in a rush into clock out. Disgusting place!! Would not recommend

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Nightlife, Bars | Review: It's a chain, but with a beachside view in Downtown Santa Barbara, why not?  Glad to see that there was a somewhat new vegetarian/healthy option: delicious spinach enchiladas.  Generous portions and never ending chips and salsa are definitely worthwhile!  Water was slow to come out, which is never good for a Mexican restaurant...um, hello-JALAPENOS in the salsa!  Cough-choke-cough.  Slow to be seated as well on a Sunday afternoon when the house was nearly empty.  Would come back again, but probably not my first choice for eating downtown.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: Absolutely amazing!! We dined with a large group and really benefitted from its family style portions that were great for sharing. Our waitress was very knowledgeable about the food and the wine. Each dish was very unique and delicious! I would recommend this restaurant to anyone and hope to visit again.

Categories: Nightlife, Breweries, Restaurants, Food, Pizza, Beer Bar, Bars | Review: Stopped by here for the first time after living in the area my whole life. I'm glad I did! Had some beer and pizza with the family. They have a large selection of beers on tap. The pizza was tasty, we had the meat pizza and then one with pepperoni jalapeños and mushroom. Great location too with indoor and outdoor seating.

Categories: Garage Door Services, Restaurants, Live/Raw Food, Food, Home Services, Mediterranean, Keys & Locksmiths, Burgers, Vegetarian, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Vegan, Salad | Review: There menu has an array of creative vegetarian and vegan dishes. Personal favorites include the Brussels sprouts, gluten free pizza, and street tacos. I take my non-vegetarian friends and family here and they absolutely love it!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: Been to Sama Sama a couple of times and have a clear impression. In summary: over-priced, under-delivered, hipster, with annoying lighting and uncomfortable seating. 

Let's start with the food. With a 'tapas' style presentation you must know your portion will be small, and very expensive. $15 for fried rice. $15 for a couple of fried chicken wings. $15 for a chicken steamed bun. I'm sorry but when my bill is $50 for three dishes with no alcohol, and I'm STILL HUNGRY, this just pisses me off. The food has a good taste, but I can't stomach the prices.

The other yelpers have noted the dirty and uncomfortable seating. I won't bother with the rest of the details. I just hope they do something about their pricing and create more value for customers.
Categories: American (New), Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Servicen kunne have været bedre, men maden er rigtig god og drinks'ne er også gode!

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: What an awesome experience! We waited in line for 15 minutes but it was worth it. It took nearly all that time to decide what we wanted. The menu is large. I ended up getting an ice cream sandwich made with two different flavors and chocolate chip cookies. Superb.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Great cafe style place with history and character that's hard to find these days. The staff is always extremely friendly and happy and makes us and our dog always feel great. The food is consistent and delicious. I always go for the tuna melt and my fiancé and Dad say the steer burger can't be beat! We come here at least once a month, it's our top choice for a nice, outdoor lunch with tons of people watching at the intersection. You'd be crazy not to give it a try!

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: They say kid friendly but there are no changing tables in the women's or men's room. We had a great time there until we realized that and had to take off in order to change our daughter. I'll update my rating when they update their bathrooms to make this place legit kid friendly because the food, vibe and drinks were awesome. So as a local this was especially disappointing...

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: Took my husband here for Valentine's Day and had a fantastic time! Our server (forget her name, but she's a petite brunette) was attentive and friendly. Food came out regularly and each dish was delicious! We ordered:

Patatas bravas - delicious with just the right amount of heat
Brussels sprouts - these will make ANYONE love brussels sprouts!
Gambas with chipotle sauce - again, just the right amount of heat. 4 shrimp and side of cilantro rice
I like pig butts - BBQ pulled pork sliders, delish!
Some "Brie" Sauce - probably the best thing we got - sweet and a little savory. I could eat plates and plates of this

I was also surprise at how yummy the coconut mojito was - definitely getting that again. Thanks for a great dinner!

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese | Review: Nice Sushi and Teriyaki

We had a large birthday dinner with14 family and friends, so reserved the one long table they have. So many types of calamari, clams, sushi and rolls, it was fun to sample. The shredded cabbage salads were very tasty and well prepared, highly recommended. The teriyaki chicken was said to be scrumptious. The sushi prep and presentation was outstanding!

The only downside that night was the order taking and plate service for our large group. One end of the table had their drinks and sushi very quickly, while the other end waited 10 minutes longer. It may have been the type and requests for certain menu items. Plus, the place was packed, so it was all OK.  http://sushiteri.com

Categories: Burgers, Salad, Restaurants, Desserts, Food, Chicken Shop | Review: Kyles has been my family's "go-to" place during COVID  and "back to school".  We have gone to Kyles weekly since they opened and we love Silvergreens too.  
Servers are always wonderful.  It is easy to customize food orders (I need the jalapeños to come off my Kickin' Cowboy)
Kyles is such a positive place (they gave us a giggle when they offered a roll of toilet paper with the take out orders during COVID shut down).  They have a cool ice cream menu (pun intended).  You have to try their ice cream-cookie sandwiches and their shakes.  And Kyles has a great wine list.  
Thank you Kyle and Family!

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Not impressed.  Came here after a quick bite down the street.  Intrigued by the many reviews, I gave it a try. Custom ice cream sandwich it was.  

-the ginger cookie was too hard to bite 
-generic ice cream flavors

Overall I was not too impressed.  If they had warmed up the cookie prior to creating the sandwich I think it would have been better.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: Best night ever at EOS! Dance floor was popping off! That Chinese bartender really knows how to hook it up! Made my night! Be back soon!
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: One of the best non gentrified taco places in SB. Most taco places in SB is super expensive, but Lily's is super affordable.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: By far my favorite club in Santa Barbara. They always have the best DJs in town, If you're looking for a place to dance this is the spot. They have two dance floors so you can switch up the music without switching clubs. I love their patio area to cool off on, super cute and they even have a bar out there too! The patio bartender Nelson is the man, makes awesome cocktails and is super hilarious always remembers I love Jameson with a Sprite back, not to mention he's smokin hot. Actually most the people that work there are. Over all if you're looking for a great night out downtown this is the spot.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: Small dinner menu but great options!

I ordered the pork tacos from the small plate section and was amazed at how great they were.  I enjoyed the trendy ambiance and the paper covered tables that you could color on. 

Definitely recommend.

Categories: Caterers, Restaurants, Spanish, Personal Chefs, Event Planning & Services | Review: Thank you so much for such an amazing experience. Great food, wonderful service!!! Chef Benjamin and Donna were super professional and nice. Definitely something we will order again.
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: We had the halibut picado and the shrimp and crab enchiladas with the premium margaritas. 5 stars for all. Nice outdoor patio.

Categories: Irish Pub, Nightlife, Bars, Pubs | Review: Literally the best bar in Santa Barbara, cannot say how amazing this place is. Matt is amazing. Hands down the best bartender/sever I've ever had.

Categories: Desserts, Restaurants, Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Delis, Food Trucks | Review: This place does not even list their actual address on Yelp. Their actual address is at Montecito and Milpas (this is on the complete east opposite side of where the Yelp address is). When I called the number listed here (which by the way is also a PERSONAL cell phone, the girl I talked to said "oh well usually when people call, we tell them our actual address). Ummmm wtf? People aren't always going to call before coming in for business. List your real address on Yelp, it's not that hard. Secondly, they don't even give actual hours they're open. Be sure to call before  because if it's similar to our case, they might be randomly closed. I would love to try your churros, but I would love even more if the business could practice simple common sense.

Categories: Caribbean, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Cajun/Creole, Vegan | Review: Noticed the restaurant from the street and never would have guessed they've only been open for 6-7 days. They had a breakfast and lunch menu, but no official dinner menu yet during their soft opening. The lunch menu had a variety of options including some hearty dishes, so you won't be missing out eating here in the evenings, even now. I've had some pretty decent Caribbean food over the years, and this place is pretty solid. I'd be willing to bet that they're even better in a couple months. We had the curry goat stew, slow cooked pork, duck prosciutto arugula salad, and the fried okra, and would order them all again. 

Also, the design and buildout are great, and there's a good sized patio.

Categories: Bakeries, Cafes, Coffee & Tea, Food, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: This place is absolutely delicious!!  I have never been here before so I asked for a recommendation from the girl at the cash register.  She made several recommendations, but one really stood out to me and it was a breakfast item... it's called the "Sunny" with Soyrizo added.... talk about tasty! I will definitely be back here! And of course I got cupcakes to go for the kids!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: The chocolate chip ice cream was great until I found a big old hair in there.  They don't wear hair nets so that probably explains why.  But it totally ruined the experience.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: Love this place.  Best fish 'n chips and great owners.  They're very likable and the food is just great. 


If looking for fish n chips or other UK food, it's a great stop.  

You will have to find parking on the side streets as it's in state street.  It has some seating for sure but I always get an order to go.  

It's definitely a craving and I end up last minute idea to rub over there.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Gastropubs, American (Traditional), Cocktail Bars, Nightlife, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New) | Review: Great food! Amazing drinks! Very friendly staff! We had the short rib poutine, and the grilled cheese (added roasted jalapeños) both dishes were fantastic! Chatted with the Chef while we had specialty cocktails at the bar. Definitely going to come back next time in SB!

Categories: Farmers Market, Food, Fast Food, American (New), Bars, Wine Bars, Breakfast & Brunch, Cafes, Restaurants, Nightlife | Review: A real surprise. Not your typical greasy spoon. Tasty options, even for a healthy eater. Burgers look great, salads and bowls are a treat. This spot is part of or adjacent to the Encina Lodge by Cottage Hosp. 

Go soon cause the locals are learning about this restaurant.

Categories: Caterers, Restaurants, Desserts, Bakeries, Event Planning & Services, Gluten-Free, Food, Cupcakes | Review: Placed a large order of cupcakes for a birthday party. Cupcakes were delivered on time to the restaurant. They were absolutely delicious and the HIT of the party! Great friendly customer service! 
Thanks so much!
Theresa & Mike Bowers

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Irish, Nightlife, Pubs, Gastropubs | Review: My Irish last name is on the wall so that is a plus. The service was good and my friends food was OK - Good, however my Steak Pie had almost no filling. a FEW pieces of potato and carrots. I think there was three small pieces of steak. When I opened the crust the pot was half full.  Almost sent it back when I discovered the lack of filling.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, Party & Event Planning, Food, Venues & Event Spaces, Arts & Entertainment, Social Clubs, Specialty Food | Review: We are still spinning after the most magical wedding a couple weekends ago! We can't stop talking about how happy we were with our venue choice: there are many magical places to get married in Santa Barbara, but the University Club was simply perfect. Breanna was very great to work with, she answered all many our questions :) The staff at the event were amazing as well! One guy (I think his name was Brandon) kept making sure I had champagne throughout the night-- so sweet when you're busy talking to all your guests. The food was stellar. The space itself is just set up for a stress-FREE wedding day, it's centrally located and it's gorgeous. What else could you need?!

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Argentine, Empanadas, Food | Review: Just heard about this delicious place and je friendliest chef ever.  The Empanadas were amazing and if very good quality of cheeses and meats. He only serves Empanadas and uses Rori's ice cream kitchen on the weekend. He is very polite and friendly as well.

Categories: Cajun/Creole, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: 3.75 stars with room to grow for this family run, seems like all brothers and friends joint. 

The Veggie Scramble is a 3.75 star dish. A bit over cooked on the veggies, but the taste was all there. 

The Rancher Omelette was a 3.25 dish. Again, ingredients good, but the execution left it all a bit soggy. Maybe this being a relatively new place a has it still in training mode. 

Regular coffee is better than the latte. 4 Stars. 

Service is awesome and a real plus to attention. 5 Stars. 

The veggies seemed Beignets are a 4.25 star item. Enjoy them like a fancy waffle or crispy pancake. 

I'm confident this place will pull it all together and push the "Cajun" a bit more. Come check it out, I'll come back again and bring the family.

Categories: Performing Arts, Arts & Entertainment, Caterers, Party & Event Planning, Comedy Clubs, Venues & Event Spaces, Event Planning & Services, Nightlife | Review: Went Saturday to see Laurie Kilmartin and it was great.  Drinks are cheap, plenty of laughs to be had and next to the airport makes it feel like a super incognito location.  All said the show was a blast.

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services | Review: Great food, excellent service but no reservation restaurant. First come, first serve so you need to come at 6-645 to eat earlier than 8pm for dinner. 
It's worth it!

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: My friend and I are obsessed with poke and this is one of the better poke spots in SB. 

Food: I got the small and my friend got the medium. Portion size for the raw fish were good for both! First scoop of crab meat, seaweed salad and masago were free. 

Location: If you live in IV, you'll need a car or have to take public transportation to get here. 

Atmosphere: The inside of the shop is actually half poke shop and half boba shop. There's a good amount of seating space inside and it's clean. There's usually not many people who stay and eat here so there's always seating space! 

I'd recommend this place if you're craving poke!

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: The atmosphere is vibrant with moody warm lighting. The bartenders make tasty drinks packed with youth and timelessness. It's great. The food always changes. The staff is attractive. Everything feels clean. The food is delicious. Everything feels home cooked, in a professional way. I want to come here every day.

Categories: Desserts, Food, Bakeries, Specialty Food | Review: Regardless of whether these macaron are made fresh daily or shipped up every few days (in reference to the one star review), the fact is that they are delicious, well priced for macaron, and come in a startling variety of truly lovely flavors (salted caramel and hazelnut passion are my current favorites). 

I was pleasantly surprised to find out that they now offer a loyalty reward punch card. Now I have no excuse to not come back soon!
Categories: Thai, Restaurants | Review: Good service, nice foods, not expensive. I go this place once a week, but I always satisfied the taste! Love it!

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese | Review: DON'T BELIEVE WHAT YOU READ!!!  

A week ago I felt sorry for this place.   So many one star reviews, one after the other after the other....    Looked to me that someone had it out for them.   All looked so fake, all by absolute Yelp newbies, profiles that had never written a Yelp review.   I flagged and I flagged and I flagged, but Yelp disagreed.    Decided the reviews are sincere and let them stand.   I didn't get it.  

A few days ago Wok got their first Five star review, then another, and another, and another.   I think a total of 8 or 9 in two days!!!   Again newbies, again first reviews.   You don't have to be a brain surgeon to recognize that someone was doing damage control.   I flagged and flagged and flagged, again Yelp for the most part disagreed and has decided that the reviews are sincere.    Maybe it is just a coincidence that most of them use the same "not like Panda Express" reference.    I mean some people get hit by lightning time and time again.    

So my curiosity got the better of me.    I went there for lunch yesterday.   I was expecting to see a sign twelve feet across that said "5 stars on Yelp gets you a free meal!!!!"   But it wasn't there. 

The place has no décor, no atmosphere, they have those super light aluminum chairs that cheap restaurants seem to love so much, my guess is that they are $12 apiece.    I was happy to see that I was the only non-Asian in the place, I took that as a good thing, might speak well of the food.  

When I ordered I asked the person at the counter what he would recommend.   His answer was "Get anything that you want."   Thanks dude, very helpful.

I ordered the Mongolian Beef, always a favorite.  I ordered the entree since it already comes with rice.   It was $9.72 before tip.    I was disappointed when I got my plate, a ton of rice and a teenie little bit of meat.   I eat at other Chinese places and for my $8 or $9 lunch I usually get a soup, I get an egg roll, a cup of tea and perhaps a little salad... oh and SO much more meat.   

I wasn't offered tea but was offered water, half a glass (pictured) I guess that is because of the drought.    

All in all I was completely underwhelmed.  The food was expensive and while quite tasty, it seemed like a very poor value.   But I did feel like I accomplished something.    Maybe the one star reviews were sincere.    And in my humble opinion the five star reviews have to be 100% fake!    Sorry Yelp, we will just have to agree to disagree.   

Oh and if you read my review and you agree with me, please flag any reviews you suspect as fake.  Hey, flag mine too if you want.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Great. So many different flavors. Tried a few. 

We got chocolate covered strawberry...really good.  

Also got salted caramel chip...sweet but solid. 

In a small you can get 2 flavors (really like 2 scoops) so you are able to mix flavors too. 

Price point is on par with other ice cream places. 

Wish there was locations closer to OC, but we will be back next time in SB.

Categories: Burgers, Restaurants | Review: I don't know what some yelpers are thinking when they say this place is better than In and Out. The Habits French fries and burgers just do not seem as quality as In and Out. That being said,  the cheeseburgers and fries are pretty good here. My girl ordered the Cobb salad, which basically sucked. But hey I should not have expected much of a salad from a burger joint. The ordering system that they use is good though. They give you one of those restaurant type of buzzer things when your food is ready which makes waiting for your food very convenient. One peeve that I had with this place was that they were playing a bunch of Christmas music on NEW YEARS day.
Categories: Food, Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Chinese | Review: It's a wonderful place to have dinner here. The rib rice noodle is pretty awesome!!!!!!

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: We visited from Colorado and had the Uber driver bring us anywhere he suggested. This place was DELICIOUS. At 7PM on a Friday we walked right up to the counter with plenty of open seating. Many other popular spots had reviews about long waits and crowded areas, so we were anxious as to where we should try. This place was not on any main-stream list of hot spots, but it was absolutely the tastiest Asian cuisine. I highly recommend for those trying to find something local and unique.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Went here because of Yelp rating. So worth it. We arrived early for dinner, no waiting. Walk up, order and sit, they being the dinner to your table. 
Chips and salsa are complimentary. Perfect chips, light and crisp. Salsas  vary, delicious and spicy.
We each ordered the halibut and shrimp enchiladas, one regular and one with green sauce, no cheese. Very good tortillas, generous portions, wonderful salad dressing as well. 
This is definitely a place we will return to when passing through SB again.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: My husband and I went to Bouchon for our anniversary and it was perfect! Everything is so fresh and the service was wonderful. We love to eat so we had 5 courses and had wine pairings as well. My favorites were the Scallop trio, Crab cakes, Wagyu steak, truffle potato cake (YUM!) and the Blueberry upside down cake. The other courses were good as well, but these were my favorites. Everything was cooked perfectly. It even made great left overs since we over ordered. I wish we had more people so we could try more things. Next time I would like to have more people and reserve the Cork Room. Looking forward to going back.

Categories: Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Gluten-Free, Food, Sandwiches, Bagels | Review: This is my favorite place to get work done outside of the office. They serve great coffee and food (awesome blueberry scones), and actually provide outlets at the tables for people who want to get work done (unlike some other coffee places in the area that intentionally don't *cough*french press*cough*). I've sat here for hours before working and ordering coffee/food/tea and they were totally chill about it. Super good vibes.

Categories: Bars, Dive Bars, Nightlife | Review: Dive Bar. It's just what you expect from a dive bar. A little dirty. A little funky. The pool table runs on quarters with soft rails. The bathroom is best avoided but satisfactory. The bartenders are friendly. The patrons are also friendly, and the drinks are stiff.

Categories: Bars, Pubs, Lounges, Tapas/Small Plates, Nightlife, Comfort Food, Restaurants | Review: We headed to Globe to check it out for an after dinner drink and found it pretty hopping with people.  Good energy, environment and the drinks were good.  I am giving it 3 stars based on two things.  The first, the bartender was wonderful, but over stretched.  It was a Friday eve, lots of people coming in and she was the only one behind the bar to attend to the bar folks as well as all the other orders coming in. We sat at the bar and weren't in a hurry, but for a new restaurant on a weekend, being more prepared would have made for a better experience.

The second bit really set a negative tone to the otherwise friendly atmosphere.  We came in and asked if some seats were taken at the bar and the gentleman said he was saving them.  Which is all fine and good.  Then a girl, approached and reiterated that those were her seats and she was saving them for her and her fiance, a bit on the nasty side. Ok, great.  We got it.  The thing that was irksome was that those seats didn't get used for an hour, we asked again if we could borrow one just until the person came back and she said no, holding these seats for her fiance and emphasized that he either worked there or was the chef (not sure, had a hard time getting past her unfriendly attitude).  IF that was the case, then one would think you would want to promote an inclusive, friendly environment if you were representing the establishment.  When THE fiance did come around, he seemed very nice and although I don't know him, I am fairly certain he would have been embarrassed by his girlfriend's high school antics (she continued to throw looks and a comment here and there at us), most people would have been.  

I do want to try the food, I have heard great things, but to be honest, if negative Nelly is a fixture there, I won't be stopping in.

Categories: Breweries, Restaurants, Sports Bars, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Food, Bars | Review: Great little place for lunch or dinner. I had the chicken sandwich, joined by garlic fries, and was quite satisfied. 

Here's a little tip: If you order a burger during happy hour, you get a free beer.

Speaking of beer, the home brews are delicious. Especially the pomegranite wheat. YUM!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: A-OK, good service, kinda cramped dinning area.
Generous with the chips and salsa.

The food was standard, maybe I'll go back and try other items, but I wasn't impressed.

Can I have some booze in my margarita? 
This is very good mango juice, but did I not show you my ID?

Categories: Lounges, Bars, American (New), Nightlife, Modern European, Restaurants | Review: ALWAYS LUCKY AT Café Luck. Made our reservation via Open Table 8:00. We arrived early so we headed to the bar. Martinis....Very large doubles for 11.00. It may have been a triple pour. Shaken ice cold with frosting. Our table was ready on time. This place has the feel of the south of France. If everybody spoke French you just could be on the Mediterranean. Domaine De Crista Grenache Cote du Rhone at 28.00. Seared Foie Gras with apricot & arugulla. Second course, Octopus braised with a vinaigrette radishes and grapefruit.  Octopus I have cooked or had served to me was like eating the rubber steal belted tires off your car.  This was remarkable very tender and I have to say the best I have ever had. Washed it down with the Mas de La Day Grenache/ Syrah from Provence 33.00 For the main course I had the duck again. It's my favorite confit style it falls off the bone. The most amazing however is the floating Island. I haven't figured it out....do they make them to order or are they pre-made? And if they are pre-made how do they keep them aloft. I used to make them in my restaurant by poaching lightly in a crème anglaise a la Escoffier. They would only stay afloat just long enough. I am going back again just to check this amazing light and fluffy olfactory sensation. Finished off with an Armangnac. The service was great too.

Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: Honestly, there isn't much in Carp by way of "ethnic" eating, but Siam Thai isn't bad.  The prices are relatively reasonable and they are able to accommodate gluten free diners (a friend is on a gluten free diet).  

The wait staff is friendly and the service is fast.  I love that they have outdoor seating in the Thai-style "bungalows".  Reminds me of when I went to the islands off of Thailand. 

Another reason I like them is that they don't treat Asian diners differently.  I've found that some places treat you differently when you're asian, like you're expected to know something and/or that you get less courteous treatment.  

Overall, a yummy Thai place in Carp!

Categories: Gelato, Food, Restaurants, Italian, Vegetarian, Gluten-Free | Review: Came to SB for a random day trip with my other half. We had intentionally wanted to find a restaurant that had a view of the beach and water, but then ended up walking on State St. for a restaurant. When we actually got lost, we stummbled in front of Pascucci. The cool thing about this restaurant is that they do offer outdoor seating so you get the best of both worlds when it comes to indoor/outdoor seating and with the ambience of downtown SB ! 

So once inside, the restaurant had such an amazing delicious scent, without a doubt this was the place we were going to eat. The smell of garlic, herbs and fresh ingredients filled the entire restaurant! 

We were nicely seated in these private booths that sat near the fireplace, VERY intimate and romantic which was what me and my other half wanted, so it was perfect !

The menu has alot to offer. Many choices, primary Italian food, but of all kinds and prepared in many ways. They have a page specifically of traditional items and then they had a page full of their specialities. Pricing was very reasonable, and the plate sizes are enough to feel on the borderline of too full to finish the last bite.

All in all, the food was so fresh and delicious, and the waiter service was very attentive. On our next day trip to SB we will most likely end up at this restaurant at the same booth, hopefully with the same waiter- this time we will try something new from their large menu choice!

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services | Review: We chose Via Maestra because of the rave reviews it got from the yelpers here. 

The food was alright (nothing fabulous and relatively expensive for what you get), but what really annoyed us was how we were treated as customers.

We were anticipating a sit-down meal, but when we arrived for a late lunch we were told that meals should be ordered at the counter. Fine, no problem.  

We chose our meals and the waitress brought out our salads. We took our utensils out of our napkins and then the waitress rushed out as we were about to take our first bites and said that she needed to take a salad back. Huh? Apparently one of us was not "entitled" to a salad with our main meal. So she confiscated the salad and took it back to the kitchen. Then not a moment later another waitress left the kitchen with the same salad and brought it to another table who was "missing" their salad. Ridiculous! Not only is it exemplary of bad service to remove a dish from a table once it's been served (and it's a dish that worth less than a dollar really -- just some dressed greens with some parmagian cheese!!!), it's also rather unhygienic. A good restaurant should just whip up another salad, especially since it's their mistake and it's so cheap and easy to fix. 

The entrees weren't particularly filling and it's a lot of hype. Also it's quite expensive for what you get. A pasta dish without any meat for over $13 - all wheat, no meat? C'mon!  Pasta is a cheap filler. Our other entree was a small salad with a few strips of cold fish. We left hungry. 

Bottom line: Via Maestra 42 did not meet our expectations and we wouldn't return.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Palapa is a Mexican restaurant on upper State Street and you are likely to find mostly locals in this area. 

I really enjoyed the Shrimp Caldo, which had an abundance of shrimp as well as vegetables and it came with chopped onions, cilantro, and lemon that I could add myself. We also shared the combination plate with the cheese enchilada and Chile relleno with Refried beans and red rice.

The waiters were always polite and helpful and as soon as we sat down one of them brought us chips and salsa. The restaurant also offers different Mexican soda pops, beers and a really unique drink called Jamaica, which is a non-alcoholic drink made with hibiscus flowers. When the weather is decent, you can sit outside on the patio next to palm trees.

Categories: Restaurants, Ethiopian, French | Review: wow. Not sure why all the great review about service. Went here a couple of weeks ago and the dark haired male manager/wait staff (?) was less than polite.  Our table was reserved yet it took more than 30 minutes to seat us and we were 10 minutes early. His attitude was very "L.A."  Like it was our fault they can't manage reservations promptly and did not offer any sort of concession or apology.  Disappointing as we liked the food (had the lamb) but could not recommend this spot as it felt they were the customers, not my husband and I. Just read another recent review about the what sounds like the same individual. Hopefully they can reconcile the issue as there are too many great choices to risk bad reviews and waste what appears to be a talented kitchen.

Categories: Food, Farmers Market, Specialty Food, Fruits & Veggies | Review: We went to the Tuesday and Saturday markets to get ingredients for meals that we made during our vacation. Fantastic selection, friendly people, a but expensive but worth the money for such high quality ingredients.

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: In the mountains high above Santa Barbara, in what seems like the middle of nowhere, is an almost magical place that serves great food and drinks. An old stage coach stop from the late 1800's is the setting of this almost magical place. My friends and I were there on a Friday afternoon and there were people waiting outside for a table. Our reservations were for 1:30 pm and it was still crowded, so if possible, I would recommend making reservations. The French dip, tri-tip sandwich, and ribs with onion rings on the side were what my friends ordered. The onion rings looked fabulous and my friends confirmed they were, as were the sandwiches and ribs. I had the roasted vegetable salad and it was delicious. We had Cold Spring Tavern's bacon and asparagus quiche for breakfast that morning and it was the best quiche I had ever had. Our server that day was very personable and provided excellent service. My previous visit to the Cold Spring Tavern was about 23 years ago and the food was great then, so I am pretty confident the great quality is consistent.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: The food is decent! However, the owner is a total B! Her name is Preale. Not sure how to spell it.  We went in there with a party of 5 and she was being so rude to us and to her staff. I asked the very nice server if we could speak to a manager and she said the women we had the issue with was the owner. Wow!! We won't be back and sure hope her staff finds a better job! Those poor people.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Great food, always the same quality every time. The wait time for a table is long, so don't show up super hungry. However, if you don't want to wait for a table, you can always stand and order food and drinks from the bar.

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Seafood, Burgers, Tacos, Sandwiches, Mexican, Bars, Food, Beer Bar, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Nightlife | Review: Like many of the other reviews, the food and setting are good. A great place for lunch by the beach.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: This place is killer.

Amazing ice cream and the handmade waffle cone was great. The server was also really friendly and let my wife try a bunch of different flavours.

We went back a bunch of times while on vacation and one thing I did notice was it gets really busy (line out the door busy), really quick, but then it will be fine in like 10 minutes.

Categories: French, Cafes, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Sandwiches, Bakeries | Review: Their crossants are killer!  So flakey and perfectly buttery!  The pastries here are as close as it gets to the real deal, meaning France!  Trust my word because when I visited France, I had a pastry/crossant every morning and out there it's just the absolute best!  I think for me to compare it to ones from France should speak for itself.

The second time I went, I happened to be there at the perfect time, because the crossants just came out of the oven, and they were freshly warm!  I nearly cried of uber excitement over the crossants because I finally found a place that reminded me of France!

Not only are their pastries to die for, but also their coffee!  I've had their cappuccino and latte...  No other place can compare to how they pour the right amount of espresso and millk to perfection!  I love my lattes tasting more on the espresso side and they made it happen!
