I heart Renaud's!!!

Categories: Pizza, Nightlife, Italian, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Found this place on yelp, glad we called for a reservation, they just do priority seating...got there just in time! Sat
Down 545/6 before the rush came. Wasn't sure what salami to order, the server was very helpful, sorry I can't remember your name! 

Started with trio of olives and salami, yum! 

Had the margerita doc with burrata, little more to add burrata but that's the way to go! The special was great too, meat sauce topped with a few cheeses and meatballs, we added spicy salami, delicious! 

Dough had great flavor, just the right crispness and thickness. Toppings incredible, so many to choose from.

Big pizza lover, definitely enjoyed! Cute place. Maybe dinner tomorrow night there? ;)

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Right across the street from the beach. There will most likely be a 20 minute wait, and if so, consider yourself lucky. Patio is very tiny but I always take my boxer mix & dog friendly is mostly all I review & look for. Free little muffins for starts. It is pricey, But it is right across from the beach so I wasn't expecting to find a cheap diner. The country potatoes were yummy, the portion sizes were satisfying and the flavors were delicious. Winner!

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: I was eagerly anticipating the opening of Santo Mezcal so naturally I had to be there on their first day opened to the public! If you are familiar wiith Los Agaves this place is operated by the same great team and the newest addition in restaurants by Carlos Luna. I had expected it to be jam packed when we arrived but luckily we were able to get seats at the beautiful bar area right away. It's always fun sitting at the bar socializing with the bartender. Sean was great and he created some awesome hand crafted cocktails! 

I started with the Margarita Picante which was perfectly made! You have the choice of fresno mezcal or serrano tequila. I went with the serrano tequila and really enjoyed it. This drink is made with agave nectar, lime, and has a rim with chipotle salt. 

My second drink of choice was La Paloma Clasica and again stuck with the tequila but you do have the option of mezcal as well. If you are a fan of grapefruit order this! It was very light and refreshing and didn't have a bitter taste as grapefruit can be.

As for the food I decided on the pulpo tacos. The serving presentation was great all around but once I took that first bite I was let down by the overcooked octopus. It was rubbery, chewy, and the flavor was lacking. I debated a good while on sending it back since I don't like to insult the cooks but I couldn't just waste my meal so I sent it back and management was kind and gracious enough to accept it and thanked me for letting them know. Hence the five stars!

Meal Tip: If you are not satisfied with your food please don't hesitate to let them know! They are all about making the customer happy.

I was able to replace my meal and decided to stick with something I know and went with the Mole Poblano. I've had the mole at Los Agaves and knew it wouldn't let me down. This meal includes a grilled butterflied chicken breast served over their delicious mole poblano, and their signature pyramid shaped Mexican rice and black beans in the cute little tostada shell.

The Empanadas de Camaron is a must try! These were perfectly cooked and the filling of shrimp and cheese melted in my mouth. A great appetizer to start with. My friend also ordered the Ceviche Verde which she let me try and it was just as good. The fish was tender and the green sauce (cilantro basil) was very flavorful.

How does this place compare to Los Agaves? I would say it has the same great taste and flavors but with a twist. Most of the choices on the menu do look familiar but they are different and the choices of seafood are expanded. 

Overall I'm excited we have a new restaurant that's clearly here to stay. The location is within walking distance from the Funk Zone and a few blocks from our beautiful beach. Give this place a try!
Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Restaurants, Cafes | Review: Such a great coffee shop, so welcoming and cool staff, super delicious breakfast and coffees.

Categories: Ramen, Vegetarian, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Restaurants | Review: We were going to eat at CPK tonight. Saw this place while trying to find street parking. So glad we stopped by. Food was great and portions were HUGE. Service was excellent. Don't remember the waiter's name but he was awesome. Great smile and kept us laughing. Tonkotsu ramen was best we had. Flavorful and good amount of meats compared to other places. Kids finished everything and they normally don't. Will definitely come back.

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Seafood, Burgers, Tacos, Sandwiches, Mexican, Bars, Food, Beer Bar, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Nightlife | Review: This place was the BEST when I worked in Carpinteria! My favorite, go-to lunch spot. Can't beat the ambiance of the outside eating area, especially on a nice, sunny (typical) day there.

Prices are very fair, and would recommend the BBQ chicken salad or the veggie burger.

Brought my family there when they visited and they loved it too. Check this place out!!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: I feel bad writing this, because I want to like this place. I grew up working in a dingy small town diner in Wyoming. This was pretty bad, though.

Food was bad. The eggs were poached pretty nicely, maybe slightly under cooked. The Hollandaise sauce was probably from a mix, but not awful. The paprika sprinkled on top was terrible. The large square of soggy hash browns was worthless to me.

Service was bad. I never got a coffee refill. I never was checked up on. Luckily I managed to pay and escape.
Categories: Pizza, Restaurants, Italian, Vegetarian | Review: Food is average. Too salty for my boyfriend and I. Kinda disappointed after looking at so many good reviews. The waitress is very nice
Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: try the fresh fruit summer cobbler ice cream.  It is unreal.

Categories: Wineries, Food, Breweries, Nightlife, Bars, Arts & Entertainment, Champagne Bars, Pubs, American (New), Brewpubs, Restaurants | Review: Great drinks, good food and daycare???!! Yes, they do! This place is amazing. They have a room for the kidos to hang out for a bit while adults play next door. We will be back with the kids soon!!

Categories: Sports Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Bars, Food, Barbeque, American (Traditional) | Review: Very good BBQ. I love their pulled pork and brisket. They also have a delicious smoked Tri-Tip. Beers on tap are decent, and they have a nice little bar inside. They have a ton of TVs for sports fans which makes this a great place to feast for a good bargain.
Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Simply the best there is. You may think it's expensive before you taste it but after tasting it the price becomes unimportant.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Great service and excellent food!!  Attentive and very polite.  If you're looking for a healthy meal you will not go wrong here.  Vegan friendly we will be back!!

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: I just don't see going back to Bouchon anytime soon. It's rather pseudo fine dining but the only thing we liked about it was the lighting. It's probably more geared toward tourists. 
Both the salmon and seabass were the size of a 2 inch square - I thought they had accidentially given us an appetizer portion. The (few) veggies were dry and over salted. The waiter had very little nuance and interupted us several times, asking the same questions. Bouchon also charges $35 as a corkage fee on weekends which is absurd. Opal is $20 and they often even waive it for us. Same with Mollie's (!), both of which are just around the corner. And better. Bouchon is another pricey restaurant - don't be fooled by the ambiance, seems like they're just about profits and posing.

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: Beautiful ambiance ;) we sat on the patio very nice! Good food and music as we'll. we were a party of 11 set menu was a bit pricy but worth it

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: This place opened this past weekend.  We had two macho burritos, one with chicken and one with carnitas.  I liked their carnitas because it's not overseasoned and salty like other places I've tried.  It's just tender pork.  My husband said he still likes the chicken burrito at Los Arroyos better.  The burritos here are a reasonable size, and they bring you complimentary chips and salsa while you wait for your order.  The service is friendly and fast.

Categories: Korean, Restaurants, American (New), Mexican, Tacos, Nightlife, Fast Food, Bars | Review: The tacos were fresh and delicious, and the batting cage was good entertainment for the kids. We sat outside at some semi-shady picnic tables, and for whatever reason, I really loved the atmosphere. My only complaint is that I didn't know everything would come drizzled in a spicy sauce, so I had to go back and order extra tacos, without sauce, for my kids. Otherwise, the food was great--I particularly liked the crispy shrimp tacos. Wish we lived here so we could go back and try them all!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Burgers, Restaurants, Food, American (Traditional) | Review: I stopped by one afternoon for a late lunch and ordered a chili cheeseburger.   The meat was way overcooked and the bun had been toasted until it was burned around the edges.   They weren't busy, so don't know why they couldn't pay attention to cooking my burger.   The chili tasted like it came out of a can.   At least they put pickles on this (but no onions).     

The dining room also needs a good cleaning.   The tables were smeared off, rather than wiped clean.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: What can I say? I wondered why the line was out the door and when I got to the front, I realized it was because people take their sweet fucking time sampling as many flavors as they want. I tried a few things and loved the Sri Lankan Cinnamon, but went with Mint Chocolate, which was awesome. Great ice cream, but I'm not going to jump on the "world's greatest ice cream" bandwagon just yet.

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Desserts, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Seafood | Review: Food was good, service was prompt and overall very nice. Wasn't especially memorable, but it is a solid, easy to access and very lovely restaurant.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: This place was delicious! We were having drinks next door at The Good Lion and decided to pop in around 9:30 for dinner. Service was very quick! We ordered the Chinese fried rice with sausage- SO good! The pork ribs were insane! And the salad with egg, beans and potato was so delicious with the peanut dressing. We also ordered the tofu with broccoli which was slightly spicy (in a good way) with lots of flavor/ garlic. Highly recommend!

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: We went on Mother's day for lunch.  It was very busy and there was an hour wait.  But I would say that the wait wasn't so bad.  We went in and sat at the bar while waiting for our table.  We ordered a beverage and an appetizer.  About the time we were finished, our table was ready.  The food was delicious once we received it.  There was a little confusion on our order, but overall they accommodated us for the wait.  We had the clam chowder bowl, which was delicious.
Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Great flavors, great service and location!   Must try this one. ... and check out the parking lot patio!

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Tex-Mex, American (Traditional), Burgers, Food, Sandwiches, Mexican, Juice Bars & Smoothies | Review: Fresh food, great service. Lots of options for vegans and vegetarians.

We got burgers and veggie stir fry. The Natural Burger had a soy patty and was juicy. The caramelized onions added a great flavor component. Loved this burger, flavorful and delicious. Also got the Zen Burger - this was on the bland side. I wouldn't get this again. Both came with a side of chips and salsa. I'd prefer the option to get it with a salad instead of the chips - but the blue corn chips were yum too. 

The veggie stir fry tasted only like soy, no hint of ginger. I didn't mind, I wanted to eat clean so I was happy with that. But it would've been quite plain and uninspiring for when I would have craved a good Asian stir fry. 

Friendly and quick service. Don't miss the Natural Burger!
Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: Deeeeelicious and beautiful outdoor atmosphere. Two thumbs up for the cheese sambosik

Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Arts & Entertainment, Local Flavor, Food, Wineries | Review: Wine is one of our hobbies, and we love the Sanford wines, especially the Pinots. We were on the wine trail the day before and didn't have time to stop in at the Sanford Winery, and accidentally found ourselves standing in front of this tasting room, so we had to go in. The woman who presided over the tasting was super nice and knew her stuff. The wines were excellent. We were with a guy who thought Napa cabs were the only thing worth drinking, and he was very impressed and surprised with the quality of the wines here. Honestly, there wasn't anything we didn't like. Yes, Sanford wines are not cheap. It's good wine made in smaller batches and you have to pay more for that. We can't afford to drink it every day, but we really notice a difference when we open a bottle.

Categories: Food, Specialty Food, Korean, Chinese, Ethnic Food, Restaurants, Vietnamese | Review: Closed Sundays. Who closes on a Sunday? especially after Halloween weekend when pho is the best hangover cure!


thumbs down in my book.

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: Just tried their menu tonight, sat in the bar area by the window so we could enjoy the moon, the ocean & Palm trees. Ambience was warm and clean, loved being able to see everything , the bar and kitchen. Ordered the duck taquitos, which came in halves on top of mole & beans, meat was tender & flavorful. Also the pulled pork ribs with a green sauce, these came on thick memelas ,So juicey, the red sauce also added some more flavors to this dish. Delicious. Hector came over and chatted, he let us know of the Oaxaca origin of their food. Super nice , the staff was friendly, Marco our server was attentive and easy going during our visit.  This is the first weekend of happy hour , excited to go back and check out the menu & drinks!

Categories: Pizza, Food, Restaurants, Bubble Tea, Sandwiches, Food Delivery Services | Review: Mane - never heard of this place before but I'm glad I'm not into corporate food chains. 

P.O.C. surprised me and I'm glad. Until someplace else pops up - this will be my spot in Santa Barbara.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: Fantastic spot to eat while in Santa Barbara!! Try the eggplant and goat cheese ravioli, simply delicious!!! The evening's special was Italian sausage and spinach ravioli, absolutely delicious as well! We went on a Tuesday night and it wasn't very busy, but the restaurant is small and tables are close to one another. I'd suggest making reservations. Ambiance is elegant/romantic. Our waiter, Andy, was spot on. He was working on 6-7 different tables and he never missed a beat. Food, ambiance, service...excellent!

Categories: Restaurants, Delis, Food, Specialty Food, Grocery, Sandwiches | Review: Got sandwiches to go for a day trip to the beach . I had the turkey with cranberry sauce & brie and it was so delicious he had a pasta salad and just loved it. They were selling pop up store items and had a good selection of imported Italian packages canned food &pasta. Will definitely come back here for sandwiches for the beach! I highly recommend . It was about a 10 minute wait on a busy sunday a few weeks ago

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, Bakeries | Review: This is always one of my favorite stops in Santa Barbara on the Funk zone. They have a good breakfast sandwiches as well as baked goods. They're inside dining is pretty cute, you feel like you're in a warehouse with high ceilings. They also have outdoor seating during the pandemic and before so that is also an option.

Categories: Food, Japanese, Sushi Bars, Do-It-Yourself Food, Restaurants | Review: Fresh, fast and easy. The bowl portions are generous and taste great. For the price you pay you could spend the amount at a fast food place. It had a similar speed and setup as subway (but better). I've driven past this place for a long time and I'm glad I finally stopped by.

Categories: Seafood, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Very happy.... Me and my fiance came by on a Saturday night after a long day at the zoo with our 20 month old. He needed a nap and we both were worried about how he'd be. They were so attentive, helpful and catering to us I can't imagine how they could of done any better. I got the carribean lobster that was in point too. On top of that we had a great view for sunset.

Categories: Vietnamese, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: In Santa Barbara, there isn't too much choice when it comes to seeking some legitimate Vietnamese cooking.

I've heard a lot of mixed reviews from Saigon Noodle House, but my experience from the other evening was satisfactory. Perhaps it was because I haven't had phó in the longest time, but this restaurant certainly made me crave the dish once more.

Unlike Noodle City off of Hollister, Saigon Noodle boasts an deceivingly large dining space, designed very classically like your go-to Vietnamese restaurant: red leather booths, large flatscreen with sports on, bamboo assorted throughout the restaurant, etc. 

And while astonishingly empty (had me worried for a moment), their phó (#6 Large) was fantastic. The broth was wholesome and they were very generous with the varying meats. With quick and attentive service, I'll be hard-pressed to drive the extra distance to Noodle City with solid Vietnamese close by! Cheers.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: The best Mexican food!!! Take out and delivery is amazing. Everything has been so delicious. They are busy but so friendly and worth the wait. 

Recommend enchiladas pizole and margaritas

Categories: Mexican, Tacos, Restaurants | Review: Does Cuernavaca need another five star review? Why not, it's a place that's easy to love. I usually get the simple tacos which are great. On a few occasions, we picked up 30 or so for a family get together. The restaurant is run by some very nice people who make you feel welcome, like you're a family friend.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: My hubby & I were in SB last Wednesday & wanted to try something different for lunch.... We parked & Aldo's was the first place we came too..
Loved the outdoor dining area...  Although it was lunch time, the service was great.....
I had Chicken Piccata with fettuccini in a olive oil/garlic sauce...  The sauce on the chicken was excellent....    You could tell that it was made fresh with a tang of fresh lemon juice... So yummy....
Hubby had a salmon dish with mashed potatoes...   We both had fresh. Steamed veggies too....  
We will definitely return here the next time we come to town.....

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Bakeries | Review: everytime we tried coming here it was closed or busy. We finally made it. 

I ordered the Eggs Rose and you better like olives or you wont like this at all. 
It was pretty good but not sure if it would order it again. I would get any of the breads, we got some and all where great.

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Wonderful food, incredibly flaky service. I've had their croissants, chicken salad, chicken vincenza pannini, and a nice poached eggs on toast with roast peppers, all of which were delicious. However, perhaps it is just being understaffed or under- trained or underpaid, but the people working there screwed up something about my order every time!  I love the food and will keep going back, but you have to go in with a sense of humor and a lot  of patience.

Categories: Health Markets, Food, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Specialty Food, Grocery, Restaurants, Delis, Meat Shops | Review: Such a gorgeous grocery store! I love that they carry a lot of local brands and products that you want to find another grocery stores. Prices are high, but the quality and selection is well worth occasional splurge. Their coffee and juices are also delicious, a great pick me up on your way out! Very clean and well stocked, service is wonderful. Worth checking out!

Categories: Soup, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Bars, Italian | Review: Delicious food and incredible atmosphere! This place is definitely busy for a reason, and it's not just the food - they score major points in my book for a classy ambiance. I came here on 3/10 craving pasta and I was not disappointed. I ordered the fusili tartufati and it was superb! They were very very busy, but the servers were on top of everything and their manager was a very nice young man who seemed to have eyes in the back of his head. He was extremely accommodating when I made some special requests. I'll definitely be back!

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Service was great, however place needs a GOOD clean.   Almost walked out, but based on reviews thought we would still give it a go.  Had fried tofu for the first time (not bad) and. Chicken satay to start which was good.  I got the pumpkin curry which was very nice and also the pineapple fried rice with vegetables, however the vegetables overpowered the little rice that there was so that was a little disappointing.  Waitress was very nice.

Categories: Restaurants, Delis, Food, Specialty Food, Grocery, Sandwiches | Review: A nifty small deli with high-end ingredients and potently flavored sandwiches. As the spelling of Metropulos might suggest, this place has a Greek influence. That's also apparent with the gyro sandwich, moussaka, and Greek cheeses that they sell.

The cheese case doesn't have a big selection, but is filled with quality. It's like the owner only stocks cheese they personally approve. I gave a regional cheese a try - specifically the La Panza Gold from Rinconada Dairy near San Luis Obispo which was like manchego, but much better. There was also a very nice selection of cured meats including a Fra'mani mortadella which Whole Foods has yet to discover.

The purpose of the visit wasn't for cheese, but for lunch. I had that day's sandwich special with mortadella, salame, burrata, tomato, and olive tapenade on a ciabatta bun. It wasn't your everyday sandwich and had a bit of a kick to it. When looking through the entire menu, most of the sandwiches had something spicy in them. Just be forewarned that their style is big and bold. Thankfully, I went to my favorite local winery (Jaffurs) before eating here because tasting wines after eating here massively changes the palate.

Having lunch here is a scream of a deal. Even the expensive food like Jamon Iberico was only $70 per pound. There is plenty of outdoor seating but if the weather isn't co-operating, there are only a couple of tables inside to eat so plan accordingly.

Categories: Bars, Mediterranean, Restaurants, Nightlife, Moroccan, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Italian | Review: Best Happy Hour in Santa Barbara!!! Beautiful views, great food and drinks.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Wow! Amazing !! Amazing food amazing service!! Recommend "Hope" she's an amazing waitress not only did she specialized in my customizations she even offered great options! Truly an amazing place to eat if your down in Santa Barbra!! I had the seafood pasta and asked for a little more sauce because I like my pasta creamy!! Their scallops and shrimp were cooked to perfection!!! My husband had the clam chowder ! He loved it, he finished it to the last Drop!!

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Tiki Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: Came in here for some apps and drinks and Mar was our server . She was so sweet and recommended some amazing drinks for us . I had a spicy margarita and my friend had the millennial falcon fizz and some yummy empanadas . I'm so happy this place is open . Can't wait to be back !

Categories: Bubble Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food | Review: Great location, friendly staff but the star ingredient BOBA is sorely undercooked. In order to call yourself a legitimate BOA joint, you must master the texture. Sadly this place needs help in the area.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Los Agaves but an order of magnitude more hip. Big plates, big energy, big service. Vibrant date night atmosphere paired with the classic Milpas Mexican menu. More pricey than their sister restaurant but I find the added swank to be worth the extra dollar signs. A great addition to the funk zone!

Categories: Nightlife, Wine Bars, Bars, American (Traditional), Tapas/Small Plates, Tapas Bars, Food, Arts & Entertainment, Wine Tasting Room, Wineries, American (New), Restaurants | Review: The grilled oysters were so good and kombucha was great too! The kombucha is on tap! We weren't super hungry or we would've had the daily special! We were there when someone else ordered  the grilled cheese and flatbread...OMG it looked so yummy!!

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Food, Desserts, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Caterers | Review: I think I've been spoiled by all the yougut places in the Bay Area.  You know the ones that let you fill your own cup and add your own toppings and you pay by the ounce. I'm such a huge do-it-yourselfer that being served at Pinkberry just took away from the experience.  Not to say the yourgut wasn't good, it was a great end to a perfect evening.  But I prefer to pay with the knobs on the machine and pick and choose the amount of toppings.

Maybe one day, Pinkberry will trust us to give us more control over our yourgut.  Oh, and that stuff they sell on the wall, crazy expensive and ridiculous but cute to look at.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: My wife and I dined here on her birthday just last Friday. We both agree that it was one of the top 5 meals we have experienced this year. We both got the pan seared chicken breast with corn and pea risotto with an incredible gastrique. We were blown away by the flavors, presentation and service. Salads and desserts were incredible as well. Excellent wine selection. Well worth the price. Slam dunk at the buzzer, folks.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Honestly I went for the experience after hearing about the hype and it ended up being alright. The ice cream wasn't spectacular but the biggest downside was the price e.g. it was very expensive for a less than a pint of ice cream (around $7.50). If it weren't at such a high price point I might consider going again.

Categories: Salad, Restaurants, Pizza, Sandwiches, Italian | Review: Service with a Smile ! Loved this place! Great Service (hard to find these days) and delicious food! We did not have time to wait for the deep dish pie...but we saw it go by and it looked legit! I am from Chicago and I am always looking for delicious pizza! We ordered thin crust and it was tasty. Nice selection of beer and a big plus on State street ...the kitchen is open late!

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: yummy ice cream!!!! what more can I say.  The challenge was choosing the flavor.  They are all good.  Hits the spot after a delicious dinner at Eureka.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: This is by far the worst Starbucks I have ever been to.... waited FOREVER in a short line and then waited 15 minutes for egg bites that when I finally asked said it would be "just another minute"... do yourself a favor, head down the road a bit farther and go to another Starbucks!!
This place needs A lot of training and reorganizing to step up to the level of service most of us are accustomed to receiving at a Starbucks.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: Worst service ever! Went in with my friend to grab a delightful dessert. The blonde girl at the registered was super annoyed for no reason right as we walked up to pay. She didn't greet us at all. She told us the total under her breath, so we asked her to repeat the amount. Her eyes nearly rolled to the back of her head, I kid you not. 
No idea what her problem was, but not very friendly.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: If you want to grab a cocktail with the girls or a desert drink with your honey this is the spot.  If you're after a meal maybe not my first choice. 

The food is tasty, the staff is friendly, and the drinks are great but the price tag can add up.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Venues & Event Spaces, Peruvian, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Nightlife, Latin American, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, Mexican | Review: Fun atmosphere and nice small plates and tapas. We loved the homemade chips with guacamole and salsa, quinoa fritters, octopus salad, and pork belly quesadillas. Our server was attentive and knowledgable. The margaritas were great. Very busy on the weekends, reservations recommended for the best tables.

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: To start off, I was a tad skeptical upon arriving as the line to get in (yes, there is a LINE) was mildly intimidating, but The Palace Grill so generously compensates for this with a two man Zydeco group just outside the entrance (not too close, and not so loud that conversation becomes tedious).  

The Lady and I waited for perhaps 20-25 minutes to be seated which is normally longer than I would prefer to wait, but this isn't Cheesecake Factory, people!  The Palace Grill is an experience that starts the moment you are in line to be seated and does not end until you are out the doors and around the block.  Even then, you will continue to relive the night for days to come.  

Our waiter was a kind and knowledgeable gentleman that attended to our experience with the courteousness of a seasoned garson, but also the joyous enthusiasm one would come to expect of a restaurant emulating the New Orleans spirit.  The intervals between drinks, meal and dessert were well executed and we were made to feel cared for without feeling smothered by over-attentiveness.

While the Lady ordered a Coke, I decided to go for something a bit stronger.  The Palace Cajun Martini suited my tastes perfectly with its infusion of Jalapeno peppers.  I do not recall having had a spicier libation, or a better drink served to me in a mason jar!

I toned down my sense of adventure for the main course, opting for more traditional Cajun fare: Jambalaya.  I can say without so much as a hint of hyperbole that this is, hands down, the best Jambalaya I have ever had.  Perfectly spicy, even amounts of chicken and shrimp; I cleared my plate with all the restraint I could muster (though I came close at certain points to grabbing the dish with both hands, unhinging my jaw and dumping the meal into my mouth).

The Lady decided on a Blackened Ahi Tuna which I was only able to sample momentarily before the fish too was devoured with an equal (albeit more graceful and feminine) enthusiasm.  This fishious was so delicious, with its peppery outer crust and ever so soft inner core, it was a fabulous tangent to my Jambalaya.

For dessert, it was unanimous: The Louisiana Bread Pudding Souffle.  We had spied this delicacy being feasted upon through the window while standing in line, so there was little dilly-dallying when the time came to decide on our post-entree decadence.  It was interesting to note that the request for dessert must be placed at the time you order your entree.  They explain that the Souffle takes thirty minutes to prepare, but you can't help but wonder if this tactic is employed to better guarantee a dessert order.  As we all know, it's much harder to justify dessert on a full stomach...

In short: It rocked.

As The Lady and I stumbled away from The Palace Grill into the cool Santa Barbara night, the sounds of Louisiana Zydeco receding behind us, the tastes of the evening remained in our minds, and on our lips.  Should you find yourselves Santa Barbara bound, do make time for this Cajun gem far from the swamps of the bayou, but right at home in the vibrant heart of one of coastal California's most beautiful cities.

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: Ordered the mixed fried seafood plate and what was really nice was that each piece of seafood was very lightly fried and with some care vs just dumping it in thick batter.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: the absolute BEST Mexican food I have ever had... and I am from Texas and I know my Mexican food.  I practically joined yelp so I could tell all of you people to not just walk, but RUN, to this place.  One of the highlights of my California road trip.  The salsa bar is great... the seafood enchiladas were amazing... I'll dream of this place every day until I get to go back!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Mexican, American (Traditional) | Review: Domingo's has no air conditioning, and a long wait. We walked in and found a booth (self seating) and waited 20 minutes for our table to be bussed, and a menu.  We placed our orders quickly, and waited another 30 minutes for our food. I considered walking out at least 5 times but didn't because I didn't want to take the time to go elsewhere and wait, even for the air conditioning. The food isn't bad but I wouldn't give it more than 3 stars because of the long uncomfortable wait.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, American (Traditional) | Review: I am a fan of Garrett's - I have been coming here for several years now as a local. It is reasonably priced and you get a lot for what you pay for. Make sure to try the Chicken Fried Steak or the Monte Cristo. Word of advice if you are ordering the Monte Cristo: make sure you order your eggs scrambled if you want the "smaller" sized french toast pieces of sandwich - if you order it any different, you will receive a large single Monte Cristo (think sandwich). Coffee is fresh and promptly served. My only minor complaint is the stools at the counter cannot be moved so if you plan to go alone be aware of this - you may feel cramped next to the people beside you. Also the parking situation is not the best but that is not necessarily within their control. I give this place a 4/5.

Categories: Nightlife, Soul Food, Restaurants, Comfort Food, Local Flavor | Review: I've been a couple times after clubbing downtown. This place alwayss hits the spot! 
Its next to velvet jones, kind of hidden on the right side. 
Anyways, they seriously have the best mac&cheese filled with tater tots, jalepeno bits, bacon, etc....
Always satisfies the drunchiess!!!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: The Working Bee cafe embraces all of the great things that a small town cafe offers. The staff is friendly, warm, and welcoming and the food was amazing. We had breakfast and it tasted like everything was from scratch. I would definitely recommend it!

Categories: Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants, Pizza, Italian | Review: pizza is going downhill and consistency has suffered as of late. giovannis pizza used to be really good, but it seems theyre beginning to cut corners, or perhaps they have new kitchen staff. wont be eating there regularly anymore.
Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Ramen | Review: Happy place indeed! Came to Nikka for lunch with work friends. The food was great! excellent flavors, very large portions. Will come back for sure!

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese, Szechuan, Soup, Seafood | Review: This is a nice little Chinese place with delicious food. Of course I needed chili paste with my Chow Fun, but they brought it right away. My husband said his chicken and vegetable dish was the best he's ever had, and we've had hundreds! Sweet owners and chef seemed to care about our food!
We will be back! 
Thanks for such a pleasant meal!

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Sushi Bars | Review: We finally got out to try Sun Sushi - so glad we did! This place definitely lives up to its reviews. 

I had the salmon 6-steps, and in addition to the killer presentation, I found the fish itself to be just perfect. Really fresh, cut just right (I am so picky about that), with correctly prepared and sized rice ball. 

My husband ordered the box sushi roll and the Albacore Delight roll. He said the Albacore roll was good but the box sushi was completely out of this world amazing, and gave me a bite so I could agree with him. Yes, it was, and I'll order it next time! For those who have been to Sakana and are hooked on their box sushi - this is a great alternative.

My daughter got the chef's choice sushi - 10 pieces of whatever the chef thought was best. This was pretty incredible, and I'm sorry for not grabbing a photo. There was no skimping on it as some restaurants try to do - no, this had all the premium nigiri: toro, uni, jumbo shrimp, two kinds of premium yellowtail... The birthday girl was suitably impressed with her order and even more excited when the waitress surprised her with tempura green tea ice cream at the end of the meal.

One of the highlights of the service here is that the servers take the time, when they bring your order, to point out and tell you what each thing on your plate is. So if you aren't enough of a sushi expert to be able to tell what everything is at a glance, never fear!
I definitely recommend this place - a little pricier but totally worth the spend, and it comes with great service and a pleasant, traditional atmosphere.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian, Pizza | Review: Love this place and very grateful for its existence.
Awesome service. 
Pilgrimage worthy food. 
Can't wait to make the trek back to Bettina!

Categories: Steakhouses, Local Flavor, Nightlife, Fish & Chips, Restaurants, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: Arch Rock has free corkage, so I took advantage and brought a bottle.  It was pretty amusing when the waitress asked if we wanted her to open it.  That would be YES, thank you.

The cioppino was good.

Categories: Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: Once again, a hit. I had the sblta which is a salmon blt with avo on a bomb foccacia bread. I had as a side their grilled veggies. A legit hit. And I NEVER eat bread! Of course I worked out at Fitzone plus I had 15000 steps or I would have had it lettuce wrapped lol. Hubby had the grilled salmon special- delish! The garlic mash was bomb and he also had the grilled veggies. Super delish! We washed it down with a bottle of Mac Murray Pinot. The waitresses were wonderful too. Natalie and Hailey. Our dog Lulu witnessed the whole debacle!

Categories: Szechuan, Chinese, Nightlife, Karaoke, Asian Fusion, Restaurants | Review: We were disappointed in our take-out from this restaurant. We ordered mushu pork & the casings were not included.
The other entrees were not outstanding.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: I love great Mexican food, and Los Agaves definitely has it. The bf and I stood in a long line and had to wait a while to get a seat, but it was worth it. They have amazing guacamole and tomatillo sauce, as well as great salsas. I ordered the molcajete and it was solid, but not as good as my favorite spot in Mexico. Yes, I realize I'm comparing the food to actually eating in Mexico, but still, I always have high expectations. The rice was outstanding and the tortillas were nice and thick. My bf's dish was yummy too. I'll definitely come back to give it another try.
Categories: Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Bars | Review: If you're looking for a place to end your night, Tonic is it! Tonic is where you find your new age EDM and shots.

Categories: Food, Vietnamese, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: was my go to pho place in sb for its pricing but after I forgot my glasses and called them, they reassured me they would keep it and not throw it away just for me to find out later when i came to pick it up that they threw away my glasses because the frame was broken  i don't see them throwing away their broken chairs and or fixing their bad service, so how do they think they have the right to throw away an expensive pair of glasses regardless of its condition, especially after i explicitly told them not to throw it away so i could come pick it up

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: What a delight and treat on a short vacation from Los Angeles. Alyssa behind the Bar was friendly, upbeat and provided amazing service. Tossing and shaking the drinks like a BOSS! Buffalo wings were full of meat and the buffalo cauliflower was recommend. Stop in! It's worth it!
Categories: Restaurants, Chinese | Review: Has been a long since I have been here. Came a few weeks ago for a quick lunch and was not disappointed. The food was good and the service was descent.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: I have yet to have something I don't love. Their salmon salad and eggplant salad are amazing and their drunken noodles with tofu are my favorite. If there's a line it's worth the wait.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: had lunch here apr.21 with a group of 17. most had some sort of seafood.  my patty melt burger was about half cooked! most of the meals were in the $20.00 range, my burger was $16.00. if the "chef" can't cook a simple patty melt well done i can only wonder what he did to the seafood! will never go here again, but will tell everyone i know about this place.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: I have been here a handful of times. I give 5 stars to the DELICIOUS Green Tea Creme Brulee- yummmmm! My favorite sushi was the jellyfish. Great atmosphere, very trendy and cool, but loud. A little on the pricey side so its more of a special occasion type place.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: Very rude place. I made reservation early in the day and then later in the day they said they couldnt find my name. How hard would it be to properly notify someone,huh? I will spare the chefs for poor management this time.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: I always liked this place and considered it to be one of the better Mexican joints on Milpas. It just seems more authentic then some of the others and they had more vegetarian options. Plates come with rice and beans and they have sopas. Free chips and salsa as usual. Good service and god food.

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: One of my favorite places. The atmosphere is fantastic, the people is draws are inclusive and fun, and their sandwiches are to die for. The vibe is the best when it's not covid and there is a live band playing music, but even without that, the food speaks for itself. I make sure to stop here every time I am in Santa Barbara!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: My friend wanted to thank me by taking me to lunch, and this is where she wanted to go and I'm so glad that we did!

The server brought some warm chips and a mild salsa to the table. My friend likes spicy so I asked if they had a spicy salsa and it was brought right over ..very good and spicy. 

She had the shrimp chile relleno, offered me a taste and I must say it was an excellent  dish with a nice tomato cream sauce surrounding a chile stuffed with large shrimps and cheese.

I had the chicken alambre with tender chunks of chicken, mushrooms, onions, melted cheese, sliced avocado and tomato. If you get this meal ask for the tortillas on the side as the way this was presented, the not fresh made tortillas got a bit soggy and fell apart. 

Very good service, nice atmosphere (tablecloths and cloth napkins!). I will def go back here for more!

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Irish, Nightlife, Pubs, Gastropubs | Review: Drinks and atmosphere were great! And bartender was very knowledgable of the drinks and alcohol and very friendly and helpful.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: Friday night early reservations during film festival. Great location and atmosphere yet with only fair food quality and poor attention to service we will probably dine elsewhere in the future.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: I love this place. I've gone through most of the food menu here and it's all quite tasty. The "nice rack" (rack o lamb)  is among my faves, along with filet con crema, and the prawns. 

Mixology is great here - blood orange margaritas/martinis when blood oranges are in season, their ginger mojito is delicious, and see if they have bleu cheese stuffed olives on hand. But save me some.

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants | Review: A great place to go if you're looking for a view. The food is pretty tasty, a little greasy but good food to cure a hangover. The burger with the onion ring and bacon (i think?) is to die for, and the fish and chips are pretty bomb too. Also, great cocktails. And you can't beat sitting on the pier, overlooking the ocean!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars | Review: Tasty beverages and cozy atmosphere, perfect for a Tuesday night cocktail. Order at the bar even if sitting at a table. Small enough place, so not a problem ordering and getting drinks from the bar.

Categories: Bakeries, Sandwiches, Food, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Great spot...wonderful service.   But the icing on the cake...David Segall........the best live music in SB ever :)

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Tiki Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: Stumbled upon this bar Sunday around 3pm while we were exploring Funkzone; it was relatively empty so we were able to sit wherever we wanted. 

The bartender is knowledgeable and accommodating, the cups for cocktails are very instagram-able if that's your thing, and the decor is well thought out with open-air beach vibes. 

The drink list is LONG and had something for everyone in our group- strong drinks, creative drinks, and twist on classic drinks. 

Definitely recommend a pit stop here if you're in the mood for a creative cocktail!

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Best sandwiches ever! I never loved the thought of living in SB for 2 years (you can hate me all you want) but this was one of the things/places that made me grow to love the city.

I used to go every Sunday night while I was waiting for my laundry to dry next door and order a #22 (roast beef with avocado I believe) with a small diet coke. The owner, Arash was always there and always greeted me with a smile and hug. He always remembered my usual and we would just sit a watch either football, baseball or basketball. 

The ingredients are always fresh and sandwiches quickly made. What amazed me the most is that after 2 years of not being able to return, he still remembers me and my order! So if ever you visit Santa Barbara, I highly recommend stopping by for a sandwich. It's worth it!

Categories: Food Delivery Services, Food, Specialty Food, Fruits & Veggies, Nutritionists, Health & Medical | Review: I met Mia working on her apartment. I was immediately impressed by her positive can do attitude. It takes a special person to turn a disaster like COVID into an opportunity. She took her skills form years as a resort chef and made them available to locals who may not be able to go to the posh resorts even if they were open. 
She takes time to talk with each new eater to learn their likes and dislikes and comes up with a new surprise for them as often as they would like. 
My wife and I have been very happy so far. My daughter can be a little harder to please but I think that is partially my fault for spoiling her. Anyway if you are looking for an affordable alternative to wondering what to make for dinner at least several nights a week I would highly recommend giving her a try. 
Gary

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: It was almost 2pm on a Saturday when we decided to get sushi for lunch. The Thai/Japanese combo was a little peculiar but we were starving so we gave it a shot. There were only 2 other tables occupied and our server arrived with a big smile. 

We ordered the "Love Shrimp" which was 6 grilled shrimp on skewers with a Thai spicy (really spicy) dipping sauce. The shrimp were fresh and tasty. We love the spicy but maybe not when you're going to eat something as delicate as sushi/sashimi afterwards since your taste buds need a minute to get back to normal in between bites. Also, the Thai chili spicy and the Japanese wasabi spicy get you at different spicy levels so that's also something to think about. 

Then I got the Sushi Combo which was 7 orders of sushi (chef's choice, I like that) plus a choice of Cal or spicy tuna roll. I got the latter. The chef's choice for the day was, yellowtail, tuna, seared tuna, albacore, salmon, mackerel and...can't remember one more. I loved 5 out of the 7 and the spicy tuna was good, not great. What I loved about the sushi was how small the rice was. My pet peeve is sushi with big roll of rice. The sashimi slices were really thick (borderline too thick) and big but 5 out of 7 were so tasty, even when my little ball of rice was already gone. It was like I had the sashimi plate. The salmon was also exceptional. 

My husband had an order of his favorite, salmon sushi, as well as the shrimp and asparagus tempura roll. He liked them both and ate most of my spicy tuna roll. 

So the sashimi quality was great. I'm not sure why they decided to combine Thai and Japanese...the decor was a bit mismatched and confusing.  I'm not sure if I'd go here if I wanted Thai food. 

They should really start a lunch special bento box or a lunch-sized sushi combo to feed the lunch crowd. They only have one menu for lunch/dinner but it's basically a dinner menu so the prices are a bit high for lunch and so are the portions. I would definitely like to see them do a busier lunch with a lunch menu.

Categories: Asian Fusion, Seafood, Restaurants, Poke, Food | Review: This place surprised me in so many ways. 1) the prices are not bad at all, 2) you get a very generous portion of fish (this coming from a male who eats A LOT), and 3) the staff was super upbeat and friendly. Great place to use your AXXESS!

Categories: Burgers, Restaurants | Review: patty melts and french fries. YUM YUM YUM!!!
This place always hits the spot. From the delicious homemade shakes, to the killer tuna and char burgers, this hits THE SPOT :)

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Food Delivery Services, Caterers, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Wedding Planning, Nightlife, Food, Event Planning & Services | Review: My husband and I could not have picked a more amazing place to get married.

Just after we got engaged, my fiance and I were both working two jobs.  I was applying to graduate school and the thought of also having to plan  a wedding on top of everything else we had going on was daunting, to say the least.  When we stumbled upon the Rincon Beach Club, it was like a miracle.  The facility already provided the food, the tables, the chairs, the dining ware, the lighting, the sound system, the service and the guidance!  To top it all off, the outdoor setting and ambiance is exactly what we were looking for.  Perfect for the summer evening wedding we were picturing.

When the big day came, Melissa (our on-site coordinator) took care of everything.  From coordinating the ceremony to reminding me to eat my dinner, she thought of everything so my new husband and I didn't have to.  Her experience and attention to all the details that were important to us made the night exceptional -- for us and all of our guests.  Even my impossible-to-please, has-a -critique-for-everything friend said it was the best wedding he had ever been to!

I highly recommend this venue to anyone looking for a budget-friendly venue in the Santa Barbara area that caters to the patrons that need more than a little help planning an event.  Their reputation throughout Santa Barbara is well known for having delicious food and excellent service.  If I had to plan my wedding all over again, I'd still choose the Rincon Beach Club as my venue.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: I had the Pad Thai, Pineapple curry, Tom yum! It was all amazing and the service was excellent! I will definitely be back soon!!

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Food, Wineries | Review: Awesome?  Can't say that here since Municipal owns that word.  I bring that up because Dave Potter who is the owner and wine maker for Municipal is also the owner and winemaker for Potek.    This new wine label is going to be a higher end label.  Grapes sourced for Potek are carefully selected and would more often than not be considerably more expensive than most wineries would normally use.  While I feel Dave is the master of the blend, Potek is focused more on varietals.

The winery is outside of the Funk zone and is in the new Mill building!   A very nice space and is a producing winery.   The tasting area is spacious and quite beautiful.  They have a courtyard which will be shared by the other tenants of the building as well as their own private patio.   Once the restaurant opens next door they will be doing sit-down wine and food pairings by reservation.

I visited Potek during an extended soft opening.  We tasted a dry Riesling which is made in a process more commonly used for red wines.  Skins are left in after pressing and removed later.  We also had a tasty Pinot Noir as well as a Syrah.   

The winery will be open to the public on or around August 1st.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Cute design outside and inside! 

We ordered the chicken wings, crispy rice salad, and weeping brisket. All pretty good! Probably wound order the crispy rice salad again though because it was mainly deep fried rice and not much else. Interested in trying some of their new items! 

They partnered with a cute froyo place in the same building! We got the mango sticky rice froyo

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: They included a 20% service charge in the price of the food in advance, but the taste and service of the food was terrible. They included sparkling water that I didn't order. They didn't apologize and haven't refund yet.  I think she did it on purpose and that was clearly racist behavior. They even sell Asian food. The chef who makes Mie Goreng must have never eaten Mie Goreng before.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New) | Review: So let me start by saying.... When u walk in the place is unimpressive. However, Roy works magic when he cooks. We had the cream of artichoke soup which was an oral orgasm, pork loin which was an amazing mixture of sweetness and tenderness, the chicken Marsala was also amazing and the flavors in the pasta with chicken pasilla and jalapeño cream sauce was a delight. Roy you are the first 5 out of 5 I have given. If you have the opportunity to stop by I highly recommend you do.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Delis, Breakfast & Brunch, Tapas/Small Plates, Cafes | Review: Great lunch menu, especially salads, if you can sit outside.  Inside, the environment is dull, and they need to bring bright colors and a good designer.  On the other hand, outside is lovely.  Therefore, wait for a table outside and you won't regret it.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, American (New), Wine Bars, Nightlife, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Event Planning & Services | Review: In terms of major cities in California, San Diego, San Francisco and Los Angeles have always had a special place in my heart. But with this recent trip up to central California, it seems that Santa Barbara will be making this triad of favorites a quartet.

It's been a while since I've been in Downtown Santa Barbara... so long that I hardly remember it. Nonetheless, I fell instantly head over heels for this little section of the coastal city. With its impressive landmarks and affable locals, what's not to love? 
And of course, a block of restaurants that would make any epicure squeal with delight.

On this particular night, we decided to try Jane, a restaurant just a couple stores down from the prodigious Arlington Theatre, and we were far from disappointed.

We were seated immediately at 6 or so on a Friday night, in a stunning ambiance. The menu is full of original dishes that play up what would otherwise be considered traditional. After a minute or so, our group decided on splitting the "Pots" of Eggplant Pate and Olive Tapenade and the Goat Cheese Pancakes with Smoked Salmon as appetizers. I ordered the Half Papaya & Dungeness Crab salad, two others the Veal Marsala and the last person ordered the Grilled Duck Breast. We already planned ahead on finishing the meal with a slice of Coconut Cake. Since we ended up sharing everything anyway, here's my take on it all:


"Pots" of Eggplant Pate and Olive Tapenade
Though both "pots" (which were really just cups) of pate and tapenade were very good, I liked the eggplant pate just a bit better because it had a softer texture and flavor. The olive tapenade was just a bit bitter, but still very tasty.

Goat Cheese Pancakes with Smoked Salmon
Though at first glance it doesn't look like much, I couldn't agree more with the serving. Goat cheese can be extremely rich, and too much of it can really kill a dish with a lot of potential. So in this case, having a smaller portion for this type of dish, just a little salty and creamy but flavorfully harmonious in every way, was ideal.

Veal Marsala
WOW. The veal could not have been cooked any better! Though thin, it was so juicy and the marsala sauce was to DIE for. Smoky and earthy, I could have eaten this dish ten times over. Not to mention, the side dishes - linguine and mixed vegetables - were also good, attesting that they pay attention to EVERYTHING, not just the main.  

Grilled Duck Breast
Though just the tiniest bit gamey for my taste, the duck was also cooked to perfection. The wine sauce added sophistication as did the sauteed ginger. Yum!

Half Papaya & Dungeness Crab salad
Unfortunately, there was one thing that held this whole entire experience back - this salad. Otherwise, Jane would have absolutely been five stars in my book. There was hardly any dungeness crab to be seen, and the portion was, admittedly, pretty small for a meal salad. Don't get me wrong - the salad was fresh and the curry dressing that came with it was delicious, but if the table had not shared everything, I probably would have left Jane still hungry.

Last but not least....

Coconut Cake
WOW. Light, fluffy, moist, sweet... everything you would want to find in a great cake. The anglaise that came with it was thin and creamy and -- this description does not do justice for this wonderful dessert. 

Jane is a great place to dine. The service is quick, the food is outstanding and the ambiance is one-of-a-kind. My only regret was ordering a salad... but otherwise, everything else was terrific! I can't wait to be back in Downtown Santa Barbara!!
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Excellent authentic Mexican food. Lots of choices and GREAT salsa and chips. Really good veggie burrito. Will return

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breweries, American (Traditional), Brewpubs | Review: We were seated and waited for over 10 minutes and never got any type of service. Nobody came to ask us for drinks, appetizers, or even a welcome. Plenty of staff and people all around us had food and drinks. Seems a bit odd and unfortunate to me.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Food Delivery Services, Caterers, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Wedding Planning, Nightlife, Food, Event Planning & Services | Review: Rincon Beach Club was an AMAZING venue and catering service for our July 2016 wedding. We couldn't have been happier with how everything turned out. Carol, our coordinator and owner, is AMAZING to work with. She knows the business and makes great suggestions along the way. We booked this venue about a year and a half out and Carol walked us through each step of the way. From the group tasting event all the way through the day of our wedding - everything and everyone was professional, courteous, open to suggestions, and so accommodating. Carol made the day go extremely smoothly. She worked with our vendors, organized our ceremony rehearsal, and helped us pick a very complimentary menu. Many of our guests have told us that this was the best wedding venue they've ever been to. Not to mention, the food quality is TOP NOTCH. Everything from the cocktails, to the appetizers, to the plated meals and dessert was perfect, tasted amazing, and was visually appealing. I can't express how wonderful our experience with Carol and Rincon was. The only downfall is that it's all over now, which makes us very sad! Thank you for everything Carol and Rincon!

Categories: Restaurants, Food, French, Coffee & Tea, Bakeries, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Very good sandwiches! The cappuccino was outstanding too. We stopped by with our kids and the owner wanted to give us the last two pastries for free. They were fully sold out because of the Memorial Day weekend. I hope this place stays here for a long time as we are planning to come back.

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: I had awesome fish and chips, large portion and my son had a huge ahi sandwich. Very cute environment (wouldn't call it a hole in the wall). Sat outside on the patio, no view as located in a strip mall. Everything looked fresh and service was fast. I definitely would return.

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: Atmosphere-4
Food-3
Service-3
---------------
When we walked in I had high hopes that we found an amazing sea food restaurant. I was there with my son (19), daughter (16), and wife. We had calamari (below average), salmon (dry and very average), fish and chips (very average), clam chowder (good), and salads (good). Service was poor/slow (waited on drinks/big pet peeve of mine). Pricey for what ya get!
Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Love this restaurant!! Best service ever. There's this cute Asian boy there and he'd love it if you told him a good joke!!

Categories: Steakhouses, American (Traditional), Desserts, Food, Restaurants | Review: Great food and pie. Love the fried chicken.  Have been going there for years. Home made pies that I take home for the next day. I do not like noisy restaurants and it is quiet and you can actually talk in a normal voice.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Food and price are good. And Ethan( Eason?) offered great recommendation and servicemy parents and I were very satisfied, especially after one-day drive

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Barbeque, Food, Sandwiches, Wine Bars, Diners, Bars, Specialty Food, American (Traditional), Nightlife | Review: Magnificent, marvelous and mouth watering. Two years ago I stumbled upon this wonderful place just looking for a special outdoor dining spot on a weekend visit to SB. The reviews seemed promising and because we were so blown away we have now been back 10 times from LA. The food is the undeniable star here, from an ingenious take on egg McMuffins to the ash smoked eggplant which I devoured, (eggplant is my LEAST favorite thing) this kitchen is impeccable. The deliciously pillowy black cod in a delicate green curry laced with carrot ribbons was so good I didn't even share a bite. The pork shank with the 38 spiced mole was fall off the bone elegant and divine. We adore the avocado gazpacho with bee pollen rim, the oak tagliatelle, and the desserts are unbelievable. Basil ice cream? Homemade Beignets? Semifredo with stiff soft marshmallows and caramel de leche? Course after course this place is just simply spectacular. Upon our second visit we actually went two nights in a row to taste everything. For a while we never wanted to write a review because we wanted to keep Barbareño a secret, but everyone must go. Please make a reservation and make me wait weeks to get in, because I want everyone to enjoy this place as much as we do.

Categories: Restaurants, Chicken Wings, Sandwiches, Breakfast & Brunch, Pizza, Burgers | Review: This place is awesome. The food is the very good and fresh. The owner makes sure you are taken care of .
I will stop every time I am in the area.5 stars all the way.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: We love coming to this spot on our way north or south - their molé just does it.  Sadly with the seating outside, there were just too many flies - perhaps some fly traps would help.  But then there were the sad sad sad nachos that really were just glued with cheese chips.  Nothing special and truly a big disappointment given the quality of the other food.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (New), Food | Review: Build your own hash for brunch was amazing!  Great little place with outdoor seating and delicious food.  Staff was very friendly too!
Categories: Noodles, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Creperies, Chinese, Asian Fusion | Review: Their ramen and pork buns are really good. Price is a little bit steep but all in all worth it!
Categories: Restaurants, Vietnamese, Vegetarian | Review: Just went back the other day and they don't serve the vietnamese sandwiches at this location anymore! BOOOO!!!!

Categories: Bars, Nightlife | Review: Nestled off the main strip known as state street on West Carrillo, not far from the 101 freeway at all, this spot gives you a welcoming... you are at your place, kind of feel. Perhaps you can call it the CHEERS of Santa Barbara. The regular folks that come through will tell you that.

I was introduced to this place last night while checking things out around town. It's very clean, lighting is balanced and the mood is one which simply says "take a load off your shoulders and have a good time." The staff is extremely accommodating. Fran has worked with the establishment for a couple decades now and she's the epitome of the reason why Mel's Place has been around since 1963. 

My small clan shot the breeze with her for a while... we are on a call her by her handle basis now (visit this place and learn what it is for yourself). She filled us in on the history of the joint. She even cracked open the billiard room for us to squeeze in a game.

The game room features darts, pool among other things like a jukebox you can DJ for a reasonable cost. All in excellent condition.The menu has eats and drinks that are reasonably priced.

I read that this spot is closed and no longer in business. Visit and see for yourself that such is far from the truth. You can check out their website to learn more about it: www.melslounge.com

After your visit, I'm sure you'll have some nice things to put up on yelp about them too.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Wow! What a great restaurant! We came here for lunch on a Saturday and are blown away by the quality of food and the "covid experience." 
Very nice outdoor/patio dining area, all of the staff are wearing masks and respecting social distancing. We ordered the Tacos de Chorizo De Crab and the Cazuela Poblana and they were full of flavor and absolutely delicious. 
The chips taste homemade and come out warm, they also come with three different salsas which were all great! 
Jeremiah was our waiter and he was very friendly, helpful, and attentive. 
We will definitely be back!

Categories: Restaurants, Barbeque | Review: Terrible service and overly expensive! It took 10 minutes to put in a drink order and there was almost no one in the place. We asked for water and never got any. 10 minutes after the 8 of us placed our orders the waitress came back to the table to inform us that an arbitrary number of us had to pay a split fee. 3 of the 4 couples at our table ordered the combo BBQ plate (which is $23 and obviously enough food for 2 adults) and the waitress wanted to charge $5 each to put 1 order on 2 plates. So after finishing our round of drinks we told her to box up our orders. We paid our $130 bill and headed to a beach park to eat the BBQ. What we saw in our to-go boxes was less than impressive. The combo plates were such small portions, one couple who ordered tri-tip received 2 very thin slices. The tri-tip sandwich had just as much meat and was on tasteless white bread. 
If the manager hadn't insisted we pay a splitting fee, our party would probably have spend over $200 with drinks and dessert. We weren't rude or being out of control in the restaurant and there was no reason for us to be treated the way we were. Definitely not worth the high prices and deplorable service, 2 thumbs down!

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services | Review: This is a hidden gem in a wonderful city. 

I'm normally not a huge fan of Italian food, but this place is superb! We visited on a Friday night, and the there was less than a 5 minute wait to be seated. Bread was brought to the table within minutes of sitting down, and was so good, we ended up requesting more. The Bruschetta appetizer was fantastic and our entrees were just as good. 

I ordered the Lamb Chop and my husband ordered the Lasagna, and we swapped about midway through the meal, so I was able to try both. The lamb was cooked perfectly  and the lasagna was some of the best I've ever had at a restaurant. 

Via Maestra 42 is a quaint little place with wonderful atmosphere and service, and not to be missed if you are in Santa Barbara and hankering for delicious Italian food.

Categories: Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Bars | Review: For Santa Barbara, I would say that this is perhaps the cream of the crop. Why?

-Large Dance Floor? Check
-People Actually Dancing? Check
-Decent Music? Check
-Decent Looking People? Check
-Minimal Amount of Creepers? Check
-Nice Bartenders? Check (although there is this one girl who is on my shit-list)

Basically if you want to dance and have a good time this is the place to go. Although I'm serious when I say this: they need to play more black music (house/techno/electro has been heard ad-nauseum, we need a healthy mix/balance). Also, I love Wildcats for their music, but DAYUM do they have a disproportionately high creep-a-creepin population who are very persistent. 

Aside from the dancing, I love this place because of the bartenders. Normally I go to my boy Jeremie, who to be honest, I'm not sure if he knows me. Regardless he always treats me right and that's why I'm a fan. He serves me whether I am only ordering water for the night or ordering drinks and he doesn't even give me a lip when I only order water. But all the other bartenders have been really nice to me too, except for this one girl, but we ended up going to another bartender who made it right. If you're wondering who Jeremie is, he's about 5'5, blond, with spiky hair, and always has a great demeanor.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Service was good and quick. They had outdoor seating which is always a plus. The beef tacos would good and the tortillas were as well. Ordered queso fundido which was a tad on the salty side but still decent. The atole was delish. Would have liked to get more sides of salsa.

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Seafood, Burgers, Tacos, Sandwiches, Mexican, Bars, Food, Beer Bar, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Nightlife | Review: Quick-service type of place with picnic tables outside.  Don't forget your sunscreen because there is not a lot of shade!  Otherwise, we enjoyed the food here - the shrimp tacos were yummy : )!  The only thing that would have made them better is if the tortillas were grilled!

Categories: Specialty Food, Fruits & Veggies, Food | Review: Lane Farms has great strawberries, corn, heirloom tomatoes, and pixie tangerines.  Other than these, most of the produce comes from mega-farms and only seems to be locally grown, because of the quaint fruit-stand structure.  Ralphs organic section has better produce at a better price.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: A couple of UCSB professors and I came in for lunch. I thought the service was horrible. We had to flag a server down for chips and water and to even order. It was not acceptable. I asked for the manager, who took a while to come see us. The server said that they were hit with a bunch of people and a waiter called in. Mind you, there was not a lot of people in the restaurant. The manager came and heard me out. He apologized and informed us not to worry about the bill. What was impressive was how EVERY single waiter and waitress came to our table and apologized and asked us to come back and give them a second chance. I honestly was not considering coming back. The service people who came up to us were VERY sincere. I now feel bad for even complaining. I have never been won over so fast. They really did make it up. I will DEFINITELY be coming back. And I will then post an other review.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars | Review: Great restaurant located within Canary Hotel.

Large portions, delectable libations and exceptional bartenders (impressive Bourbon selection).

We opted for the F & F burger (with Fig and Bacon Jam) and the Kale Salad; solid choices, a must try!

4 forks up for FINCH & FORK.

Categories: Pizza, Burgers, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Stumbled upon here after we planned on getting a drink next door. This has been on my list of places to check out so I was stoked to finally make it here. 

I suggest getting here for happy hour or booking a table on Open Table. We got here at 5:30 pm and it quickly filled up by 6:00 pm! Parking is tight and thanks to my rear view camera I was able to get out with ease!!! But take this as a word of caution since it's a tight lot.

The decor is like I stepped back into what I imagined grandma's doll house to be (in a good way) and they played mainstream country which was fun. Decor is definitely kitschy. Outside was adorable with lights and picnic style tables. Would have loved to sit outside if I had brought a sweater for the one week it was cold in SB!

Now onto the food and service. Our waitress was really friendly and nice. Considering she was the only waitress I saw in the joint, she did a pretty good job checking in on us. Food came out fast but drinks were a bit slow as the night progressed along with seeing our waitress. No biggie, the food and drinks were good and I was in good company. 

The Blind Date was one of the better bacon wrapped dates. Bigger than Milk and Honey and I liked the bacon texture better.

Habanero Pork Sliders had good spice but I rather save room for something else.

Brussel Sprouts are shredded, sautéed. Wish they had a crisp and more flavor to them. I LOVE Brussels sprouts and this was disappointing.

Classic Cheeseburger had good flavor but the fries were not crispy to my liking.

Kai's Pizza recommended by our waitress and was delish! It even stayed crispy when I ate the leftovers the next couple day. 

Manhattan was pretty awesome and would get it again and the Moscow Mule was really good. Not too sweet for me. 

I suggest checking out this place next time your in the area!

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: We actually looked at another sushi restaurant but found some of the reviews not as favorable as we had hoped. I have lived in Hawaii with a well known sushi chef in Honolulu and traveled throughout Japan. Sushi is a love of mine and when on a vacation or trip I definitely wanted epic so the reviews led me here. We were incredibly glad that we did. We made an early reservation so they could fit us in and our server, Sal, was one of the coolest dudes around. We ordered a bunch of incredibly rolls, nigiri and sashimi and he even suggested some. It was truly an epic dining experience and one of the BEST sushi restaurants I have visited. I cannot wait to go back. ARIGATO - KEEP RULING!

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Nightlife, Seafood, Bars | Review: The place itself looks clean and well kept, the menu is mostly seafood based, the staff is friendly too. The food itself is just decent, nothing to rave about. The wait time is also another issue, waited a long time for appetizers and also entrees. Maybe it was an off day, if  you're here for the view and not the food, you'll have a good time.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars | Review: Food was decent but service was absolutely horrible. We ordered Sushi and a dinner plate and it took forever to get any help for drinks. If you want quick and crapy Japanese food I recommend this place.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food | Review: Went in about a week ago and no one even smiled at any time.
Way too serious. It was a saturday morning.
I ordered a coffee and muffin for "here" they put it in a GLASS beer cup type thing.
Ok so how does that keep anything warm???
Today went in and asked about a muffing, couldnt remember the name and the blond looked at me like i was crazy. i called it an everything muffin or morning muffin with coconut.
It wasnt there and I asked if they carried it anymore and she said no. 
I said no? and she kept saying no...
wow and I bought a 2 cards this winter with that buy a coffee and get the coffee free if you buy a $25 gift card. well what do i spend it on if no more of my favorite muffins.

Look them up on line and its called a wholesome lowfat muffin.
maybe I can give the card away to someone as a gift.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea | Review: Nice, small & convient location located on State Street near one of the entrances to Paseo Neveo. Luv the White Chocolate Ice Blended Coffee drink. Also...they have a good selection of pastries and treats.
