Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese | Review: This is not the absolute best or worst sushi stop in town. This is a great late night dinner place if you want something fishy (open late!). There is nothing special about the sushi here as it is typical without much wide variety. Not too flavorful or inspirational to the pallet. However, worth a late night taste, especially after a late movie or concert. The service is quick as well, which is always a plus in my books ;)

Categories: Seafood, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: We were hungry and we knew that it has 2 stars but it was the only restaurant which has table  for dinner during the memorial day weekend. I wish we had better options but Moby Dick has table ready and we hoped for the best.

The clam chowder was too hot and feels like it was microwaved. The fish dishes was very bland. The service was good. The staff was attentive and the manager came and refill the bread. No food poisoning but for the location, the food should be much better.

Categories: Fast Food, Chinese, Restaurants, Szechuan | Review: Wow. My second visit to this restaurant. Amazing food, much of which is especially spicy hot.   I made the mistake on the first visit of ordering spicy dishes "medium" which is generally not very spicy at typical local Asian and Chinese restaurants--but knocked my socks off.  This second visit I toned it down and was easier on the palette. Family favorites include vermicelli with pork, tofu in white sauce, and saut sweet pea leaves.   I am not a huge fried rice fan but was impressed with how flavorful it was. Clearly some unique menu items are offered here such as baby octopus, which was yummy, and bullfrog (no I'm not quite that adventuresome ).   If you are looking for Panda Express style food you won't find it here.   If I have any complaints it is service is ok, not great; and just a tad pricey, though I can handle that as the food is that good.  Clearly our family's new favorite Chinese Restaurant in Santa Barbara.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I have no idea why there is always a line outside this place!!!  The food is bland, small portions and not really good Mexican food at all!  There are plenty of amazing restaurants serving amazing Mexican food nearby - go to those instead!!!!!  You will be glad you did.
Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: Amazing coffee and the chocolate croissant was to die for Aldo the almond croissant was really really good.

Categories: Restaurants, Barbeque, Soul Food | Review: Loved their previous spot at wildwood and lots of the menu items are the same.  Great fried chicken sandwich and chopped sandwich! BBQ sauces are relish and fried are great too!!  Santa Barbara is short on good bbq so this spot checks the box! Definitely recommend!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Very nice people working at the restaurant, and hard working, but unfortunately, I didn't get the hype. There was a long wait time for the meal and it was expensive for the meal that was received. None of the taco meat (beef/chicken) had any flavor (ni siquiera sabor sal). The food was unsatisfying.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Was excited to come here since it was right by the dog beach. Had my dog with me and was able to get seating right by the sand.  The staff I have to say was not the best. From the hostess to our waiter, I wasn't pleased. The tone in their voices and mannerisms just was a complete turn off. I just wanted to enjoy a cocktail by the beach and get my daughter a snack before we walked our dog on the beach. I don't think I'd come back here mostly because of the service. Happy hour is to-go only and not offered sitting down.

Categories: Bars, Nightlife | Review: THIS LOCATION NOW OUT OF BUSINESS
Went here on a monday night when it was pretty empty and depressing. Loved it! Small, dark and cramped, with barely enough room to fit the pool table. The bartender was a plucky older woman named Fran. She poured us nearly clear drinks ($6 well) and tolerated our silly behavior with just the right amount of attitude. I've since heard that Paseo Nuevo (trendy shopping area) had to be built around it because they refused to sell...which only adds to this bar's cred in my book. Sad to say, however, I recently glimpsed the bar on a Friday night and thought I saw a collegey crowd spilling out...

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: If you really crave poke, then I guess this place is worth it. Otherwise, prices are a bit expensive for a rather small portion. The fish was not fresh at all when I came the first time, but it was a little better the second time. I'd rather just save my stomach for when I go home to Orange County, where there's plenty of decently priced, quality poke. 

Also, the last time I came here, the worker at the register did not know what BOGO means. How can someone who works at a food place not know what BOGO means? I just thought that was funny.

Categories: Steakhouses, Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: I can't comment on the steak or the service, because I only rolls during the Covid 19 panic. The gentleman who took my order was very kind for the brief interaction we had. Given the look of the place, the large amount of workers inside, and the grub hub drivers waiting outside to grab the orders I felt confident the rolls would be good. I didn't bother Yelping. Lesson learned. I ordered the two special rolls for the day, The crunchy roll and the orange fire roll. One was $12 and the other was 14. The ingredients sounded like a good combination, although they touted the Fire roll as being really hot, which is a bonus for my tastes. I brought the rolls home and they were fairly sized so the portion was adequate, but once my family and I actually ate them, disappointment reared it's ugly face. The quality of the ingredients was severely lacking. The crunch roll seemed like they used stale crumbs, the rice was almost doughy, and left your mouth coated with a dry doughy feeling which took from the other ingredients that should have saved the meal. The same issue with the rice went for the fire roll, only it was even more disappointing as it had salmon in the center and a beautiful slice of tuna on top. But looks can be deceiving, the sushi was actually not very fresh and it couldn't save the day, rather it only added to the sad meal. I'm not an avid sushi eater, but I've had it at least a hundred times in my life, and this was easily the worst I've ever had from an actual sushi restaurant (buffets and grocery stores don't count). If you're in the mood for rolls, keep rolling for your own sake.

Categories: American (New), Mexican, Restaurants | Review: You have to try this place!  It's small and super busy... the traditional preparations are sooooo yummy!! The quantity of food is crazy and the quality is excellent. Everything tastes delicious and homemade.  I had fish tacos and hubby had the Chile Rellenos - totally amazing! Homemade guacamole is on point too. Reasonably priced.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, American (Traditional), Venues & Event Spaces | Review: Very traditional old Santa Barbara eatery. Very crowded the walls are full of pictures red leather booths, food is okay but the drinks are 5/5! We ordered a vodka Martini and it was double the size. The filet minion was just okay. I make a much better one at home. The service is very good. Friendly place where all the old Santa Barbara residence hang out. To me it is just a coffee shop with a huge bar. Would I go back? I won't say I will not. For sure it will not be a destination dining place for me.
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Chicken Mole was incredible! Our new fav to visit prior to a SBB concert! Crowded and hot but worth it!

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Nightlife | Review: Average restaurant, location is in strip mall.  Our party had country fried steak, lacking flavor, the corned beef hash was average. The lime chicken salad was ver small portion, served with thin grilled chicken and was average as well. The best was the driving down the coast along shoreline drive, very beautiful.

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis | Review: We were driving north on 101 and needed a good lunch spot--thanks to Yelp, we knew to stop here!  
Quick and delicious lunch, very satisfying and tasty.
I had the chicken and peso pannini--very good, sizeable enough that I was grateful that I had ordered a half sandwich.
Our group also got the meatball sandwich, the tritip and salsa, and the BBQ pork, and everyone was very happy.
Shop has a pleasant vibe; offers fun specialized sodas; pleasant staff. 
We'll remember this for a future trip!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: The portions are large and the food is generally good, but for the price, I would expect more.  This is one of those establishments where you wait in line to order (like a McDonalds) and then you have to try to find a place to seat.  A "server" then brings you your food, but you have to get all your drinks, utensils, salsas, etc. There are much better Mexican restaurants all over Santa Barbara, including within a stone's throw of Los Agaves.  I've been to this restaurant several times, and each time I was disappointed.
Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: One of my top 5 places for Mexican I've ever been. My go-to is the #16 with a large horchata. And I try to get there on a day when they have chips.

Categories: Cafes, Diners, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Great place for wanting something greasy after a late night drinking. After a much needed, and too much drinking night. My friend and I woke up with a hung over, and we only had less than 6 hours of sleep and had to drive to Norcal. We needed something fast, greasy, and open at 8am.

Came here for their burrito, saw the menudo and got it as well. The chorizo burrito was pretty good, needed more seasonal. Right size though, it was required to order 2. The menudo was good (well...for a hung over person) Plenty of tripe, plenty of puffed corn... a little more flavor can be added. After eating we were off to a 5 hours drive to norcal hungover...

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: By far the best Mexican food that I have eaten. Not very fancy in the front, but inside they have a pleasant decor. We got the stuffed Serrano and Poblano peppers. Very unique and quite delicious! Salsa bar is really nice.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Was disappointed at the amount of styrofoam used in take out. No recycling with styrofoam.
The woman at the register was too busy on her cell phone and several people had to wait for her to finish entering whatever.
Love the coconut soup and service.

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, American (New), Restaurants | Review: The food was tasty and the service was great! Joam was very nice and accomodated our large group making our experience all the better.

Categories: Bakeries, Cafes, Coffee & Tea, Food, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: One a recent trip to Santa Barbara, I was looking for a quick bite before hitting the road. Of course, I whipped out my trusty Yelp, and checked out what was near us. You, wonderful Yelpers, have once again hooked me up, and I thank you!

CrushCafe is both a really homey (like, literally, I think it used to be a home) cafe with tasty breakfast and lunch treats, at a very reasonable price. They serve Intelligentsia coffee. I can comment only on what we had. The hubby had water (lots of water) and the Hangover Cure (which explains the water), and he loved it. Like, couldn't stop raving about it. I had a caramel latte (which was OK, I like my caramel lattes sweet, and it wasn't) and the Peace, Love and Granola. It was super tasty, and I liked their homemade granola. 

Next to the Cafe, they have a Cupcakery. We ordered the Vanilla Bean and the Cookies and Cream for the road. Too bad we didn't try it right there, because we would've easily bought a half dozen. They were both that good. The hubby ate his in two bites and started immediately wondering if we could order online (and no, they won't deliver to the Bay Area, but if you are in Isla Vista, they will). 

Next time, we're in SB, we will definitely come back to CrushCafe!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Bars, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Nightlife, Seafood, Cuban | Review: Their drinks are perfect. Not too sweet but full of flavor. Great vibe, especially on a Saturday. My friend got the piña colada and she was afraid of it bring too sweet like they normally are. Bug to her surprise...it wasn't. I got the mojito which as always just tastes like a non-alcoholic drink. I like tasting a little alcohol but it was still good.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: I recently went with a group of coworkers. We ordered a large amount of tapas to share. Not one dish that came out I can complain about. It was all delicious! We also ordered everything on the dessert menu. It was great! The drinks are great and the atmosphere is funky and fun! I will be returning with frequency!

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch, Diners | Review: Great service big menu great coffee. Stopped in for brunch during a road trip. Incredible friendly and efficient staff. Highly recommend.

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: Usually this location is great but I had a terrible experience here today. I had mobile ordered to skip the line because I only have a 10 minute break when I got there I saw they were out of my bagel. Usually you just let the barista know you're here and they ask you what other item you would like instead so I go to the barista and he says oh yeah they don't have your bagel or whatever it is and then walked away to finish making a drink I was still standing there a little confused he just basically blew me off and he came back and said yeah you can like wait in line and get a refund. I waited in line for 15 minutes so I could just ask for a different bagel.

Categories: Korean, Barbeque, Restaurants | Review: Small restaurant, waited a good 40 minutes for a table for two. Other than that the food was delicious! A little more on the pricier side especially since you're limited to options. If you want more variety of options, you have to pay more since they have different food options. They also have a fee for plates of food you don't finish. I like gen Korean bbq in Oxnard more just because it's cheaper and you get more bang for your buck. However, i really like having a Korean bbq place closer to home. Overall delicious spot for a date night!

Categories: Steakhouses, Cafes, American (Traditional), Seafood, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Sandwiches | Review: Breakfast is our favorite meal of the day so we eat every place we can find that serves breakfast. Joe's is a blast from the past and the food is great. Nothing extraordinary about the place except just good food in a fun atmosphere. It's been here a long, long time so obviously it pleases a lot of people. GREAT corned beef hash (the main attraction for hubby). Servers are "old school" and do a fabulous job of keeping the coffee hot and the diners happy. Definitely a MUST.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Event Planning & Services, Food, Caterers | Review: Rori's is a hidden gem in the SB Public market!

The ice cream is delicious! There are so many different flavors including salted caramel and mint patty chip. Apparently the salted caramel is the most popular flavor. I tried the sample of it and it was really yummy and had a distinct caramel taste to it. I ordered the special flavor: wild berry toast crunch. It's a creamy berry vanilla ice cream with mini-crispy chocolate balls. 
The best thing is the cone! It's so fresh since I saw a worker making the cones right there. The cone is crispy and adds a good crunch to the ice cream. One of the best cones I've had, since it's slightly sweetened. Even better is that they don't charge you extra for the cone! 
The service is great, and the guy was patient when I sampled several different flavors. The only downside is that the size of the scoop is tiny for the price. I wish they gave bigger scoops. It also melted pretty fast. 

I would say this now my go-to ice cream place since I love the ice cream so much. Also because I can't seem to separate from this ice cream addiction..

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Good authentic taqueria food. Their arne asada street tacos fresh are my favorite. Seating/Tables are limited so get your food to go and walk 2 doors down to Oreana Winery and have a glass of their 2017 Chardonnay which pairs excellently with Mexican food.

Categories: Pizza, Italian, Fast Food, Restaurants | Review: Was good many yrs ago. Over priced the owner is a total jerk (Johnny I believe). him messing with hells angels. Said dude they will come back blow your place up. He put on some tough guy NY dago shit. I eat hells angels for breakfast attitude. This is a late night rip off for  young drunks with nowhere else too go. After 9 tax and excess fees can order 30 pie can get 8 slices for 50. All employees even bouncer are rude they take questions from owner. If your gonna be a rip off thief at least make a better pie. You TURD.

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Wineries, Wine Tasting Room, Food | Review: Wonderful! The wines were all superb. Our host was Steve and he was so friendly and so well informed. Our favorite wine was the rosé! I definitely recommend it!

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars | Review: Ordered shabu-shabu and I thought it was fairly priced. The meat was a little tougher than most shabu joints I have been to however I can say that overall this place had good service and food.
Categories: Restaurants, Barbeque, Chinese | Review: This place is legit. You would be a fool not to come here. They got a sweet little playlist going on to.

Categories: Bubble Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food | Review: We showed up on a Monday afternoon, and they were out of both matcha and taro for their milk tea, and out of boba for the drinks. I ordered a large drink and then they gave me a small sized order. I eventually was able to get a lavender milk tea with strawberry boba.  The tea flavor is pretty good, with a nice aftertaste.  Good chewy texture for the boba. Overall 4/5, with -1 star for not having inventory for the most common flavors.  

Otherwise, kind of loud. Has some games in the venue, but not much seating

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Came for dinner and had a great meal. The bao buns were fantastic as well as the sweet potato dumplings. Sticky ribs were tender and super tasty. Uni shooters were pretty good, however, not quite on the level of Arigato's uni shooters. The most expensive item we ordered, kung pao cauliflower, was a very large portion, and tasted exactly as advertised, like kung pao. Service was great as well.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: Was recommended by a chef from another establishment ....we went to dinner in June 2017...The back patio was quaint and inviting. ..The staff was accommodating. ..BUT...that was it.!...The menu was sparse at best....The one item we came for was out and the others were no longer available on the menu.
So we ordered wings and rice plus two  drinks. ..which came to $70.00!!!  We ate the rice...but there may be a few bones,  which they call wings left on the table. ..
If you're looking for a good meal walk on by....

Categories: Restaurants, Italian, Pizza | Review: To first start off, we made reservations for this establishment after our trip to the zoo and came here based upon their review of their food. Once we got there, the hostess that was ignoring, told us that our names weren't on the list, my husband said let's go and I should of went along, but I told him I wanted to try their pizza. She gave us an option to sit at the bar. We sat and was taken our order by the bartender which she got us our drink, my husband doesn't drink so he asked for a refreshment list and they didn't have a variety, so the bartender offered him a cocktail mixer which was fine. We put in our order and I'm not kidding you 1 hour and 25 minutes until we got our pizza, they messed up on my husbands order and brought him a pepperoni pizza instead of a meat lovers, so he sent it back and he has to wait another 30 minutes while I had my pizza. I felt bad and waited with him and my pizza was cold. The overall customer service lacked, and the food is overrated and overpriced. We could of gone elsewhere and had a better experience. The bartender later apologized and said she didn't know why the kitchen staff is taking so long making the pizza. The establishment was too busy packing take out orders than attending to the guests dining in. What a shame Bettina, we found you on yelp based on your high reviews and it was a horrible experience we had. Very snotty staff who seem like they don't care.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Cafes, Restaurants | Review: I came in for dessert only, and I was quite surprised about how good they were. Because I was with a group, we ordered the tiramisu, bread pudding, and strawberry shortcake. The tiramisu was pretty good, and so was the bread pudding, but I thought the shortcake was kind of a ripoff for the price (even though it tasted good). Not bad for an energy pickup after a day of shopping at La Cumbre Plaza.

Based on that experience, I'd come here again and try the main dishes.

Categories: Food, Live/Raw Food, Restaurants, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Asian Fusion, Poke, Seafood, Food Delivery Services | Review: Came here on our way out of SB , it was a hit with me and the hubby. The poke was fresh, amazing sauce and the owner pretty amazing as well. 
Keep up the amazing work.
We'll be back the next time around in SB
Categories: Pizza, Nightlife, Italian, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Awesome place! Best pizza I have had outside of Italy. Been going to this place for over two years now. I recommend the tripe!

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: From the master minds behind the beloved Los Agaves brand, comes another incredible restaurant, Santo Mezcal! Located at the base of the Funk Zone, Santo Mezcal boasts fresh, contemporary Mexican cuisine and eclectic libations. Delicious food, packed with savory flavor and fresh ingredients. 

Artisan, Craft Cocktails 
Try the Margarita Picante with Mezcal, Margarita Jamaica, Sant Old Fashioned or the La Flora Ahumada. 

For those of you familiar with Los Agaves, you might expect everything on the food menu to be incredible and you'd be correct! It is! I absolutely loved the tender Ceviche Pulpo, succulent Camarones Zarandeados and rich Enchiladas Cabo Azul. 

Santo Mezcal will leave you exclaiming, "muy richo" after every bite and sip! 

The menu is beyond reasonably priced and the staff are genuinely eager and enthusiastic to please. The design of the restaurant is beautiful, modern and functional. It's causal, sophisticated, elegance at it's best that oozes Santa Barbara charm.  

Santo Mezcal is a wonderful addition to our coastal community that everyone involved in should be proud of. I highly recommend supporting this great, local business!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: 5 star view, 4 star service , 2-3 star food.  Maybe having alcohol first would have made us think food was better?
Fish not so  fresh tasting and food over salted. Bathroom lineup is weird.  Locals and sorry we won't come back until service and food improves. Maybe we'll try again for a simple breakfast when we're desperate for nice ocean view on a weekday.
Also got a Suprise extra charge on a Caesars for anchovies that wasn't mentioned.

Maybe just enjoy the beach walk but be prepared for  lots of dogs and loads of tar on your feet or shoes after. Other SB beaches aren't as bad for tar and require leashes.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: We went here for our anniversary. I made reservations for 630 and called them that we were going to be late. She gave me some options and we made it work... they made it work! When we got there, around 7, they had a table set up for us. They put rose petals on our table and a personalized anniversary card that made it even better. We started off with a goat cheese tart. It had pomegranate, goat cheese, and some geeens. It was awesome. My wife ordered the sea bass and said it was wonderful. She loved it. Thanks chef! I scored some points. Hahaha. I ordered the rack of lamb. It came a little rare, but the flavor was great. I took it home and had it the next day with an egg over easy... try it, you'll love it! For dessert we ordered the creme brûlée. It was really good. Everywhere we went the rest of the weekend, my wife couldn't stop talking about it. Great place and great service. Oh yeah, our waiter just happened to be from New Mexico... hmmmm... what are the odds? We're from New Mexico... he was great too. Sine it was a special occasion, the cost didn't matter. It is pricey! Go there! They'll take care of you! Oh and the reviews that say to dress up... meh... I saw some people that weren't, but overall everyone was dressed up a bit. I was probably overdressed in a suit and tie. I could have lost the tie and been fine. We want to go back.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, French, Salad, Sandwiches, Desserts, Bakeries, Food | Review: I am in love with their tres simple menu avec fresh and local ingredients. I love getting the sandwich with an egg on top of it, pain de main, grureyare melted cheese, ham, grilled onions, i add mushrooms. the flavors pop and melt and oodle in your mouth. The salad that it comes with is so fresh and the cherry tomatoes are so sweet, it all comes together with their house vinagrette. 

I've come for breakfast and it is light and not bad, but definently worth a little more for your buck if you come for lunch. 

no time? grab a sandwich to go! the tuna sandwich...3 out of 5 stars. maybe try the roast beef?
Categories: Bars, Nightlife, Pubs, Comedy Clubs | Review: Visiting from out of town, bartender Nathan was super fun and made us Kamikazes the right way unlike that place Tonic. Goos times had by all!

Categories: Bakeries, Food, Delis, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Local Flavor, Italian, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: On Easter, we drove up to Santa Barbara to eat and relax. It was such a nice day and perfect for some outdoor dining.

Enter: Pierre Lafond. A charming French bistro ocated on the busy and popular State Street, AKA  drunk street for UCSB students.

We waited for 5 minutes before being seated on the patio. I noticed that service was pretty damn slow. I'm not sure why, but based on other people's reviews, that seems to be normal here.

We order 2 bellinis, lobster putanesca for me and salmon club sandwich for him. 

Bellinis were standard, but hit the spot. I mean, gave me tingles in my head. 

Lobster putanesca was good, except for the lobster itself. Booo. It was too hard an chewy. I did like the angel hair pasta and the sauce though. 

S had to send his sandwich back because the bacon was not cooked fully. He likes bacon crispy and not fatty like it came. Can't blame him!

We had another bellini each to finish off the meal! 

Overall, I'd come back. But, I would order something different, along with a mimosa next time. 

Side note: An artist was sitting behind me and sketched a caricature of us. It was a cute touch to lunch, although he just got my back,  but it still looks like me! Didn't really look like S' face though. Oh wells. It was Easter, so we bought it off him for $10.

pix- http://pandaliciousforme.blogspot.com

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Okay, so you are not going to find gourmet flavors here, or 31 choices, but it's on the wharf. That is what they have going for them.  And damn it, sometimes you want that ice cream on the sea.  Expect your simple classic flavors and that's it.  I found Cherry Vanilla.  Why, I haven't had that in years!  My fave now is Charry Garcia.  But it started as a child in Thriftys with two scoops (cylinders) of cherry vanilla and chocolate chip.  And it just matured.  But here was cherry vanilla to take me back a few decades.  

And there is nothing like the simple pleasure of eating your ice cream cone with the salty breeze in your hair, the seagulls floating by, people watching and boat watching, and walking along the wharf back to your hotel...

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Crab cake Benedict: best I ever had. 
Great outdoor patio and friendly staff. 
Overall: highly recommended

Categories: Patisserie/Cake Shop, French, Food, Restaurants, Bakeries, Cupcakes | Review: The Little Things Bakery made the most beautiful and delicious wedding cake for our wedding. We love lemon cake and were thrilled to see that as an option. They were accommodating and helpful during the ordering process. We settled on half lemon and half chocolate for a three tier cake and a sheet cake for 150 people. They were also the most affordable in the area. The cake tasting was great and the fee was even applied towards the final bill. They worked well with my other vendors as they had to deliver to The Hilton Santa Barbara Beachfront resort and our florist Besame Florals.

Categories: Mexican, American (Traditional), Burgers, Restaurants | Review: Lovely little shack on the side of the road.

We ordered:
cheeseburger - it was just okay...pretty plain tastes but the beef had a decent saltiness to it.
pastrami double cheeseburger - the pastrami was good.  well done but like the wifey said in her review it just fell apart on the first bite...not constructed too good but when have I ever been ashamed to get a little dirty for the sake of gluttony?  good!
fresh fries-crispy and good
chili cheese fries - chili was sort of a let down...I make chili (for hours with very careful attention to details) very well so most chili is ehh to me.

SERIOUSLY?!
refills-.50
side dressing-.60

Great little road stop but they have a lot to learn.

Categories: Lounges, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants, Hookah Bars, American (New) | Review: I just left blush on a Wednesday during fiesta and I cannot say I had a better time. My new friend and bartender Justin hooked it up the entire night with great recommendations and the best drinks in town. Needless to say I will be back very soon. The service the drinks everything is amazing I wish I had order food!

Categories: Italian, Salad, Pizza, Restaurants, Sandwiches | Review: LOVE Ginos!  I am so happy to have found this place.  They have wonderful specialty pizzas (I recommend the Polpetti, with basil, onions, and meatballs).  They also offer really great slices for lunch.  The garlic knots are delicious, and salads are great as well.  Best pizza in SB!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Latin American, Mexican, Seafood | Review: Best, most unique tacos in Southern California 
Fresh, organic ingredients used
There salsas are also the best too!

Categories: Fast Food, Chinese, Restaurants, Szechuan | Review: Real Deal! Some say too expensive for Chinese food. Really? Is $13 too expensive for a veggie omelette next door at CK? They use really fine ingredients that I am sure must be brought in from SGV, not found in SB. The food is prepared by chefs, not generic cooks. 

This style or region of Chinese food is very spicy and garlicky so if that is not your thing this place is not for you. But if authentic Szechuan is your thing than SB / Goleta has only one real choice. Lucky Dragon, I have been about twenty times since they opened and not one bad bite of food.

Categories: Restaurants, Chicken Wings, Sandwiches, Breakfast & Brunch, Pizza, Burgers | Review: Not so sure what the hype is, our chipotle chicken sandwiches were drenched in sauce and our food took forever. #Bummer

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars | Review: Love this place! Food is great and pickled veggies rock. Staff is very friendly and helpful. The roof of the hotel is gorgeous as well.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Gelato, Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt | Review: This place is so good! There are many flavors to choose from and the people there were so nice allowing us to sample which ones we liked so that we could order. The names are kinda hard to pronounce though because their Italian. But I loved this place. It's very cute! Tucked right in the middle-ish of downtown, I guess. If we ever come back to SB, we're visiting again! Great after dinner dessert to celebrate our 2 year anniversary!

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: After reading the positive reviews we wanted to try Olio E Limone.  We made a reservation.  Upon checking in the host asked " are you fast or will you take your time eating?"  Reply "Is there a time limit for dinner? Did not know the restaurant was fast food."  We were told it depended were he would seat us.  The host really spoiled our experience.  Food ok.  Waiter, Gustavo, attentive, but we will not return.

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Ramen | Review: Well first things first, it was packed but we got seated pretty quickly which was a plus. The server got to our table after about 10 mins of being seated, they seem to have been short on servers and it was busy so I understand. I would say the lost points on the pricing and size. First relatively more expensive than most ramen places, where the bowl (w/o add ons or subs) is around 9 or 9.50. Here, they all start out at 11.50 (they close between dinner and lunch times) and add-ons are pricey as well ranging between a $1 and $1.50 where other places are .50/.75. And there bowl is definitely smaller than most places too; so those were the minuses. The ramen was extremely tasty and broth was super flavorful. A must try when in the area!

Categories: Gluten-Free, Gastropubs, Vegan, Restaurants, Indian | Review: This is the best Indian food we have had! The Nirvana chicken, tandoori shrimp,garlic naan,and chicken marsala are now our favorites.The servings are enough to share and the food is delicious. Definitely try this restaurant and its true authentic Indian food. Located on State State with several parking structures near by, great place for lunch or dinner. We will definitely be back when in the area. Cheers!

Categories: Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Another great meal at Roy! Had the pork tenderloin with cranberry sauce, vegetables, and his super yummy potato cakes ... soup & salad included ... for $20!!! Everything was excellent!! Love this place!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: I have been to Empty Bowl a number of times and every time I go it has been a great experience. All of the employees are very helpful and kind. Having great people serving you is half the enjoyment of a great restaurant. 

I have tried a number of items off the menu all of which are great. If I had to choose one or two as my top choices it would be the pot stickers, which are house made. They are unreal, so great! And my favorite main dish is the Tom Yum noodles, simple yet full of flavor.  

Empty Bowl has such a great thing going and I can't wait to enjoy this many more times in the future. Keep up the great work!!
Categories: Restaurants, Indian, Himalayan/Nepalese | Review: Great food, great service! Highly recommend the buffet as a nice variety!

Categories: American (New), Bars, Restaurants, Lounges, Nightlife | Review: Meh...I was super excited to check out this relatively new spot in Santa Barbara that I somehow hadn't heard about until this weekend. The Ambiance is great, it reminds me some what of West Hollywood, some Beverly Hills patios. The service was also very good. Our server was very friendly and attentive enough without being overbearing. I was so disappointed by the food. For just under $200 we each had a drink, a salad, an entree and split a dessert. Drinks were fine, salads were extremely small and didn't have much to them. The calamari salad had a great flavor and all of the ingredients were very fresh but it had no more than 5 eensy weensy pieces of grilled calamari hidden under a small pile of lettuce and avocado. Same goes for the gingered beet salad. I'm not someone who needs a ton of food and I'm also not cheap when it comes to dining out but these salads felt like a major rip off for what we got. For entrees, I had the Halibut and my boyfriend had the mussels-the salads at least had some flavors going on in them but the entrees were extremely underwhelming. Was a single spice used? Salt, pepper? They were not ridiculously small like the salads but they were small on flavor for sure. I've never had such a bland meal. I hate to say this but it was one of my least favorite meals I've had in Santa Barbara. The dessert-a strawberry cake of some sort was delicious! If I'm going to shell out $100 per person I'd pick somewhere else in town like Sama Sama or Lark-where you leave with amazing flavors going on in your mouth and still get to enjoy a great ambiance with your meal. I would come back here for drinks, and dessert...maybe, but probably not.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars | Review: This place is only getting 5 because I can't give more. So good. 

Had the scallops and my husband had the short ribs. They were so tender. 
We ordered a banana shake to go. Not even close to being disappointed. 

Very happy we chose this place.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Desserts, Wine Bars, Bars, Italian, Nightlife | Review: Dream come true!!
A few years back, I read Mollie's story in her cookbook and imagined one day bringing my brother (a Culinary Arts student at the time) here for dinner! For his birthday, I saved up my nickels and made the reservation! The salmon fettuccine with pink sauce special was divine, and arrived at our table in a whimsically lavish shell! Mollie is an excellent & elegant chef! Welcoming atmosphere & service made the occasion celebratory and memorable!! 
Grazie mille!!

Categories: Restaurants, Vietnamese, Vegetarian | Review: Wow! $10 for a bowl of pho ? It was so so so small....portions ! Would only come here if I was desperate and or super hungry; and would need two bowls. Noodles they put in weren't even the size of my fist. Broth was okay.... Meatballs were each chopped up into 4 slices per meatball ..... Seriously , this place is a joke

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: Very authentic. Almost as good as home. A little expensive but almost worth it. As usual with American "fish n chips" too much fish and not enough batter.

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Best Tiramisu I have ever had! My husband who doesn't like Tiramisu wolfed it down, which says a lot. The cake is really moist with bold flavour of espresso and rum, and the cream is very velvety. We will come back to try their other pastries.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Bakeries | Review: This is truly and Santa Barbara find. Their pastries are obviously amazing and mainly what they are known for, but they sandwiches are what I recommend. Located around the corner from the Santa Barbara Roasting Company next to Le Reve Organic Spa & Boutique, this is a place not to be missed!

Categories: Breweries, Food | Review: Great atmosphere indoor and out! Awesome band, We Three, rocked the place! They were a perfect blend of rock, blues and folk, while we enjoyed the delicious variety of brew!

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Seafood, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Venues & Event Spaces | Review: After multiple visits I have yet to find a meal that I want to return to order (although I snitched one of a friend's chicken wings and enjoyed it). 

My tuna melt today was so sub par it is hard to describe. Suffice it to say unenjoyable, inedible.

On two different visits, I have had to go to the bar to order our drinks because our server did not come to our table to take our order after we were seated. 

I am soooo disappointed. I hope they get it right. I want this place to work. 

The noise level has been addressed by others so no need to say anything more about it.

Having said all this it was full on a Sunday night close to 7pm with patrons the ages of my kids so maybe I  am not the target customer.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Tiki Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: This was the highlight of our stay in Santa Barbara. We went both nights, first night midweek was very chill and less busy with tiki music, next night was closer to the weekend and they were busy and playing some nineties gangsta rap and both vibes were really great! Bartenders are very friendly and mix great drinks...plus it's like nothing is open late in this town? Like everything seems to be open 12-5 for some reason but I'm in vacation ya know?!? I want to have after dinner drinks and hang out...so It was nice to find such a great bar to finish off our evening.

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: Superb!  We love to meet our friends there especially when it is Lobster Fest.  Tercel (? - not sure if I got the name right) was probably one of the very best waitresses I have had.  She was so very interested, fast and happy .... wow! Enterprise, see you soon.  We are most happy.

Categories: Bars, American (New), Tapas Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife | Review: I recently visited Nectar with a friend for dinner. I enjoyed being at that beautiful bar and especially enjoyed their blood orange margarita. For dinner my friend and I shared the chicken kebabs with flatbread they make in - house, and the lettuce pockets as well as the salmon entree. We loved the food and the drinks. Service was perfect as was the location.  A unique place with unique menu offerings. A must try if you are Santa Barbara.

Categories: Garage Door Services, Restaurants, Live/Raw Food, Food, Home Services, Mediterranean, Keys & Locksmiths, Burgers, Vegetarian, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Vegan, Salad | Review: Met one of the owners Jamal, really sweet man!! We had the umami burger!!! Delicious!! The tacos and empanadas and both delicious!! My 10 yo son loved the calypso smoothie and their amazing back Ava and my mom enjoyed the chocolate cake!! Amazing service. Came in at 2:00 pm on a Sunday!!  We will definitely be coming back!!

Categories: Bubble Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food | Review: Last week with the heatwave we had, this place was a must stop to cool us down!  Even as crowded as it was our order was done fairly quickly and it was just perfect.  The girl at the register was friendly and quick.  We all had different drinks , mine was a slushy type with almond, coconut and strawberry with strawberry boba.  It seems to be a fun place to just stop and have a drink, they have huge games, which can keep you busy as you wait for your order, and a nice seating area to just chill!!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: A no frills taco joint where the food is the focus. Authentic and extremely inexpensive. My only tip is to order more than you think you'll eat to avoid waiting in the long line for seconds.

Categories: American (Traditional), Vegan, Restaurants | Review: Who said vegan had to be boring.  So nice to have a vegan/vegetarian upscale restaurant option.  Food is creative but more importantly, delicious.  We had the zucchini lasagna.  It was crisp and refreshing with great flavor imparted by the pesto.  The lotus bowl was also delicious but a lot of food.  All the flavors worked well together.  We ended the evening sharing the chocolate fudge torte.  Chocolate decadence.  Can't wait to come back and explore the menu.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Lounges, Seafood, Spanish, Italian, Nightlife, Mediterranean | Review: Great food, great customer service, the staff was very knowledgeable and helpful. The roasted Mary's chicken was the best chicken we have ever had.

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: This place is busy! we stopped by on a Tuesday night without any reservation and luckily we walked in right when they opened and was able to grab one of the remain free table. by 5:45, all most all the tables are filled already, so make sure you make a reservation if you plan to go to this place. 

Now the food, some are good and some are ok. We ordered the popcorn crawfish and the sample platter. To me, the popcorn crawfish has a lot of batter so I really didn't taste the crawfish much , the dip however was pretty good. Next came the sample platter, for the price I say don't order it, just get something else on the menu. The major portion of the platter consist of grilled Vegetation and rice (which actually both were pretty tasty). Also on the platter was the coconut shrimp, which is good. The blacken cat fish - delicious, and the filet mignon (could use some seasoning) and chicken which was a little dry. 
 
The sample platter also comes with a Louisiana Bread Pudding Suffle... by the time this came out we both were stuffed so we took a few bite of this and got it to go. I think the Suffle was the highlite of the whole meal, it's remind me of cinnamon buns but with a consistency of moist bread with their sweet whiskey sauce poured over it. it was amazing!

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: Omg they let you taste test with the cutest little spoons, and everything is so freaking delicious. Of course I'm partial to pumpkin pie cause anything pumpkin is a must try for me! Also I really enjoyed the dark chocolate chips and nibs!!! Ugh get in my belly!!! Staff super friendly!!! Good amount of seating inside!!!

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: I love this place. They serve really close to Asian food though it was a kinda fusion style. 

Always get their Korean glass noodles. I love it a lot. 
Bf loves the pork belly bao as it is so fatty.
I like the shrimp bao a little more. 
They had seasonal items all the time. 
Price was reasonable. 

I'm looking forward to their new location now!

Categories: Fast Food, Food, Acai Bowls, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants | Review: Didn't know what the hype was about this so I decided to give this place a try. My friends recommended me the Original flavor and that's what I got. My goodness, this is probably one of the best tasting smoothies I've ever had before! It's very fruity and the texture of the smoothie is very smooth. This definitely takes the cake in the ballpark. Beats Jamba Juice. I can't wait to come back and order 20 more smoothies from here!

Categories: American (Traditional), Seafood, Sandwiches, Restaurants, Burgers, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Really tasty food and pretty big portions for the price. I wish we could've taken pictures of our food but we ate it too fast. The clam chowder was full of clams but i have tasted better. I do rate it better than average because the amount of clams. The shrimp roll was very tasty. It was the best shrimp roll i've had in a long time. My son loved his calamari. It was cooked to perfection, served with 2 tasty sauces. My husband got the pulled pork sandwich, overflowing with meat. Be forewarned..it had fried jalapeños...spicy! He really liked it. Overall, well worth out detour to our destinations. Thanks Yelpers for helping us find this hidden gem!

Categories: Wine Bars, Restaurants, American (New), Nightlife, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: The uni was not fresh and not properly cleaned tonight and the stone crab was not as tasty as I recall.  

The grilled scallop, in the scallop and pork belly dish, was disappointing.  The grilled scallop was raw and had an unpleasant taste that caused me to question the freshness of the scallop. 

The fall salad was served with only two tiny slices of persimmon. Unbelievable!! Mentioned to server (Emily) if this is how the salad is typically served and she responded that she'll check with the chef.  Emily later returned with a plate of sliced persimmon but did not answer my question nor give an apology from the chef if there should have been more slices in the salad.

As if things could not get worse, I learned that they no longer have the lemon curd on the menu. Again, no specific explanation when asked why.  

The food and quality was so off  tonight  + the lack of explanations from the server prompted me to ask if there were any changes recently.  Sure enough there was!  New management (same manager from the Hollywood location) and new chef since mid-November.  Finally, it was beginning to make more sense to me. 

Would like to know where the previous chef is working - food was much better prepared and lemon curd was on the menu!

Overall experience was that the seafood was not fresh, poorly prepared and service was disappointing.  =(  But still left a decent tip for Emily because it's holiday weekend. 

When I called to speak with the manager.  The manager asked for a call on Wednesday. Wow, talk about poor service!  How about calling me back on Wednesday??  

Again, after tonight's experience, I am much wiser not to ever return.  Sad to say that my once favorite restaurant has gone downhill.  Seafood no longer fresh but they still charge you premium price for it!?! Time for this place to close up shop, stop ruining people's dinning experience and wasting people's money.

Categories: Restaurants, Indian, Pakistani | Review: the tandoori food plater was perfect. Fresh and not too oily. Good value for the price. It was also awesome that an old Indian guy was the chef! Made it taste more authentic : ) service was good.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars | Review: I recently had the pleasure of visiting the Good Lion for the first time on Sunday night and I'm upset that I haven't been to this cocktail bar before. Amazing atmosphere, the drinks are top notch, music selection was on point, and all of bartenders were on their A game. Thanks for taking care of the M8RX family again, we'll be back!

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Tap Thai had a fun, hip environment. We were greeted at the door, seated and promptly attended to. An Axxess deal brought us in, but the prices were so reasonable that we didn't even need to use it to get a cheap dinner (even though we still did). The selections were simple, but robust. The classics were there and a few extra dishes for those needing something new.

We got the Yellow curry and the Drunken Noodle. The drunken noodle could have been more flavorful, but was still a great dish. The Yellow curry was amazing--definitely on the sweet side, but I had no problems with that. 

I would absolutely recommend this place and encourage people to go support this great little Thai spot. It may not be the most "authentic" place you've ever gone, but you will have a great experience.

Categories: Food, Meat Shops, American (New), Burgers, Breakfast & Brunch, Specialty Food, Restaurants | Review: The best thing they had going the bel campo burger is no longer available :(
Their meat quality is great but their other burgers are mediocre and really disappointing.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: The location is on point! Its located right in the harbor in SB. I will definitely be back because the service here was great, which I feel is very hard to find now a days with restaurants. Also the guys who work here are very easy on the eyes which also added to a great overall experience. 4 stars and will be coming back! :)
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Love the fresh fish and salads here, and the nachos are hands-down the best in town.  Gets very busy so be prepared to wait.

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Desserts, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Seafood | Review: This place was amazing Alana and Chelsea were amazing the food was excellent and a good price I would highly recommend this place

Categories: Acai Bowls, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Bubble Tea, Desserts, Food | Review: Pinkberry can learn from this place.  Self serve is so much better, more flavors of yogurt, and more toppings as well.

Cons?  Yogurt isn't as tasty as Pinkberry's.

Categories: French, Food, Restaurants | Review: Welp, I discovered the secret ingredient to the secret sauce everyone keeps talking about.  

It's FOOD POISONING!!!!!!  ***ding ding ding! bells! whistles! alarms!*** What do I win for figuring it out??  One full miserable day of heaving and hoeing while being curled up on the bathroom floor for hours, complimentary of the chef!  Aw shucks guys, you shouldn't have.

Really, I shouldn't have and I never will again - eat here that is.  I should have known after eating a meal where __everything__ tasted like fishy mustard that consequences would be had.  At least aside from feeling explodingly crappy, I've never looked better from the waist down!

Categories: Tuscan, Seafood, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife, Italian | Review: We were in town to celebrate our honeymoon the weekend after Christmas. Made reservations 5 hours ahead of time on a Sunday for 8pm. We let the hostess know we had a stroller with a newborn and asked if they could accommodate us. They said no problem. We walked all the way from our hotel by the harbor to the restaurant. I am a new mom, so I even breastfed right before dinner in a dressing room on the way over just to make sure our newborn would sleep during the dinner. We were starving. Checked in with hostess and she said it was going to be a little bit of a wait. No problem. Then she came back after a few minutes and informed us that she "didn't believe there was room for us and a stroller", but told us we could go to the pizzeria next door. Most Italian food restaurants are a little bit more family oriented. What a disappointment. If you have children, I wouldn't recommend this place. Disgraceful.

Categories: Barbeque, Restaurants, Chicken Shop, Hawaiian | Review: Came here on a Thursday evening to check out this new place! I got the hoagie and fries and my boyfriend got the baby back ribs plate. The baby back ribs had a really seasoned and tasty rub on it and the meat was tender and literally came off the bone so easily. Downside was that the hot bbq sauce wasn't spicy at all, pretty much tasted like a regular bbq sauce with no kick to it :/ the meat on the hoagie was tender but tasted dry and bland with no particular taste or seasoning like the baby back ribs did. The fries however were very crispy and were really good. Overall this restaurant was good but nothing out of this world.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Incredible food. This is the gold standard. I go for the curry noodles every time but also know the pad Thai and hangover noodles are very good.

Categories: Restaurants, Salad, Soup, Sandwiches, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: fantastic!! we drove in from LA and pretty much stopped right here. really sunny day, wonderful outdoor seating. the service was friendly and quick. nothing hit the spot better than a big, hearty, healthy sandwich with great ingredients. i had the turkey with brie, and i got it on foccacia bread. i liked that they have many toppings. i'm a toppings beast.

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: This is my favorite new spot in town, excellent every time we go and we look forward to going every time. The baos resemble a taco but the flavors fuse together in very interesting ways. The pork belly and kfc are outstanding. Cant wait to go back!

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: Very solid and gourmet Italian joint with classy service.

Their white sauces are to die for.

The burrata appetizer tasted just as good as Osteria Mozza in LA. The prosciutto pears were also an elegant starter.

The pasta dishes were freshly made. Lobster ravioli was a fine choice in a white sauce as was the make your own fettuccine Alfredo.

Categories: Tapas Bars, Restaurants | Review: Went do dinner here about a week ago, and absolutely loved my meal!  Our server was great.  Food and drinks amazing! I had the "Meet the French" which I loved.  It was so delicious.  My gf had the paellita which was also good.  Even the chips and salsa were good!  We both had mojitos (not that strong) but tasty.  The place itself was pretty dead for a Friday night, but I liked the decor of the restaurant.  Good for a date night if you're in the Santa Barbara area.

Categories: Seafood, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, Farmers Market, Restaurants, Food | Review: I absolutely love the SB Fish Market above all other fish markets in Santa Barbara. This is an incredible place to buy fresh, local, seafood. From rock crab, to black cod, uni to oysters, octopus to lobster, SBFM is the BEST seafood source in Santa Barbara, short of catching it yourself in the ocean. The prices are reasonable and the staff is very helpful. They are also on SB Axxess, so save your 25% off for the those big purchases when you need to buy a lot of seafood and keep more money in your wallet for ... I don't know, drinks or dessert. :)

Categories: Restaurants, Hot Dogs | Review: I bought 3 hot dogs to share with my GF.  We had the Pastrami Dog, Santa Barbara Dog, and Dave's Dog. They all tasted pretty good. 
I have a couple of complaints.  I split the dogs in half to share.  Depending on which half i got I may or may not have gotten any bacon meaning the bacon slice was quite small. Also, the dogs themselves are the smaller variety.  We both ended up with heartburn which is very uncommon.  The cost also is a bit high. I do understand they need to make a profit.
Given they get very high reviews overall, take my review with a grain of salt.  For myself, I probably won't go back.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: When it's good, it's some of the best ice cream. you can tell it's made from high quality ingredients in an artisanal way, but OCCASIONALLY I experience poor customer service or have an off prepack from the store. if they could improve their consistency with their new ventura production facility, I could give them 5*

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Fish & Chips | Review: There is no picture posted because I was hangry!!! Definitely delicious, hubby's michelada was the high light of the night. The asada quesadilla was amazing. I had the enchilada with shrimp in the white sauce. Good place for our first night here

Categories: Gelato, Food, Restaurants, Italian, Vegetarian, Gluten-Free | Review: We enjoyed the food which included a panini, spaghetti bolognese, and ravioli dishes. The outdoor Covid seating is comfortable and the service is attentive. Loved the bread. We could have had that with a glass of wine and been content, but recommend you don't miss out on the other great items on the menu.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, Vegan | Review: Santa Barbara is seriously lacking in Vegan cuisine so I appreciate the effort but I think the food and atmosphere miss the mark for me.

The seating seems a bit odd, especially, the cave-like two seater areas.  We originally were seated there but it was too claustrophobic and it sounded like we were in a box.  We tried to move to window view seat but those were only for 4 persons, even though it wasn't busy.  Moving to the main dining room presented larger tables but uncomfortable chairs.  Overall, it seems a bit pretentious to me and not exactly a place to relax and have a chill lunch.

My meatball sandwich was rather flavorless but I did like the bread it came in.  For $12+ dollars I find it a bit lacking and kind of insulting.  My girlfriend had the quiche which she couldn't eat because of the puree consistency and awful flavors.   So a quick lunch turned into $40+  easily.  Not exactly a place I want to drop in for a quick & easy lunch.

I've been to Portland, I've been to SF and what I would love to see Santa Barbara embrace is a vegan restaurant that doesn't try to compete on the high-end but provides really good, simple vegan food at a decent price.    At lot of us don't need fancy table settings, $20 entrees and all the LA bistro type settings but a varied, seasonal menu that features local Farmers Market ingredients that you can come back to again and again.  

Overall, I won't be back and that's a shame because we need more vegan representation in town.  I just wish Santa Barbara didn't treat vegan food as this unique, expensive rarity that you go to once every 6 months.

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: I have to downgrade my initial review.  My husband and I had breakfast this morning after a bike ride.  I ordered the Rollex and Eggs in Your Face.  His EIYF was great as in eggs nicely poached on top of perfectly toasted bread.  Mine was not so great.  The Wheat Wrap was greasy and the eggs inside were hard and rubbery and dry. I had one bite's worth of the tomato spread and there were a scanty 2 slices of avocado.  I like The Shop and I will continue to eat here but hope it's better next time.
Categories: Pubs, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Awesome, fun, friendly place. The people, beer, atmosphere are just perfect for this little beach town.

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: Food is really good service isn't so great. I've been frequenting this place for some time and like many local establishments have dealt with crummy service. On a recent visit I decided I won't be returning. When I said I was picking up an online order then the snotty girl told me "actually it was a phone in order" I know that sounds pretty harmless, tone is everything. Starting off with actually doesn't usually lead to a kind conversation. Then when I paid cash and I was asked how much of a tip I wanted to leave. (They have a tip jar beside the register), if I was going to leave a tip I would have put it in  there not hand it to this annoying little girl holding my change as ransom. Not to mention you just made me a damn sandwich there was no special service offered, you don't get a tip for doing your regular damn job. Tips are for hard working people that provide a special service not for bitchy little girls that slap some meat and condiments on some bread. If you're hungry the food is good here but you'll get a side of bitch wether you order it or not.

Categories: Restaurants, Mongolian, Chinese | Review: You MUST have the Mongolian BBQ/buffet here. Don't even bother getting a menu. Just head to the BBQ line, grab a bowl, and start filling it with frozen, thinly sliced meat, noodles, veggies, and sauce. I got beef, noodles, scallions, brocolli, and tofu. One scoop of garlic sauce, three scoops Mongolian BBQ sauce, and one scoop chili sauce. Then watch the chef dump the bowl onto his grill and cook it up, mouth watering. 

The buffet is not as fun, but still good. Fried shrimp, chili eggplant, suckling duck, fish fillets, and the usual suspects... fried rice... sweet and sour pork... etc. 

Real bang for your buck, you can eat all you want from either station. YUMmO!!

Word of advice, avoid the soft serve ice cream. Tastes plasticky.

Categories: Restaurants, French, Bars, Nightlife, Wine Bars, Mediterranean | Review: This is a beautiful restaurant with fantastic food. We spent our 12 year wedding anniversary there. Could not been more romantic.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: Came Here for dinner on my husband's birthday dinner before we went out. I called in for reservations letting them know it was his birthday. On our table had fresh red rose pedals with a birthday card from the chef!! My husband had the duck and I had the wagyu beef, which sounded good. I dont know what I was thinking ordering it since I don't eat meat!! But it was so flavorful I tried to enjoy it the beat I can. My husband sure did enjoy some of mine too. I wished I asked for a vegetarian item. However, the service was excellent!

Categories: Juice Bars & Smoothies, Restaurants, Food | Review: Not sure how many blenders drinks I've had for all these years. It's one of my favorite spots. Tasty and healthy. Very satisfying. Better than Jamba Juice.

Categories: Asian Fusion, Seafood, Restaurants, Poke, Food | Review: We ordered 2 Poke ceviche bowls & 2 spam musubi via door dash earlier this week, during covid-19 season. Poke bowls are very filling but not that delicious, have had better elsewhere. Musubi was ok tasting too.

Categories: Burgers, Cafes, Restaurants, Mexican, American (Traditional) | Review: Went here to get dinner for me and my son dinner and I ordered him the chicken nugget meal when my order was ready I asked for BBQ sauce for my sons meal. I was told I had to pay for it, who doesn't give free BBQ sauce??? Are you doing that poorly that you have to charge for everything??? Won't be going back. Next they'll be charging for napkins.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: Ok I'm redoing my review simply because they're not honoring their yelp coupon. Why is it on here if it's not even valid. I've been coming here for a while and when I saw the coupon on yelp; like everyone else I wantes to take advantage of it. Get there to find out they won't honor it. Not cool

Categories: Education, Food, Chocolatiers & Shops, Specialty Food, Special Education, Desserts | Review: Excellent gourmet Chocolates. A little on the pricey side for what you get but its worth the little extra for such rich, decedent chocolates. Each little piece is around $2-$3.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican, American (New) | Review: WOW! I was totally blown away by this place. I went in not knowing what to expect, and it was just such an awesome surprise, I am definitely going back!

Been to SB a bunch of times, but had never heard of this place. I took advantage of the Travelzoo voucher so we got to try a lot. We sat upstairs in a cozy booth. Very romantic. Started with the chicken quesadillas which were so fresh and yummy, and the nachos with beef. OMG. Their chips are so thin and crispy, they were seriously the best nachos I have ever had.
For entrees I had the chicken mole which was SICK. The best mole I've ever had. My boyfriend doesn't even like mole, but he was so impressed. He had the steak tacos which came with this amazing chimichurri sauce. So good.

For dessert we had the whiskey bread pudding. I didn't think I'd like it since I hate whiskey, but it disappeared in 45 seconds. We inhaled it. For drinks, I had the blood orange margarita, and my boyfriend had the pomegranate ginger. They don't use mixes here, just fresh juices, and you can totally tell, it makes such a huge difference.

Next time I come to Santa Barbara we are going to have to stop here. Their happy hour deals look great too. Come here for an amazing meal and great drinks!!!

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: A local highly recommended this place.  It's insanely popular and difficult to get a reservation.  So plan ahead!

Food:  everything was cooked well.  No dish stood out.  My only complaint is food here can be slightly one dimensional.... with a heavy emphasis on sour/acidity.  From the mocktails to the apps and main courses... everything was sour.  The curry popcorn was so addicting we might have asked for 2nds, 3rds or 4ths?  Yums

Ambiance:  very cool hip vibes and very busy!  

Service: fast efficient and friendly.  We had a baby stroller with us and they accommodated 

The restaurant location is a little hidden.  Be patient when you're looking for this place as it's tucked away past a common area.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Diners, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Sooooo I will give them that it was Labor Day Weekend and were super busy BUT I have dined several times over a Labor Day Weekend and usually have a much better experience.

First, the procedure for being seated is a little....disorganized. It relies on the honesty of all. There is simply a clip board on the front door and you just write your name and number in your party. If they call your name and you're not there, you're skipped. Also, there's no one there to talk to as to how long they anticipate the wait to be.

So we waited. And waited and waited. Its nice to know that, "hey, it's going to be at least an hour until you're seated." We were listed 3rd on the list and we didn't think it was going to take A WHOLE HOUR.

Once seated, our server was super grumpy. Not in the mood for any smiles at all. We ordered our food and drinks military style. It was then ANOTHER 45 minutes to get our food. Dude, we are talking standard stuff off their menu with no modifications. The food was good but not worth a 2 hour wait. Had we been able to zip in and out, it would have been an entirely different experience. 

I got the hash, it was pretty good but I wouldn't get it again. The cinnamon roll was da Boooomb! Hubby reports the chicken sandwich was decent and would order again.

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: If you are a fan of authentic New Orleans creole cuisine this is definitely a place for you. The atmosphere is very entertaining and a great place for a date or family dinner. The servers are friendly and remind you that southern hospitality.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Good food, but not my favorite atmoshpere. A bit trendy & yuppie, but if you're looking for that kind of social crowd before a nice dinner, you'd like this place.
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: I love this restaurant! Great atmosphere, excellent drinks, amazing food and wonderful staff! It's always a fun party when I go!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Fun, hip ice cream place with delicious, unusual flavors. The frozen treats are made after ordering which makes the ice cream super fresh and tasty. I appreciate the attention to detail and all the available toppings. Each rolled ice cream order comes with whip cream, drizzle, and at least 3 toppings. This place is perfect for a treat after a movie, after dinner at some of the amazing nearby restaurants, cooling down after a hot day, or to close out a date night at the ice rink. LA trendy with a great product and show. Go check it out.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: Whether you're dining in or out (I'm currently writing this in the midst of the Coronavirus pandemic), this place is a must. We've been getting take out from here for the past 3 weeks and it's incredible. Must tries: get the burrata, insalata italiana, sand dabs and any pasta dish. This family-owned place is a Carp classic.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: Another great meal at Tratt! Great service from Rigo and drinks by Ivo. We too our 9 year old grandson and he loved his spaghetti and meat balls. Everyone else had the mushroom pasta which is my go to dish. Tratt is a friendly locals place with consistently good food, drinks and service.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food | Review: Super cool cats work here. Passing through and need a pick me up. Got the fresh pot of light roast and pastry on a Sat night. Thanks for the good coffee deets.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: Best poke in town hands down. Employees at this location are awesome and generous with portions, and the fish is fresh and delicious. The rice is just right, and they mix in the fish with sauces so everything is flavorful. They know how to do poke right here.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Food Delivery Services, Food, Personal Chefs, Caterers | Review: Top notch prepared meal delivery service! The food is delicious! Wonderful for busy families, great adult and kiddo menu choices. Love the reusable glass containers too.

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Ahh Andersens.. I've been getting the pastries here with my dad, and watching "momma Andsersen" in action for a long time. Some may see her directness as rude, but I think it's a European trait as my Dutch Grandmother is very much like her. 
I've usually bought the delicious goodies to take to my boyfriend, and finally got him to go eat here. Well, food was delicious as it always is when I go, but of course it had to be "small servings Saturday" because what has always been large portions were very small today. Anyhow the food was great! The atmosphere, well if you like snooty old ladies and their annoying Yorkshire terriers then not so much. 
I highly recommend  trying their breakfast, and if you can't handle the crowd of over dressed and overly pampered pooches, get a nice pink box of goodies to take home!

Categories: Garage Door Services, Restaurants, Live/Raw Food, Food, Home Services, Mediterranean, Keys & Locksmiths, Burgers, Vegetarian, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Vegan, Salad | Review: Amazing gem on the edge of town. Came here for a late lunch and were treated with an unusual Morroccan Couscous dish that really surprised and delighted me. A hearty, savory tomato stew base with excellent balance. Highly recommend this amazing place!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (New), French | Review: What a disappointment this was. 

The decor is old school, the service is old school, the cuisine is old school. And not necessarily in a nice traditionalist-done-right sort of way. More in a tired, on its last knees sort of way.

The starter was a green salad with half-an-ounce of smoked salmon bits, a quarter of a small avocado and 3 grapefruit segments. Tiny, boring, expensive. Enough said.

Next was a New Zealand bass in a saffron + lobster sauce and roasted fennel. The fennel was the only thing enjoyable. The sauce was overpowering everything else, and the fish was overcooked, so totally dry. We both had a $32 overcooked fish. Ouch. When the server asked how the fish was at the end (we had left out the driest parts on our plates and given up), I told him what the problem was. He simply replied "I am sorry to hear. It never happens" and went his merry way.

Then on to dessert... We picked the strawberry shortcake with ice cream. It could have been nice but I am sure that whoever prepared the dough confused the sugar for the salt. The shortcake was incredibly salty and not sweet at all. So we just did not eat the shortcake, asked for the check, and left totally defeated.

How anyone would want to go there when Bouchon is 30 yards away is beside me. Bouchon is everything this place is not: great service (super accommodating), great food, great wine, consistently.

PS: If the waiter/waitress gives you a speech going something like this "it is traditional to have cheese before dessert here. Can I offer you some cheese?". They are just offering you to pay $15 for a small plate of cheese. There is nothing generous about this offer.

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Desserts, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Seafood | Review: Came here around lunch time on a Monday and the place was barely open when we walked in.  We asked to be seated in the patio overlooking the Santa Barbara Pier so we had a nice view.  The sun was out so it helped warm us a bit.  

The server brought our menus a few minutes after we were seated.  she said she was the only one serving right now and apologize for any delays. since there was no other patrons other than us, we didn't really feel any delays. We were a large group of about 10.

While we were deciding on our order, the server brought us water and warm bread with butter.  We asked for olive oil and balsamic vinegar which she also promptly brought.  We decided on a few orders of appetizers to start.  The appetizers included oysters and calamari which were tasty and fresh.For our main courses, each of us ordered different ones. We also ordered a couple bottles of wine to go with our meal.  I had the spicy seafood pasta but for some reason it wasn't spicy at all.  The taste was good nonetheless so I didn't have any complaints. I tried my friend's dishes and they were good as well. The price wasn't too bad considering the location and view from the restaurant as well as the food. 

Overall, a good dining experience. I wouldn't mind going back to the restaurant if I have a chance to go back to santa Barbara.

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, Italian, Pizza, Restaurants | Review: Not very special, not a great value...in fact kind  of expensive for  what you get.  Pizza lacked sauce and was pretty salty.  I ordered the vegetariana and was disappointed.

Categories: Restaurants, Salad, Sandwiches, Italian, Pizza | Review: Great outdoor seating on State St. Service was great, staff was accommodating to my older parents who needed a little extra heating. We all enjoyed their wine selection. The spaghetti and meatballs and mushroom linguine were very good. The butternut squash ravioli could have been a little more flavorful. Overall lovely experience, will most likely return for wine and apps!
Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Great tacos at a fantastic price. Incredibly nice staff and a very clean dining area.

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: Back again for another great lunch on the sunny patio for some much needed relaxation and delicious seafood with my man. Some of the best views in SB! Needed to recognize our great waitress Katherine. She was incredibly friendly and genuinely cared the we and the other guests around us were enjoying our food and had everything we needed. Nothing like service with a smile overlooking the ocean! Katherine was definitely one of the best servers we've had over our many, many visits here. She's a keeper!

Also love that there was live music when we came for happy hour the week before. Keep it up Bluewater, we love you!

Categories: Specialty Food, Health Markets, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Food | Review: I really wanted to love this place. From floor to ceiling, from the storefront to the back patio, everything looks perfectly charming and bright. But the service was disorganized and imbalanced. There was one girl who was assembling orders, packaging them, running to the back to refill water for the patio people, pouring drinks into cups, and then running to the front to help seated customers.
