She was understandably not happy. Meanwhile, her other coworkers, who are perfectly capable guys, were just leisurely standing around or taking their sweet time taking orders. When it was my turn the cashier guy was barely listening. So of course, he gave incomplete and incorrect info to the same frazzled girl who inevitably got my order wrong. So I had to repeat myself by checking against the e-receipt from my cellphone because I guess she couldn't ask her coworker to get it straight and help her out. I had a large order of cupcakes, cookies, and other pastries---all unreasonably overpriced btw. What was most disappointing of all was that none of the items tasted up to par. Not a single one.

Ready for more?

I had also ordered a hot chocolate as my secondary measuring stick for any bakery that offers it (primary gauge is for the baked goods, of course). If they can properly make a cup of "chocolat chaud", then I can trust that the rest of their ingredients and methods will be high quality. I know you may think it's an unfair expectation, but if the bakery is confident enough to offer it (in french no less), then gosh darn it, it better be right. 

Verdict: Fail. What I received was hot water with a hint of chocolate. I couldn't even finish it because who drinks water with lightly sprinkled chocolate powder, let alone pay a pretty penny for it? A packet of Swiss Miss instant hot chocolate with just water, not even milk, would have been more satisfying.

I will not be coming back and have unsaved it from my yelp bookmarks.
Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: I love this place. I'm gluten free and they take it very seriously. Their tofu green curry is my go-to.
Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: terrible service. not worth the hassle, there's better quality poke in the area. server did not wear a mask.

Categories: Pizza, Food, Restaurants, Bubble Tea, Sandwiches, Food Delivery Services | Review: Great tasting calzones at a decent price. The ordering process is kind of funky the first time you go in, but it's entertaining once you see other people get all confused with the iPad and no employee at the register. If know how to read you're pretty much good.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches | Review: Needless to say, the pickle bar is great! = )

Sandwiches were great too! And very large, so very easy to split between two people or share for later. The club sandwich comes LOADED with avocado. Will definitely be back to try other sandwiches. There is nice indoor seating but very cute outdoor seating as well! And the view overlooks the Presidio and the mountains, cant ask for a nicer view downtown.

And they take Axxess so dont forget your card!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Cafes, Restaurants | Review: Food was okay & service was terrible. Our waitress was clearly not excited to be working on a Saturday night, which is understandable, but please don't dampen our night with your rudeness. I give it an extra star than what it deserves because the ambience was cute and the lighting was chill. I definitely expected more out of the experience.

Categories: Gelato, Food, Restaurants, Italian, Vegetarian, Gluten-Free | Review: The hotel suggested this place.
 I was disappointed .  Salads and bread were good.
Entrees - halibut smelled fishy, pasta on both entrees seemed to be reheated so it was mushy and tired.
Eggplant parm tasted good but was burnt on top as if reheated one too many times and was just mushy.
I felt like the entrees were prepared from a buffet line - heavy and indistinguishable .
I agree the ambience was good, but did not matter as much to me as what I was eating.
With so many choices in Santa Barbara I would pass.

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: We came here very excited celebrating our anniversary and we ordered the tri-tip sandwich, and the chili sampler. Everything was delicious. Very charming little hidden gem. We were walking towards the back admiring this place we asked one of the workers we found on the back if he could please take us a picture and he said" NO I'm busy right now" lol oh geez what a great first impression on our first visit!!! That's why I took a star off. The service not the greatest. It's not very welcoming. What a shame.

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: Such a nice place to go for a reasonably priced, great quality seafood meal.  Lots of variety on their menu and the staff is happy to answer questions if you'd like help deciding (like I sometimes do).  Highly recommend!

Categories: Lounges, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants, Hookah Bars, American (New) | Review: Came on a Saturday night after a show at the bowl and the vibe was awesome for a sleepy town like SB. DJ was playing perfect lounge music, drinks were kinda expensive but whatever. If I lived here I would be a regular.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, Party & Event Planning, Food, Venues & Event Spaces, Arts & Entertainment, Social Clubs, Specialty Food | Review: Very old school environment.  Went to a 70-something birthday party.  Good cheer everywhere.  But the food...institutional.  I have seen better food in hospitals.  It was also meager.  Even though the portions were small, many left it on the plate.  Must have been the chefs day off.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (New), Food | Review: Amazing food. Probably best chorizo I've had. Very friendly service,great location and interior. Great job guys !

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: My wife and I absolutely love Tamira! We get take-out from here very frequently, and I've also been in a few times for their lunch buffet. In all honesty, I think Tamira's combination of consistently high-quality, tasty food and affordable prices is simply unmatched in Santa Barbara. 

One thing I have to particularly compliment Tamira on is their consistency, but across time and across different items on the menu. Everything we've gotten here has been absolutely delicious, with amazing combinations of spices, cooked to exactly the right amount of doneness. 

Personally, my favorite items on the menu are the saags, the malai kofta, and the biriyanis, as well as the veggie samosas.

Categories: Sandwiches, Vegan, Cafes, Vegetarian, Restaurants | Review: This was a great restaurant for me to take my vegetarian girlfriend to.  We both loved the food and received some very good service,

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: Our family goes to Bouchon for special occasions - because it is a very special restaurant! The service is top notch and the food is the best that Santa Barbara has to offer. The chef presents a farm-to-market mix of wonderful dishes.  The number of locally made wines that are available is incredible.
Plan to make a reservation and spend the evening dining. I love the duck and we always finish with a cheese platter and the tawny port sampler.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, American (New), Wine Bars, Nightlife, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Event Planning & Services | Review: Second time at Jane, it was just as good as the first if not better! Very nice atmosphere, excellent friendly service and delicious food and drink. What more can you ask for. Thanks Jane!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Acai Bowls, Restaurants, Food | Review: We used yelp to find this place and what a treat! The smell of fresh fruit fills the air when one walks into the small restaurant. With blenders whirling and a line of 10 hungry souls, we were torn between choices of porridge, smoothies and fruit! My husband had the cashew milk oatmeal filled with almonds, cinnamon, fresh berries and bananas while I customized my oatmeal (his was five stars plus and mine was four based on me not knowing to add cashew milk etc. to duplicate taste) so go with the menu option where they have it down pat! Could not wait to go back next day before flying out. They are about 3 miles from airport. It was 7am and they do not open until 8 on Saturday!  What a disappointment! We were salivating  for one of their fresh berry bowls.   Earlier risers disappointment. Eating greasy eggs and bacon at airport while dreaming of healthy mouthfuls at Backyard  Bowls!!!!

Categories: Education, Food, Chocolatiers & Shops, Specialty Food, Special Education, Desserts | Review: Really amazing and I don't say that often. Make sure to get one of the hot drinks. I get the Marie Antoinette. Gives you tons of energy too. I'd go there every day if it wasn't so expensive.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I ordered the shrimp fajitas, $24. The shrimp had absolutely no flavor.  They were served with black beans and rice.  The beans and rice were served on a plate that was smaller than an average teacup saucer. Probably less than 2 tablespoons of each, hardly a serving. The corn tortillas were delicious and so was the corn and black bean salsa. The service was ok, not great but not horrible. I'm being generous with 2 stars.  Will I go back? Probably not.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Event Planning & Services, Food, Caterers | Review: Located in a great hipster-like atmosphere.  As far as their flavors go, I only tried one flavor while I was there (honey almond crunch) but it was absolutely perfect.  My friend got something nutella based and said she loved it.  As for the waffle cone it was perfectly sweet, nice and crispy.  The reason why I gave them 4 stars is because the ice cream was melting when I ordered it.

Categories: Japanese, Sushi Bars, Restaurants | Review: Chirashi don, $13 for lunch!

The dish is fast to serve up, filling, well-priced and no complaints about the chef's choice of fish.

It's simply a bowl of sushi rice, topped with an assortment of sashimi. My fish were very ample cuts, too big sometimes and I had to cut them in half. Two slices each of four fish (salmon, maguro, yellowtail and some other white fish), two shrimp and some avocado and krab salad. Effectively 10 very large nigiri, once you doled out the rice amongst the toppings. 

It also comes with some miso soup.

The fish assortment is perfect for a typical sushi eater, nothing crazy and obscure. And for being "scattered sushi" it was nicely presented in a pretty bowl. I uploaded a photo.

Very pleased with this.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: We went on a Sunday and were told an hour and a half wait, which turned out to be over two hours. Good for them. I know they can't predict the wait time, just know weekends are crazy busy there.
Once seated, service was exceptionally slow. Six people - one did not get their drink until half way through the meal. One person did not get their meal until the others had almost finished. They did comp the meal, but still!
The food is adequate. Service terrible. 
Of course, it's all about the location right on the beach.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Great food! We stumbled upon this place. Visiting UCSB from Fair Oaks. Ordered the calamari and yellow curry with tofu and brown rice! So delicious! Their motto is "you only live once, lick the bowl."  Really, great food. Server was very nice too!

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Do-It-Yourself Food, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Bars | Review: You order, they give you the meat and you cook it! I love this concept! Don't get me wrong there's absolutely nothing like being waited on but every now and then there's a place for this. I mean look at how popular the Korean BBQ and Japanese do it yourself places are! There are some items they do cook in the back (certain seafoods and such). 
Like some have mentioned...it is rather communal. You have to go use one of there connected grills in the middle of the restaurant. All the seasoning takes place there unless you decide to take the meat back to your table. But then you lose your spot...so all my magic is done by the grill. Both times I've been there (liked it so much that I went twice in one week!) the fellow grillers were very friendly and courteous. We all seemed to understand the process and timeliness involved. The helpful waitress (Jill) instructed that each steak averages about 4 to 5 mins for each side for a nice Med center. She was about right give or take a min or two. Of course the higher you place onthe grill, the hotter, which made for much faster cook time for my steak 
Oh yeah Steak! We ordered one T Bone and Ribeye. The cut size seemed decent for the price. Placed them on the grill after adding my basic mixture of salt, pepper and garlic with just a touch of A-1 sauce. Went up high for the quick cook.
In the mean time...helped ourselves to the lovely salad bar, baked potato, beans and bread stations. You help yourself which I did. Don't even know if it was all you can eat but I went back twice and was only charged once. The salad was pretty basic but very fresh! Kind of like the old school salad bars us 40 year olds grew up on in the 70's. Garbanzo beans and canned beets. You can't beat that! The croutons omgosh!!! These puppies are worth eating by themselves. I think they fry them with lots of butter and worth every calorie I must say! Dressing choices...your usual suspects. All great!
The beans are pretty tasty. A great touch to go with the meal. The large piecesof white bread of old Americana...big and whole white fluffy. Great to grill with a slab of butter on the grill. Baked potatos were perfected baked. The batch we got weren't big so i took two...lol! Enough indulgeance on the the sides. 

Went back after about 10 minutes to pick up my meats and OMG!!! I can actually grill. Go figure??? My wife claims I can't cook at home but the two times we were here I somehow showed some hidden skill grilling. I just said it has to be the grill they had! Wish i took pics because both steaks came out perfect with a touch of pink. For the money...$15.00 to $17.00 range, the quality of meats was perfect! Not tough at all and a two steps above something you would get at Norms.  And please people...don't compare to places like Mastros or Mortons. Different price point and atmosphere. This will actually go up against anything at Chilis, Outback or Black Angus though. 

The atmosphere is busy. Lots of families and loud but still pleasant. Its something you should try once. The wait staff very friendly. The rest of staff also very friendly and attentive. Drinks never empty! Definitely better than the other local steak place, Clementines. Haven't been to Sly's but if it ain't Mastros or like, I ain't paying their prices! So go try it for yourself and try your grill skills! So if your ever in CArpinteria...I suggest you try it. At least check the place out since it it a historical piece of history for locals. Something about being an old hotel! Coolski!

Categories: Restaurants, Specialty Food, Do-It-Yourself Food, Seafood Markets, Food, Caterers, Seafood, Breakfast & Brunch, Event Planning & Services | Review: Not a traditional fish market in the sense that you don't view the fish in the cases - they just have a menu with prices. Also really surprised there was nothing local. Closest thing was some flounder from Baja. All the other fish was from far away places.

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: What haven't I already painted about the Cold Spring Tavern? It's been the location and subject matter of so many a brushstroke. 

A women's volleyball team once reunited there only to proposition Scottie and I to get into their van and party. We were hardly twenty-five, them in their forties. We declined, but will forever wonder: with what gusto would they barrel down the 154 into Santa Inez Valley? Would they gamble at that casino at the edge of Solvang, or dunk their heads in more booze and bob for apples?

Sundays at Cold Spring Tavern are filled with forest and dappling light, the Cold Canyon Arc Bridge looming in serene wooded bliss. The whispering stream of the spring. A stand-up bass, perpetually slapped by a bearded hippie. Motorcycles. Tri-tip. The wish that old California will live on through the muck of artificial intelligence and denim shorts. I look at the old jail cell, the eighteenth-century Cold Spring Tavern structures in general, and wonder what becomes of our  experience post phone app dog filter. We are jailed, interacting through the barrier of digital walls. I often want to touch someone's arm, and have them look at me, like, really see me, and have that be that. 

It's what happens to me in the Cold Spring Tavern, usually a nudge from Scottie; a cowgirl shorty here, a nice-looking steak there. What more California thing can one find but a tri-tip topped with salsa, the jalapenos lush with seeds? Then a horseradish sauce. A red sauce. A cup of beer. Scottie shivering slightly in the shaded wood, in his usual t-shirt and jeans. We never bring a jacket. 

He says, "Cheers, old boy," and we swig our whiskey rocks. Our large cups of beer. We are glowing in the twilight that seems permanent in Santa Barbara mountains for Santa Barbara is a biblical site of tri-tip and beach. Of grape leaves and olive oil and the Cold Spring Tavern. She really is the most beautiful. 

Scottie loves Her too. It's why we've continued to drive here week after week, the hour and a half from LA a gleeful sing song journey. We keep old California in our back pockets this way and don't care if we're the last people in the world to romanticize the smell of linseed or hickory. We dip into Santa Barbara, an artist and his manager, our bellies occupied by meat and Fritos. Our souls full of dappled light.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Combo Burritos are expensive when compared to your average hole in the wall Mexican joint, $8.99 to be exact. They're even more expensive than Chipotle's burritos, is it better than them? Not sure. Although their burritos are good, very good at that, I'm not sure they're worth that much. I wouldn't have a problem paying $2 less, similar to what Chipotle charges, but charging THAT much for a burrito you expect the best burrito you've ever had and it simply wasn't. It was good, just not THAT good.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food, Bubble Tea | Review: Definitely the best boba in IV! I like the different flavor options they have. I had the horchata milk tea which was so yummy! Definitely very sugary, but I think it went well with the flavor. The boba itself is decent compared to the other places in IV. This is for sure my go-to boba place if I'm ever craving it.
Categories: Tuscan, Seafood, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife, Italian | Review: Really good foood!!!! Service is also great! The tiramisu had a lot of alcohol in it so I didn't love it!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: This was the first time eating here. I had seen awesome pictures and the food did not disappoint. I had the Bangkok Street Noodle Soup. It was delicious. I literally left the Bowl empty. Lol. I will be coming back. I just hope it's soon. The service was great. @raulvsfood

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: I was really looking forward to dinning at this restaurant because of the great reviews but sadly left disappointed. I ordered bruschetta and what I got for $15 were two tiny pieces of bread with tomato on it and NO olives. I went there celebrating a birthday and told them that when I made the reservation. What I got was no dessert with no candle to blow. Service was super slow and our waitress neglected us, was it because we were young and everyone there was older? Anyhow, I regret over tipping her, she didn't deserve her generous tip. We ordered the duck ravioli and the tortellini. Both were decent but nothing to die for.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Had sopes for my meal. Had 2 chorizo & one carnita sopes. They were delicious!! The shell that was served with the meat & beans was very good. Service was exceptional as always. Kudos to the chef.

Categories: Moroccan, Restaurants | Review: Overall, I liked this place. Chef Karim is a nice guy and anyone who has a framed photograph of Don Adams in the front of his restaurant is OK by me. Maxwell deserves our honor and reverence. And the food was divine (get the bistilla).

However!

Everybody I went with went into fits of ecstasy over the belly dancer, but I didn't really get it. I guess I'm just crotchety. It was irritating as hell when the dancer and her little groupies wouldn't stop pestering me to get up and DANCE! DANCE! CMONNNNN DANCE!!!11one. If I wanted to dance, I would have gone to a freaking cotillion.

And thank god I wasn't paying for it. I can't remember what the total was for our group, I was too busy sliding from my chair unconscious. All I can say is, I've never paid that much for a meal in my poor backwoods redneck life.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Japanese, Restaurants | Review: What a hidden gem. Snuggled between On the Alley and Brophy Bros is Sushi GoGo, a Japanese place that offers cheap and delicious sushi with a fantastic view of the harbor. Although the interior of the restaurant is very tiny and consists of a counter and drink fridge, there's actually a decent amount of smoke-free seating outside.

With an expansive menu, there's so much to choose from, between sushi, udon, bowls, and even the addition of a poke bowl, per a paper sign on their storefront. Based off of how fresh and cheap their sushi was (the most expensive roll topped out at just under $16, and all included 10 pieces), that seems to be the dish of choice here. Emily L. got the green dragon roll and I got the rainbow roll, and both were delicious. For fans of grilled eel, the green dragon roll comes with tender, soft slices and a light drizzle of sauce. While the slicing of their rainbow roll wasn't super clean (some pieces had two types of fish or shrimp), it was all fresh. I was a fan of the white fish they used, which came lightly marinated in what possibly was shichimi togarashi.

If you're not willing to pay the premium for juice, tea, soda, sake, beer, or wine (plenty of options, all inexpensive), the water dispenser can be hard to find. It's at knee level and right next to the door. 

There's no frills or fuss here. A single woman works as the server and busser, with one cashier and a pair of chefs milling around in the back. They weren't particularly personable, but they were efficient and quick at getting everything out! As some Yelpers have suggested, it's a good place for take-out or if you're in a rush.

Categories: Restaurants, Ethiopian, French | Review: This place is like a hidden gem! I came here for dinner and the ambiance was great. Dim lighting, with a silent black n white movie playing in the background. The staff was friendly. We came kind of late (8pm) since they closed at 9, but everyone was still really nice. We ordered 3 appetizers and one entrée (we prefer smaller dishes). Its my first time eating French food in like a year, so i was really excited.

we got the Diver Scallops, Pate, Slow roasted Tripe, and braised lamb shank

I must say the Tripe is definitely a winner! Its slow cooked with season vegetables to tender perfection. Garnished with fresh Parmesan cheese. It was probably the most mind blowing dish of the entire meal. I was taking their really yummy bread and soaking up the remaining sauces. THAT should tell you how delicious it was. If you've never tried tripe and want to give it a go, i would definitely recommend coming to Petit Valentien. The owner/chef even came out and pointed at the devoured tripe dish and give us the thumbs up. He obviously knows we loved the dish. 

The diver scallops were delicious as well. Perfectly cooked and garnished with this Asian flavored sauce. But it definitely couldn't outdo the tripe.

The braised lamb shank was delicious. FALL OFF THE BONE TENDER! Usually lamb has a very distinct flavor a lot of people aren't a fan of, but the way this was prepared you only get a small hint of the lamb flavor, the rest was just mouth watering goodness. 

Overall this place definitely deserves 4.5 stars! great fooood and great service! worth a visit!!!

recap:
1) TRIPE!!!
2) chill ambiance
3) TRIPE!!!!!!!
4) Braised Lamb Shank!!!
5) TRIPE!!!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I had their catered food and it's not the same as in the restaurant. 

Restaurant: delicious, professional, non-greasy, and true to size.

This is mostly a review for the catering service they offer. 

Catering: plain, very greasy, very unprofessional, not friendly.
It's almost as if they didn't want to be there. They are expensive for the service they offer. Didn't meet expectations presentation wise. The meats and tortillas are cooked/sized different than inside restaurant. Witnessed minor fiascos that could have been prevented if they had been prepared.

Save yourself a headache and a stomachache hire a more experienced restaurant that is true to catering. 

El Zarape restaurant GOOD.

El Zarape catering TERRIBLE.

Categories: Steakhouses, Restaurants | Review: Only restaurant that I have been asked to leave and also pay. Was in Goleta on business and didn't know the area. Figured it was close and how could they screw up. 

1st they forgot about me. Saw several people seated after me have there orders taken and actually get their food before I had ordered. I complained that I was forgotten about, sorry was the response. 

2nd in an attempt to help me, 3 different servers all brought me salads at about the same time. After the first one, they left the second and third one on my table. 

3rd they forgot my steak. I again questioned about it and in about 1 minute I got my Porterhouse. Cooked on both sides but cold in the middle, and I am talking about just the edge. 

4th time I complained about the steak and could they actually cook it, they asked me to leave for being impatient with them. On top of it they wanted me to pay for the whole thing, including the 3 salads. 

I told them I wasn't and they called the police, who then escorted me off the property. I was told that I couldn't come back there EVER. 

Crappy part, I didn't even get my beer that I had ordered. 

Do not waste your time there.

Categories: Salad, Restaurants, Pizza, Sandwiches, Italian | Review: OMG! This pizza reminds me of the original Giordano's pizza pie in Chicago--the best! This pizza is literally amazing! The cheese, the sauce, the crust, and toppings... it was so good. I was salivating when it arrived!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Burgers, American (Traditional), American (New), Restaurants | Review: I have breakfast here pretty often, this morning was not the norm.... Met my girlfriend for a quick breakie, sat in the first booth, I would not recommend on sitting their the fruit flies are pretty bad at that table..  My friend arrived before I and had her beverage, no one came to the table to offer a beverage not even a bus boy. I finally got the attention of manager. This was a first for me, I had to wave my arms in the air and said " excuse me may I have some coffee?"  That's a first for me in any restaurant. I love the farmer boy restaurant but was very surprised at service and cleanliness. I will be back.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Irish, Nightlife, Pubs, Gastropubs | Review: Typical locals style, no frills, good time, Irish bar. Stiff drinks, good prices, all of the expected bar amenities. We'll stop in again next time we are in town.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars | Review: I dined at Finch & Fork three nights ago when my parents were in town.  Overall it was one of my best dining experiences, possibly ever.  Now I work at Outpost in Goleta another Kimpton property, so got a bit of special treatment, but as for quality of food and service, I was blown away.  Flatbreads are great, fresh and quality ingredients.  We had pickles, and deviled eggs which were stellar, as were the chicken wings.  Finally the scallops and ribs were two of the best entrees I've ever had.  Also not to forget the onion soup which was on point.  However we were too full for desert, which was a good thing.  One of the best meals I've ever had.  Chef James is great people and I will be back often...

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: We had the best graduation party for our son, on the beautiful patio, under the Avocado tree of La Hacienda!  The food was outstanding as usual.  Authentic, homemade and absolutely delicious!  The Horchata and Jamaica (all homemade) was a big hit.  The owners are sisters, Luz (Chef) and Juanita (front house manager) are so accommodating and kind, they made my party planning so easy.  My guests all commented on this how amazing this "hidden jewel of Old Town Goleta".  Thank you La Hacienda!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: The Burrito Mojado is incredible. It's a huge burrito that's completely full of chicken. Especially for such an inexpensive restaurant, they deliver great meals.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New) | Review: I was pleasantly surprised to see this place open for lunch. The wild caught salmon piccata melted in my mouth and was perfectly paired with veggies. Roy took our order, cooked it and served it. Not sure why it wasn't over flowing with more people. 

We promised to go back and try it again and again during the special anniversary pricing of $15, lunch or dinner, same price.

Categories: Specialty Food, Fruits & Veggies, Food | Review: I live right around the corner from this stand and it is a constant disappointment. It is difficult to find a piece of fruit that is not bruised and they often source from large companies. Not exactly a "local" farm stand.

Categories: Health & Medical, Traditional Chinese Medicine, Acupuncture | Review: I've been to many acupuncturists through the years and Tram Pham is one of the best. She is a ray of sunshine every time she enters the room. She is extremely caring, warm, and highly competent. 

She's helped me deal with horrible pre-menopausal symptoms and other maladies. She is immensely skilled as an acupuncturist and has helped me with Chinese herbs, too. I've always been a fan of acupuncture and I look forward to my visits here. I feel very comfortable with her. After she inserts the needles, she'll tuck a call button under your thumb so you can press it if you need her to come back. She also lets you choose the aromatic oil to help you relax or energize you. Usually I like lavender or citrus.

If you've never had acupuncture or just need to find someone to help you feel better or reduce pain, I recommend Tram. She will carefully review a health form you'll need to complete before your appointment. She will answer your questions and discuss treatments. She even called me on a weekend to help answer a question I had about my herbs. I never feel rushed in her office and there's lots of positive energy. It's peaceful and very calming. Her front desk staff are on point and very helpful and caring. 

The office is super clean and modern and not cramped. Parking is easy as the office is located on the first floor of an office building and they have a big parking lot.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: This is my favorite Thai restaurant in town. They have really good lunch prices with most dishes being around $8. I love their pad Thai and panang curry. The tofu and bok choy dish is also REALLY REALLY good. I like really spicy food and they are usually really good at making it the right level of spiciness. Plus their rice comes in triangular pyramids which is a cool touch. They do have weird hours so make sure you check if they are open before you go!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Small delightful hole in the wall at the end of downtown State St.  We had just finished running along the harbor and were STARVING.  Where to find food?  Yelp --  Nearby --?  Lilly's Tacos!  SO HAPPY we found this spot!

She serves ONLY tacos.  If you're looking for something else, this is not the place to visit.

For $1.35 you have the traditional simple tacos with ONLY the tortilla and meat.  After you purchase they have a stand with cilantro, onions and different salsa/hot sauces to add on top of your tacos.  Overall simple but tasty.  The meat is chopped up and WELL cooked but add all the toppings and you have a very flavorful taco.  

If you're adventurous I think I saw eye, head and cheeck?  I'm not THAT adventurous so I just stuck with the carnitas and carne asada... I ate 3 and left perfectly full.  The boyfriend ate 4 and he similarly felt the same.

By the time we left around 12pm there was a line out the door!  Get there at the off-hour!

Categories: American (New), Pizza, Pubs, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Beer Bar | Review: Really enjoyed our take out from yellow belly tonight. The staff was friendly and helpful. It would be a 5/5 but they forgot to include the soup with the grilled cheese (after the bartender was asked). Very good food.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: The food is delicious, but the service is consistently terrible. We had a reservation for 7:30 PM. We didn't get seated until 7:45, and didn't know who our waiter was until 8:15pm! We asked four different people for water, including the owner, it was ridiculous and almost humorous how long it took them to bring us water. It was Valentine's Day, so we really had no other options. This was the second time we ate there, and had to ask for a waiter after zero service.... If this restaurant was in a city, they would go under in a heartbeat, but they have a handful other restaurants to compete with in a small town. Bottom line, it's poorly managed.

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Food Delivery Services, American (New), Event Planning & Services, Restaurants, Food | Review: The chefs were kind and I really enjoy that the food shop was warm since it was a cold day outside. I wish there were more vegan/vegetarian options but the linguine marinara pasta was delicious. It wasn't amazing. The sauce was more soupy than a sauce, but my friend's exact same dish's sauce looked up to par. They offered free garlic bread with our dishes and that was amazing. I wish there was more to the noodles and sauce. If you like simplicity, this is your place! I'll be back to try the zucchini pasta and the assorted veggies though.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: After a long day of wine tasting at the funk zone, I was ready to eat some good food! Got a recommendation for this place from one of the wineries we tasted at, and was not disappointed. 

It can get really crowded on a Saturday night - up to an hour wait for a party of 3+. Fortunately, I used the Yelp waitlist to cut down my waiting time. 

We were seated outside, where they had heating lamps that kept us warm, and I enjoyed sitting street-side to wave to the landsharks driving by. They start you off with fresh chips and three salsas - the darker one was my favorite. My friend and I shared a dish, cazuela pibil, and it was delicious! It's served with fried plantain, beans, guac, and the habanero salsa. The pork had so much flavor, it was a bit salty/fatty but the habanero salsa was a perfect pairing to add a bit of acidity. 

I gave it four stars because I thought the service could've been better, and it's so crowded there isn't much of a waiting area other than on the sidewalk... but I will definitely be back during a slower time!
Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: Good atmosphere, good coffee, except for the coffee grinds at the bottom of my cup. Friendly staff, I'll be back.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Absolutely wonderful. My girlfriend and I spent our 1 year anniversary here and the manager, Armando, went all out. He reserved us a table at the front of the restaurant by the window without me asking. He sent us a complimentary appetizer; it was a delicious prawn and coconut dish that was priced at $13, and it was the best prawns I have ever had. Later when we ordered dessert the chefs wrote "happy anniversary" on the plate; again, not prompted. 

I will never visit Santa Barbara without coming to Opal. It is a must for a special night out.

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: I immediately loved this place as soon as I walked in. Great patio setting, live music playing, friends and family eating delicious food. Perfect summer night dinner. Kebabs were so good (chicken and lamb). Tabouli and appetizers, so tasty. Large selections of drinks and wine. Staff was excellent. Can't wait to return.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Reasonably priced, nice sized portions. We shared the tom kah & red curry with chicken. Staff was friendly & service was quick. 

But if you want to cut straight to the chase, get the sticky rice with mango slices. They shape the the rice into a cute little pyramid. The mango was fresh & the rice was the right stickiness.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: If your on a budget like most of us, forget it. Too expensive for the tiny portions they serve. On a positive note, their Italian is unique, maybe it's authentic. I wouldn't know, but just not a place for the people with a tight budget.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, American (New), Restaurants, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Bars | Review: The restaurant is located in the bottom of a hotel in downtown Santa Barbara, a great location near shopping and the hustle and bustle downtown. The interior is beautiful and has a great relaxed yet classy atmosphere. 

The food is very tasty. I came for lunch with some friends and they had $15 dollar bottomless mimosas. I had eggs and breakfast sausage but they had some more adventurous options as well like poached eggs with crab and asparagus.

Categories: American (New), Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Best Mexican food in town.  Hands down.  Never been disappointed.  Extensive menu that you can't go wrong with.   Waiters are attentive and constantly making their rounds and topping off your chips.  They just opened a place in Goleta, so I'm super stoked on that.

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants | Review: We enjoyed lunch at Longboards before heading home to Orange County. The tables are pretty close together on the patio, but it didn't bother us too much since it wasn't a packed house. We found our way through and loved our table that was right up against the glass wall facing the boat harbor. 
Our server, Ernesto, gave us a rundown of his favorites. We took his advice and ordered the Harbor Burger and the Maui Rings. We shared both and it was a perfect amount, leaving us satisfied but not stuffed. Ernesto was a wonderful server. He was polite and welcoming. The staff seems to work well together, helping each other out with table clearing, getting checks, refills, etc.
The reason for the 4-star review is that the prices were a little steep. Good thing the portions were large enough to share. Also, they laid down sawdust to soak up a spill that had happened nearby. In theory, this is a great idea, but given how windy it was, the dust was bothersome while trying to eat. They swept it up once they realized it was bothering people.
We would happily visit Longboards again, but we might try eating inside if we could enjoy the same beautiful view.

Categories: Bars, Mediterranean, Restaurants, Nightlife, Moroccan, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Italian | Review: Came here for a quick beer. Scott was a great bartender and recommended an awesome Japanese liquor to me. 
I would definitely come back for the drinks and service. I saw many people enjoying the food around me and it looked spectacular!

Categories: Seafood, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Can't say enough good things about this place. Our waiter, Steven was the bomb! He has such s great personality and made our entire meal so fun. Our food was amazing. Calamari strips off the chain!!! Drinks were delish!! Coming back fo aho

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: This place is at a great location with such nice view but just because they are a front beach restaurant does not mean is all great. it was not that busy yet, another party showed up after we did, and were taken care of before us. My food was all cold, Walter the waiter kept ignoring us when we asked for a glass of water and creamers for the coffee which again took him 10 minutes to bring it.


100% DISAPPOINTED... I THINK 7-11 WOULD GIVE YOU BETTER TREATMENT...

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: The prices went up from the last time I was here when it was Poke Me. Food was about the same, but not sure if it's worth the price now. Main issue was with the service.. one guy was obviously drunk, the coworkers were telling him to go home, he was slurring, not quite walking straight, wasn't listening to my order and almost overcharged me if someone hadn't intervened and was just overall a mess. Created an environment I didn't want to stay and eat my food at

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: Fantastic, fantastic, fantastic...Little did the staff know that my wife and I had just spent over an hour down the street from the restaurant cleaning our daughter and our car after our daughter got sick on the drive up from LA (besides the point right?) yet somehow they had a feeling that we may have been a little out of sorts, so they did their best in getting us a table right away.. this was the closest place for us to walk to after the mess. However, because we were promptly seated outside, they did not have a high chair/booster chair for our daughter when they seated us, so she spent the whole lunch on my wife's lap (it happens, no big deal, we are not pretentious people who think that everything in life has to be perfect at all times). when the manager delivered our bill after our ABSOLUTELY DELICIOUS MEAL!!! (really, really good food) she walked over to my wife and said "I am so sorry that your daughter had to sit on your lap during your lunch, for your inconvenience we have taken your meal and drinks off of the bill, please accept our apology again". Such professionalism and class! On top of the good food, they obviously have good people that work for them.

Thank you again Honor Bar for unknowingly turning around what had started to become a frustrating road trip.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Honestly this used to be my go to burrito joint. I paid almost $10 for a basic breakfast burrito, it was mostly tortilla, and the meat they put in was way overcooked. I was the only guy waiting for food and it still took 20 minutes to get done, and the salsa bar was mediocre. My friends went 2 days later and had some seriously underwhelming meals as well. Such a bummer. I won't go back

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: The best coffee and pastries in town. My absolute favorite is the princess cake (marzipan). It is amazingly delicious!!!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Beautiful open and sunny space.
Food is excellent, great flavors and generous portions. The chips were the only mediocre thing about the place. Would come again for sure.

Categories: Juice Bars & Smoothies, Local Flavor, Food | Review: If you're looking for fresh juice without sugar or other artificial ingredients, check out Juice Ranch. 

There are a variety of bottled juices to choose from and a menu that lists the ingredients in each with the health benefit of the drink.  

I ordered the Jalepina and my boyfriend ordered the Greens N Ginger. Both drinks were super tasty, and neither had too bold of an ingredient (Ie. The jalapeño wasn't too overwhelming and neither was the ginger). 

Each juice comes in a glass bottle that costs $2 on top of the drink price. If you return with the bottle, you can turn it in for $2 back or use it for another drink purchase. 

I'd definitely return to Juice Ranch for a healthy, refreshing juice.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Tex-Mex, American (Traditional), Burgers, Food, Sandwiches, Mexican, Juice Bars & Smoothies | Review: This area of State Street has plenty of restaurants with outdoor dining in the street, which is closed to car traffic. The Natural Cafe is one of them. Food is ordered inside and brought out to you. My veggie quesadilla came with a side salad and choice of dressing. Husband got the cabo fish tacos. The food was reasonable, tasty, and plentiful!

Categories: Asian Fusion, Seafood, Restaurants, Poke, Food | Review: Amaaazing Poke bowls! I love how you can customize your bowl anyway you please which is always a plus when you want to try different combinations! It's like a subway but for seafood lovers! 

Will definitely be back!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: I usually travel to SB for work. Decided to give this place a try. I went on a Thursday at 12:30pm and it was dead. Only one person there. Had the small fish and chips. Not that impressive. The fish was standard, I wouldn't make the effort to stop by. It was average.

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Jazz & Blues, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: Theyyyyyyy're baaaaaccckkk!!! After being closed due to the pandemic, The Imperial have reopened. The mixologist are thrilled to be  back, serving up yummy drinks and advice.

The  Imperial gives off that 70's vibe with a speakeasy feel. It actually jogs my memory of my parents living room of when I was a teen.
No, my parents didn't serve up drinks in their living room. 

The drinks  are amazingly tasty. Cucumber gimlet, Imperial Mai Tai for me, and an Old Fashion for the hubs; each  made with care and love by the mixologist.

The vibe of this establishment is friendly and homey. You can plop yourself down on big lounge couch, booth, sit in the patio or belly up to the bar. My choice is the bar. You can exchange banters with the bartender or fellow patrons.

There's nothing negative about this establishment. Ample parking and hidden  for those of you who are secret drunkies. LOL Just an all around amazing bar.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Event Planning & Services, Food, Caterers | Review: Contrary to a couple reviews, the customer service here was fantastic. The girl working (I can't remember her name) was so nice and helpful and gave us so many options since it was our first time. We ended up getting a milk chocolate chip (?? I think that's the flavor) and banana malt and holy moly it was HUGE!! Not to mention delicious as well. I just HATE the paper straws. Kinda useless after like 5 slurps. But overall great experience!

Categories: Food, Specialty Food, Seafood, Cajun/Creole, Restaurants | Review: What a fun dinning experience.  The food was very tasty. A bit pricy. Drinks were fairly priced and delicious. The shrimp is my favorite. Great overall experience.  Will be back.
Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Love this place, very authentic. Inexpensive. Best Chile Verde burrito in SB

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: Mouth watering, utterly amazing, jaw dropping good sushi here!  From the moment we stepped into this cute little restaurant, I was pleasantly pleased.  The manager was at the hostess podium, he greeted us immediately and promptly seated us with a huge smile.  Overall the customer service here is superb.  

All the rolls that we ordered was made with fresh ingredients, any good sushi eater can taste the fresh fish used in each roll.  The presentation of each roll was simple and elegantly presented with a smile from the sushi chef.

I thought the pricing was decent and not to expensive, but I'm sure others might disagree.  In my opinion, the price is worth the fresh ingredients used in there food.  The decor of the restaurant was simple and modernly chic.  

Overall, this place is yum-o and I shall return on my next visit back up here.

Categories: German, Restaurants | Review: It's a kitchy space with an authentic feel, owned and operated by actual Germans, and I have no comments on the food, as I was unable to sample it. The service was appalling, even by central european standards. Two friends and I arrived around 7:30 on a Saturday night and the place was full, but we were promised a table would clear out in about five minutes. My party and I waited 20 minutes for a table, which would have been fine if the owner/server hadn't unapologetically left us to dodge other customers in a small walkway directly in front of the bathrooms and made faces at us for not making reservations ahead of time. We eventually were seated, and, after taking a few minutes to make up our minds, tried to get the attention of the wait staff for an additional 20 minutes. We thought Veronika would finally turn to us but then watched in horror as instead she went over to the CD player, turned on a song, and proceeded to waltz around the room trying to get people to sing while placing filthy hats on their heads. As she approached, she seemed surprised we wanted to order, declaring it was "our time," gesturing to herself and some fellow song singers. Deciding we'd had enough, we walked out and went down the street to Flavor of India instead.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: Came here for mussels and some appetizers but the service was extremely slow and not to mention forgetful. The mussels weren't as good as they were before but I guess you can attribute that to different chefs working or something. Still waiting for the check to be delivered as I write this.

Categories: Beer Bar, Breakfast & Brunch, Vegan, Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: I've been in here more times than I can count, and for the most part I always had a good time. The beer garden is great and it's always a good friendly vibe. Last night, I went in for a beer and I hit I in my vape in the beer garden. I always hit my vape back there and no one's ever told me it was against policy. Last night I was kicked out for hitting my vape, and the bar tender couldn't have been more rude and inconsiderate about it. I mean come on man, did you wake up on the wrong side of bed. I've seen people drunkenly stumble in and get noticeably belligerent. I had to awkwardly walk out, after been declined my beer and while people sitting at tables stared at me. Good Job policing the likely evils of my vape considering the pain, trauma, and hell it brought on the innocent customers of Modern Times.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: DO NOT EAT HERE! 

You would be better going across the street and finding a dead fish on the sand. No joke. My husband and I waited almost 40 minutes for our breakfast. If I had a dollar for every time the waitress said "sorry" to us and everyone around us, I would be a millionaire! Not only was the service awful, but the food was just as bad. Greasy bacon, little muffins that tasted like stale bread and potatoes were at least a week old. 

No wonder they once had over 1,200 locations and now have....one?! That speaks volumes. Don't be fooled. Upon leaving and using the restroom, the odor was fowl, the floors were a mess and in the back hallway there was trash lined all over the floors. Even the 11 year old little girl waiting to use the toilet decided against going and "holding it" to find another place!! 

Sam Battistone, the original owner, would be flipping over in his grave if he saw what a  terrible place it turned out to be. Many, many, many, many other choices in the area, don't waste your time and forget about the nostalgia.....it is dead and gone.

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: We went there for the first time today and both had the General Sanders because it looked so good. It was as good as it looked and we'll be back to try some of the other menu items that look equally tempting.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: Quite simply the best sushi I've ever had. Beautiful presentation, and the restaurants's atmospheres feels modern and lively. The "Bay City Roll" and Fluk n' Crab" are magical!

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Beer Bar, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Food Delivery Services, Food, Restaurants | Review: I'm sure this place is good for lunch, on a beautiful day.  But we went on a chilly night and sat under the kind of tent you sit under at an outdoor wedding.  You not only have to look at the menu on your phone (which a lot of restaurants do now) but you have to order on your phone.  You can't just tell your waiter what you want.  As a result, despite following the instructions - asking for two of our items as appetizers and two as main course, everything came out together.  Our waiter couldn't come to us and ask if that's the way we really wanted it?  We could have told him no.  But we never saw our waiter again once he greeted us.  Wine at twelve dollars for 8 ounces is served in a plastic glass, as are the cocktails.  Their best whiskey?  Jack Daniels.  I haven't drank that swill since college.  The very generous tip is added for you even though it isn't deserved.  Food is served on paper plates!  The prices are upscale, the experience is not.  The food is a solid B.

Categories: Irish Pub, Nightlife, Bars, Pubs | Review: Cool staff, good atmosphere, and none of that wannabe upscale dance club ish that fails miserably like Tonic. But maybe that's just my style - I like the drive bar types. If you catch it when there's a live band this is probably the place to be in Santa Barbara. Usually one of the least crowded spots and the super long bar ensures you don't have to maul people out of the way just to get a drink. 

Only downside might be the sometimes older crowd and creepers. Try to go college nights (Thursdays). This is my spot in Santa Barbara, I've started many a black-outs here.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: Eos is one of the better clubs in Santa Barbara. It features a dance floor, indoor/outdoor bar, lounge area that has sofas and tables, and an outdoor area with seating available. Outside can get pretty croweded on a warm night, so head to the indoor bar to buy your drinks for quicker service and fewer elbows.
The decor is intended to be Mediterranean. While I'm not sure it achieves this, it's not bad. I've seen worse attemps at style in Santa Barbara clubs. They do showcase different artists on their walls, and sometimes have special events featuring art.
The resident DJ's play a pretty good selection of electronic music. Guest DJ's tend to be well selected too. When I feel like dancing this is my top choice. 
Drinks run about $6- $7. There is never a cover on Thusday nights as this is college night in Santa Barbara meaning no covers. Sometimes on Saturdays there is a $5 cover.
The crowd seems to be more mid-20's to mid-30's. Some college students come here too, but not in the same high numbers you see at other clubs.
It's locate close to State Street where most bars and clubs are in Santa Barbara. So if you feel like bar hopping, it can be included in the evening.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food | Review: Surprisingly great mint chip. More creamy than sweet. The waffle cone was a winner. A bit pricey, but expected on the pier and the scoops are huge/splittable.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Cafes, Coffee Roasteries, Coffee & Tea | Review: They added some yummy looking breads for toast to their pastry selection,  I will be getting that when I return for a breakfast latte.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Santo Mezcal is an upper scale restaurant by the same people nas Los Agaves, another chain of local restaurants here in Santa Barbara. Having had almost every dish at Los Agaves, I came in subconsciously comparing it to their dishes. The quedo fundido is deeelish! Also had the pulpo (octopus) as the main dish and it was very well spiced and tender. Drinks were on point and their salsa was definitely more flavorful. 

Above all though, their service was excellent.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: My friends and I were craving some Thai food, so we all decided to try TAP! 

Food: I love the complementary shrimp chips that are super fresh! I ordered the pad Thai, while my friends ordered the Drunken Noodles, yellow curry, an pineapple fried rice. The noodles in the Pad Thai was flavorful, but the chicken pieces were a bit bland. However, I had a genius idea and dipped it in the sweet chili hot sauce. I had a small taste of the curry, and it was very creamy and rich with coconut milk. The drunken noodles had a good kick of spiciness. Overall, the food was yummy. 

Interior: There are nice hand painted art on the wall. It doesn't have a "traditional Thai" restaurant feel to it. Instead, it has a modern touch of a Thai cafe, which I like. 

Tip: Make sure to check in on yelp to get a free gigantic thai iced tea that comes in a mason jar!

Categories: Bars, Seafood, Food, Cocktail Bars, Steakhouses, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: Service is good.
Food is just good.
Trying so hard to be amazing it shows.
Food portions are small.
Al la carte menu. So vegetables are separate like its a five star restaurant,which it is not.
My biggest problem here is the food portions and quality is way over priced.
Oh well since I live far from this place I won't be back.

Categories: Dance Clubs, Nightlife, Bars | Review: After my friends celebratory birthday dinner the gang decided they were in the mood for some dancing so the WIldcat lounge was just the place to go. There is a $5 cover to get in, not bad. It's not a huge place just a few booths on the right when you walk in, and the bar on the left. In the back there are a couple more rooms and the dance floor. We got there around 10:30 so the place was not too busy. Around 11 the dance floor started filling up. They had a DJ there playing crowd pleasing tunes, and a couple girls dancing on small, raised private stages. I'm told it gets packed later in the evening but  we didn't stick around too long. Fun place to go if you want to dance the night away.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Wish I could give 0 stars, the staff and service is an abomination. Rude, snobby, arrogant and the list goes on. Had a party of 30 coming in for a wedding and I'm glad we got to see the true colors of the staff before our event. Will NEVER waste a single dime here and suggest you do the same. Hung up on me multiple times trying to clarify details with them. Use this as a warning

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Stumbled across this place spontaneously and i am so glad that i did! Sandwiches are a lil pricey but trust.. its totally worth the quality and experience! My boyfriend and i shared the crispy chicken west and chicken & walnut pesto. Both were lit AF. The decor of the place was very young, hip & trendy. I loved how they had cute wooden game boards on each table. It definitely kept us entertained while waiting for our food. Great experience and A1 quality food. Wish there was one of these in the Bay Area!

Categories: Wine Bars, Restaurants, American (New), Nightlife, Seafood, Cocktail Bars, Bars | Review: Nice little spot... While up in SB for the anniversary 'staycation', we finally got to Hungry Cat (always thought I'd try mu local LA location first, but alas, it was time to get Goin).

Started with Oysters on the half, and they were superb.  Fantastic first impression. Thought about the urchin, but I'm not THAT brave quite yet.  Had a hand-crafted cocktail that was awesome as well... Also did a platter, and finished with the famous Beard Award-winning Bread Pudding, which I coudnt stop eating even though I was full. 

-1* for service; not terrible, but not stellar. I wasn't wow'ed, I guess I should I say

Can't wait to do LA next week!

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Awesome place. We came for dinner and we had an amazing time! 

We got the ceviche verde. I had never had halibut ceviche but it was amazingly fresh and just the right amount of tangy. 
We got the octopus as our main dish. The octopus was very tender. It was the best octopus I had ever had.
We had the flan for dessert which I highly recommend. Great way to end the meal. 

Overall the food was amazing. The service was good and the complimentary chips and dip was above touch. 

Tip: the portion size, particularly of the appetizer was on the larger side. An appetizer and main dish for the two of us was plenty of food.

Categories: Food, Coffee Roasteries, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Salad, Sandwiches | Review: I was really excited to visit Dean: A Coffee Shop because there is a lack of good local coffee shops in Goleta - especially ones with ample seating.

I love the airy feel of the cafe. It's bright, open, and has many seating options (indoors and outdoors). The color scheme reminds me of Copa Vida in San Diego. 

The baristas are very friendly and attentive to when customers enter and have questions (5/5).

I originally was looking for a pour over but was told that they didn't offer it, so ordered an Americano and an Iced Oat Milk Matcha. I was surprised that by the time I finished paying and sanitizing my hands, that my drinks were already ready. The speediness made me wonder if the drinks were already pre-made.

The price of the drinks were $5+ which I'm willing to dish out for a local shop; however, I didn't feel like the taste of the drinks lived up to the price.

Americano: 1/5
The Americano tasted bland and overly diluted. I couldn't taste the flavor of the beans which was disappointing. Nevertheless, this can be an easy fix by adjusting the water to espresso ratio.

Iced Oat Milk Matcha: 2.5/5
Dean's Oat Milk Matcha was very sweet for my taste. The matcha flavor was there but I would prefer an option that was unsweetened. I was also surprised by how large the portion was for this drink and how fast it came out. For a Oat Milk Matcha, it wasn't bad, but it wasn't the best I've had.

I'll be back again to try their other drinks, fingers crossed that the other ones are good!

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: A group of 8 of us went here on a Saturday night. We did have to wait a bit for a reservation, but it was worth the wait. Food was good, service was really nice.

Categories: Seafood, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Restaurants, Burgers | Review: Love this place. Great food made fresh. From the delicious tacos to the tasty breakfast bowls....eating here is great any time of the day.
Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis | Review: Had an amazing, beautiful, and kind server named Ava C. I would 10/10 recommend this fine establishment

Categories: Salad, Restaurants, Pizza, Sandwiches, Italian | Review: Great atmosphere and servers are very nice!  Love the food Green salad with ranch was delicious and the old Chicago deep dish pizza was amazing!

Categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: I have never written a negative review before, but couldn't believe what we were told over a cheap free roll. We decided to go here for a quick lunch while getting Starbucks. Just checked in and the free roll for spending $20 or more, I was told "we don't do that cause the manager said not to offer it anymore, and we don't do yelp." Really? Then take it down off yelp and don't do false advertising. We spend 50$ by the way. Sushi was not that good... No wonder it has bad reviews.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Active Life, Golf | Review: Come on SP... you can do better!  
I regret joining this year with the extremely poor playing conditions.  SP used to be a "tolerable" place to golf but right now it really is a waste of money.  My advice is to cancel the club house plans and focus on bringing this golf course back to life.  What a missed opportunity.  Disappointed preferred club member and neighbor.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: Great poke but a little expensive for what you get! If you're looking for a quick meal it's a good place for it. Nice and clean place!

Categories: Lounges, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants, Hookah Bars, American (New) | Review: I was looking on Yelp and decided to try Blush for our big Saturday Bachelorette dinner. The vibe and ambiance is very trendy and cool, however when we got there I was unpleasantly surprised with the service. Every little thing took very long! From us getting sat to the ordering of the drinks to the getting the tab split was a struggle. I do, keep  in mind there was a big group outside but all their attention seemed to be on them and took us 2 hours to eat and get our check paid for 5 girls. Also when I wanted a refill on my second jack and diet, it took so long that they brought it after the check was paid. Despite the lack of service, the Blush burger was excellent and the scampi was delicious. I was disappointed in their Brussel sprouts because it had no prosciutto in it when it was said to be on the menu (that was also late).

Categories: Mediterranean, Restaurants, Salad, Greek | Review: It definitely feels corporate which is strange being in Santa Barbara where everything feels very homemade mom and pop. I was excited to see some of the different menu options for Mediterranean style food. It was good, but seemed a little bit overpriced given the fast food corporate kind of style, feel and taste of the restaurant
Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: average Thai food. nothing spectacular peanut sauce was lackluster.

Categories: Lounges, Bars, American (New), Nightlife, Modern European, Restaurants | Review: Quite simply, if they offer you a table upstairs in the "loft" wait for a table downstairs.  The atmosphere upstairs is horrible unless you have a large group.  You feel isolated and like you are at the kids table.  The food was OK.  Pan seared sea scallops were abit salty.  the short ribs were a bit overcooked and the sauce was a little burnt - and therefore sticky.  The drinks taste like they have no alcohol yet were $10 each.  I must say the service was pretty good after a slow start.  There are much better restaurants in Santa Barbara

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: The food is very tasty. It's a good location. Bad Service.

The restaurant was never full or busy, yet none of our drinks or food came out at the same time. It's a small restaurant and shouldn't be too hard to manage staff.

Our evening started with cocktails. I ordered a Fitzgerald. The waitress said she never heard of it.  I asked if she wanted me to google the recipe and she said yes. So I did. I was thinking she could have taken the order and googled the recipe behind the bar. I didn't really mind, just an observation. (very simple drink, gin, club soda, simple syrup, lemon and bitters) 

She took our drink order to the bartender who seemed annoyed. (not necessarily at us, but we don't know) The bartender made one drink and brought it over, and then went back to bar to make the next drink. When she came with each of the drinks, no smile, no words. She wasn't friendly or personable. She set them down and that was it.

We ordered an appetizer to share and we each ordered an entree. After the appetizer only one entree was brought to us. We waited for a few minutes not wanting to start eating until we both had our entree and flagged the waitress over when it didn't arrive. She said, "I meant to tell you we serve tapas style, so the next entree won't come out until you finish this one". 

That was a surprise.

Had we known that all meals were to be shared, we would have discussed different options. Luckily we both were okay with what the other had ordered. But what if we would have ordered the same thing?  At $29.00 and $30.00 for the entrees, I think we should have been told that important piece of information. The portion size was between what one would usually expect of an appetizer and and actual entree. 

This was my second time at Sama Sama. I really hope they do some training with their staff. A smile and making people feel like invited guests goes a long way.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: We ordered the shrimp chips which were passable and came with some tasty sambal, peanut sauce and some kind of garlic paste.  The mee goring came next and it was so salty we literally couldn't eat it.  It looked and tasted like undercooked ramen noodles soaked in oil and heavy soy.  Next was the nasi goreng which was overcooked and seemed more like risotto.  It was passable but still barely edible.

My opinion may be jaded by the fact I actually lived in Indo and know what these foods should taste like.  None of the classic spices and tastes of the cuisine was in our meals.  It may pass as tasty if I was drunk and had the munchies.  Most of the other customers seemed more interested in drinking than eating (although they didn't even serve Bintang).

Service was good but didn't matter because of the food.

If you want real Indo food, don't come here.

Categories: Dive Bars, Bars, Nightlife, Karaoke | Review: I love dive bars, but this place is not for me. The bartenders are terrible at paying attention to the patrons. The crowd isn't fun. The floor is ALWAYS wet. You are basically asking for a hangover with their drinks.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Irish, Nightlife, Pubs, Gastropubs | Review: There's not much one can say about an Irish bar that hasn't been said before.  However, I would ask for the following to change:
- Turn. The. Music. Down.  It's bad enough that the tunes are driven by a digital juke box, and any sorority lush can throw on some terrible, terrible dance music in an "pub like" atmosphere, but it's worse when the music is entirely too loud for it's own good.  
- I say "pub like" because this isn't a pub.  Pubs are chilled out and the only din that is rising is that of the patrons, not a booming sound system.  No, this is a bar and--unfortunately--it's verging on a club with the stupidity of the music selections

The size is great.  The decor is perfect.  Everything else about this place is fantastic, but you're treading a very fine line to becoming a pathetic excuse for a club.  Lose the juke box and you should be starting down the right road.
Categories: Sandwiches, Vegan, Cafes, Vegetarian, Restaurants | Review: Delicious vegetable drinks. Beets and kale and cucumber and so on. Go get yourself a boost!

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Wonderful food and service!  The lemon grass salmon was superb!  Service was exceptional. They even threw a nice gesture of putting happy anniversary on our dessert plate for me and my spouse.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: After a night out at Wildcat nothing satisfies my post club late night munchies more than the Milo burrito filled with chicken, black beans, sweet platano, and queso fresco. During the day you can also find delicious meals of tacos, tortas, and tamales all reasonably priced

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Lounges, Seafood, Spanish, Italian, Nightlife, Mediterranean | Review: Stopped in with a group of seven for a bachelorette party dinner and our group was split as far as satisfaction. Three enjoyed their food, three did not and I was indifferent.

I'll note that the three women happy with their meals only ordered flatbreads and a burger, nothing spectacular. The others ordered the halibut, chicken and scallops. 

I had the branzino and while it was cooked nicely, I just didn't enjoy the flavor of the sauce. We all split the lemon tart for dessert and I found it to be disappointing as well.  Out of our entire group, no one said they would be interested in coming back.

I can say the service was great! We were running late for our reservation and they were happy to bump us half an hour to the next time slot.

Our server was extremely attentive (but with seven women sitting at a table what guy wouldn't be haha). The drinks were strong and service was quick.

Beautiful ambiance in the restaurant just wish the food was better.

Categories: Pizza, Restaurants, Italian, Vegan, Gluten-Free, Sandwiches | Review: Mesa pizza is the best, they have a nice guy working there that hooked me up with breadsticks free of charge. He was really personable. I always go here for pizza and will continue to. I'm reviewing only because he asked but happy to support a local business.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: The best chicken fried steak I've ever had in my life! Unreal. I eat it whenever I see it on the menu but wow... I will be back here every time I come to Santa Barbara. By far the best meal I've had here I'm Santa Barbara. Our server Jesse was fantastic as well. The patio was the perfect place to sit. Kudos to the chef and staff!
Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Great coconut shrimp appetizer and an outstanding rib-eye steak. Excellent service and location.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Burgers, American (New) | Review: Eureka is definitely the best stop in Santa Barbara for either a quick bite after shopping in Paseo Nuevo or exploring state st. Elaine along with my server Will were great at hosting me to where I would be dining this afternoon and explaining the menu to perfection! The food was exceptional and came out very quickly! They also have a great prices, and a huge beer selection on which I'm fond of! If your questioning where to eat in downtown Santa Barbara, save some time and head straight to Eureka!

Categories: Asian Fusion, Seafood, Restaurants, Poke, Food | Review: Super yummy poke bowl ever! Ordered online n picked up. Very friendly service, fresh seafoods and colorful design inside. Good place makes good food leads a good emotion. AWESOME!

Categories: Restaurants, Indian, Pakistani | Review: I want to make this as short as I can. 

No. Just no. I know you're all thinking, I'm Indian, of course, she'll say no. HOWEVER, I'm saying no because:

1. We waited 10 minutes before anyone took our order and drinks (which I would have wanted first and foremost).

2. When my Pa asked for a recommendation of what lamb dish to get, the server didn't know how to answer this question and had to get someone else.

3. We waited 40 minutes before we actually got our food.

4. No rice came with our dishes and we had to pay extra for rice.

5. The serving was so small that you could eat one whole serving yourself and still be hungry.

6. We asked for medium spice and it was extremely mild.

7. No one came to check to see if we were ok when we weren't.

There are more reasons why but why waste more space. They are a bunch of racists that treat Indian customers like crap but treat their white customers like God. 

I can't believe this place got a Zagat rating. I want everyone to know this place was bad, service wise and food wise. 

DO NOT GO HERE!!!!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, American (Traditional) | Review: Quick service & friendly as well :) We were super happy with our experience here! The food is really good & staff is so friendly and quick about things. They do get busy so expect to wait a little.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I LOVE this place! Their freshly made chips and avocado salsa are to die for. I used to hit up Lillys once a week before finding this place, but no more. Monys stole my taste buds away!

Categories: Mexican, Vegan, Restaurants, Vegetarian | Review: Thankful for more vegan options in SB! If you love Chipotle you will LOVE the vegan version of Vegan GreenGo! You have the option to pick your protein, a bowl, salad, or burrito and your sauces and fix ins. Must try their jackfruit nachos! They are a tad spicy so if you can't handle spice choose a different protein. The perfect on the go Vegan Mexican food that doesn't break the bank!

Categories: Food Trucks, Event Planning & Services, Mexican, Street Vendors, Caterers, Restaurants, Food | Review: They catered our graduation party and it was delicious as well as affordable. After getting quotes from other taco catering companies they were the least expensive. Great at responding via email and phone.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Everything was delicious from start to finish! Fresh chips and 3 delicious salsas are included with your meal. The white sangria was bubbly and refreshing. Only one bathroom however which was a minor inconvenience.

Categories: Garage Door Services, Restaurants, Live/Raw Food, Food, Home Services, Mediterranean, Keys & Locksmiths, Burgers, Vegetarian, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Vegan, Salad | Review: Started off bad by ended up so good I will definitely come back again. Firstly - great menu and really good quality of food and prices are reasonable. I think a lot of the items are organically sourced, and I'm vegan so the menu was awesome for me. My friend is not vegan and was happy for me to choose the dished and we shared -something I can never do in a conventional restaurant. We had potatas bravas and it was tasty and well presented. We also shared the BLAT and that was really good to - I liked the bun it was served on and even though we didn't need more food, I wanted to try the baclava. First thing my friend said was "that's really good ice-cream" and it was soy ice-cream and really delicious and the baclava was heavenly.
So 10/10 for the food. Ordered chai tea and it was very strong and got cold quickly. 
The only reason it started out bad was we were totally ignored on arrival. It was busy - 1.50pm and 3 parties in front of us waiting. We stood for 10 minutes (yes, I timed it) and did not get acknowledged once. No smile, no eye contact, no explanation - just left to stand while we waited for the staff to clear tables and get people seated. I watched 3 tables get seated - not one was given a menu until another few minutes had passed.
