So not only do you stand and wait patiently, you have to wait again for a server to come and give you a menu - surely that could have been done so at least you are sitting and have something to do. It was 2.10 before we were seated with a menu in front of us. That's a long time to wait. 
Anyway - the food was great and the service was ok. That's better than the other way around.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Empty Bowl Gourmet Noodle is a Santa Barbara staple, located in the Public Market, even after all of the switch outs of the many little eateries in the Public Market, Empty Bowl has stood on top. 

Owners and Chefs Nui Pannak, Jerry Lee, and Emre Balli infused street foods in their own way from Thailand and Taiwan all the way to Santa Barbara, changing the way people see the culinary status of SB. Interesting enough, Co-owners Jerry and Emre are Certified sommeliers. They offer an exclusive wine pairing with their handmade dumplings which are delightful.

THE FOOD-

Every time I come here, I always start off with their Spring Rolls ($9.95), the peanut sauce that is presented with the rolls is excellent and make it such a good starter.

As far as their ramen goes, I usually order their Bangkok Street Noodle ($14.95), or their Long Tail Boat Noodle ($14.95)

For pastas, I like to order their Tom Yum Noodles ($13.95), and their Hangover Noodles ($13.95).

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

This is a go-to spot for anyone that loves ramen and noodles. I would definitely recommend Empty Bowl to everyone. Five stars all the way.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: I miss good English pub food and this is some of the closest I've had in California

The fish and chips were good   The fish was large and the batter was tasty   It's the chips I had a tiny issue with.  I prefer less crunch and more potato flavour  on the other hand they had curry sauce  

The staff is lovely.

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: What are you doing?!?!!! The Yuzo wings went from ...in my opinion BEST IN CALIFORNIA to competing with Wing Stop. Whoever's idea this was should be fired. Sorry guys, stop listening to the woke.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: It is so gooood here. I love how they have tons of choices and they don't skimp. It's so satisfying and everything is fresh!! I definitely recommend!! Also you don't feel  fat after eating here, which is always a plus!

Categories: Juice Bars & Smoothies, Breakfast & Brunch, Brazilian, Restaurants, Food, Salad, Acai Bowls | Review: BAC salad with tri tip is my favorite salad in town. Great portions and lots of deliciously seasoned tri tip!

Categories: Food, Hawaiian, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, American (New) | Review: I got a torikatsu plate to-go with jasmine rice, macaroni salad, and mixed organic greens. It looked better than it actually tasted (and I paid about $11). The pieces of pork were pretty thin, but I liked the sauce! I was mainly disappointed at the rice. I love firm white rice; however, the rice I ate was almost too hard to eat. The texture wasn't very pleasing, but I've only tried this place once so maybe it's not a recurring thing.

Categories: Asian Fusion, Seafood, Restaurants, Poke, Food | Review: I am a returning customer already impressed with the quality of food. The quality of service this time around was outstanding. Amanda was so helpful in letting us taste what we wanted and what we were not too sure about. She even offered us a sample of the leechee slushii. We were highly impressed by Amanda's service and wanted to ensure PokeCeviche was aware of the 5 star employee that served us. If you want good food and great service check them out!!

Categories: Pakistani, Restaurants, Buffets, Seafood, Halal, Indian, Vegetarian, Vegan, Food, Salad, Desserts | Review: Great, cheap buffet, and excellent service which seems to be difficult for buffet places. They kept bringing the naan and drinks without hesitation, i love that! Best Indian in SB, id say.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breweries, American (Traditional), Brewpubs | Review: Ate here with party of seven tonight, May 1. Everyone was happy with their food, excepting one who didn't care for the chili. Pizza, burgers, meat loaf, soup, salads all good (Minor critique on the lack of chunks in blue cheese dressing). Service was prompt and attentive. Prices are competitive. Obviously, good selection of beer. I had a coffee-flavored stout that was very good.

Categories: Chinese, Restaurants, Szechuan | Review: Worst Chinese food I've ever had. No wonder this place was empty. 
The "chefs" came out from behind the cashier station and seemed bothered to have to work. One was coughing the other stared at us as he walked by. 
We ordered General Tsos chicken and combination chow mein. 
The chicken came out in record time... because it was RAW! We kindly asked if they can throw it back in since we were still waiting on the other dish anyway. And asked for white rice (dry asf). 
The chow mein came out- chicken was undercooked, the one shrimp piece was undercooked and the beef looked microwaved. The whole thing was too greasy and flavor less. 
The General Tsos comes back out- still Undercooked!! The picture attached is it the SECOND time. Smh. 
I ate the tiny pieces I could bc I was too hungry and getting angry. 
Also- don't let the white cloth tables fool you. The ones they actually use are covered in paper and filthy. The soy sauce has a nice layer of dirt and dust and the chili oil sauce is all oil and also filthy. 
$32 later- I have to go cook my own chicken, am still hungry and have a stomach ache.

Categories: American (Traditional), Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Such a cool place. Off the beaten path for sure, but definitely still busy and for good reason. The history is awesome and the food is even better. Highly recommend the tri tip French dip. The only disappointment was the $2 upgrade to onion rings with my sandwich came with literally 4 onion rings. They're delicious, but I expected more than 4. Next time will order them as a starter instead. All in all, it's fantastic. Expect to wait. Got there 20 minutes before they opened and still had to wait almost half an hour for a table. It's popular for a reason.

Categories: Salad, Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, Food Delivery Services, Personal Chefs, Caterers, Food | Review: Amazing experience!!! Perfect for this quarantine. The service is awesome and the food was incredible. Highly recommend!!

Categories: Dance Clubs, Nightlife, Bars | Review: This place is a lot of fun. The owners are nice. Usually a $5 cover charge. The music is average, about usual for any place in Santa Barbara. But this is the place we always go by the end of the night, with the best mix of people. Upholstered red walls are really cool, the place has really nice decorations. Go-go dancers occassionally (though I've never been fond  of them). Outside area with booths and another bar where you can smoke. There are two sets of bathrooms in the hallways leading to outside, so if there's a line at one you can usually go right into the other. Sunday is gay night, but it's not very big.

Categories: Health Markets, Coffee & Tea, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Restaurants, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, American (New), Specialty Food | Review: I'm a huge Blenders fan and decided to spice up my repertoire with this local alternative. Didn't encounter the long lines at 3:00pm, the Berry Bowl was quite tasty and really large portions even for the "small" bowl. It's just not for me. 

Pros: Fresh berries, innovative menu options, friendly customer service. For the ingredients and portions, I feel the price is reasonable. 

Cons: I don't like granolas with clusters, but this could be my own personal vendetta. Flavor got too repetitive after 5-6 bites. 

Despite the reasonable price, I probably wouldn't risk going back to order something again unless I had a friend to share it with. This is due to not enjoying my huge Berry Bowl (size small) and forced myself to finish it so it wouldn't go to waste.

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: Came in during happy hour on Friday night with the wifey and 3 little guys.  Restaurant was very busy but service was excellent. Waitress was very attentive and friendly. Immediate refills of drinks, bread, etc.  She never skipped a beat. After reading some mediocre reviews, I expected the food to be  the same. I had the grilled seafood skewer. It was great. Scallops were top notch, shrimp was cooked just right.  Very flavorful. Excellent deserts too! Great atmosphere, noisy, upbeat, good music. The kitchen is right in the middle of the dining area with glass partitions so you can see everything going on. Priced slightly steep (i.e. $18 for a $15 plate) but overall a good choice.  I guess the pricing goes with the territory.  It's Santa Barbara for goodness sakes.
Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars | Review: Wow amazing find, great quality fish and flavors. Even better than many sit down places.

Categories: Fast Food, Delis, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: Every time I have been here they get my order wrong  this place is completely incompetent. If you want to order do it in person so you can watch and correct cause even my on line orders have been wrong and it's written down! Can't blame that on not hearing me properly. It's so sad

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: Super fun, super loud on a busy night.  everything is well done and the setting can't be beat in Santa Barbara.  A place for the young at heart.  These people love what they do, have great energy and want you to enjoy yourself.  

Take a moment to watch everyone working in the kitchen.  Just a great vibe to see everyone working/playing and kicking out some really interesting combinations.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: Came here on a Thursday night during a fall trip to Santa Barbara.  There was a 30 minute wait at 8 pm, place was bustling.  We decided to eat our meal at the bar rather than wait for a table.  
The three bartenders were great! They had great recommendations for drinks and for the items on the menu.  Ordered a couple of tapas (the octopus was great!).  Then ordered the chorizo and pollo paella.  The paella was just okay - i was underwhelmed.  Normally the rice is flavorful from the broth and from saffron.  This one seemed lacking in flavor.
Overall, service was great.  I might come back and try something else on the menu next time.

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: BE WARNED!!!! Just finished breakfast at Anderson's on State street, SB. HONESTLY the food was Terrible and the service was even Worse!!! We've been going to Anderson's for years, and supported them through Covid, I often talk to the owner's daughter Charlotte, who generally is very pragmatic and sweet however today since so much went south on our order, I went up to her to let her know about the super screwed up order, not only was she rude but very unprofessional! All we were trying to achieve was get our right order, but we were told we'd have to wait an additional 15 minutes...!!!! When they screwed up the Orders in the first place. We ordered the champagne kit, which we received after our food was almost finished , then we ordered 2 breakfast croissant sandwiches, they only brought 1 and we ordered 2 crab Benedicts, we only got one. Also we ordered , 1 slice of their princess cake and raspberry linzer, which we never received at all. My husband had to go inside to either get a refund or get it to go!!! UGH!!! What made the situation worse is that Charlotte's rude attitude, she proceeded to tell me that she had a full restaurant and that she was busy...hummm, really! I thought I was one of those paying customers. I'm assuming most people go out to eat, for something different that they cannot make at home and good service...Right, or else what's the point of eating out and spending $170.00 on breakfast!!!! I usually never write anything negative, bcs I believe shit happen and every day is different however when the the food and service is awfully and the owners have an unprofessional shitty attitude, DON'T GO, it will be a waste of your MONEY and TIME. The food is honestly not good. It's more of a tourist type of restaurant, nothing special! I regret going there and NEVER will go again.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: This is one of my romantic, trendy favorites in SB, although the ingredients have slipped a bit, and the wait staff is unpredictable.
Categories: Restaurants, Indian, Himalayan/Nepalese | Review: Great food, service and atmosphere under new ownership. The chicken momos were nicely spicy and authentic. Yum!

Categories: Dance Clubs, Nightlife, Bars | Review: I don't have a problem with people just doing their job, but I do have a problem with people who are dicks. The bouncers at wild cat are on power trips, and completely out of line. The bar itself is pretty fun, but my experience with the bouncers alone is enough for me to never go back.
